# SOC Standard Operating Procedures (Consolidated)

> This document is a consolidated export of all SOPs, Playbooks, and Templates.



---

## File: AGENTS.md
# AGENTS.md вҖ” SOC Standard Operating Procedures Repository

> This file is the **single source of truth** for all AI agents and human contributors working on this repository.
> It is symlinked to: `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`

---

## рҹҺҜ Project Identity

### WHY вҖ” Purpose
This repository provides **vendor-agnostic, production-ready SOC Standard Operating Procedures** that any organization can adopt to build or mature their Security Operations Center. All content follows NIST, MITRE ATT&CK, and ISO 27001/27035 frameworks.

### WHAT вҖ” Scope
- 53 Incident Response Playbooks (PB-01 to PB-53)
- Operations Management (Shift Handoff, KPIs, Team Structure, CTI, TLP)
- Detection Engineering (Sigma rules, YARA signatures)
- Simulation & Purple Teaming
- Training & Onboarding Curriculum
- Executive Reporting Templates
- All content is **bilingual** (English + Thai)

### HOW вҖ” Technology
- **Format**: Markdown (`.md`) with Mermaid diagrams
- **Diagrams**: Mermaid.js (rendered natively on GitHub)
- **Detection Rules**: Sigma YAML, YARA
- **Quality Tools**: `python3 tools/check_links.py`, `python3 tools/export_docs.py`
- **No build system required** вҖ” raw Markdown works on GitHub directly

**Maintained by**: [cyberdefense.co.th](https://cyberdefense.co.th) | Line OA: `@cyberdefense`

---

## рҹ“Ғ Directory Structure

```
SOCSOP/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00_Getting_Started/        # SOC 101, Quickstart, Glossary
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 01_SOC_Fundamentals/       # Building roadmap, budget, tech stack
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 02_Platform_Operations/    # Data governance, deployment procedures
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 03_User_Guides/            # Detection engineering, tool integration
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 04_Troubleshooting/        # Standard troubleshooting methodology
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 05_Incident_Response/      # IR Framework + 53 Playbooks + Runbooks
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Playbooks/             # Individual incident playbooks (PB-01 to PB-53)
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Runbooks/              # Tier 1/2/3 Runbooks (EN+TH)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 06_Operations_Management/  # Shift handoff, metrics, team structure, CTI, TLP
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 07_Compliance_Privacy/     # ISO 27001, PCI-DSS, NIST CSF, PDPA
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 08_Detection_Engineering/  # Sigma detection rules, YARA file signatures
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 09_Simulation_Testing/     # Purple team guides, Atomic Red Team maps
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 10_Training_Onboarding/    # Analyst curriculum, training checklists
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 11_Reporting_Templates/    # Monthly/quarterly reports, incident forms
в”ңв”Җв”Җ tools/                     # Utility scripts (export, link check)
в””в”Җв”Җ assets/                    # Images (hero banner, etc.)
```

## рҹ“ӣ File Naming Conventions

| Rule | Example |
|---|---|
| English docs end with `.en.md` | `Phishing.en.md` |
| Thai docs end with `.th.md` | `Phishing.th.md` |
| Bilingual docs **always** come in pairs (EN + TH) | `Framework.en.md` + `Framework.th.md` |
| Language-neutral docs use plain `.md` | `README.md` |
| Use `PascalCase_With_Underscores` for filenames | `SOC_Team_Structure.en.md` |
| Detection rules use lowercase with underscores | `win_multiple_failed_logins.yml` |
| YARA rules use lowercase with underscores | `ransomware_extensions.yar` |

---

## рҹ“„ Document Structure Requirements

Every SOP document **MUST** contain these sections in this exact order:

### 1. Title & Metadata
```markdown
# Document Title

**ID**: PB-XX (for playbooks)
**Severity**: Low/Medium/High/Critical (for playbooks)
**Trigger**: What causes this playbook to activate
```

### 2. Mermaid Flowchart
Every document must include at least one `mermaid` diagram visualizing the process or workflow.

````markdown
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Start] --> B[Step 1]
    B --> C{Decision}
    C -->|Yes| D[Action]
    C -->|No| E[Other Action]
```
````

**Mermaid rules**:
- Thai versions must have **localized labels** (not English copy-pasted)
- Use `graph TD` (top-down) for process flows
- Use `graph LR` (left-right) for timelines/career paths
- Keep diagrams concise (max ~10 nodes)
- Use `style` for color coding when useful

### 3. Main Content
Structured with numbered sections (`## 1.`, `## 2.`, etc.) and actionable checklists:
```markdown
-   [ ] **Action Item**: Description of what to do.
```

### 4. Related Documents (Cross-Links)
Cross-links to other documents in the repository. Insert **before** References.

```
## Related Documents          <!-- English -->
## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ (Related Documents)  <!-- Thai -->

-   Document Name вҶ’ relative/path/to/file.en.md
```

**Cross-link rules**:
- Playbooks вҶ’ IR Framework, Incident Report Template, related Playbooks
- Operations docs вҶ’ SOC Metrics, Assessment Checklist, IR Framework
- Always use **relative paths** (e.g., `../05_Incident_Response/Framework.en.md`)
- Every file must link to at least 2 other internal documents

### 5. References
External links to authoritative sources. Always include at least 2 references.

```markdown
## References
-   [NIST SP 800-61r2](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-61/rev-2/final)
-   [MITRE ATT&CK](https://attack.mitre.org/)
```

**Preferred reference sources** (in order of priority):
1. NIST (SP 800-61, 800-53, CSF)
2. MITRE ATT&CK
3. CISA
4. SANS
5. OWASP
6. ISO 27001/27035
7. SOC-CMM
8. FIRST CSIRT Services Framework

---

## рҹҢҗ Bilingual Content Rules

| Rule | Details |
|---|---|
| Every EN doc must have a TH counterpart | No orphaned single-language files |
| Thai docs are **localized**, not literal translations | Adapt terminology naturally |
| Mermaid labels must be localized | `Alert[а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮа№Җаё•аё·аёӯаёҷ]` not `Alert[Alert]` |
| Section headers include Thai + English | `## 1. аёҷаёҙаёўаёІаёЎаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ (Defining the Problem)` |
| Keep technical terms untranslated | SIEM, MITRE ATT&CK, IOC, TLP, YARA, Sigma |
| References section header stays as `## References` | Same in both EN and TH versions |
| Related Documents header is bilingual in TH | `## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ (Related Documents)` |

---

## рҹҺЁ Tone & Style Guide

### Writing Style
- **Actionable**: Write procedures as clear, step-by-step instructions
- **Imperative mood**: "Check the logs" not "The logs should be checked"
- **Concise**: Short bullet points, no long paragraphs
- **Professional**: Suitable for SOC analysts and CISO-level readers

### Formatting Rules
- Use `-   ` (3 spaces after dash) for bullet points
- Use `**Bold**` for key terms and action items
- Use backticks for commands, tools, and technical identifiers: `nslookup`, `SIEM`, `T1566`
- Tables for structured data (KPIs, staffing models, severity matrices)
- Numbered sections for sequential processes (`## 1.`, `## 2.`)

---

## рҹӣЎпёҸ Playbook Conventions

| Property | Rule |
|---|---|
| **ID Format** | `PB-XX` (e.g., PB-01, PB-02 ... PB-53) |
| **Standard Sections** | Analysis вҶ’ Containment вҶ’ Eradication вҶ’ Recovery |
| **Mermaid Flow** | Must visualize the triage/decision process |
| **MITRE ATT&CK** | Reference relevant Technique IDs (e.g., T1566, T1078) |
| **Severity Levels** | Low, Medium, High, Critical |
| **Checklists** | Use `- [ ]` for operational steps |
| **Cross-Links** | Link to IR Framework, Incident Report template, related PBs |

### Playbook Index (PB-01 to PB-53)
```
PB-01 Phishing               PB-11 Suspicious Script      PB-21 AWS S3 Compromise
PB-02 Ransomware             PB-12 Lateral Movement       PB-22 AWS EC2 Compromise
PB-03 Malware Infection      PB-13 C2 Communication       PB-23 Azure AD Compromise
PB-04 Brute Force            PB-14 Insider Threat         PB-24 Zero-Day Exploit
PB-05 Account Compromise     PB-15 Rogue Admin            PB-25 DNS Tunneling
PB-06 Impossible Travel      PB-16 Cloud IAM              PB-26 MFA Bypass
PB-07 Privilege Escalation   PB-17 BEC                    PB-27 Cloud Storage Exposure
PB-08 Data Exfiltration      PB-18 Exploit/Vulnerability  PB-28 Mobile Compromise
PB-09 DDoS Attack            PB-19 Lost Device            PB-29 Shadow IT
PB-10 Web Application Attack PB-20 Log Clearing           PB-30 API Abuse

PB-31 Cryptomining           PB-41 VPN Abuse              PB-51 AI Prompt Injection
PB-32 Supply Chain Attack    PB-42 Email Account Takeover PB-52 LLM Data Poisoning
PB-33 OT/ICS Incident        PB-43 Watering Hole          PB-53 AI Model Theft
PB-34 Network Discovery      PB-44 Drive-By Download
PB-35 Data Collection        PB-45 Rootkit/Bootkit
PB-36 Credential Dumping     PB-46 SIM Swap
PB-37 SQL Injection          PB-47 Cloud Cryptojacking
PB-38 Wiper Attack           PB-48 Deepfake Social Engineering
PB-39 Living Off The Land    PB-49 Typosquatting
PB-40 USB Removable Media    PB-50 Unauthorized Scanning
```

---

## рҹ”Қ Detection Rules

### Sigma Rules (`08_Detection_Engineering/sigma_rules/`)
- **Format**: Sigma YAML specification v2
- **Naming**: `<category>_<description>.yml` (e.g., `win_multiple_failed_logins.yml`)
- **Required fields**: `title`, `status`, `description`, `logsource`, `detection`, `level`, `tags`
- **Tags**: Must include MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs (e.g., `attack.t1110`)

### YARA Rules (`08_Detection_Engineering/file_signatures/`)
- **Format**: YARA rule syntax
- **Naming**: `<family>_<indicator>.yar`
- **Required**: `meta`, `strings`, `condition` blocks

---

## вң… Verification & Quality Checks

### Before Every Commit
```bash
# 1. Check all internal links are valid
python3 tools/check_links.py

# 2. Regenerate consolidated manual
python3 tools/export_docs.py
```

### Pre-Commit Checklist
- [ ] `check_links.py` passes вң… (no broken internal links)
- [ ] `export_docs.py` regenerates `SOC_Manual_Consolidated.md`
- [ ] Both EN and TH versions updated if content changed
- [ ] Mermaid diagrams render correctly
- [ ] Related Documents section present with cross-links
- [ ] References section present with at least 2 credible sources
- [ ] README.md updated if new documents were added
- [ ] No duplicate lines (known issue вҖ” scan after batch edits)

---

## рҹ“қ Git Commit Conventions

**Pattern**: `<Action> <What> (<Details>)`

```
Add SOC Team Structure document (EN/TH) with org chart, roles, career path
Fix duplicate lines from flow insertion, regenerate consolidated manual
Add cross-links between all documents (Related Documents sections)
Standardize Operations Management docs: Add flows and references
```

**Actions**: `Add`, `Fix`, `Update`, `Remove`, `Refactor`, `Standardize`

---

## рҹ“– README Maintenance

When adding new documents:
1. Add a row to the relevant section table in `README.md`
2. Follow the existing table format with English and Thai links
3. Keep the table alphabetically sorted within each section

---

## вҡ пёҸ Known Pitfalls & Gotchas

| Pitfall | Solution |
|---|---|
| `multi_replace` tool duplicates lines when inserting flows | Scan for duplicate lines after batch edits with `grep` |
| Mermaid nodes with parentheses break rendering | Use `["text with (parens)"]` square bracket syntax |
| Thai text in Mermaid can overflow | Keep labels short, use abbreviations |
| Symlinks not followed on some Git platforms | Verify CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules render on GitHub |
| `check_links.py` only validates internal links | External URLs (References) are NOT checked automatically |
| `SOC_Manual_Consolidated.md` must be regenerated | Always run `export_docs.py` after any content change |

---

## рҹ”’ Security & Confidentiality Rules

- **NEVER** commit real credentials, API keys, or tokens
- **NEVER** include client-specific data (company names, IPs, domains)
- **NEVER** reference internal infrastructure details
- All examples must use **placeholder values**: `[COMPANY]`, `[IP_ADDRESS]`, `[YYYY-MM-DD]`
- Keep all content **vendor-agnostic** вҖ” no Splunk, QRadar, Sentinel-specific content
- Use generic terms: "SIEM query" not "SPL query", "EDR" not "CrowdStrike"

---

## рҹҡҖ Common Workflows

### Adding a New Playbook
```
1. Create `05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/<Name>.en.md`
2. Create `05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/<Name>.th.md`
3. Include: Metadata, Mermaid flow, Analysis/Containment/Eradication/Recovery
4. Add Related Documents (вҶ’ Framework, вҶ’ Incident Report Template)
5. Add References (MITRE ATT&CK technique + 1 more)
6. Update README.md table
7. Run: python3 tools/check_links.py
8. Run: python3 tools/export_docs.py
9. Commit with: "Add PB-XX <Name> playbook (EN/TH)"
```

### Adding a New Operations Document
```
1. Create `06_Operations_Management/<Name>.en.md`
2. Create `06_Operations_Management/<Name>.th.md`
3. Include: Mermaid flow, structured content, checklists
4. Add Related Documents (вҶ’ SOC Metrics, вҶ’ Assessment Checklist)
5. Add References (NIST/SOC-CMM/SANS)
6. Update README.md table
7. Run verification scripts
8. Commit with: "Add <Name> document (EN/TH)"
```

### Updating Existing Documents
```
1. Edit both EN and TH versions
2. Verify Mermaid diagrams still render
3. Verify cross-links still work
4. Run: python3 tools/check_links.py
5. Run: python3 tools/export_docs.py
6. Commit with: "Update <Name>: <what changed>"
```

---

## вқҢ Do NOT

- вқҢ Use vendor-specific terminology (`"Splunk query"` вҶ’ use `"SIEM query"`)
- вқҢ Create single-language documents (always EN + TH pair)
- вқҢ Skip the Mermaid flowchart in any SOP document
- вқҢ Use absolute file paths in cross-links (always relative)
- вқҢ Commit without running `check_links.py` and `export_docs.py`
- вқҢ Leave References section empty (minimum 2 sources)
- вқҢ Hardcode organization-specific details (keep vendor-agnostic)
- вқҢ Include real credentials, client data, or internal infrastructure info
- вқҢ Modify `SOC_Manual_Consolidated.md` directly (it's auto-generated)
- вқҢ Create deeply nested directories (max 2 levels deep)
- вқҢ Skip the Related Documents section (every file must cross-link)
- вқҢ Use inline HTML in Markdown (except README badges/footer)

## File: CHANGELOG.md
# Changelog

All notable changes to the SOC Standard Operating Procedures will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

---

## [2.23.15] - 2026-04-26

- Post-incident improvement path вҖ” connected PIR outputs to remediation intake, monthly governance, quarterly risk acceptance, and board follow-up so recurring weaknesses cannot stop at documentation.
- Governance return loop вҖ” added closure rules and return-path checks so funded actions, accepted risks, and recurring findings flow back into operational tracking.
- **README.md** вҖ” updated the current version to `v2.23.15`
- **VERSION_TRACKER.md** вҖ” updated repository version metadata

## [2.23.14] - 2026-04-26

- Post-restoration monitoring / exit criteria path вҖ” connected enhanced monitoring, war room exit, and final incident closure so teams know when recovery is stable enough to step down and close.
- Closure governance вҖ” added monitoring exit checks, handoff requirements, and explicit closure records for technical, business, and external-obligation completion.
- **README.md** вҖ” updated the current version to `v2.23.14`
- **VERSION_TRACKER.md** вҖ” updated repository version metadata

## [2.23.13] - 2026-04-26

- Service restoration / business rollback authority path вҖ” connected recovery-phase restore, rollback, reconnect, and return-to-service decisions to explicit approval owners and evidence gates.
- Recovery governance вҖ” added minimum restoration evidence, decision records, and return-to-service validation requirements across IR framework, escalation, decision logging, and incident reporting.
- **README.md** вҖ” updated the current version to `v2.23.13`
- **VERSION_TRACKER.md** вҖ” updated repository version metadata

## [2.23.12] - 2026-04-26

- Evidence chain / legal hold / retention path вҖ” connected evidence handling, forensics, data governance, and decision logging so regulated or board-level cases have explicit custody and retention control.
- Evidence governance вҖ” added legal-hold triggers, archive/release checks, and minimum retention records for forensic and incident evidence.
- **README.md** вҖ” updated the current version to `v2.23.12`
- **VERSION_TRACKER.md** вҖ” updated repository version metadata

## [2.23.11] - 2026-04-26

- Decision authority matrix вҖ” added explicit approval ownership for containment, shutdown, blocking, notification, public statements, and risk acceptance across severity levels.
- Decision logging вҖ” connected escalation guidance to the incident decision log so major approvals can be recorded with facts, owners, and follow-up review points.
- **README.md** вҖ” updated the current version to `v2.23.11`
- **VERSION_TRACKER.md** вҖ” updated repository version metadata

## [2.23.10] - 2026-04-26

- Crisis command / war room governance path вҖ” added activation thresholds, commander model, update cadence, and handoff expectations across IR framework, severity, shift handoff, communications, and incident reporting.
- War room records вҖ” added explicit commander, cadence, and decision-log fields so major incidents can be handed over and closed with complete governance evidence.
- **README.md** вҖ” updated the current version to `v2.23.10`
- **VERSION_TRACKER.md** вҖ” updated repository version metadata

## [2.23.9] - 2026-04-26

- Media / public statement path вҖ” connected communications, executive dashboard, and board review documents for public-facing incidents and reputation-sensitive cases.
- Communication controls вҖ” added public-statement triggers, minimum controls, and board-level evidence expectations for cases with media, customer-trust, or public-pressure impact.
- **README.md** вҖ” updated the current version to `v2.23.9`
- **VERSION_TRACKER.md** вҖ” updated repository version metadata

## [2.23.8] - 2026-04-26

- Customer / regulator communications path вҖ” added explicit outbound communication workflow, approval boundaries, and handoff records across communication, PDPA, and incident-report documents.
- Communication governance вҖ” added minimum outbound communication package, sender approval model, and tracking fields for customer, regulator, vendor, and media-facing messages.
- **README.md** вҖ” updated the current version to `v2.23.8`
- **VERSION_TRACKER.md** вҖ” updated repository version metadata

## [2.23.7] - 2026-04-26

- Executive / legal / privacy notification path вҖ” connected IR workflow to PDPA notification, incident reporting, and board escalation documents.
- Notification records вҖ” added explicit notification decision records, escalation paths, and board-escalation criteria for material incidents and regulated-data cases.
- **README.md** вҖ” updated the current version to `v2.23.7`
- **VERSION_TRACKER.md** вҖ” updated repository version metadata

- MkDocs configuration for documentation site

## File: CLAUDE.md
# AGENTS.md вҖ” SOC Standard Operating Procedures Repository

> This file is the **single source of truth** for all AI agents and human contributors working on this repository.
> It is symlinked to: `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`

---

## рҹҺҜ Project Identity

### WHY вҖ” Purpose
This repository provides **vendor-agnostic, production-ready SOC Standard Operating Procedures** that any organization can adopt to build or mature their Security Operations Center. All content follows NIST, MITRE ATT&CK, and ISO 27001/27035 frameworks.

### WHAT вҖ” Scope
- 53 Incident Response Playbooks (PB-01 to PB-53)
- Operations Management (Shift Handoff, KPIs, Team Structure, CTI, TLP)
- Detection Engineering (Sigma rules, YARA signatures)
- Simulation & Purple Teaming
- Training & Onboarding Curriculum
- Executive Reporting Templates
- All content is **bilingual** (English + Thai)

### HOW вҖ” Technology
- **Format**: Markdown (`.md`) with Mermaid diagrams
- **Diagrams**: Mermaid.js (rendered natively on GitHub)
- **Detection Rules**: Sigma YAML, YARA
- **Quality Tools**: `python3 tools/check_links.py`, `python3 tools/export_docs.py`
- **No build system required** вҖ” raw Markdown works on GitHub directly

**Maintained by**: [cyberdefense.co.th](https://cyberdefense.co.th) | Line OA: `@cyberdefense`

---

## рҹ“Ғ Directory Structure

```
SOCSOP/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00_Getting_Started/        # SOC 101, Quickstart, Glossary
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 01_SOC_Fundamentals/       # Building roadmap, budget, tech stack
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 02_Platform_Operations/    # Data governance, deployment procedures
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 03_User_Guides/            # Detection engineering, tool integration
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 04_Troubleshooting/        # Standard troubleshooting methodology
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 05_Incident_Response/      # IR Framework + 53 Playbooks + Runbooks
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Playbooks/             # Individual incident playbooks (PB-01 to PB-53)
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Runbooks/              # Tier 1/2/3 Runbooks (EN+TH)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 06_Operations_Management/  # Shift handoff, metrics, team structure, CTI, TLP
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 07_Compliance_Privacy/     # ISO 27001, PCI-DSS, NIST CSF, PDPA
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 08_Detection_Engineering/  # Sigma detection rules, YARA file signatures
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 09_Simulation_Testing/     # Purple team guides, Atomic Red Team maps
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 10_Training_Onboarding/    # Analyst curriculum, training checklists
в”ңв”Җв”Җ 11_Reporting_Templates/    # Monthly/quarterly reports, incident forms
в”ңв”Җв”Җ tools/                     # Utility scripts (export, link check)
в””в”Җв”Җ assets/                    # Images (hero banner, etc.)
```

---

## рҹ“ӣ File Naming Conventions

| Rule | Example |
|---|---|
| English docs end with `.en.md` | `Phishing.en.md` |
| Thai docs end with `.th.md` | `Phishing.th.md` |
| Bilingual docs **always** come in pairs (EN + TH) | `Framework.en.md` + `Framework.th.md` |
| Language-neutral docs use plain `.md` | `README.md` |
| Use `PascalCase_With_Underscores` for filenames | `SOC_Team_Structure.en.md` |
| Detection rules use lowercase with underscores | `win_multiple_failed_logins.yml` |
| YARA rules use lowercase with underscores | `ransomware_extensions.yar` |

---

## рҹ“„ Document Structure Requirements

Every SOP document **MUST** contain these sections in this exact order:

### 1. Title & Metadata
```markdown
# Document Title

**ID**: PB-XX (for playbooks)
**Severity**: Low/Medium/High/Critical (for playbooks)
**Trigger**: What causes this playbook to activate
```

### 2. Mermaid Flowchart
Every document must include at least one `mermaid` diagram visualizing the process or workflow.

````markdown
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Start] --> B[Step 1]
    B --> C{Decision}
    C -->|Yes| D[Action]
    C -->|No| E[Other Action]
```
````

**Mermaid rules**:
- Thai versions must have **localized labels** (not English copy-pasted)
- Use `graph TD` (top-down) for process flows
- Use `graph LR` (left-right) for timelines/career paths
- Keep diagrams concise (max ~10 nodes)
- Use `style` for color coding when useful

### 3. Main Content
Structured with numbered sections (`## 1.`, `## 2.`, etc.) and actionable checklists:
```markdown
-   [ ] **Action Item**: Description of what to do.
```

### 4. Related Documents (Cross-Links)
Cross-links to other documents in the repository. Insert **before** References.

```
## Related Documents          <!-- English -->
## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ (Related Documents)  <!-- Thai -->

-   Document Name вҶ’ relative/path/to/file.en.md
```

**Cross-link rules**:
- Playbooks вҶ’ IR Framework, Incident Report Template, related Playbooks
- Operations docs вҶ’ SOC Metrics, Assessment Checklist, IR Framework
- Always use **relative paths** (e.g., `../05_Incident_Response/Framework.en.md`)
- Every file must link to at least 2 other internal documents

### 5. References
External links to authoritative sources. Always include at least 2 references.

```markdown
## References
-   [NIST SP 800-61r2](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-61/rev-2/final)
-   [MITRE ATT&CK](https://attack.mitre.org/)
```

**Preferred reference sources** (in order of priority):
1. NIST (SP 800-61, 800-53, CSF)
2. MITRE ATT&CK
3. CISA
4. SANS
5. OWASP
6. ISO 27001/27035
7. SOC-CMM
8. FIRST CSIRT Services Framework

---

## рҹҢҗ Bilingual Content Rules

| Rule | Details |
|---|---|
| Every EN doc must have a TH counterpart | No orphaned single-language files |
| Thai docs are **localized**, not literal translations | Adapt terminology naturally |
| Mermaid labels must be localized | `Alert[а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮа№Җаё•аё·аёӯаёҷ]` not `Alert[Alert]` |
| Section headers include Thai + English | `## 1. аёҷаёҙаёўаёІаёЎаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ (Defining the Problem)` |
| Keep technical terms untranslated | SIEM, MITRE ATT&CK, IOC, TLP, YARA, Sigma |
| References section header stays as `## References` | Same in both EN and TH versions |
| Related Documents header is bilingual in TH | `## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ (Related Documents)` |

---

## рҹҺЁ Tone & Style Guide

### Writing Style
- **Actionable**: Write procedures as clear, step-by-step instructions
- **Imperative mood**: "Check the logs" not "The logs should be checked"
- **Concise**: Short bullet points, no long paragraphs
- **Professional**: Suitable for SOC analysts and CISO-level readers

### Formatting Rules
- Use `-   ` (3 spaces after dash) for bullet points
- Use `**Bold**` for key terms and action items
- Use backticks for commands, tools, and technical identifiers: `nslookup`, `SIEM`, `T1566`
- Tables for structured data (KPIs, staffing models, severity matrices)
- Numbered sections for sequential processes (`## 1.`, `## 2.`)

---

## рҹӣЎпёҸ Playbook Conventions

| Property | Rule |
|---|---|
| **ID Format** | `PB-XX` (e.g., PB-01, PB-02 ... PB-53) |
| **Standard Sections** | Analysis вҶ’ Containment вҶ’ Eradication вҶ’ Recovery |
| **Mermaid Flow** | Must visualize the triage/decision process |
| **MITRE ATT&CK** | Reference relevant Technique IDs (e.g., T1566, T1078) |
| **Severity Levels** | Low, Medium, High, Critical |
| **Checklists** | Use `- [ ]` for operational steps |
| **Cross-Links** | Link to IR Framework, Incident Report template, related PBs |

### Playbook Index (PB-01 to PB-53)
```
PB-01 Phishing               PB-11 Suspicious Script      PB-21 AWS S3 Compromise
PB-02 Ransomware             PB-12 Lateral Movement       PB-22 AWS EC2 Compromise
PB-03 Malware Infection      PB-13 C2 Communication       PB-23 Azure AD Compromise
PB-04 Brute Force            PB-14 Insider Threat         PB-24 Zero-Day Exploit
PB-05 Account Compromise     PB-15 Rogue Admin            PB-25 DNS Tunneling
PB-06 Impossible Travel      PB-16 Cloud IAM              PB-26 MFA Bypass
PB-07 Privilege Escalation   PB-17 BEC                    PB-27 Cloud Storage Exposure
PB-08 Data Exfiltration      PB-18 Exploit/Vulnerability  PB-28 Mobile Compromise
PB-09 DDoS Attack            PB-19 Lost Device            PB-29 Shadow IT
PB-10 Web Application Attack PB-20 Log Clearing           PB-30 API Abuse

PB-31 Cryptomining           PB-41 VPN Abuse              PB-51 AI Prompt Injection
PB-32 Supply Chain Attack    PB-42 Email Account Takeover PB-52 LLM Data Poisoning
PB-33 OT/ICS Incident        PB-43 Watering Hole          PB-53 AI Model Theft
PB-34 Network Discovery      PB-44 Drive-By Download
PB-35 Data Collection        PB-45 Rootkit/Bootkit
PB-36 Credential Dumping     PB-46 SIM Swap
PB-37 SQL Injection          PB-47 Cloud Cryptojacking
PB-38 Wiper Attack           PB-48 Deepfake Social Engineering
PB-39 Living Off The Land    PB-49 Typosquatting
PB-40 USB Removable Media    PB-50 Unauthorized Scanning
```

---

## рҹ”Қ Detection Rules

### Sigma Rules (`08_Detection_Engineering/sigma_rules/`)
- **Format**: Sigma YAML specification v2
- **Naming**: `<category>_<description>.yml` (e.g., `win_multiple_failed_logins.yml`)
- **Required fields**: `title`, `status`, `description`, `logsource`, `detection`, `level`, `tags`
- **Tags**: Must include MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs (e.g., `attack.t1110`)

### YARA Rules (`08_Detection_Engineering/file_signatures/`)
- **Format**: YARA rule syntax
- **Naming**: `<family>_<indicator>.yar`
- **Required**: `meta`, `strings`, `condition` blocks

---

## вң… Verification & Quality Checks

### Before Every Commit
```bash
# 1. Check all internal links are valid
python3 tools/check_links.py

# 2. Regenerate consolidated manual
python3 tools/export_docs.py
```

### Pre-Commit Checklist
- [ ] `check_links.py` passes вң… (no broken internal links)
- [ ] `export_docs.py` regenerates `SOC_Manual_Consolidated.md`
- [ ] Both EN and TH versions updated if content changed
- [ ] Mermaid diagrams render correctly
- [ ] Related Documents section present with cross-links
- [ ] References section present with at least 2 credible sources
- [ ] README.md updated if new documents were added
- [ ] No duplicate lines (known issue вҖ” scan after batch edits)

---

## рҹ“қ Git Commit Conventions

**Pattern**: `<Action> <What> (<Details>)`

```
Add SOC Team Structure document (EN/TH) with org chart, roles, career path
Fix duplicate lines from flow insertion, regenerate consolidated manual
Add cross-links between all documents (Related Documents sections)
Standardize Operations Management docs: Add flows and references
```

**Actions**: `Add`, `Fix`, `Update`, `Remove`, `Refactor`, `Standardize`

---

## рҹ“– README Maintenance

When adding new documents:
1. Add a row to the relevant section table in `README.md`
2. Follow the existing table format with English and Thai links
3. Keep the table alphabetically sorted within each section

---

## вҡ пёҸ Known Pitfalls & Gotchas

| Pitfall | Solution |
|---|---|
| `multi_replace` tool duplicates lines when inserting flows | Scan for duplicate lines after batch edits with `grep` |
| Mermaid nodes with parentheses break rendering | Use `["text with (parens)"]` square bracket syntax |
| Thai text in Mermaid can overflow | Keep labels short, use abbreviations |
| Symlinks not followed on some Git platforms | Verify CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules render on GitHub |
| `check_links.py` only validates internal links | External URLs (References) are NOT checked automatically |
| `SOC_Manual_Consolidated.md` must be regenerated | Always run `export_docs.py` after any content change |

---

## рҹ”’ Security & Confidentiality Rules

- **NEVER** commit real credentials, API keys, or tokens
- **NEVER** include client-specific data (company names, IPs, domains)
- **NEVER** reference internal infrastructure details
- All examples must use **placeholder values**: `[COMPANY]`, `[IP_ADDRESS]`, `[YYYY-MM-DD]`
- Keep all content **vendor-agnostic** вҖ” no Splunk, QRadar, Sentinel-specific content
- Use generic terms: "SIEM query" not "SPL query", "EDR" not "CrowdStrike"

---

## рҹҡҖ Common Workflows

### Adding a New Playbook
```
1. Create `05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/<Name>.en.md`
2. Create `05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/<Name>.th.md`
3. Include: Metadata, Mermaid flow, Analysis/Containment/Eradication/Recovery
4. Add Related Documents (вҶ’ Framework, вҶ’ Incident Report Template)
5. Add References (MITRE ATT&CK technique + 1 more)
6. Update README.md table
7. Run: python3 tools/check_links.py
8. Run: python3 tools/export_docs.py
9. Commit with: "Add PB-XX <Name> playbook (EN/TH)"
```

### Adding a New Operations Document
```
1. Create `06_Operations_Management/<Name>.en.md`
2. Create `06_Operations_Management/<Name>.th.md`
3. Include: Mermaid flow, structured content, checklists
4. Add Related Documents (вҶ’ SOC Metrics, вҶ’ Assessment Checklist)
5. Add References (NIST/SOC-CMM/SANS)
6. Update README.md table
7. Run verification scripts
8. Commit with: "Add <Name> document (EN/TH)"
```

### Updating Existing Documents
```
1. Edit both EN and TH versions
2. Verify Mermaid diagrams still render
3. Verify cross-links still work
4. Run: python3 tools/check_links.py
5. Run: python3 tools/export_docs.py
6. Commit with: "Update <Name>: <what changed>"
```

---

## вқҢ Do NOT

- вқҢ Use vendor-specific terminology (`"Splunk query"` вҶ’ use `"SIEM query"`)
- вқҢ Create single-language documents (always EN + TH pair)
- вқҢ Skip the Mermaid flowchart in any SOP document
- вқҢ Use absolute file paths in cross-links (always relative)
- вқҢ Commit without running `check_links.py` and `export_docs.py`
- вқҢ Leave References section empty (minimum 2 sources)
- вқҢ Hardcode organization-specific details (keep vendor-agnostic)
- вқҢ Include real credentials, client data, or internal infrastructure info
- вқҢ Modify `SOC_Manual_Consolidated.md` directly (it's auto-generated)
- вқҢ Create deeply nested directories (max 2 levels deep)
- вқҢ Skip the Related Documents section (every file must cross-link)
- вқҢ Use inline HTML in Markdown (except README badges/footer)


---

# рҹӨқ Contributing Guide / аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯаёҒаёІаёЈаёЎаёөаёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаёЈа№Ҳаё§аёЎ

Thank you for your interest in contributing to the SOC Standard Operating Procedures!
аёӮаёӯаёҡаё„аёёаё“аё—аёөа№ҲаёӘаёҷа№ғаёҲаёЎаёөаёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаёЈа№Ҳаё§аёЎа№ғаёҷа№Ӯаё„аёЈаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈ SOC SOP!

---

## рҹ“Ӣ How to Contribute / аё§аёҙаёҳаёөаёҒаёІаёЈаёЎаёөаёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаёЈа№Ҳаё§аёЎ

### 1. Report Issues / а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ

- Found a broken link, typo, or outdated information?
- Open a [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/nutthakorn7/SOC-SOP/issues) with:
  - **Title**: Clear description of the issue
  - **File**: Which file(s) are affected
  - **Description**: What's wrong and suggested fix

### 2. Suggest New Content / а№ҖаёӘаёҷаёӯа№Җаёҷаё·а№үаёӯаё«аёІа№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ

- Have an idea for a new SOP, playbook, or template?
- Open an issue with the `enhancement` label
- Include: topic, target audience, why it's needed

### 3. Submit Changes / аёӘа№ҲаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮ

```bash
# 1. Fork the repository
# 2. Create a feature branch
git checkout -b feat/my-new-document

# 3. Make your changes (see guidelines below)

# 4. Commit with conventional commit message
git commit -m "feat: add [Document Name] (EN+TH)"

# 5. Push and create a Pull Request
git push origin feat/my-new-document
```

---

## рҹ“қ Document Standards / аёЎаёІаё•аёЈаёҗаёІаёҷа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ

### File Naming

```
[Directory]/[Document_Name].[lang].md

Example:
06_Operations_Management/DLP_SOP.en.md
06_Operations_Management/DLP_SOP.th.md
```

### Required: Bilingual Pairs

Every document **must** have both English (`.en.md`) and Thai (`.th.md`) versions.

| вң… Correct | вқҢ Incorrect |
|:---|:---|
| `DLP_SOP.en.md` + `DLP_SOP.th.md` | `DLP_SOP.en.md` only |

### Document Structure

Each SOP should include these sections:

```markdown
# Document Title

## Purpose / аё§аёұаё•аё–аёёаёӣаёЈаё°аёӘаёҮаё„а№Ң
Brief description of what this document covers.

## Scope / аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё•
Who and what this applies to.

## [Main Content Sections]
The core content of the SOP.

## Related Documents / а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ
Links to related SOPs in this repository.

## References / аёӯа№үаёІаёҮаёӯаёҙаёҮ
External references, standards, frameworks.
```

### Directory Structure

| Directory | Content |
|:---|:---|
| `00_Getting_Started/` | Introductory materials |
| `01_SOC_Fundamentals/` | Building and planning SOC |
| `05_Incident_Response/` | IR framework, playbooks, forensics |
| `06_Operations_Management/` | Day-to-day operations SOPs |
| `09_Simulation_Testing/` | Purple team, phishing sim, atomic tests |
| `10_Training_Onboarding/` | Analyst training materials |
| `07_Compliance_Privacy/` | Regulatory compliance |
| `11_Reporting_Templates/` | Reports and dashboards |
| `11_Reporting_Templates/` | Operational form templates |
| `08_Detection_Engineering/sigma_rules/` | Detection rules (YAML) |
| `tools/` | Scripts and interactive tools |

---

## вң… Checklist Before Submitting / а№ҖаёҠа№Үаё„аёҘаёҙаёӘаё•а№ҢаёҒа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёӘа№ҲаёҮ

- [ ] Both `.en.md` and `.th.md` versions exist
- [ ] Internal links work (`python3 tools/check_links.py`)
- [ ] Document has Required Sections (Purpose, Scope, Related Documents, References)
- [ ] Added to `README.md` in the appropriate section
- [ ] Added to `mkdocs.yml` navigation
- [ ] Updated `CHANGELOG.md` with the change
- [ ] Updated `VERSION_TRACKER.md` with new/modified document
- [ ] Commit message follows [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) format

---

## рҹ’¬ Commit Message Format

Use [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/):

```
feat: add [Document Name] (EN+TH)           # New document
fix: correct broken links in [File]          # Bug fix
refactor: reorganize [Section] structure     # Restructuring
docs: update README with new entries         # Documentation update
```

---

## рҹ”„ Update Workflow

When adding new documents, update these files (see [workflow](https://github.com/nutthakorn7/SOC-SOP/blob/main/.agent/workflows/update-docs.md)):

| # | File | Action |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| 1 | New `.en.md` + `.th.md` | Create the documents |
| 2 | `README.md` | Add entry in appropriate section |
| 3 | `mkdocs.yml` | Add navigation entry |
| 4 | `CHANGELOG.md` | Add version entry |
| 5 | `VERSION_TRACKER.md` | Add tracking row |

---

## File: Playbook_Quick_Reference.md
# SOC Playbook Quick Reference Card

> **Print this page** вҖ” A compact reference for all 53 incident response playbooks.
>
> **Purpose**: Help analysts quickly identify the correct playbook, first action, and escalation path.

---

## Email & Social Engineering

| PB | Name | Severity | Key Action |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| 01 | Phishing | P2-P1 | Validate headers, block sender, remove messages, check click logs |
| 17 | Business Email Compromise | P1 | Freeze transaction, verify sender, preserve mailbox evidence |
| 42 | Email Account Takeover | P1 | Reset credentials, revoke sessions, audit inbox and OAuth rules |
| 48 | Deepfake Social Engineering | P1 | Verify by callback, freeze requested action, preserve media evidence |

## Malware & Endpoint

| PB | Name | Severity | Key Action |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| 02 | Ransomware | P1 | Isolate hosts, protect backups, preserve evidence, activate IR |
| 03 | Malware Infection | P2-P1 | Isolate endpoint, collect artifacts, scan for spread |
| 11 | Suspicious Script | P2 | Stop process, capture script, inspect parent process |
| 38 | Wiper Attack | P1 | Isolate immediately, activate DR/BCP, verify integrity |
| 39 | Living Off The Land | P2 | Review LOLBin usage, hunt persistence, block abused binaries |
| 45 | Rootkit / Bootkit | P1 | Offline scan, preserve disk image, rebuild trusted system |

## Identity & Access

| PB | Name | Severity | Key Action |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| 04 | Brute Force | P3-P2 | Lock account, block source, enforce MFA |
| 05 | Account Compromise | P2-P1 | Reset password, revoke sessions, review user activity |
| 06 | Impossible Travel | P3-P2 | Verify user, check VPN/proxy, review sign-in logs |
| 07 | Privilege Escalation | P1 | Revoke elevated access, audit group changes, hunt persistence |
| 14 | Insider Threat | P1 | Preserve evidence, coordinate HR/Legal, monitor access |
| 15 | Rogue Admin | P1 | Disable account, rotate secrets, review admin changes |
| 16 | Cloud IAM Anomaly | P2-P1 | Revoke risky permissions, audit API calls, rotate keys |
| 26 | MFA Bypass / Token Theft | P1 | Revoke tokens, force reenrollment, investigate AiTM indicators |
| 36 | Credential Dumping | P1 | Isolate host, reset exposed credentials, hunt LSASS/SAM/DCSync |

## Network & Web

| PB | Name | Severity | Key Action |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| 09 | DDoS Attack | P2-P1 | Enable mitigation, rate limit, coordinate ISP/CDN |
| 10 | Web Application Attack | P2-P1 | Block exploit path, review logs, patch vulnerable component |
| 12 | Lateral Movement | P1 | Segment network, disable compromised accounts, scope hosts |
| 13 | C2 Communication | P1 | Block C2 destinations, isolate beaconing hosts, analyze traffic |
| 18 | Vulnerability Exploitation | P2-P1 | Patch or virtual patch, block IOCs, check compromise |
| 24 | Zero-Day Exploit | P1 | Apply compensating controls, isolate exposed assets, monitor KEV/TI |
| 25 | DNS Tunneling | P1 | Block domain, isolate host, analyze encoded payloads |
| 30 | API Abuse | P2 | Rate limit, revoke API keys, review object access patterns |
| 34 | Network Discovery | P3-P2 | Identify scanner, confirm authorization, block if unauthorized |
| 37 | SQL Injection | P1 | Block payloads, patch code, check database exposure |
| 43 | Watering Hole | P1 | Block site, identify visitors, scan affected endpoints |
| 44 | Drive-By Download | P2 | Block URL, scan endpoints, patch browser/plugins |
| 50 | Unauthorized Scanning | P3 | Identify source, block if needed, review exposed services |

## Cloud & Infrastructure

| PB | Name | Severity | Key Action |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| 21 | AWS S3 Compromise | P2-P1 | Block public access, review CloudTrail, assess data exposure |
| 22 | AWS EC2 Compromise | P1 | Isolate instance, snapshot volumes, rotate keys |
| 23 | Azure AD / Entra ID Compromise | P1 | Revoke sessions, reset credentials, review Conditional Access |
| 27 | Cloud Storage Exposure | P2-P1 | Make private, review access logs, notify if data leaked |
| 29 | Shadow IT | P3 | Inventory service, risk assess, block or onboard |
| 31 | Cryptomining | P2-P1 | Terminate miner, rotate access keys, check billing |
| 41 | VPN Abuse | P2 | Disable VPN account, inspect source, review tunnel logs |
| 47 | Cloud Cryptojacking | P1 | Kill compute, revoke API keys, alert finance/cloud owner |

## Data, Supply Chain & Physical

| PB | Name | Severity | Key Action |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| 08 | Data Exfiltration | P1 | Block channel, scope data, assess PDPA notification |
| 19 | Lost/Stolen Device | P2 | Remote wipe, disable accounts, document chain of custody |
| 20 | Log Clearing | P1 | Preserve remaining logs, restore from backups, investigate source |
| 28 | Mobile Device Compromise | P2 | Lock/wipe device, revoke tokens, re-enroll MDM |
| 32 | Supply Chain Attack | P1 | Isolate affected software, validate signatures, contact vendor |
| 33 | OT/ICS Incident | P1 | Protect safety, isolate OT segment, coordinate site owner |
| 35 | Data Collection / Staging | P2 | Monitor staging area, block exfil paths, preserve archives |
| 40 | USB Removable Media | P3-P2 | Quarantine media, scan device, review DLP/file access |
| 46 | SIM Swap | P1 | Contact carrier, reset accounts, move MFA away from SMS |
| 49 | Typosquatting | P3 | Block domain, report registrar, warn users |

## AI & ML Security

| PB | Name | Severity | Key Action |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| 51 | AI Prompt Injection | P2-P1 | Block unsafe prompt path, disable risky tools, review output exposure |
| 52 | LLM Data Poisoning | P1 | Freeze training/RAG pipeline, identify poisoned records, restore trusted data |
| 53 | AI Model Theft | P1 | Stop extraction, revoke access, preserve API and storage logs |

---

## Escalation Quick Guide

| Severity | Response Time | Notify | Example |
|:---:|:---:|:---|:---|
| P1 Critical | 15 min | SOC Manager, CISO, Legal, affected owner | Ransomware, active breach, data leak, model theft |
| P2 High | 30 min | SOC Manager, Team Lead | Malware, account compromise, cloud exposure |
| P3 Medium | 2 hours | Tier 2 Analyst | Brute force, scanning, policy violation |
| P4 Low | 8 hours | Tier 1 Analyst | False positive, informational request |

## Key Contacts

| Role | Name | Phone | Email |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| SOC Manager | __________ | __________ | __________ |
| CISO | __________ | __________ | __________ |
| Legal / DPO | __________ | __________ | __________ |
| PR / Communications | __________ | __________ | __________ |
| IT Infrastructure Lead | __________ | __________ | __________ |
| Cloud Owner | __________ | __________ | __________ |
| HR Contact | __________ | __________ | __________ |

---

> **Full Documentation**: [SOC SOP Repository](https://nutthakorn7.github.io/SOC-SOP/)

>
> **аё§аёұаё•аё–аёёаёӣаёЈаё°аёӘаёҮаё„а№Ң**: аёҠа№Ҳаё§аёўа№ғаё«а№ү analyst а№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒ playbook, action а№ҒаёЈаёҒ а№ҒаёҘаё° escalation path а№„аё”а№үа№ҖаёЈа№Үаё§

## Email & Social Engineering

| PB | аёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯ | аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№Җаёҷаёҙаёҷаё«аёҘаёұаёҒ |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| 01 | Phishing / аёҹаёҙаёҠаёҠаёҙа№ҲаёҮ | P2-P1 | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ header, аёҡаёҘа№ҮаёӯаёҒ sender, аёҘаёҡаёӯаёөа№ҖаёЎаёҘ, аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ click log |
| 17 | BEC / аёӯаёөа№ҖаёЎаёҘаё«аёҘаёӯаёҒаёҳаёёаёЈаёҒаёҙаёҲ | P1 | аёЈаё°аёҮаёұаёҡаёҳаёёаёЈаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ, аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаёңаё№а№үаёӘа№ҲаёҮ, а№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ mailbox |
| 42 | Email Account Takeover | P1 | аёЈаёөа№ҖаёӢа№Үаё•аёЈаё«аёұаёӘ, а№ҖаёһаёҙаёҒаё–аёӯаёҷ session, аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ inbox/OAuth rule |
| 48 | Deepfake Social Engineering | P1 | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ callback, аё«аёўаёёаё”аё„аёіаёӮаёӯа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮ, а№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷаёӘаё·а№Ҳаёӯ |

## Malware & Endpoint

| PB | аёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯ | аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№Җаёҷаёҙаёҷаё«аёҘаёұаёҒ |

## аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯ Escalation аё”а№Ҳаё§аёҷ

| аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ | SLA аё•аёӯаёҡаёЈаёұаёҡ | аёӘа№ҲаёҮаё•а№Ҳаёӯаё–аё¶аёҮ | аё•аёұаё§аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮ |

| P2 High | 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | SOC Manager, Team Lead | Malware, account compromise, cloud exposure |
| P3 Medium | 2 аёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§а№ӮаёЎаёҮ | Tier 2 Analyst | Brute force, scanning, policy violation |
| P4 Low | 8 аёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§а№ӮаёЎаёҮ | Tier 1 Analyst | False positive, info request |

## рҹ”Ҙ аё—аёіа№„аёЎаё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒа№ҖаёЈаёІ

аёңаё№а№үа№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷа№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡ SOC SOP **279 аёҠаёҙа№үаёҷ** аёҒаёҘаёұаёҡа№„аёӣа№ғаёҠа№үаёҮаёІаёҷа№„аё”а№үаё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө вҖ” а№„аёЎа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎаёҲаёІаёҒаёЁаё№аёҷаёўа№Ң

аё—аёёаёҒаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈа№Җаёҷа№үаёҷ **workshop + lab** аёЎаёІаёҒаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІ lecture вҖ” а№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷаёҲаёҡ аё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷа№„аё”а№үа№ҖаёҘаёў

## рҹ“Ӣ аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё”

> аё„аёҘаёҙаёҒаё—аёөа№ҲаёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈа№Җаёһаё·а№Ҳаёӯаё”аё№аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё”

| # | аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈ | аёЈаё°аёўаё°а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ | а№Җаё«аёЎаёІаё°аёҒаёұаёҡ | Format |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|:---:|
| 1 | [рҹҸ—пёҸ **SOC Building Workshop**](#1-soc-building-workshop) | 3 аё§аёұаёҷ | CISO, Manager, Architect | Lecture + Workshop |
| 2 | [рҹ“ҡ **SOC Analyst Bootcamp**](#2-soc-analyst-bootcamp) | 5 аё§аёұаёҷ | Analyst T1вҖ“T3, Engineer | Lab-intensive |
| 3 | [рҹ”Ҙ **Incident Response Drill**](#3-incident-response-drill) | 2 аё§аёұаёҷ | SOC Team, IR Team | Simulation |
| 4 | [рҹ“Ҡ **SOC Maturity Assessment**](#4-soc-maturity-assessment) | 2 аё§аёұаёҷ | CISO, Manager, Director | Assessment + Workshop |
| 5 | [рҹ“Ӣ **Compliance & Gap Analysis**](#5-compliance-gap-analysis) | 2 аё§аёұаёҷ | DPO, Auditor, Compliance | Workshop |
| 6 | [рҹҺӨ **Executive Security Briefing**](#6-executive-security-briefing) | аё„аёЈаё¶а№ҲаёҮаё§аёұаёҷ | CEO, CTO, Board | Keynote + Q&A |

> **"аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ SOC аё•аёұа№үаёҮа№Ғаё•а№ҲаёЁаё№аёҷаёўа№Ң вҖ” аёӯаёӯаёҒа№Ғаёҡаёҡ, аё§аёІаёҮаё„аёҷ, а№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒ tools, аё§аёІаёҮ roadmap а№ғаёҷ 3 аё§аёұаёҷ"**

- вқҢ а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө roadmap аёҠаёұаё”а№ҖаёҲаёҷ

### аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаёҷаёөа№үаёҲаё°аёҠа№Ҳаё§аёўа№ғаё«а№үаё„аёёаё“

- вң… аё„аёіаёҷаё§аё“ staffing model + budget а№„аё”а№үаё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө

## рҹ“ҡ 2. SOC Analyst Bootcamp

> **"аёҲаёІаёҒаёЎаё·аёӯа№ғаё«аёЎа№ҲаёӘаё№а№Ҳ SOC Analyst аёЎаё·аёӯаёӯаёІаёҠаёөаёһ вҖ” аёқаё¶аёҒ triage, investigate, hunt, respond а№ғаёҷ 5 аё§аёұаёҷ"**

<table>
<tr><td>

### аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІаё—аёөа№Ҳаёһаёҡаёҡа№Ҳаёӯаёў
- вқҢ Analyst а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” alert а№Ғаё•а№Ҳа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЈаё№а№үаёҲаё°аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ңаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№„аёЈ
- вқҢ FP rate аёӘаё№аёҮ вҖ” аё—аёөаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөаёўа№Җаё§аёҘаёІаёҒаёұаёҡ noise
- вқҢ а№ҖаёҒаёҙаё” incident аёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёұа№Ҳаёҷа№ғаёҲаёҲаё° respond аёўаёұаёҮа№„аёҮ
- вқҢ а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЈаё№а№үаёҲаёұаёҒ MITRE ATT&CK, Sigma, YARA

### аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаёҷаёөа№үаёҲаё°аёҠа№Ҳаё§аёўа№ғаё«а№үаё„аёёаё“
- вң… Triage alerts а№„аё”а№үа№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡ вҖ” а№ҒаёўаёҒ TP/FP а№„аё”а№үаёЎаёұа№Ҳаёҷа№ғаёҲ
- вң… аёӘаё·аёҡаёӘаё§аёҷ incident аё„аёЈаёҡ lifecycle (Detect вҶ’ Respond вҶ’ Recover)
- вң… аё—аёі Threat Hunting а№ҖаёҠаёҙаёҮаёЈаёёаёҒ аё”а№үаё§аёў hypothesis-driven approach
- вң… а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ incident report аё„аёёаё“аё аёІаёһаёӘаё№аёҮ

</td></tr>
</table>

| | аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё” |
|:---|:---|
| **аёЈаё°аёўаё°а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ** | 5 аё§аёұаёҷ (30 аёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§а№ӮаёЎаёҮ) вҖ” **intensive** |
| **аёҒаёҘаёёа№ҲаёЎа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў** | SOC Analyst (T1вҖ“T3) В· Security Engineer В· IT аё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёўаёІаёҒа№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҷаёӘаёІаёў |
| **аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷаёңаё№а№үа№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷ** | 10вҖ“20 аё„аёҷ |
| **аёЈаё№аёӣа№Ғаёҡаёҡ** | рҹҺ“ Lecture 30% В· рҹ”¬ Hands-on Lab 50% В· рҹ“Ӣ Case Study 20% |
| **Prerequisites** | аёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ Networking (TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP) + OS (Windows/Linux) |

<details>
<summary><b>Day 4 вҖ” Advanced Detection & Hunting</b></summary>

| а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ | Module | аё«аёұаё§аёӮа№үаёӯ |
|:---:|:---:|:---|
| 09:00вҖ“10:30 | 4.1 | **Detection Engineering** вҖ” Sigma rules, logic design, FP tuning |
| 10:30вҖ“12:00 | 4.2 | **Threat Hunting** вҖ” Hypothesis-driven, data-driven, MITRE-based hunts |
| 13:00вҖ“14:30 | 4.3 | **Cloud Security** вҖ” AWS/Azure/GCP log analysis, cloud threats |

## рҹ”Ҙ 3. Incident Response Drill

> **"аёӢа№үаёӯаёЎаёЈаёұаёҡаёЎаё·аёӯ cyber attack аё”а№үаё§аёў scenario аёҲаёІаёҒа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№ҢаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ вҖ” аёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷаё—аёөа№ҲаёҲаё°а№ҖаёҲаёӯаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ"**

<table>
<tr><td>

### аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІаё—аёөа№Ҳаёһаёҡаёҡа№Ҳаёӯаёў
- вқҢ аёЎаёө IR Plan а№Ғаё•а№Ҳа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№Җаё„аёўаёӢа№үаёӯаёЎ
- вқҢ а№ҖаёҒаёҙаё” incident а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§аё—аёөаёЎаёӘаёұаёҡаёӘаёҷ а№ғаё„аёЈаё—аёіаёӯаё°а№„аёЈ
- вқҢ Communication аёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ SOC вҶ” IT вҶ” Management аёҘа№ҲаёЎ
- вқҢ а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЈаё№а№үаё§а№ҲаёІ detection аё•аёұаё§а№„аё«аёҷаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷ аё•аёұаё§а№„аё«аёҷа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷ

### аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаёҷаёөа№үаёҲаё°аёҠа№Ҳаё§аёўа№ғаё«а№үаё—аёөаёЎаё„аёёаё“
- вң… аё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮ incident а№„аё”а№үаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡ
- вң… аё„а№үаёҷаёһаёҡаёҲаёёаё”аёӯа№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№ғаёҷ IR process **аёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№ҖаёҲаёӯаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ**
- вң… аёқаё¶аёҒ communication аёӮа№үаёІаёЎаё—аёөаёЎ (SOC вҶ” IT вҶ” Mgmt)
- вң… аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡ detection аё”а№үаё§аёў real-world TTPs

</td></tr>
</table>

| | аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё” |
|:---|:---|
| **аёЈаё°аёўаё°а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ** | 2 аё§аёұаёҷ (12 аёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§а№ӮаёЎаёҮ) |
| **аёҒаёҘаёёа№ҲаёЎа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў** | SOC Team В· IR Team В· IT Operations В· Management |
| **аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷаёңаё№а№үа№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷ** | 10вҖ“30 аё„аёҷ (а№Ғаёҡа№ҲаёҮаё—аёөаёЎа№ҒаёӮа№ҲаёҮаёӮаёұаёҷ) |
| **аёЈаё№аёӣа№Ғаёҡаёҡ** | рҹҺІ Tabletop 40% В· рҹҹЈ Purple Team 40% В· рҹ“Ӣ Debrief 20% |

## рҹ“Ҡ 4. SOC Maturity Assessment

> **"аёЈаё№а№үаёҲаёёаё”а№ҒаёӮа№ҮаёҮ-аёҲаёёаё”аёӯа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёӮаёӯаёҮ SOC аёӣаёұаёҲаёҲаёёаёҡаёұаёҷ аёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎ roadmap аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡаёӣаёЈаёёаёҮаё—аёөа№Ҳаё§аёұаё”аёңаёҘа№„аё”а№ү"**

<table>
<tr><td>

### аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІаё—аёөа№Ҳаёһаёҡаёҡа№Ҳаёӯаёў
- вқҢ а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЈаё№а№үаё§а№ҲаёІ SOC аё•аёҷа№ҖаёӯаёҮаёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳ level а№„аё«аёҷ
- вқҢ аёҘаёҮаё—аёёаёҷа№„аёӣа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§а№Ғаё•а№Ҳа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЈаё№а№үаёҲаё°аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡаёӣаёЈаёёаёҮаё•аёЈаёҮа№„аё«аёҷаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷ
- вқҢ аёӮаёӯ budget а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎа№Ғаё•а№Ҳа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө data аёӘаёҷаёұаёҡаёӘаёҷаёёаёҷ
- вқҢ а№ҖаёӣаёЈаёөаёўаёҡа№Җаё—аёөаёўаёҡаёҒаёұаёҡ benchmark а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№ү

### аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаёҷаёөа№үаёҲаё°аёҠа№Ҳаё§аёўа№ғаё«а№үаё„аёёаё“
- вң… аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ SOC аё”а№үаё§аёў **7-domain maturity model** (Level 1вҖ“5)
- вң… а№ҖаёӣаёЈаёөаёўаёҡа№Җаё—аёөаёўаёҡаёҒаёұаёҡ industry benchmark
- вң… аёҲаёұаё”аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ improvement аё•аёІаёЎ **ROI аёӘаё№аёҮаёӘаёёаё”**
- вң… а№„аё”а№ү **12-month roadmap** аёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎ budget estimate

</td></tr>
</table>

| | аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё” |
|:---|:---|
| **аёЈаё°аёўаё°а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ** | 2 аё§аёұаёҷ (12 аёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§а№ӮаёЎаёҮ) |
| **аёҒаёҘаёёа№ҲаёЎа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў** | CISO В· SOC Manager В· Security Director В· IT Director |
| **аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷаёңаё№а№үа№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷ** | 5вҖ“15 аё„аёҷ (key stakeholders) |
| **аёЈаё№аёӣа№Ғаёҡаёҡ** | рҹ”Қ Assessment 50% В· рҹ”Ё Workshop 30% В· рҹ“Ҡ Presentation 20% |

## рҹ“Ӣ 5. Compliance & Gap Analysis

> **"аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ compliance gap а№Җаё—аёөаёўаёҡ ISO 27001 / NIST CSF / PDPA вҖ” аёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎа№Ғаёңаёҷа№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮ"**

<table>
<tr><td>

### аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІаё—аёөа№Ҳаёһаёҡаёҡа№Ҳаёӯаёў
- вқҢ аёҲаё°аёӮаёӯ ISO 27001 а№Ғаё•а№Ҳа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЈаё№а№үаё§а№ҲаёІ gap аёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳаё•аёЈаёҮа№„аё«аёҷ
- вқҢ PDPA аёҡаёұаёҮаё„аёұаёҡа№ғаёҠа№үа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§а№Ғаё•а№ҲаёўаёұаёҮа№„аёЎа№ҲаёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎ
- вқҢ Auditor аёЎаёІа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§аё«аёІ evidence а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№ҖаёҲаёӯ
- вқҢ аё«аёҘаёІаёўаёЎаёІаё•аёЈаёҗаёІаёҷаёӢа№үаёӯаёҷаёҒаёұаёҷ а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЈаё№а№үаёҲаё°аёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈаёўаёұаёҮа№„аёҮ

### аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаёҷаёөа№үаёҲаё°аёҠа№Ҳаё§аёўа№ғаё«а№үаё„аёёаё“
- вң… а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№ғаёҲ ISO 27001, NIST CSF, PCI DSS, PDPA а№ғаёҷа№ҖаёҠаёҙаёҮаёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙ
- вң… аё—аёі gap analysis а№„аё”а№үаё”а№үаё§аёўаё•аёҷа№ҖаёӯаёҮ
- вң… а№ғаё«а№үаё„аё°а№Ғаёҷаёҷаё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮа№ҒаёҘаё°аёҲаёұаё”аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ remediation
- вң… а№Җаё•аёЈаёөаёўаёЎ audit а№„аё”а№үаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёЎаёөаёӣаёЈаё°аёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙаё аёІаёһ

</td></tr>
</table>

| | аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё” |
|:---|:---|
| **аёЈаё°аёўаё°а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ** | 2 аё§аёұаёҷ (12 аёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§а№ӮаёЎаёҮ) |
| **аёҒаёҘаёёа№ҲаёЎа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў** | Compliance Officer В· DPO В· IT Auditor В· Security Manager |
| **аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷаёңаё№а№үа№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷ** | 10вҖ“20 аё„аёҷ |
| **аёЈаё№аёӣа№Ғаёҡаёҡ** | рҹҺ“ Lecture 30% В· рҹ”Ё Workshop 50% В· рҹ“Ӣ Planning 20% |

### рҹ“… Agenda

<details>
<summary><b>Day 1 вҖ” Framework Deep Dive & Assessment</b></summary>

| а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ | Module | аё«аёұаё§аёӮа№үаёӯ |
|:---:|:---:|:---|

## рҹҺӨ 6. Executive Security Briefing

> **"аёӘаёЈаёёаёӣаёӘаё–аёІаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң cyber аё—аёөа№Ҳаёңаё№а№үаёҡаёЈаёҙаё«аёІаёЈаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёЈаё№а№ү вҖ” аёҒаёЈаё°аёҠаёұаёҡ, аё•аёЈаёҮаёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё”а№Үаёҷ, actionable"**

| | аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё” |
|:---|:---|
| **аёЈаё°аёўаё°а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ** | аё„аёЈаё¶а№ҲаёҮаё§аёұаёҷ (3 аёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§а№ӮаёЎаёҮ) |
| **аёҒаёҘаёёа№ҲаёЎа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў** | CEO В· CTO В· CIO В· CISO В· Board of Directors |
| **аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷаёңаё№а№үаёҹаёұаёҮ** | 5вҖ“50 аё„аёҷ |
| **аёЈаё№аёӣа№Ғаёҡаёҡ** | рҹҺӨ Keynote 60% В· рҹ’¬ Interactive Q&A 30% В· рҹ“Ӣ Action Planning 10% |

### рҹ“Ӣ а№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒ 3 аё«аёұаё§аёӮа№үаёӯ (аёҲаёІаёҒ 5)

| аё«аёұаё§аёӮа№үаёӯ | а№Җаёҷаё·а№үаёӯаё«аёІ |
|:---|:---|
| рҹҢҚ **Cyber Threat Landscape 2026** | аё аёұаёўаё„аёёаёҒаё„аёІаёЎаё—аёөа№ҲаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡаёҳаёёаёЈаёҒаёҙаёҲа№„аё—аёў вҖ” case study аёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ, а№Ғаёҷаё§а№Ӯаёҷа№үаёЎ, аёӘаё–аёҙаё•аёҙ |
| рҹҸ—пёҸ **"Why We Need a SOC"** | Business case, ROI, cost of breach vs cost of prevention |
| рҹ”’ **Ransomware Readiness** | аёӢа№үаёӯаёЎаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№„аёЈ, аёҲа№ҲаёІаёўаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№ҲаёҲа№ҲаёІаёў, insurance, legal implications |
| рҹҮ№рҹҮӯ **PDPA for Executives** | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҙаё”аёҠаёӯаёҡаёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаёҡаёёаё„аё„аёҘаёӮаёӯаёҮаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаёҒаёІаёЈ, аёҡаё—аёҘаёҮа№Ӯаё—аё©, аёӘаёҙа№ҲаёҮаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаё—аёі |
| рҹ’° **SOC Investment Strategy** | Build vs Buy vs Hybrid, budget planning, measuring success |

### рҹҺҒ Deliverables

- рҹ“Ҡ Executive Summary Deck (PDF)
- рҹ“Ӣ Board-Ready Action Items
- рҹ“ҡ Reference Materials

## рҹ’° Pricing & Options

### аёЈаё№аёӣа№ҒаёҡаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаёҲаёұаё”аёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎ

| аёЈаё№аёӣа№Ғаёҡаёҡ | аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё” | а№Җаё«аёЎаёІаё°аёҒаёұаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| рҹҸў **In-house Training** | аёҲаёұаё”аё—аёөа№ҲаёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё—аёөа№ҲаёӮаёӯаёҮаёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈ, аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡ content аё•аёІаёЎ industry | аёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈ customize |
| рҹ‘Ҙ **Public Class** | а№Җаёӣаёҙаё”аёӘаёЎаёұаё„аёЈаёЈаёІаёўаёҡаёёаё„аё„аёҘ, аё•аёІаёЈаёІаёҮаёӣаёЈаё°аёҒаёІаёЁаёҘа№Ҳаё§аёҮаё«аёҷа№үаёІ | аёҡаёёаё„аё„аёҘаё—аёұа№Ҳаё§а№„аёӣ, аёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈаёӮаёҷаёІаё”а№ҖаёҘа№ҮаёҒ |
| рҹ’» **Online Live** | аёӘаёӯаёҷаёӘаё”аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ Zoom/Teams | аё•а№ҲаёІаёҮаёҲаёұаёҮаё«аё§аёұаё”, remote teams |
| рҹҺҜ **Custom Program** | аёӯаёӯаёҒа№Ғаёҡаёҡаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈа№ҖаёүаёһаёІаё°аё•аёІаёЎаё„аё§аёІаёЎаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈ | аёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈаё—аёөа№ҲаёЎаёөаё„аё§аёІаёЎаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈаёһаёҙа№ҖаёЁаё© |

### аёЈаёІаё„аёІ

| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈ | аёЈаё°аёўаё°а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ | аёЈаёІаё„аёІ |
|:---|:---:|:---:|
| SOC Building Workshop | 3 аё§аёұаёҷ | аёӘаёӯаёҡаё–аёІаёЎ |
| SOC Analyst Bootcamp | 5 аё§аёұаёҷ | аёӘаёӯаёҡаё–аёІаёЎ |
| Incident Response Drill | 2 аё§аёұаёҷ | аёӘаёӯаёҡаё–аёІаёЎ |
| SOC Maturity Assessment | 2 аё§аёұаёҷ | аёӘаёӯаёҡаё–аёІаёЎ |
| Compliance & Gap Analysis | 2 аё§аёұаёҷ | аёӘаёӯаёҡаё–аёІаёЎ |
| Executive Security Briefing | аё„аёЈаё¶а№ҲаёҮаё§аёұаёҷ | аёӘаёӯаёҡаё–аёІаёЎ |

> рҹ’Ў **Early Bird** вҖ” аёӘаёЎаёұаё„аёЈаёҘа№Ҳаё§аёҮаё«аёҷа№үаёІ 30 аё§аёұаёҷаёЈаёұаёҡаёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаёҘаё”аёһаёҙа№ҖаёЁаё©<br>
> рҹ’Ў **Group Discount** вҖ” аёӘаёЎаёұаё„аёЈ 5 аё„аёҷаёӮаё¶а№үаёҷа№„аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡаёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаёҘаё”аёҒаёҘаёёа№ҲаёЎ<br>
> рҹ’Ў **Bundle** вҖ” аёҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаёІаёҒаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІ 1 аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаёЈаёұаёҡаёЈаёІаё„аёІаёһаёҙа№ҖаёЁаё©

## вқ“ FAQ

<details>
<summary><b>аё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёЎаёөаёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷаёӯаё°а№„аёЈаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷ?</b></summary>

- **SOC Building Workshop / Maturity Assessment** вҶ’ аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЈаё№а№үаёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ IT Security
- **SOC Analyst Bootcamp** вҶ’ аёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ Networking + OS
- **IR Drill** вҶ’ аёЎаёө SOC team аёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ (аё—аёёаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡа№„аё”а№ү)
- **Compliance** вҶ’ аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЈаё№а№үаёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ Information Security
- **Executive Briefing** вҶ’ а№„аёЎа№ҲаёҲаёіа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёЎаёөаёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ technical

</details>

<details>
<summary><b>а№„аё”а№үа№ғаёҡаёЈаёұаёҡаёЈаёӯаёҮ (Certificate) а№„аё«аёЎ?</b></summary>

аё—аёёаёҒаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаёЎаёө Certificate of Completion аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёңаё№а№үаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷаё„аёЈаёҡаё•аёІаёЎа№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№Ң

</details>

<details>
<summary><b>аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡ content аё•аёІаёЎаёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈа№„аё”а№үа№„аё«аёЎ?</b></summary>

а№„аё”а№үаё„аёЈаёұаёҡ аё—аёёаёҒаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаёӘаёІаёЎаёІаёЈаё–аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡ:
- Scenario аё•аёІаёЎаёӯаёёаё•аёӘаёІаё«аёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ (Banking, Healthcare, Manufacturing, Telco)
- аё•аёұаё§аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёҲаёІаёҒ tools аё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈа№ғаёҠа№үаёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳ
- а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ/аёҘаё” module аё•аёІаёЎаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёңаё№а№үа№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷ

</details>

<details>
<summary><b>аёӘаёӯаёҷаёҒаёөа№Ҳаё„аёҷ? аёЎаёөаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№Ҳаёіа№„аё«аёЎ?</b></summary>

- **аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№Ҳаёі**: 5 аё„аёҷ (In-house) / 10 аё„аёҷ (Public Class)
- **аёӘаё№аёҮаёӘаёёаё”**: 20вҖ“30 аё„аёҷ (аёӮаё¶а№үаёҷаёӯаёўаё№а№ҲаёҒаёұаёҡаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈ)
- **Executive Briefing**: 5вҖ“50 аё„аёҷ

</details>

<details>
<summary><b>аёЎаёөа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈа№ғаё«а№үаёҒаёҘаёұаёҡа№„аёӣа№ғаёҠа№үаё•а№Ҳаёӯа№„аё«аёЎ?</b></summary>

аё—аёёаёҒаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаёЈаё§аёЎ **SOC SOP Template Pack 279 аёҠаёҙа№үаёҷ** (EN/TH) вҖ” аёҷаёіа№„аёӣа№ғаёҠа№үаёҮаёІаёҷа№„аё”а№үаё—аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ӯаё”аёўа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎаёҲаёІаёҒаёЁаё№аёҷаёўа№Ң

</details>

## File: 07_Compliance_Privacy/Compliance_Gap_Analysis.en.md
# Compliance Gap Analysis SOP

**Document ID**: COMP-SOP-003
**Version**: 1.0
**Classification**: Internal
**Last Updated**: 2026-02-16

> Procedures for **assessing organizational compliance posture** against regulatory frameworks, identifying gaps, prioritizing remediation, and tracking progress. Covers ISO 27001, NIST CSF, PCI DSS, PDPA, and GDPR mapping.

---

## Compliance Frameworks Matrix

| Framework | Scope | Applicability | Review Cycle |
|:---|:---|:---|:---:|
| **ISO 27001:2022** | Information Security Management System | All organizations | Annual |
| **NIST CSF 2.0** | Cybersecurity risk management | Critical infrastructure, voluntary | Annual |
| **PCI DSS v4.0** | Cardholder data protection | Payment card processing | Quarterly + Annual |
| **PDPA** | Personal data protection (Thailand) | All Thai data processing | Annual |
| **GDPR** | Personal data protection (EU) | EU citizen data processing | Annual |
| **CSA CCM v4** | Cloud security controls | Cloud service providers | Annual |

---

## Gap Analysis Process

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[1. Define scope & frameworks] --> B[2. Inventory current controls]
    B --> C[3. Map controls to requirements]
    C --> D[4. Identify gaps]
    D --> E[5. Risk-score gaps]
    E --> F[6. Create remediation plan]
    F --> G[7. Assign owners & timelines]
    G --> H[8. Track & report progress]
    H --> I[9. Re-assess]
    I --> C

    style A fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff
    style D fill:#dc2626,color:#fff
    style F fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
```

### Phase Details

| Phase | Activities | Output | Duration |
|:---|:---|:---|:---:|
| **1. Scope** | Identify applicable frameworks, business units, systems | Scope document | 1 week |
| **2. Inventory** | Document existing policies, processes, tools, controls | Control inventory | 2 weeks |
| **3. Map** | Map each control to framework requirements | Control mapping matrix | 2 weeks |
| **4. Identify** | Compare implemented vs required вҶ’ find gaps | Gap register | 1 week |
| **5. Risk-score** | Score each gap by impact Г— likelihood | Prioritized gap list | 1 week |
| **6. Plan** | Define remediation actions, resources, budget | Remediation plan | 2 weeks |
| **7. Assign** | Designate owners, set deadlines | RACI + timeline | 1 week |
| **8. Track** | Monitor progress, report to stakeholders | Progress dashboard | Ongoing |
| **9. Re-assess** | Validate remediation effectiveness | Updated gap register | Quarterly |

---

## SOC-Specific Control Areas

### Detection & Response Controls

| Control Area | ISO 27001 | NIST CSF | PCI DSS | Key Questions |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---|
| **SIEM deployment** | A.8.15 | DE.CM | 10.6 | Is SIEM covering all critical log sources? |
| **Log collection** | A.8.15 | DE.CM-3 | 10.2 | Are all required sources onboarded? |
| **Alert monitoring** | A.8.16 | DE.AE | 10.6.1 | Is 24/7 monitoring in place? |
| **Incident response plan** | A.5.24 | RS.RP | 12.10 | Is IR plan documented and tested? |
| **Vulnerability scanning** | A.8.8 | DE.CM-8 | 11.3 | Is scanning running at required frequency? |
| **Penetration testing** | A.8.8 | PR.IP | 11.4 | Is annual pentest conducted? |
| **Access management** | A.8.2 | PR.AC | 7.1 | Is least privilege enforced? |
| **MFA** | A.8.5 | PR.AC-7 | 8.4 | Is MFA enabled for all admin access? |
| **Encryption** | A.8.24 | PR.DS-1 | 3.4 | Is data encrypted at rest and in transit? |
| **Backup & recovery** | A.8.13 | PR.IP-4 | 9.5 | Are backups tested regularly? |

### Data Protection Controls (PDPA/GDPR)

| Control | PDPA Section | GDPR Article | Assessment Questions |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---|
| **Lawful basis** | В§24 | Art. 6 | Is lawful basis documented for each processing activity? |
| **Consent management** | В§19 | Art. 7 | Is consent freely given, specific, informed? |
| **Data subject rights** | В§30вҖ“36 | Art. 15вҖ“22 | Can requests be fulfilled within 30 days? |
| **Breach notification** | В§37 | Art. 33вҖ“34 | Can we notify within 72 hours? |
| **DPO appointment** | В§41 | Art. 37 | Is DPO appointed with adequate authority? |
| **DPIA** | В§26 | Art. 35 | Are DPIAs conducted for high-risk processing? |
| **Cross-border transfer** | В§28 | Art. 44вҖ“49 | Are adequate safeguards in place? |
| **Records of processing** | В§39 | Art. 30 | Are processing records maintained? |

---

## Gap Risk Scoring

### Scoring Matrix

| Factor | 1 вҖ” Low | 2 вҖ” Medium | 3 вҖ” High | 4 вҖ” Critical |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Business impact** | Minimal disruption | Moderate impact | Significant loss | Business-critical failure |
| **Regulatory risk** | Advisory finding | Minor non-compliance | Material non-compliance | Regulatory action / fine |
| **Exploit likelihood** | Unlikely | Possible | Probable | Active exploitation |
| **Data sensitivity** | Public data | Internal data | Confidential / PII | Restricted / regulated |

### Risk Score Calculation

| Combined Score | Priority | Remediation Timeline | Reporting |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| **13вҖ“16** | рҹ”ҙ Critical | < 30 days | Weekly to CISO |
| **9вҖ“12** | рҹҹ  High | < 90 days | Monthly to CISO |
| **5вҖ“8** | рҹҹЎ Medium | < 180 days | Quarterly review |
| **1вҖ“4** | рҹҹў Low | Next audit cycle | Annual review |

---

## Remediation Tracking

### Remediation Plan Template

| Field | Value |
|:---|:---|
| **Gap ID** | GAP-____-_____ |
| **Framework** | ISO 27001 / NIST / PCI / PDPA |
| **Control Reference** | ______________ |
| **Current State** | ______________ |
| **Required State** | ______________ |
| **Gap Description** | ______________ |
| **Risk Score** | _____ / 16 |
| **Remediation Action** | ______________ |
| **Owner** | ______________ |
| **Budget Required** | аёҝ____________ |
| **Target Date** | ____-__-__ |
| **Evidence Required** | ______________ |
| **Status** | рҹ”ҙ Open / рҹҹЎ In Progress / рҹҹў Closed |

### Progress Dashboard

| Metric | Formula | Target |
|:---|:---|:---:|
| Overall compliance score | (Controls met Г· Total controls) Г— 100 | вүҘ 85% |
| Critical gaps open | Count of score 13вҖ“16 open | 0 |
| High gaps overdue | Count of score 9вҖ“12 past due | 0 |
| Mean time to remediate (critical) | Avg days gap open вҶ’ closed | < 30 days |
| Mean time to remediate (high) | Avg days gap open вҶ’ closed | < 90 days |
| Gaps reopened | Gaps that failed re-validation | < 5% |

---

## Audit Preparation

### Pre-Audit Checklist

| Category | Items | Owner |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Documentation** | Policies, procedures, standards current | Compliance Manager |
| **Evidence** | Screenshots, logs, configurations collected | SOC Lead |
| **Access** | Auditor accounts provisioned | IT Admin |
| **Interviews** | Staff briefed on roles and processes | Department Heads |
| **Testing** | Recent scan/pentest reports available | Security Engineer |
| **Remediation** | Previous findings addressed with evidence | Control Owners |

### Evidence Collection Matrix

| Control Type | Acceptable Evidence | Collection Frequency |
|:---|:---|:---:|
| **Technical** | System configs, screenshots, tool exports | Real-time / daily |
| **Administrative** | Policies, procedures, meeting minutes | As updated |
| **Operational** | Logs, reports, incident records | Daily / weekly |
| **Physical** | Photos, access logs, visitor records | Monthly |

---

## Reporting

### Compliance Report Structure

| Section | Content | Audience |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Executive Summary** | Overall score, critical findings, trend | Board / CISO |
| **Framework Status** | Per-framework compliance percentage | Management |
| **Gap Register** | All open gaps with risk scores | Control owners |
| **Remediation Progress** | Timeline tracking, overdue items | Project managers |
| **Risk Heat Map** | Visual risk distribution | Board / CISO |
| **Recommendations** | Prioritized next steps | Management |

### Reporting Cadence

| Report | Frequency | Audience |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Critical gap alert | Immediate | CISO |
| Compliance dashboard | Weekly | Security team |
| Progress report | Monthly | Management |
| Framework assessment | Quarterly | Board / CISO |
| Full audit report | Annual | Board / External |

---

## Metrics

| Metric | Target |
|:---|:---:|
| Overall compliance score | вүҘ 85% |
| Critical gaps open | 0 |
| Gap remediation on-time rate | вүҘ 90% |
| Average remediation time (critical) | < 30 days |
| Audit finding recurrence rate | < 10% |
| Evidence availability | вүҘ 95% |

## Minimum Evidence for Gap Closure

| Evidence | Why It Matters | Owner |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Updated control or procedure reference | Proves the gap was addressed in documentation | Control Owner |
| Operating evidence from production (ticket, log, screenshot, export) | Confirms the fix is working, not just written down | SOC Analyst / Security Engineer |
| Approval or risk-acceptance record | Distinguishes remediation from accepted residual risk | SOC Manager / CISO |
| Re-test or validation result | Shows the gap no longer fails the original check | Compliance Officer |
| Closure date and review trail in gap register | Preserves accountability and audit traceability | Compliance Officer |

## Executive Escalation Triggers for Gaps

| Condition | Escalate To | SLA | Required Decision |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Critical gap has no owner or no funded plan | CISO | Same business day | Assign owner and approve remediation path |
| Gap affects regulated data, cardholder systems, or legal reporting | CISO + Legal / DPO | Immediate | Accept risk temporarily or mandate control change |
| High-risk gap misses deadline twice | Executive sponsor | Next governance review | Escalate beyond local team remediation |
| Closure evidence is missing or fails validation | Compliance Officer + Control Owner | Within 5 business days | Reopen gap or provide evidence |

---

## Related Documents

-   [Compliance Mapping](Compliance_Mapping.en.md) вҖ” ISO 27001 / NIST CSF / PCI DSS mapping
-   [PDPA Incident Response](PDPA_Incident_Response.en.md) вҖ” 72-hour notification SOP
-   [SOC Maturity Assessment](../06_Operations_Management/SOC_Maturity_Assessment.en.md) вҖ” SOC capability assessment
-   [Vulnerability Management](../06_Operations_Management/Vulnerability_Management.en.md) вҖ” Scanning & remediation
-   [Third-Party Risk](../06_Operations_Management/Third_Party_Risk.en.md) вҖ” Vendor compliance

## References

-   [NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0](https://www.nist.gov/cyberframework)
-   [ISO/IEC 27001:2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001)
-   [PCI DSS v4.0](https://www.pcisecuritystandards.org/document_library/)


---

## File: 07_Compliance_Privacy/Compliance_Gap_Analysis.th.md
# Compliance Gap Analysis SOP / SOP аёҒаёІаёЈаё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№ҢаёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮаё§а№ҲаёІаёҮаё”а№үаёІаёҷ Compliance

**аёЈаё«аёұаёӘа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ**: COMP-SOP-003
**а№Җаё§аёӯаёЈа№ҢаёҠаёұаёҷ**: 1.0
**аёҒаёІаёЈаёҲаёұаё”аёҠаёұа№үаёҷаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёҘаёұаёҡ**: а№ғаёҠа№үаё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ
**аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё•аёҘа№ҲаёІаёӘаёёаё”**: 2026-02-16

> аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷ **аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё° Compliance, аёЈаё°аёҡаёёаёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮаё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ, аёҲаёұаё”аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮ, а№ҒаёҘаё°аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёІаёЎаё„аё§аёІаёЎаё„аё·аёҡаё«аёҷа№үаёІ** аё„аёЈаёӯаёҡаё„аёҘаёёаёЎ ISO 27001, NIST CSF, PCI DSS, PDPA, а№ҒаёҘаё° GDPR

---

## аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҡ Compliance

| Framework | аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё• | аёЈаёӯаёҡаё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷ |
|:---|:---|:---:|
| **ISO 27001:2022** | аёЈаё°аёҡаёҡаёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЎаёұа№Ҳаёҷаё„аёҮаёӣаёҘаёӯаё”аё аёұаёўаёӘаёІаёЈаёӘаёҷа№Җаё—аёЁ | аёЈаёІаёўаёӣаёө |
| **NIST CSF 2.0** | аёҡаёЈаёҙаё«аёІаёЈаёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈ cybersecurity risk | аёЈаёІаёўаёӣаёө |
| **PCI DSS v4.0** | аёӣаёҒаёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаёҡаёұаё•аёЈаёҠаёіаёЈаё°а№ҖаёҮаёҙаёҷ | аёЈаёІаёўа№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘ + аёЈаёІаёўаёӣаёө |
| **PDPA** | аё„аёёа№үаёЎаё„аёЈаёӯаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаёҡаёёаё„аё„аёҘ (а№„аё—аёў) | аёЈаёІаёўаёӣаёө |
| **GDPR** | аё„аёёа№үаёЎаё„аёЈаёӯаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаёҡаёёаё„аё„аёҘ (EU) | аёЈаёІаёўаёӣаёө |

---

## аёҒаёЈаё°аёҡаё§аёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ Gap Analysis

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[1. аёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё•] --> B[2. аёӘаёіаёЈаё§аёҲ controls]
    B --> C[3. Map controls аёҒаёұаёҡаёӮа№үаёӯаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”]
    C --> D[4. аёЈаё°аёҡаёёаёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮаё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ]
    D --> E[5. аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮ]
    E --> F[6. а№ҒаёңаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮ]
    F --> G[7. аёЎаёӯаёҡаё«аёЎаёІаёў + timeline]
    G --> H[8. аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёІаёЎ + аёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷ]
    H --> I[9. аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаёӢа№үаёі]
    I --> C

    style A fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff
    style D fill:#dc2626,color:#fff
    style F fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
```

---

## SOC-Specific Controls

### Detection & Response

| Control Area | ISO 27001 | NIST CSF | PCI DSS | аё„аёіаё–аёІаёЎаё«аёҘаёұаёҒ |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---|
| **SIEM** | A.8.15 | DE.CM | 10.6 | SIEM аё„аёЈаёӯаёҡаё„аёҘаёёаёЎ log sources аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё”? |
| **Log collection** | A.8.15 | DE.CM-3 | 10.2 | Onboard log sources аё—аёөа№ҲаёҲаёіа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷаё„аёЈаёҡ? |
| **Alert monitoring** | A.8.16 | DE.AE | 10.6.1 | аёЎаёө 24/7 monitoring? |
| **IR plan** | A.5.24 | RS.RP | 12.10 | IR plan аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§? |
| **Vuln scanning** | A.8.8 | DE.CM-8 | 11.3 | аёӘа№ҒаёҒаёҷаё•аёІаёЎаё„аё§аёІаёЎаё–аёөа№Ҳаё—аёөа№ҲаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”? |
| **Pentest** | A.8.8 | PR.IP | 11.4 | аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёҲаёІаё°аёЈаё°аёҡаёҡаёЈаёІаёўаёӣаёө? |
| **Access mgmt** | A.8.2 | PR.AC | 7.1 | Least privilege? |
| **MFA** | A.8.5 | PR.AC-7 | 8.4 | MFA аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ admin аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё”? |

### Data Protection (PDPA/GDPR)

| Control | PDPA | GDPR | аё„аёіаё–аёІаёЎ |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---|
| **аёҗаёІаёҷаё—аёІаёҮаёҒаёҺаё«аёЎаёІаёў** | В§24 | Art. 6 | аёЎаёөаёҗаёІаёҷаёҒаёҺаё«аёЎаёІаёўаё„аёЈаёҡаё—аёёаёҒаёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ? |
| **аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёўаёҙаёҷаёўаёӯаёЎ** | В§19 | Art. 7 | Consent а№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ free, specific, informed? |
| **аёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙа№ҖаёҲа№үаёІаёӮаёӯаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ** | В§30вҖ“36 | Art. 15вҖ“22 | аё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈа№„аё”а№үаё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 30 аё§аёұаёҷ? |
| **а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҘаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаё”** | В§37 | Art. 33вҖ“34 | а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮа№„аё”а№үаё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 72 аёҠаёЎ.? |
| **DPO** | В§41 | Art. 37 | а№Ғаё•а№ҲаёҮаё•аёұа№үаёҮ DPO а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§? |
| **DPIA** | В§26 | Art. 35 | аё—аёі DPIA аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ high-risk? |

## аёҒаёІаёЈа№ғаё«а№үаё„аё°а№Ғаёҷаёҷаё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮ

| аё„аё°а№ҒаёҷаёҷаёЈаё§аёЎ | Priority | а№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮаё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ | аёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷ |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| **13вҖ“16** | рҹ”ҙ Critical | < 30 аё§аёұаёҷ | аёЈаёІаёўаёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ңаё–аё¶аёҮ CISO |
| **9вҖ“12** | рҹҹ  High | < 90 аё§аёұаёҷ | аёЈаёІаёўа№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷаё–аё¶аёҮ CISO |
| **5вҖ“8** | рҹҹЎ Medium | < 180 аё§аёұаёҷ | аёЈаёІаёўа№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘ |
| **1вҖ“4** | рҹҹў Low | Audit cycle аё–аёұаё”а№„аёӣ | аёЈаёІаёўаёӣаёө |

---

## аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёҙаё”аё•аёІаёЎаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮ

| аё•аёұаё§аёҠаёөа№үаё§аёұаё” | аёӘаё№аё•аёЈ | а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў |
|:---|:---|:---:|
| Compliance score аёЈаё§аёЎ | (Controls met Г· Total) Г— 100 | вүҘ 85% |
| Critical gaps open | аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷ score 13вҖ“16 аё—аёөа№Ҳа№Җаёӣаёҙаё” | 0 |
| High gaps overdue | аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷ score 9вҖ“12 а№ҖаёҘаёўаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё” | 0 |
| MTTR (critical) | аё§аёұаёҷа№ҖаёүаёҘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҲаёІаёҒа№Җаёӣаёҙаё”вҶ’аёӣаёҙаё” | < 30 аё§аёұаёҷ |
| MTTR (high) | аё§аёұаёҷа№ҖаёүаёҘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҲаёІаёҒа№Җаёӣаёҙаё”вҶ’аёӣаёҙаё” | < 90 аё§аёұаёҷ |
| Gaps reopened | Gaps аё—аёөа№Ҳ re-validate а№„аёЎа№Ҳаёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ | < 5% |

---

## аё•аёұаё§аёҠаёөа№үаё§аёұаё”

| аё•аёұаё§аёҠаёөа№үаё§аёұаё” | а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў |
|:---|:---:|
| Overall compliance score | вүҘ 85% |
| Critical gaps open | 0 |
| Gap remediation on-time | вүҘ 90% |
| Remediation time (critical) | < 30 аё§аёұаёҷ |
| Audit finding recurrence | < 10% |
| Evidence availability | вүҘ 95% |

## аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№ҲаёіаёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёҙаё” Gap (Minimum Evidence for Gap Closure)

| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ | а№Җаё«аё•аёёаёңаёҘ | аёңаё№а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҙаё”аёҠаёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ control аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ procedure аё—аёөа№Ҳаёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё•а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ | аёһаёҙаёӘаё№аёҲаёҷа№Ңаё§а№ҲаёІаёЎаёөаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үа№ғаёҷаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ | Control Owner |
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷаёҲаёІаёҒ production а№ҖаёҠа№Ҳаёҷ ticket, log, screenshot, export | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаё§а№ҲаёІаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№ғаёҠа№Ҳа№Ғаё„а№Ҳа№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷа№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ | SOC Analyst / Security Engineer |
| approval аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ risk-acceptance record | а№ҒаёўаёҒа№ғаё«а№үаёҠаёұаё”аё§а№ҲаёІаёӣаёҙаё”аё”а№үаё§аёў remediation аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёўаёӯаёЎаёЈаёұаёҡаё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮ | SOC Manager / CISO |
| аёңаёҘ re-test аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ validation | а№ҒаёӘаё”аёҮаё§а№ҲаёІ gap а№„аёЎа№Ҳаё•аёҒа№ҖаёҮаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№„аёӮа№Җаё”аёҙаёЎаёӯаёөаёҒ | Compliance Officer |
| аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳаёӣаёҙаё”а№ҒаёҘаё° review trail а№ғаёҷ gap register | а№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ accountability а№ҒаёҘаё° audit traceability | Compliance Officer |

## Trigger аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮаё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ (Executive Escalation Triggers for Gaps)

| а№ҖаёҮаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№„аёӮ | аёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё–аё¶аёҮ | SLA | аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёұаё”аёӘаёҙаёҷа№ғаёҲаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№„аё”а№ү |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| critical gap а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө owner аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёөа№Ғаёңаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёҮаёҡ | CISO | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ | а№Ғаё•а№ҲаёҮаё•аёұа№үаёҮ owner а№ҒаёҘаё°аёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙ remediation path |
| gap аёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ regulated data, аёЈаё°аёҡаёҡаёҡаёұаё•аёЈ, аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаё аёІаёЈаё°аёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷаё•аёІаёЎаёҒаёҺаё«аёЎаёІаёў | CISO + Legal / DPO | аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө | аёЈаёұаёҡаё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮаёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§аё„аёЈаёІаё§аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёҡаёұаёҮаё„аёұаёҡа№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷ control |
| high-risk gap а№ҖаёҘаёўаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”аёӘаёӯаёҮаё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ | Executive sponsor | governance review аё–аёұаё”а№„аёӣ | аёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡа№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үа№ғаёҷаё—аёөаёЎаёўа№Ҳаёӯаёў |
| closure evidence а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёөаё«аёЈаё·аёӯ validation а№„аёЎа№Ҳаёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ | Compliance Officer + Control Owner | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 5 аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈ | а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” gap а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёҲаёұаё”аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷа№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ |

---

## Gap Analysis Methodology

```mermaid
graph TD
    Scope["рҹ“Ӣ аёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё•"] --> Inventory["рҹ“Ҡ аёӘаёіаёЈаё§аёҲ Controls аёӣаёұаёҲаёҲаёёаёҡаёұаёҷ"]
    Inventory --> Map["рҹ—әпёҸ Map аёҒаёұаёҡ Framework"]
    Map --> Assess["рҹ“қ аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ Gap"]
    Assess --> Prioritize["рҹҺҜ аёҲаёұаё”аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ"]
    Prioritize --> Remediate["рҹ”§ аё§аёІаёҮа№Ғаёңаёҷа№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮ"]
    Remediate --> Track["рҹ“Ҳ аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёІаёЎ"]
```

## а№Җаё—аёЎа№ҖаёһаёҘаё•аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё”

### ISO 27001 Controls Mapping

| Control | аё«аёЎаё§аё” | аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё° | Gap | аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|:---:|

| A.6 | Organization of InfoSec | вң…/вҡ пёҸ/вқҢ | [аёЈаё°аёҡаёё] | рҹ”ҙ/рҹҹЎ/рҹҹў |
| A.7 | Human Resource Security | вң…/вҡ пёҸ/вқҢ | [аёЈаё°аёҡаёё] | рҹ”ҙ/рҹҹЎ/рҹҹў |
| A.8 | Asset Management | вң…/вҡ пёҸ/вқҢ | [аёЈаё°аёҡаёё] | рҹ”ҙ/рҹҹЎ/рҹҹў |
| A.9 | Access Control | вң…/вҡ пёҸ/вқҢ | [аёЈаё°аёҡаёё] | рҹ”ҙ/рҹҹЎ/рҹҹў |
| A.10 | Cryptography | вң…/вҡ пёҸ/вқҢ | [аёЈаё°аёҡаёё] | рҹ”ҙ/рҹҹЎ/рҹҹў |
| A.11 | Physical Security | вң…/вҡ пёҸ/вқҢ | [аёЈаё°аёҡаёё] | рҹ”ҙ/рҹҹЎ/рҹҹў |
| A.12 | Operations Security | вң…/вҡ пёҸ/вқҢ | [аёЈаё°аёҡаёё] | рҹ”ҙ/рҹҹЎ/рҹҹў |
| A.13 | Communications Security | вң…/вҡ пёҸ/вқҢ | [аёЈаё°аёҡаёё] | рҹ”ҙ/рҹҹЎ/рҹҹў |

| Function | Category | аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё° | аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ (1-4) |

| **Identify** | Asset Management | вң…/вҡ пёҸ/вқҢ | [X] |
