## аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 4: ROI & Business Case

### аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёёаёҷаёӮаёӯаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈ "а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө SOC"

| аё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮ | аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёёаёҷа№ҖаёүаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёў/аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ |
|:---|:---:|
| Data breach (ASEAN) | аёҝ85вҖ“120M |
| Ransomware | аёҝ10вҖ“50M |
| BEC/а№Ӯаёӯаёҷа№ҖаёҮаёҙаёҷаё«аёҘаёӯаёҒ | аёҝ3вҖ“15M |
| аё„а№ҲаёІаёӣаёЈаёұаёҡ PDPA | аёӘаё№аёҮаёӘаёёаё” аёҝ5M/аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ |
| Downtime (аё•а№ҲаёӯаёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§а№ӮаёЎаёҮ) | аёҝ100KвҖ“1M+ |

### аёӘаё№аё•аёЈ ROI

```
ROI = (аё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮаёҘаё” - аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёёаёҷ SOC) / аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёёаёҷ SOC Г— 100%

аё•аёұаё§аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮ:
  а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө SOC: 2 а№Җаё«аё•аёё/аёӣаёө Г— аёҝ20M = аёҝ40M аё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮ
  аёЎаёө SOC (95%): 0.1 а№Җаё«аё•аёё/аёӣаёө = аёҝ2M
  аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёёаёҷ SOC: аёҝ8M/аёӣаёө
  ROI = (аёҝ38M - аёҝ8M) / аёҝ8M = 375%
```

---

## аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 5: Timeline аёҒаёІаёЈаёҲа№үаёІаёҮ

| аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң | аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ |
|:---:|:---|
| 1вҖ“2 | а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ JD, а№ӮаёһаёӘаё•а№Ң JobThai/LinkedIn |
| 3вҖ“4 | аё„аёұаё”аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮ, аёҷаёұаё”аёӘаёұаёЎаё аёІаё©аё“а№Ң |
| 5вҖ“6 | аёӘаёұаёЎаё аёІаё©аё“а№Ңа№Җаё—аё„аёҷаёҙаё„ (а№ғаёҠа№ү [аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯаёӘаёұаёЎаё аёІаё©аё“а№Ң](../05_Incident_Response/Interview_Guide.th.md)) |
| 7 | аёўаё·а№ҲаёҷаёӮа№үаёӯа№ҖаёӘаёҷаёӯ |
| 8вҖ“9 | Notice period (30 аё§аёұаёҷ) |
| 10вҖ“12 | Onboarding + аёқаё¶аёҒаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎ |

---

## а№ҒаёңаёҷаёҮаёҡаёӣаёЈаё°аёЎаёІаё“аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё”

### аё«аёЎаё§аё”аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёёаёҷ

| аё«аёЎаё§аё” | аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈ | аё„а№ҲаёІа№ғаёҠа№үаёҲа№ҲаёІаёўа№Ӯаё”аёўаёӣаёЈаё°аёЎаёІаё“ (аё•а№Ҳаёӯаёӣаёө) |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **аёҡаёёаё„аёҘаёІаёҒаёЈ** | а№ҖаёҮаёҙаёҷа№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ + аёӘаё§аёұаёӘаё”аёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ | 60вҖ“70% аёӮаёӯаёҮ budget |
| **а№Җаё—аё„а№Ӯаёҷа№ӮаёҘаёўаёө** | License fees, infra, SaaS | 20вҖ“25% |
| **аёқаё¶аёҒаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎ** | Cert, training, conference | 5вҖ“10% |
| **аё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҮаёІаёҷ** | аёӘаёіаёҷаёұаёҒаёҮаёІаёҷ, аёӯаёёаёӣаёҒаёЈаё“а№Ң, аё„а№ҲаёІаёӘаёІаёҳаёІаёЈаё“аё№аёӣа№Ӯаё аё„ | 3вҖ“5% |

### аё•аёІаёЈаёІаёҮа№ҖаёҮаёҙаёҷа№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ (аё•аёҘаёІаё”а№„аё—аёў вҖ” аёӣаёЈаё°аёЎаёІаё“)

| аё•аёіа№Ғаё«аёҷа№ҲаёҮ | Junior | Mid-Level | Senior |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **SOC Analyst** | 25,000вҖ“35,000 аёҝ | 40,000вҖ“60,000 аёҝ | 65,000вҖ“90,000 аёҝ |
| **Detection Engineer** | 35,000вҖ“50,000 аёҝ | 55,000вҖ“80,000 аёҝ | 85,000вҖ“120,000 аёҝ |
| **Incident Responder** | 30,000вҖ“45,000 аёҝ | 50,000вҖ“75,000 аёҝ | 80,000вҖ“110,000 аёҝ |
| **SOC Manager** | вҖ” | 70,000вҖ“100,000 аёҝ | 100,000вҖ“150,000 аёҝ |
| **CISO** | вҖ” | вҖ” | 150,000вҖ“300,000 аёҝ |

### ROI Calculation

```
ROI = (аё„а№ҲаёІа№ҖаёӘаёөаёўаё«аёІаёўаё—аёөа№Ҳаёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷа№„аё”а№ү - аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёёаёҷ SOC) / аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёёаёҷ SOC Г— 100

аё•аёұаё§аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮ:
- аё„а№ҲаёІа№ҖаёӘаёөаёўаё«аёІаёў data breach а№ҖаёүаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёў (а№„аё—аёў): ~50 аёҘа№үаёІаёҷаёҡаёІаё—
- аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёёаёҷ SOC аё•а№Ҳаёӯаёӣаёө: ~5 аёҘа№үаёІаёҷаёҡаёІаё—
- аёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷ breach а№„аё”а№ү 1 аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ/аёӣаёө = ROI 900%
```

## Staffing Model аё•аёІаёЎаёӮаёҷаёІаё”аёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈ

### Model A: SOC аёӮаёҷаёІаё”а№ҖаёҘа№ҮаёҒ (3вҖ“5 FTE)

| аёҡаё—аёҡаёІаё— | аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷ | а№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷ |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| SOC Manager (а№ҖаёҘа№Ҳаёҷаё«аёҘаёІаёўаёҡаё—) | 1 | 8/5 |
| Tier 1/2 Analyst | 2-3 | 8/5 + on-call |
| Detection Engineer (part-time) | 0.5 | аёЈа№Ҳаё§аёЎаёҒаёұаёҡ IT |
| **Coverage**: 8/5 + on-call | | |

### Model B: SOC аёӮаёҷаёІаё”аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ (8вҖ“12 FTE)

| аёҡаё—аёҡаёІаё— | аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷ | а№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷ |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| SOC Manager | 1 | 8/5 |
| Shift Lead | 2 | 16/5 |
| Tier 1 Analyst | 4 | 16/5 + on-call |
| Tier 2 Analyst | 2 | 8/5 + on-call |
| Detection Engineer | 1 | 8/5 |
| **Coverage**: 16/5 + on-call | | |

### Model C: SOC аёӮаёҷаёІаё”а№ғаё«аёҚа№Ҳ (15+ FTE)

| аёҡаё—аёҡаёІаё— | аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷ | а№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷ |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| SOC Manager | 1 | 8/5 |
| Shift Lead | 3вҖ“4 | 24/7 |
| Tier 1 Analyst | 6вҖ“8 | 24/7 |
| Tier 2 Analyst | 3вҖ“4 | 16/7 |
| Tier 3 / Hunt | 1вҖ“2 | 8/5 |
| Detection Engineer | 2 | 8/5 |
| **Coverage**: 24/7 | | |

## Hiring Pipeline

```mermaid
graph LR
    JD["рҹ“қ JD"] --> Post["рҹ“ў аёӣаёЈаё°аёҒаёІаёЁ"]
    Post --> Screen["рҹ“Ӣ аё„аёұаё”аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮ"]
    Screen --> Tech["рҹ’» аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡ"]
    Tech --> Interview["рҹӨқ аёӘаёұаёЎаё аёІаё©аё“а№Ң"]
    Interview --> Offer["рҹ“ғ а№ҖаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮаёІаёҷ"]
    Offer --> Onboard["рҹҺҜ Onboarding"]
```

## Staffing Models

### Team Size by Organization Scale

| Organization Size | SOC Tier | Min Staff | Recommended |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| < 500 employees | Hybrid/MSSP | 2-3 | 4-5 |
| 500-2,000 | Basic SOC | 4-6 | 8-10 |
| 2,000-10,000 | Full SOC | 8-12 | 15-20 |
| 10,000+ | Advanced SOC | 15-25 | 25-40 |

### Role-based Salary Guide (аёӣаёЈаё°аёЎаёІаё“, THB/а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ)

| Role | Junior | Mid | Senior |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| SOC Analyst | 25-35K | 35-55K | 55-80K |
| Incident Responder | 35-50K | 50-75K | 75-120K |
| Threat Hunter | 40-60K | 60-90K | 90-150K |
| SOC Manager | - | 80-120K | 120-200K |

### Budget Allocation Framework

| Category | % of Budget | Includes |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Personnel | 55-65% | Salary, benefits, training |
| Technology | 20-30% | SIEM, EDR, SOAR, TI |
| Operations | 5-10% | Cloud, infra, maintenance |
| Training | 5-10% | Certs, conferences, labs |

### Overtime & Burnout Prevention

| Metric | Warning | Action |
|:---|:---|:---|
| OT > 20 hrs/mo | вҡ пёҸ | Review workload |
| Turnover > 15% | рҹ”ҙ | Retention plan |
| Satisfaction < 3/5 | вҡ пёҸ | Team survey |

## аёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№ҲаёіаёҒа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙаёҮаёҡ (Minimum Inputs for Budget Approval)

| аёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ | а№Җаё«аё•аёёаёңаёҘ | аёңаё№а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҙаё”аёҠаёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| coverage model а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў (8x5, 16x5, 24x7) | а№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷаё•аёұаё§аёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷаё„аёҷа№ҒаёҘаё°аё„а№ҲаёІа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ | CISO |
| аёЈаё°аёҡаёҡ аёңаё№а№үа№ғаёҠа№ү а№ҒаёҘаё°аёӣаёЈаёҙаёЎаёІаё“ log аё—аёөа№Ҳаёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳа№ғаёҷ scope | аёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёұа№үаёҮаёҮаёҡа№Җаё—аё„а№Ӯаёҷа№ӮаёҘаёўаёөаё•а№Ҳаёіа№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ | Security Engineer |
| staffing plan а№ҒаёўаёҒаё•аёІаёЎ role а№ҒаёҘаё° shift | а№ҒаёӣаёҘаёҮ architecture а№ғаё«а№үа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷаё•а№үаёҷаё—аёёаёҷаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҮаёІаёҷаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ | SOC Manager |
| а№Ғаёңаёҷаёқаё¶аёҒаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎа№ҒаёҘаё°а№ғаёҡаёЈаёұаёҡаёЈаёӯаёҮ | аёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷ hidden cost аё«аёҘаёұаёҮа№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎаёҲа№үаёІаёҮаёҮаёІаёҷ | SOC Manager |
| аёӘаёЎаёЎаё•аёҙаёҗаёІаёҷаё”а№үаёІаёҷ cost-of-risk а№ҒаёҘаё° incident | аё—аёіа№ғаё«а№ү business case аё„аёёаёўаёҒаёұаёҡ finance а№„аё”а№үаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёЎаёөа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёңаёҘ | CISO |

## Trigger аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡа№ҖаёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёҮаёҡа№ҒаёҘаё°аё„аёҷ (Budget and Hiring Escalation Triggers)

| а№ҖаёҮаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№„аёӮ | аёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё–аё¶аёҮ | SLA | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаё—аёі |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| аёҮаёҡаё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙа№„аёЎа№ҲаёһаёӯаёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ staffing аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ tooling аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№Ҳаёі | Executive sponsor | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ | аёҘаё” scope аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёҲаёұаё”аёҮаёҡаёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ minimum viable model |
| role аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚаёўаёұаёҮаё«аёІаё„аёҷа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үа№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷ milestone | CISO + HR | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 5 аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈ | а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” backup hiring plan аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№ғаёҠа№ү MSSP аёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§аё„аёЈаёІаё§ |
| attrition, OT аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ burnout а№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷ threshold | SOC Manager + CISO | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ | аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡаёҒаё°аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎаёҒаёіаёҘаёұаёҮаё„аёҷ |
| аёҮаёҡаёқаё¶аёҒаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎаё–аё№аёҒаёҘаё”аё•а№ҲаёіаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ readiness аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№Ҳаёі | CISO | аёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎа№ғаёҠа№үаёҮаёІаёҷ | аёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙа№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёҠаё°аёҘаёӯ rollout а№ғаё«а№үаё•аёЈаёҮаёҒаёұаёҡ capability |

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ

- [аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯаёӘаёұаёЎаё аёІаё©аё“а№Ң](../05_Incident_Response/Interview_Guide.th.md)

## File: 01_SOC_Fundamentals/Infrastructure_Setup.en.md
# SOC Infrastructure Setup Guide вҖ” Hands-On Installation

> **Document ID:** INFRA-001
> **Version:** 1.0
> **Last Updated:** 2026-02-15
> **Prerequisite:** Read [Technology Stack Guide](Technology_Stack.en.md) to choose your stack

---

## This Guide Covers

Step-by-step installation of the **Open-Source Stack (Stack A)** using Wazuh. This is the recommended starting point for beginners вҖ” zero cost, full functionality.

> If you chose Microsoft Sentinel (Stack B), skip to [Sentinel Quick Setup](#sentinel-quick-setup).

---

## Part 1: Wazuh All-in-One Installation

### Requirements

| Item | Minimum | Recommended |
|:---|:---|:---|
| OS | Ubuntu 22.04 LTS / CentOS 8 | Ubuntu 22.04 LTS |
| CPU | 4 cores | 8 cores |
| RAM | 8 GB | 16 GB |
| Disk | 50 GB | 200 GB+ (depends on log volume) |
| Network | Static IP, port 1514/1515/443 open | Dedicated VLAN |

### Step 1: Install Wazuh (Single-Node)

```bash
# Download and run Wazuh installer (automated)
curl -sO https://packages.wazuh.com/4.9/wazuh-install.sh
curl -sO https://packages.wazuh.com/4.9/config.yml

# Edit config.yml вҖ” set your server IP
cat > config.yml << 'EOF'
nodes:
  indexer:
    - name: wazuh-indexer
      ip: "YOUR_SERVER_IP"
  server:
    - name: wazuh-server
      ip: "YOUR_SERVER_IP"
  dashboard:
    - name: wazuh-dashboard
      ip: "YOUR_SERVER_IP"
EOF

# Run installer (takes 5-10 minutes)
sudo bash wazuh-install.sh -a

# вҡ пёҸ SAVE the admin password printed at the end!
# Access dashboard: https://YOUR_SERVER_IP
# Username: admin
# Password: (shown in output)
```

### Step 2: Verify Installation

```bash
# Check all services running
sudo systemctl status wazuh-manager
sudo systemctl status wazuh-indexer
sudo systemctl status wazuh-dashboard

# Check Wazuh API
curl -k -u admin:PASSWORD https://localhost:55000/?pretty

# Open browser вҶ’ https://YOUR_SERVER_IP
# Login вҶ’ You should see the Wazuh dashboard
```

### Step 3: Deploy Agents on Endpoints

#### Windows Agent
```powershell
# Download from Wazuh dashboard вҶ’ Agents вҶ’ Deploy new agent
# Or use PowerShell:
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://packages.wazuh.com/4.x/windows/wazuh-agent-4.9.0-1.msi -OutFile wazuh-agent.msi

# Install with server address
msiexec.exe /i wazuh-agent.msi /q WAZUH_MANAGER="YOUR_SERVER_IP" WAZUH_AGENT_GROUP="default"

# Start service
net start WazuhSvc
```

#### Linux Agent
```bash
# Ubuntu/Debian
curl -s https://packages.wazuh.com/key/GPG-KEY-WAZUH | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/wazuh.gpg
echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/wazuh.gpg] https://packages.wazuh.com/4.x/apt/ stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/wazuh.list
sudo apt update && sudo apt install wazuh-agent -y

# Configure manager address
sudo sed -i 's/MANAGER_IP/YOUR_SERVER_IP/' /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf

# Start agent
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable wazuh-agent
sudo systemctl start wazuh-agent
```

### Step 4: Verify Agents Connected

```bash
# On Wazuh server вҖ” list connected agents
sudo /var/ossec/bin/agent_control -l

# Expected output:
# ID: 001, Name: web-server-01, Status: Active
# ID: 002, Name: dc-01, Status: Active
```

---

## Part 2: Configure Log Sources

### Active Directory Logs вҶ’ Wazuh

On each Domain Controller, configure the Wazuh agent:

```xml
<!-- Add to agent ossec.conf on DC -->
<ossec_config>
  <localfile>
    <location>Security</location>
    <log_format>eventchannel</log_format>
    <query>Event/System[EventID=4624 or EventID=4625 or EventID=4648 or
           EventID=4672 or EventID=4688 or EventID=4720 or EventID=4726 or
           EventID=4732 or EventID=4756 or EventID=1102]</query>
  </localfile>
  <localfile>
    <location>Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational</location>
    <log_format>eventchannel</log_format>
  </localfile>
</ossec_config>
```

### Firewall (Syslog) вҶ’ Wazuh

```xml
<!-- Add to Wazuh server ossec.conf -->
<ossec_config>
  <remote>
    <connection>syslog</connection>
    <port>514</port>
    <protocol>udp</protocol>
    <allowed-ips>FIREWALL_IP</allowed-ips>
  </remote>
</ossec_config>
```

On your firewall, configure syslog output to `WAZUH_SERVER_IP:514`.

### Linux Servers вҶ’ Wazuh

```xml
<!-- Auto-monitored on agent install, but add custom logs: -->
<ossec_config>
  <localfile>
    <location>/var/log/auth.log</location>
    <log_format>syslog</log_format>
  </localfile>
  <localfile>
    <location>/var/log/nginx/access.log</location>
    <log_format>syslog</log_format>
  </localfile>
</ossec_config>
```

---

## Part 3: Install Sysmon (Windows Enhanced Logging)

Sysmon dramatically improves Windows detection capability:

```powershell
# Download Sysmon
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://download.sysinternals.com/files/Sysmon.zip -OutFile Sysmon.zip
Expand-Archive Sysmon.zip

# Download recommended config (SwiftOnSecurity)
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SwiftOnSecurity/sysmon-config/master/sysmonconfig-export.xml -OutFile sysmonconfig.xml

# Install Sysmon with config
.\Sysmon64.exe -accepteula -i sysmonconfig.xml

# Verify
Get-Service Sysmon64
Get-WinEvent -LogName "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" -MaxEvents 5
```

---

## Part 4: Import Sigma Rules into Wazuh

```bash
# Wazuh uses its own rule format, but you can create custom rules
# based on Sigma detection logic

# Example: Create a brute force detection rule
sudo cat >> /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml << 'EOF'
<group name="authentication_failures,">
  <rule id="100001" level="10" frequency="10" timeframe="300">
    <if_matched_sid>60122</if_matched_sid>
    <description>SOC: Brute force - 10+ login failures in 5 min (PB-04)</description>
    <mitre>
      <id>T1110</id>
    </mitre>
    <group>attack,brute_force,PB-04</group>
  </rule>
</group>
EOF

# Restart to apply
sudo systemctl restart wazuh-manager

# Validate rules
sudo /var/ossec/bin/wazuh-logtest
```

---

## Part 5: Set Up Alert Notifications

### Email Alerts

```xml
<!-- Add to Wazuh server ossec.conf -->
<ossec_config>
  <global>
    <email_notification>yes</email_notification>
    <smtp_server>smtp.gmail.com</smtp_server>
    <email_from>soc-alerts@company.com</email_from>
    <email_to>soc-team@company.com</email_to>
    <email_maxperhour>100</email_maxperhour>
  </global>

  <email_alerts>
    <email_to>soc-team@company.com</email_to>
    <level>10</level>
  </email_alerts>
</ossec_config>
```

### Slack Integration

```bash
# Create a Wazuh integration script for Slack
sudo cat > /var/ossec/integrations/custom-slack.py << 'PYEOF'
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys, json, requests

SLACK_WEBHOOK = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/YOUR/WEBHOOK/URL"

def main():
    alert_file = open(sys.argv[1])
    alert = json.load(alert_file)

    msg = {
        "text": f"рҹҡЁ *SOC Alert (Level {alert['rule']['level']})*\n"
                f"Rule: {alert['rule']['description']}\n"
                f"Agent: {alert.get('agent', {}).get('name', 'N/A')}\n"
                f"Time: {alert['timestamp']}"
    }
    requests.post(SLACK_WEBHOOK, json=msg)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
PYEOF

chmod 750 /var/ossec/integrations/custom-slack.py
chown root:wazuh /var/ossec/integrations/custom-slack.py
```

---

## Part 6: TheHive Installation (Ticketing)

```bash
# Install prerequisites
sudo apt install -y openjdk-11-jre-headless

# Add TheHive repository
wget -qO- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/StrangeBeeCorp/Security/main/PGP%20keys/packages.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/strangebee-archive-keyring.gpg
echo 'deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/strangebee-archive-keyring.gpg] https://deb.strangebee.com thehive-5.x main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/strangebee.list

sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y thehive

# Start TheHive
sudo systemctl enable thehive
sudo systemctl start thehive

# Access: http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:9000
# Default login: admin@thehive.local / secret
# вҡ пёҸ CHANGE PASSWORD IMMEDIATELY
```

---

## Sentinel Quick Setup

If you chose **Stack B (Microsoft)**:

```
Step 1: Azure Portal вҶ’ Create resource вҶ’ Microsoft Sentinel
Step 2: Create Log Analytics Workspace
Step 3: Add Sentinel to workspace
Step 4: Data connectors вҶ’ Enable:
        вң… Microsoft 365 Defender
        вң… Azure Active Directory
        вң… Azure Activity
        вң… Microsoft Defender for Cloud
Step 5: Analytics вҶ’ Rule templates вҶ’ Enable recommended rules
Step 6: Done! You have a SIEM.
```

> Time estimate: **30 minutes** if you have Azure admin access.

---

## Verification Checklist

After setup, verify everything works:

```
в–Ў Wazuh dashboard accessible at https://SERVER_IP
в–Ў At least 3 agents connected and active
в–Ў Events visible in dashboard (real-time)
в–Ў AD login events appearing (Event ID 4624/4625)
в–Ў Firewall logs arriving via syslog
в–Ў Sysmon installed on Windows endpoints
в–Ў Custom brute-force rule triggers on test
в–Ў Email/Slack alerts received
в–Ў TheHive accessible and login works
в–Ў Take a screenshot and celebrate! рҹҺү
```

---

## Troubleshooting

| Problem | Solution |
|:---|:---|
| Agent can't connect | Check firewall: port 1514/1515 open? |
| No data in dashboard | Wait 5 min, then check agent status |
| Syslog not arriving | Check `tcpdump -i any port 514` on Wazuh server |
| High disk usage | Reduce log retention or add disk |
| Dashboard slow | Increase indexer RAM to 16 GB |
| Agent shows "Disconnected" | Restart agent: `systemctl restart wazuh-agent` |

---

## File: 01_SOC_Fundamentals/Infrastructure_Setup.th.md
# аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯаё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ SOC Infrastructure вҖ” Hands-On

> **аёЈаё«аёұаёӘа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ:** INFRA-001
> **а№Җаё§аёӯаёЈа№ҢаёҠаёұаёҷ:** 1.0
> **аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё•аёҘа№ҲаёІаёӘаёёаё”:** 2026-02-15
> **аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷ:** [аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯа№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒ Technology Stack](Technology_Stack.th.md)

---

## аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯаёҷаёөа№үаё„аёЈаёӯаёҡаё„аёҘаёёаёЎ

аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ **Open-Source Stack (Stack A)** аё”а№үаё§аёў Wazuh аё—аёөаёҘаё°аёӮаёұа№үаёҷ вҖ” аё„а№ҲаёІа№ғаёҠа№үаёҲа№ҲаёІаёўаёЁаё№аёҷаёўа№Ң аёҹаёұаёҮаёҒа№ҢаёҠаёұаёҷаё„аёЈаёҡ

---

## аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 1: аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ Wazuh

### аё„аё§аёІаёЎаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№Ҳаёі
- Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
- 4 cores / 8 GB RAM / 50 GB disk
- Static IP

### аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ

```bash
# аё”аёІаё§аёҷа№Ңа№Ӯаё«аёҘаё”а№ҒаёҘаё°аёЈаёұаёҷ
curl -sO https://packages.wazuh.com/4.9/wazuh-install.sh
curl -sO https://packages.wazuh.com/4.9/config.yml

# а№ҒаёҒа№ү config.yml вҖ” а№ғаёӘа№Ҳ IP а№ҖаёӢаёҙаёЈа№Ңаёҹа№Җаё§аёӯаёЈа№Ң
# а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§аёЈаёұаёҷ:
sudo bash wazuh-install.sh -a

# вҡ пёҸ аёҲаё”аёЈаё«аёұаёӘ admin аё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҒаёӘаё”аёҮаё•аёӯаёҷаёҲаёҡ!
# а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІ: https://YOUR_SERVER_IP
```

### аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ
```bash
sudo systemctl status wazuh-manager
sudo systemctl status wazuh-indexer
sudo systemctl status wazuh-dashboard
```

---

## аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 2: аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ Agent

### Windows
```powershell
# аё”аёІаё§аёҷа№Ңа№Ӯаё«аёҘаё”аёҲаёІаёҒ Wazuh Dashboard вҶ’ Agents вҶ’ Deploy
# аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ:
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://packages.wazuh.com/4.x/windows/wazuh-agent-4.9.0-1.msi -OutFile wazuh-agent.msi
msiexec.exe /i wazuh-agent.msi /q WAZUH_MANAGER="YOUR_SERVER_IP"
net start WazuhSvc
```

### Linux
```bash
# Ubuntu/Debian
curl -s https://packages.wazuh.com/key/GPG-KEY-WAZUH | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/wazuh.gpg
echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/wazuh.gpg] https://packages.wazuh.com/4.x/apt/ stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/wazuh.list
sudo apt update && sudo apt install wazuh-agent -y
sudo sed -i 's/MANAGER_IP/YOUR_SERVER_IP/' /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf
sudo systemctl enable --now wazuh-agent
```

---

## аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 3: аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІ Log Sources

### Active Directory
```xml
<!-- а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎа№ғаёҷ agent ossec.conf аёҡаёҷ DC -->
<localfile>
  <location>Security</location>
  <log_format>eventchannel</log_format>
  <query>Event/System[EventID=4624 or EventID=4625 or EventID=4688 or EventID=4720 or EventID=1102]</query>
</localfile>
```

### Firewall (Syslog)
```xml
<!-- а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎа№ғаёҷ Wazuh server ossec.conf -->
<remote>
  <connection>syslog</connection>
  <port>514</port>
  <protocol>udp</protocol>
  <allowed-ips>FIREWALL_IP</allowed-ips>
</remote>
```

---



### Linux Servers

```bash
# аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ rsyslog forwarding а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ Wazuh
cat >> /etc/rsyslog.conf << 'EOF'
*.* @@<WAZUH_IP>:514
EOF
systemctl restart rsyslog
```

| Log Source | аё§аёҙаёҳаёөа№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№Ҳаёӯ | аё•аёұаё§аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮ Log |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Linux Auth** | rsyslog вҶ’ Wazuh | `/var/log/auth.log` |
| **Apache/Nginx** | Filebeat вҶ’ Wazuh | access.log, error.log |
| **Cloud Trail** | S3 вҶ’ Wazuh module | AWS CloudTrail JSON |

## аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 4: аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ Sysmon (Windows)

```powershell
# а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаёІаёЎаёІаёЈаё–аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ Windows аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёЎаёІаёҒ
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://download.sysinternals.com/files/Sysmon.zip -OutFile Sysmon.zip
Expand-Archive Sysmon.zip
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SwiftOnSecurity/sysmon-config/master/sysmonconfig-export.xml -OutFile sysmonconfig.xml
.\Sysmon64.exe -accepteula -i sysmonconfig.xml
```

---



```xml
<!-- а№ғаёҠа№ү Sysmon config аёҲаёІаёҒ SwiftOnSecurity -->
<!-- аё”аёІаё§аёҷа№Ңа№Ӯаё«аёҘаё”аёҲаёІаёҒ: github.com/SwiftOnSecurity/sysmon-config -->

Sysmon64.exe -accepteula -i sysmonconfig-export.xml
```

> Events аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮ monitor: **Event ID 1** (Process Create), **Event ID 3** (Network Connect), **Event ID 11** (File Create)

## аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 4.5: Import Sigma Rules а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ Wazuh

```bash
# аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ sigma-cli
pip3 install sigma-cli

# а№ҒаёӣаёҘаёҮ rules а№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёЈаё№аёӣа№Ғаёҡаёҡ Wazuh
sigma convert -t wazuh -p wazuh rules/ -o wazuh_rules.xml

# аё„аёұаё”аёҘаёӯаёҒа№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ Wazuh
cp wazuh_rules.xml /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_sigma.xml
systemctl restart wazuh-manager
```

## аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 5: аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІ Alert (Slack)

```bash
# аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ Slack webhook вҶ’ аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІ Wazuh integration
# а№ҖаёЎаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаёө alert level 10+ вҶ’ аёӘа№ҲаёҮ Slack аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө
```

---



### Email Alerts

```xml
<!-- /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf -->
<global>
  <email_notification>yes</email_notification>
  <smtp_server>smtp.example.com</smtp_server>
  <email_from>wazuh@example.com</email_from>
  <email_to>soc@example.com</email_to>
  <email_maxperhour>12</email_maxperhour>
</global>

<email_alerts>
  <email_to>soc@example.com</email_to>
  <level>10</level>
</email_alerts>
```

### аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІ Slack Webhook

```bash
# аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ Slack integration script
cat > /var/ossec/integrations/custom-slack.sh << 'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
WEBHOOK_URL="https://hooks.slack.com/services/YOUR/WEBHOOK/URL"
ALERT_JSON=$(cat "$1")
curl -s -X POST "$WEBHOOK_URL" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d "{"text": "рҹҡЁ Wazuh Alert: $(echo $ALERT_JSON | jq -r '.rule.description')"}"
SCRIPT
chmod +x /var/ossec/integrations/custom-slack.sh
```

## аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 6: Sentinel (аё–а№үаёІа№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒ Stack B)

```
1. Azure Portal вҶ’ аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ Microsoft Sentinel
2. аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ Log Analytics Workspace
3. а№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎ Data Connectors: M365 Defender, Azure AD, Azure Activity
4. а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” Analytics Rules вҶ’ а№ғаёҠа№ү template
5. а№ҖаёӘаёЈа№ҮаёҲ! аёЎаёө SIEM а№ғаёҷ 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө
```

---



```powershell
# Quick Setup вҖ” Azure Sentinel
# 1. аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ Log Analytics Workspace
az monitor log-analytics workspace create \
  --resource-group SOC-RG \
  --workspace-name SOC-Sentinel \
  --location southeastasia

# 2. а№Җаёӣаёҙаё”а№ғаёҠа№ү Sentinel
az sentinel onboard --resource-group SOC-RG \
  --workspace-name SOC-Sentinel

# 3. а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” Data Connectors
# - Azure AD Sign-in Logs
# - Microsoft 365
# - Azure Activity
# - Syslog (Linux)
```

## аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 7: аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ TheHive (Ticketing)

```bash
# аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ TheHive 5 аё”а№үаё§аёў Docker
docker compose up -d

# docker-compose.yml
# services:
#   thehive:
#     image: strangebee/thehive:5
#     ports:
#       - "9000:9000"
#   elasticsearch:
#     image: elasticsearch:7.17.9
#   cassandra:
#     image: cassandra:4
```

> а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮ TheHive аё—аёөа№Ҳ `http://<SERVER_IP>:9000` вҖ” default: `admin@thehive.local` / `secret`

## Troubleshooting

| аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ | аёӘаёІа№Җаё«аё•аёё | аё§аёҙаёҳаёөа№ҒаёҒа№ү |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Agent а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№Ҳаёӯ | Firewall block port 1514/1515 | а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” port аё—аёөа№Ҳ firewall |
| Log а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІ SIEM | rsyslog config аёңаёҙаё” | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ IP а№ҒаёҘаё° port |
| Dashboard аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ | Index pattern а№„аёЎа№Ҳаё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮ | аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ index pattern а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ |
| Alert а№„аёЎа№ҲаёӘа№ҲаёҮ email | SMTP config а№„аёЎа№Ҳаё–аё№аёҒ | аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡаё”а№үаё§аёў `sendmail` |
| Disk full | Log retention аёўаёІаё§а№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷ | аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІ log rotation |

## Checklist аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ

```
в–Ў Wazuh Dashboard а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№„аё”а№ү
в–Ў Agent а№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№Ҳаёӯ вүҘ3 а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮ
в–Ў а№Җаё«а№Үаёҷ event real-time
в–Ў AD login events аёЎаёІаё–аё¶аёҮ
в–Ў Firewall logs аёЎаёІаё–аё¶аёҮ
в–Ў Sysmon аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮаёҡаёҷ Windows
в–Ў Custom rule аё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷ
в–Ў Alert аёӘа№ҲаёҮ Email/Slack а№„аё”а№ү
в–Ў рҹҺү аёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎа№Җаёӣаёҙаё”аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ!
```

---

## Performance Tuning

| аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷ | аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ | аё§аёҙаёҳаёөаёӣаёЈаёұаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **SIEM Indexing** | Query аёҠа№үаёІ | а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ RAM, аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡ shard size |
| **Log Ingestion** | Buffer overflow | а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ queue size, scale nodes |
| **Dashboard** | Load аёҠа№үаёІ | аёҘаё” time range, а№ғаёҠа№ү summary index |
| **Agent** | CPU аёӘаё№аёҮаёҡаёҷ endpoint | аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡ scan interval, exclude paths |
| **Alerting** | Alert delay | аёҘаё” evaluation interval |

## Backup & Recovery

| аёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷ | Backup Strategy | Recovery Time |
|:---|:---|:---:|
| SIEM Config | Daily snapshot вҶ’ S3/NFS | < 1 аёҠаёЎ. |
| Detection Rules | Git repository | < 15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| Dashboard/Reports | Export JSON вҶ’ Git | < 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| Agent Config | Centralized config mgmt | < 1 аёҠаёЎ. |
| TheHive Cases | DB dump + Elasticsearch snapshot | < 2 аёҠаёЎ. |

## аёҒаёІаёЈ Hardening SIEM Server

```bash
# 1. Firewall вҖ” аёӯаёҷаёёаёҚаёІаё•а№ҖаёүаёһаёІаё° port аё—аёөа№ҲаёҲаёіа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ
ufw allow 1514/tcp  # Agent communication
ufw allow 1515/tcp  # Agent enrollment
ufw allow 443/tcp   # Web UI
ufw deny incoming
ufw enable

# 2. SSL/TLS аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ agent communication
# (Wazuh аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІаёӯаёұаё•а№ӮаёҷаёЎаёұаё•аёҙаёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮаё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ)

# 3. Disable root login
sed -i 's/PermitRootLogin yes/PermitRootLogin no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
systemctl restart sshd

# 4. Log rotation
cat >> /etc/logrotate.d/wazuh << 'EOF'
/var/ossec/logs/*.log {
    weekly
    rotate 12
    compress
    missingok
}
EOF
```

## Infrastructure Sizing Guide

### SIEM Sizing Calculator

| Parameter | Small | Medium | Large |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| EPS (Events/sec) | 500 | 2,000 | 10,000+ |
| Storage/day | 50 GB | 200 GB | 1 TB+ |
| Retention | 90 days | 180 days | 365 days |
| CPU Cores | 8 | 16 | 32+ |
| RAM | 32 GB | 64 GB | 128+ GB |
| Total Storage | 5 TB | 36 TB | 365+ TB |

### Network Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Log Sources] --> B[Log Collector]
    B --> C[Message Queue]
    C --> D[SIEM Indexer]
    D --> E[Search Head]
    E --> F[SOC Dashboard]
    D --> G[Cold Storage]
```

### Deployment Readiness Checklist

| Category | Items | Status |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Network | VLAN, firewall rules, DNS | вҳҗ |
| Compute | VMs/containers provisioned | вҳҗ |
| Storage | Hot/warm/cold tiers | вҳҗ |
| Access | Admin accounts, MFA | вҳҗ |
| Monitoring | Health dashboards | вҳҗ |

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ

---

## File: 01_SOC_Fundamentals/SOC_Building_Roadmap.en.md
# SOC Building Roadmap вҖ” From Zero to Operational

> **Document ID:** SOC-BUILD-001
> **Version:** 1.0
> **Last Updated:** 2026-02-15
> **Audience:** IT Managers, CISOs, Security Leaders starting a SOC from scratch

---

## Who Is This For?

You have **no SOC today**. Maybe you have a small IT team that handles security "when something happens." This guide walks you through building a Security Operations Center from nothing вҖ” step by step, phase by phase.

---

## The 4 Phases

```
Phase 1 (Month 1-3)     Phase 2 (Month 4-6)     Phase 3 (Month 7-12)    Phase 4 (Year 2+)
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Foundation   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ   Detection  в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ   Response   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  Maturity    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў Buy tools  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Log sourcesв”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Playbooks  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Automation в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў Hire peopleв”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў First rulesв”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў IR process в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Threat huntв”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў Basic setupв”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Basic SOPs в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Exercises   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў SOAR       в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

---

## Phase 1: Foundation (Month 1вҖ“3)

### 1.1 Define Your SOC Mission

Before buying anything, answer these questions:

| Question | Why It Matters |
|:---|:---|
| What are we protecting? | Defines scope (servers, cloud, endpoints, OT?) |
| What are our biggest risks? | Prioritizes what to detect first |
| What regulations apply? | PDPA, PCI DSS, ISO 27001 affect requirements |
| What's our budget? | Determines build vs buy vs outsource |
| Who will staff it? | In-house vs MSSP vs hybrid |

### 1.2 Choose Your Operating Model

| Model | Team Size | Budget | Best For |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---|
| рҹҹў **Hybrid MSSP** | 1вҖ“2 internal + MSSP | аёҝ1.5вҖ“3M/yr | Small org, <500 employees |
| рҹҹЎ **Small In-house** | 3вҖ“5 analysts | аёҝ5вҖ“10M/yr | Mid-size, 500вҖ“2000 employees |
| рҹ”ҙ **Full In-house** | 8вҖ“15+ people | аёҝ15вҖ“30M+/yr | Enterprise, 2000+ employees |

> **Recommendation for beginners**: Start with **Hybrid MSSP** вҖ” outsource 24/7 monitoring, keep 1вҖ“2 people in-house for escalations and process building.

### 1.3 Select Your Technology Stack

#### SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) вҖ” Your #1 Tool

| SIEM | Cost | Best For | Learning Curve |
|:---|:---|:---|:---:|
| **Wazuh** | Free (open-source) | Budget-conscious, learning | рҹҹЎ Medium |
| **Elastic Security** | Free tier available | Flexible, scalable | рҹҹЎ Medium |
| **Microsoft Sentinel** | Pay-per-GB | Azure/M365 shops | рҹҹў Easy |
| **Splunk** | $$$ per GB | Enterprise, any environment | рҹ”ҙ Hard |
| **Google Chronicle** | Flat-rate | Google Cloud users | рҹҹЎ Medium |

> **Recommendation for beginners**: **Wazuh** (free, includes SIEM + EDR + compliance) or **Elastic Security** (free tier, powerful).

#### EDR (Endpoint Detection & Response)

| EDR | Cost | Key Feature |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Wazuh Agent** | Free | Bundled with Wazuh SIEM |
| **Microsoft Defender for Endpoint** | Included in M365 E5 | Already have it? Use it! |
| **CrowdStrike Falcon** | $$$ | Best-in-class detection |
| **SentinelOne** | $$ | Strong autonomous response |
| **LimaCharlie** | Pay-per-agent | Flexible, affordable |

#### Other Essential Tools

| Category | Free Option | Paid Option |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Ticketing** | TheHive, osTicket | ServiceNow, Jira |
| **Threat Intel** | MISP, OTX | Recorded Future, Mandiant |
| **Vulnerability Scanner** | OpenVAS/Greenbone | Tenable, Qualys |
| **Network Monitoring** | Zeek, Suricata | Darktrace, Vectra |
| **Email Security** | Built-in (M365/Google) | Proofpoint, Mimecast |
| **SOAR** | Shuffle, n8n | Palo Alto XSOAR, Splunk SOAR |

### 1.4 Minimum Viable Architecture

```
                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                    в”Ӯ   Internet  в”Ӯ
                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                           в”Ӯ
                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                    в”Ӯ  Firewall   в”Ӯ вҶҗ Logs to SIEM
                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                           в”Ӯ
              в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
              в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
        в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
        в”Ӯ  Servers   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  VPN  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ Cloud     в”Ӯ
        в”Ӯ (EDR agent)в”Ӯ в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ в”Ӯ (AWS/Azure)в”Ӯ
        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
              в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
              в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                           в”Ӯ
                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                    в”Ӯ    SIEM     в”Ӯ вҶҗ Wazuh / Elastic
                    в”Ӯ  Dashboard  в”Ӯ
                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                           в”Ӯ
                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                    в”Ӯ  SOC Team   в”Ӯ вҶҗ Monitors dashboards
                    в”Ӯ  (Analysts) в”Ӯ    Reviews alerts
                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### 1.5 Hire Your First Team

#### Minimum Team (Hybrid Model)

| Role | Count | Skills Needed | Salary Range (Thailand) |
|:---|:---:|:---|:---|
| SOC Lead / Manager | 1 | 5+ yrs security, IR experience | аёҝ80вҖ“150K/mo |
| SOC Analyst (T1/T2) | 1вҖ“2 | SIEM, basic networking, willingness to learn | аёҝ30вҖ“60K/mo |

#### Where to Find People
- University security programs (KMUTT, Chula, KMITL)
- Online communities (SANS, TryHackMe, HackTheBox graduates)
- IT staff interested in transitioning to security
- Fresh graduates + structured training (cheapest option)

### 1.6 Phase 1 Checklist

```
в–Ў SOC mission statement written
в–Ў Operating model chosen (hybrid/in-house)
в–Ў Budget approved
в–Ў SIEM selected and installed
в–Ў EDR deployed on critical servers
в–Ў First team member(s) hired
в–Ў Basic network diagram documented
в–Ў Management buy-in secured
в–Ў MSSP contract signed (if hybrid)
```

---

## Phase 2: Detection (Month 4вҖ“6)

### 2.1 Onboard Log Sources (Priority Order)

Follow the [Log Source Onboarding Guide](../06_Operations_Management/Log_Source_Onboarding.en.md).

| Week | Log Source | Why First |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| Week 1 | Active Directory / Azure AD | #1 attack target вҖ” logins, privilege changes |
| Week 2 | Firewall logs | Network visibility вҖ” who goes where |
| Week 3 | EDR / Endpoint logs | Malware, suspicious processes |
| Week 4 | Email gateway | Phishing вҖ” #1 initial access vector |
| Week 5вҖ“6 | Cloud (AWS/Azure) | Cloud misconfig is common |
| Week 7вҖ“8 | DNS + Proxy | C2, tunneling, shadow IT |

### 2.2 Deploy Your First Detection Rules

Start with these 10 Sigma rules from this repo (highest ROI):

| Priority | Rule | Detects | Playbook |
|:---:|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | `win_multiple_failed_logins` | Brute force | PB-04 |
| 2 | `proc_office_spawn_powershell` | Phishing payload | PB-01 |
| 3 | `cloud_unusual_login` | Account compromise | PB-05 |
| 4 | `cloud_impossible_travel` | Stolen credentials | PB-06 |
| 5 | `file_bulk_renaming_ransomware` | Ransomware | PB-02 |
| 6 | `proc_temp_folder_execution` | Malware | PB-03 |
| 7 | `win_admin_share_access` | Lateral movement | PB-12 |
| 8 | `net_beaconing` | C2 communication | PB-13 |
| 9 | `win_security_log_cleared` | Cover tracks | PB-20 |
| 10 | `cloud_email_inbox_rule` | BEC | PB-17 |

### 2.3 Write Your First SOPs

Start with these documents from this repo:

1. вң… [IR Framework](../05_Incident_Response/Framework.en.md) вҖ” How to handle incidents
2. вң… [Severity Matrix](../05_Incident_Response/Severity_Matrix.en.md) вҖ” P1/P2/P3/P4 classification
3. вң… [Tier 1 Runbook](../05_Incident_Response/Runbooks/Tier1_Runbook.en.md) вҖ” Day-to-day analyst guide
4. вң… [Shift Handoff](../06_Operations_Management/Shift_Handoff.en.md) вҖ” If running shifts
5. вң… [Communication Templates](../05_Incident_Response/Communication_Templates.en.md) вҖ” Who to notify

### 2.4 Phase 2 Checklist

```
в–Ў Top 5 log sources onboarded and validated
в–Ў 10 initial Sigma rules deployed
в–Ў IR Framework documented and distributed
в–Ў Severity Matrix agreed with management
в–Ў Tier 1 Runbook given to all analysts
в–Ў First false positive tuning completed
в–Ў Alert routing configured (email/Slack/ticket)
в–Ў Analysts can investigate basic alerts independently
```

---

## Phase 3: Operational (Month 7вҖ“12)

### 3.1 Expand Playbook Coverage

Deploy playbooks in this priority order:

| Wave | Playbooks | Why |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| Wave 1 (done in Phase 2) | PB-01 to PB-05 | Core threats |
| Wave 2 | PB-06 to PB-10 | Common scenarios |
| Wave 3 | PB-11 to PB-20 | Advanced threats |
| Wave 4 | PB-21 to PB-30 | Specialized scenarios |

### 3.2 Run Your First Tabletop Exercise

Use [Tabletop Exercises](../05_Incident_Response/Tabletop_Exercises.en.md):
- Start with **Scenario 1 (Ransomware)** вҖ” most likely real-world scenario
- Include SOC team, IT Ops, and at least one manager
- Score the exercise and document improvement areas

### 3.3 Establish Metrics

Track these 5 metrics from day 1 (see [SOC Metrics](../06_Operations_Management/SOC_Metrics.en.md)):

| Metric | Target (Month 6) | Target (Year 1) |
|:---|:---:|:---:|
| MTTD (Mean Time to Detect) | < 4 hours | < 1 hour |
| MTTR (Mean Time to Respond) | < 8 hours | < 4 hours |
| Alert-to-Ticket ratio | > 50% | > 70% |
| False Positive rate | < 70% | < 40% |
| SLA compliance (P1 response) | > 80% | > 95% |

### 3.4 Phase 3 Checklist

```
в–Ў All 53 playbooks reviewed (deploy relevant ones)
в–Ў All 54 Sigma rules deployed
в–Ў First tabletop exercise completed
в–Ў SOC metrics dashboard created
в–Ў Monthly SOC report to management
в–Ў Evidence collection procedures tested
в–Ў PDPA notification process documented
в–Ў At least 1 real incident handled end-to-end
```

## Phase Gate Decision Criteria

| Transition | Minimum Requirement | Decision Owner |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Foundation вҶ’ Detection | SIEM live, first critical log sources onboarded, named owner for daily monitoring | CISO + SOC Manager |
| Detection вҶ’ Operational | Core alerts tuned, Tier 1 runbook in use, escalation path tested | SOC Manager |
| Operational вҶ’ Maturity | Metrics reviewed monthly, playbooks used in production, at least one exercise completed | CISO |

## Minimum Deliverables by Phase

| Phase | Minimum Deliverables | Primary Owner |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Foundation | SOC mission, operating model, approved budget, tool shortlist, staffing plan | CISO |
| Detection | Log source inventory, first ruleset, alert routing, Tier 1 workflow | Security Engineer |
| Operational | Active playbooks, metrics dashboard, reporting cadence, evidence handling standard | SOC Manager |
| Maturity | Automation backlog, hunting plan, review cadence, control-improvement roadmap | SOC Manager + Security Engineer |

## Escalation Triggers During SOC Buildout

| Condition | Escalate To | SLA | Required Action |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Budget approval slips and blocks critical tooling or staffing | Executive sponsor | Same business day | Re-scope roadmap or secure interim funding |
| No owner for monitoring, incident handling, or engineering workstream | CISO | Within 24 hours | Assign accountable lead before next phase |
| Critical log source or endpoint coverage remains unavailable past planned date | SOC Manager + Business owner | Same business day | Accept blind spot formally or fix dependency |
| New regulatory requirement appears during build | CISO + Compliance Officer | Within 5 business days | Reprioritize roadmap and scope |

---

## Phase 4: Maturity (Year 2+)

### 4.1 Advanced Capabilities

| Capability | When | How |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Threat Hunting** | Month 12+ | Proactive searches beyond alert-driven |
| **SOAR Automation** | Month 12+ | Automate repetitive tasks (enrichment, blocking) |
| **Purple Teaming** | Month 15+ | Test your detections with [Purple Team Guide](../05_Incident_Response/Purple_Team_Exercises.en.md) |
| **Threat Intelligence** | Month 12+ | Feed integration with [TI Guide](../06_Operations_Management/TI_Feeds_Integration.en.md) |
| **Compliance Audit** | Month 18+ | Use [Compliance Mapping](../07_Compliance_Privacy/Compliance_Mapping.en.md) |

### 4.2 SOC Maturity Levels

Use the [SOC Maturity Scorer](../tools/soc_maturity_scorer.html) to assess:

| Level | Description | You're Here When... |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| 1 вҖ” Initial | Ad-hoc, reactive | No formal process |
| 2 вҖ” Managed | Basic monitoring, some SOPs | End of Phase 2 |
| 3 вҖ” Defined | Documented processes, metrics | End of Phase 3 |
| 4 вҖ” Quantitative | Data-driven, KPIs tracked | Year 2 |
| 5 вҖ” Optimizing | Continuous improvement, automation | Year 3+ |

---

## Budget Planning

### Option A: Budget SOC (Open-Source Stack)

| Item | Year 1 Cost | Notes |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| SIEM (Wazuh) | аёҝ0 | Self-hosted, open-source |
| Server hardware / VM | аёҝ200KвҖ“500K | 2вҖ“3 servers or cloud VMs |
| EDR (Wazuh Agent) | аёҝ0 | Included |
| Threat Intel (MISP + OTX) | аёҝ0 | Open-source |
| Ticketing (TheHive) | аёҝ0 | Open-source |
| Staff (1 Lead + 1 Analyst) | аёҝ1.3вҖ“2.5M | Thai market rates |
| Training / Certs | аёҝ100вҖ“300K | SANS, CompTIA, online courses |
| **Total Year 1** | **аёҝ1.6вҖ“3.3M** | |

### Option B: Mid-Range SOC (Commercial + Open-Source)

| Item | Year 1 Cost | Notes |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| SIEM (Elastic / Sentinel) | аёҝ500KвҖ“2M | Pay-per-GB or license |
| EDR (Defender / CrowdStrike) | аёҝ500KвҖ“2M | Per-endpoint licensing |
| Vuln Scanner (Tenable) | аёҝ500KвҖ“1M | Annual license |
| Staff (1 Lead + 3 Analysts) | аёҝ3вҖ“5M | 8Г—5 or 16Г—5 coverage |
| MSSP (after-hours) | аёҝ1вҖ“2M | Night/weekend coverage |
| Training | аёҝ300вҖ“500K | SANS, vendor-specific |
| **Total Year 1** | **аёҝ5.8вҖ“12.5M** | |

### Option C: Enterprise SOC

| Item | Year 1 Cost |
|:---|:---:|
| SIEM (Splunk / Chronicle) | аёҝ3вҖ“10M |
| EDR (CrowdStrike / SentinelOne) | аёҝ2вҖ“5M |
| SOAR (XSOAR / Splunk SOAR) | аёҝ2вҖ“5M |
| Staff (8вҖ“15 people, 24Г—7) | аёҝ10вҖ“25M |
| Training + Certs | аёҝ1вҖ“2M |
| **Total Year 1** | **аёҝ18вҖ“47M** |

---

## Analyst Training Path

### For Complete Beginners (Month 1вҖ“3)

| Week | Topic | Resource |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| 1вҖ“2 | Networking fundamentals | CompTIA Network+ / YouTube |
| 3вҖ“4 | Linux & Windows basics | TryHackMe "Pre-Security" path |
| 5вҖ“6 | Security fundamentals | CompTIA Security+ study |
| 7вҖ“8 | SIEM basics | Your SIEM vendor training (free) |
| 9вҖ“10 | Log analysis | TryHackMe "SOC Level 1" path |
| 11вҖ“12 | IR fundamentals | This repository + SANS webinars |

### Recommended Certifications (Progressive)

```
Beginner        Intermediate      Advanced
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”ӮCompTIA   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ CySA+    в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ SANS     в”Ӯ
в”ӮSecurity+ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ BTL1     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ GCIH     в”Ӯ
в”ӮSC-900    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ SC-200   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ GCFA     в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   ~аёҝ10K        ~аёҝ15вҖ“30K       ~аёҝ100вҖ“200K
```

### Free Training Resources

| Resource | What You Learn |
|:---|:---|
| [TryHackMe](https://tryhackme.com) | SOC Level 1 & 2 paths (free tier) |
| [LetsDefend](https://letsdefend.io) | SOC analyst simulation |
| [CyberDefenders](https://cyberdefenders.org) | Blue team challenges |
| [SANS Webcasts](https://sans.org/webcasts) | Free expert sessions |
| **This Repository** | Real-world SOPs, playbooks, detection rules |

---

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

| вқҢ Mistake | вң… Instead |
|:---|:---|
| Buying expensive SIEM before hiring people | Hire first, then choose tools with them |
| Trying to monitor everything on day 1 | Start with top 5 log sources |
| No playbooks, just "figure it out" | Use this repo's playbooks from day 1 |
| Alert fatigue (too many noisy rules) | Start with 10 rules, tune, then add more |
| No metrics вҖ” can't prove value | Track MTTD/MTTR from week 1 |
| 24/7 with 2 people (burnout) | Use MSSP for after-hours until you can staff properly |
| Skipping tabletop exercises | Run quarterly вҖ” cheapest way to find gaps |
| No management reporting | Monthly 1-pager to CISO/CEO |

---

## Quick Start Checklist (30-Day Sprint)

```
Week 1:
в–Ў Read this entire guide
в–Ў Write your SOC mission statement (1 paragraph)
в–Ў Identify your crown jewels (most critical systems/data)
в–Ў Get budget approval

Week 2:
в–Ў Install Wazuh or chosen SIEM on a VM
в–Ў Deploy 5 agents on critical servers
в–Ў Configure Active Directory log forwarding
в–Ў Read the Tier 1 Runbook

Week 3:
в–Ў Import top 10 Sigma rules
в–Ў Test: can you see a failed login in the SIEM?
в–Ў Test: does an alert fire for 10+ failed logins?
в–Ў Set up email/Slack notifications for alerts

Week 4:
в–Ў Print Severity Matrix and post it on the wall
в–Ў Assign someone to check alerts daily
в–Ў Handle your first alert end-to-end
в–Ў Document what you learned
```

---

## This Repository вҖ” Your Reading Order

If you're starting from zero, read these documents in this order:

| Order | Document | Why |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| 1 | **This guide** (you're here!) | Overall roadmap |
| 2 | [IR Framework](../05_Incident_Response/Framework.en.md) | How incidents work |
| 3 | [Severity Matrix](../05_Incident_Response/Severity_Matrix.en.md) | P1/P2/P3/P4 |
| 4 | [Tier 1 Runbook](../05_Incident_Response/Runbooks/Tier1_Runbook.en.md) | Daily operations |
| 5 | [SOC Team Structure](../06_Operations_Management/SOC_Team_Structure.en.md) | Roles & shifts |
| 6 | [Detection Rules Index](../08_Detection_Engineering/README.md) | What rules to deploy |
| 7 | [Log Source Onboarding](../06_Operations_Management/Log_Source_Onboarding.en.md) | How to add logs |
| 8 | [PB-01 Phishing](../05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Phishing.en.md) | Your first playbook |
| 9 | [SOC Metrics](../06_Operations_Management/SOC_Metrics.en.md) | Measuring success |
| 10 | [Tabletop Exercises](../05_Incident_Response/Tabletop_Exercises.en.md) | Testing readiness |

## File: 01_SOC_Fundamentals/SOC_Building_Roadmap.th.md
# а№ҒаёңаёҷаёҮаёІаёҷаёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ SOC аёҲаёІаёҒаёЁаё№аёҷаёўа№Ң

> **аёЈаё«аёұаёӘа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ:** SOC-BUILD-001
> **а№Җаё§аёӯаёЈа№ҢаёҠаёұаёҷ:** 1.0
> **аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё•аёҘа№ҲаёІаёӘаёёаё”:** 2026-02-15
> **аёҒаёҘаёёа№ҲаёЎа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў:** IT Manager, CISO, аёңаё№а№үаёҡаёЈаёҙаё«аёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈаёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ SOC а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ

---

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаёҷаёөа№үаёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡа№ғаё„аёЈ?

аё„аёёаё“ **аёўаёұаёҮа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө SOC** аё§аёұаёҷаёҷаёөа№ү аёӯаёІаёҲаёЎаёөаё—аёөаёЎ IT а№ҖаёҘа№ҮаёҒа№Ҷ аё—аёөа№Ҳаё”аё№а№ҒаёҘаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӣаёҘаёӯаё”аё аёұаёў "а№ҖаёЎаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаёөаёӯаё°а№„аёЈа№ҖаёҒаёҙаё”аёӮаё¶а№үаёҷ" аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯаёҷаёөа№үаёҲаё°аёһаёІаё„аёёаё“аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ SOC аё•аёұа№үаёҮа№Ғаё•а№Ҳа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёөаёӯаё°а№„аёЈа№ҖаёҘаёў вҖ” аё—аёөаёҘаё°аёӮаёұа№үаёҷ аё—аёөаёҘаё° Phase

---

## 4 а№ҖаёҹаёӘ

```
а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 1 (а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 1-3)      а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 2 (а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 4-6)      а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 3 (а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 7-12)     а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 4 (аёӣаёөаё—аёөа№Ҳ 2+)
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  аё§аёІаёҮаёЈаёІаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ    в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ      в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ    в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  аёһаёұаё’аёҷаёІаё•а№Ҳаёӯ     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў аёӢаё·а№үаёӯа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯв”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ Log   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Playbooks  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Automation в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў аёҲа№үаёІаёҮаё„аёҷ     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Rules а№ҒаёЈаёҒ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў IR process в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Threat huntв”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮаёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷв”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў SOP аёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў аёӢа№үаёӯаёЎ        в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў SOAR       в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

## а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 1: аё§аёІаёҮаёЈаёІаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ (а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 1вҖ“3)

### 1.1 аё•аёӯаёҡаё„аёіаё–аёІаёЎаёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ

| аё„аёіаё–аёІаёЎ | аё—аёіа№„аёЎаёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ |
|:---|:---|
| аёӣаёҒаёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёӯаё°а№„аёЈ? | аёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё• |
| аё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮа№ғаё«аёҚа№ҲаёӘаёёаё”аё„аё·аёӯаёӯаё°а№„аёЈ? | аёҲаёұаё”аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡаё§а№ҲаёІ detect аёӯаё°а№„аёЈаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷ |
| аёҒаёҺаё«аёЎаёІаёўаёӯаё°а№„аёЈа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ? | PDPA, ISO, PCI |
| аёҮаёҡа№Җаё—а№ҲаёІа№„аё«аёЈа№Ҳ? | аёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё” build vs buy vs outsource |

### 1.2 а№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒа№ӮаёЎа№Җаё”аёҘ

| а№ӮаёЎа№Җаё”аёҘ | аё—аёөаёЎ | аёҮаёҡ/аёӣаёө | а№Җаё«аёЎаёІаё°аёҒаёұаёҡ |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---|
| рҹҹў **Hybrid MSSP** | 1вҖ“2 аё„аёҷ + MSSP | аёҝ1.5вҖ“3M | аёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈа№ҖаёҘа№ҮаёҒ <500 аё„аёҷ |
| рҹҹЎ **In-house а№ҖаёҘа№ҮаёҒ** | 3вҖ“5 аё„аёҷ | аёҝ5вҖ“10M | аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ 500вҖ“2000 аё„аёҷ |
| рҹ”ҙ **Full In-house** | 8вҖ“15+ аё„аёҷ | аёҝ15вҖ“30M+ | а№ғаё«аёҚа№Ҳ 2000+ аё„аёҷ |

> **а№Ғаёҷаё°аёҷаёі**: а№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎаёҲаёІаёҒ **Hybrid MSSP** вҖ” outsource 24/7 monitoring а№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ 1вҖ“2 аё„аёҷа№„аё§а№үаё”аё№а№ҒаёҘ escalation

### 1.3 а№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ

#### SIEM вҖ” а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ #1

| SIEM | аё„а№ҲаёІа№ғаёҠа№үаёҲа№ҲаёІаёў | а№Җаё«аёЎаёІаё°аёҒаёұаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Wazuh** | аёҹаёЈаёө (open-source) | аёҮаёҡаёҲаёіаёҒаёұаё”, а№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷаёЈаё№а№ү |
| **Elastic Security** | Free tier | аёўаё·аё”аё«аёўаёёа№Ҳаёҷ, scale а№„аё”а№ү |
| **Microsoft Sentinel** | аёҲа№ҲаёІаёўаё•аёІаёЎ GB | а№ғаёҠа№ү Azure/M365 |
| **Splunk** | а№ҒаёһаёҮ | Enterprise |

> **а№Ғаёҷаё°аёҷаёі**: **Wazuh** (аёҹаёЈаёө, аёЈаё§аёЎ SIEM + EDR + Compliance)

### 1.4 аёҲа№үаёІаёҮаё—аёөаёЎа№ҒаёЈаёҒ

| аё•аёіа№Ғаё«аёҷа№ҲаёҮ | аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷ | а№ҖаёҮаёҙаёҷа№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ (а№„аё—аёў) |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| SOC Lead | 1 | аёҝ80вҖ“150K/а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ |
| SOC Analyst | 1вҖ“2 | аёҝ30вҖ“60K/а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ |

---


#### EDR (Endpoint Detection & Response)

| а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ | аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё аё— | а№Җаё«аёЎаёІаё°аёҒаёұаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Wazuh** | Open-Source | аёҮаёҡаёҲаёіаёҒаёұаё”, DIY |
| **CrowdStrike Falcon** | Commercial | аёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈаёӮаёҷаёІаё”а№ғаё«аёҚа№Ҳ |
| **Microsoft Defender for Endpoint** | Commercial | аёӘаё аёІаёһа№Ғаё§аё”аёҘа№үаёӯаёЎ M365 |
| **LimaCharlie** | Cloud-native | Startup, MSSP |

#### а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯаёҲаёіа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷаёӯаё·а№Ҳаёҷ а№Ҷ

| аё«аёЎаё§аё” | а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯа№Ғаёҷаё°аёҷаёі | аё§аёұаё•аё–аёёаёӣаёЈаё°аёӘаёҮаё„а№Ң |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Ticketing** | TheHive / Jira | аёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё„аёӘ |
| **TI Platform** | MISP / OpenCTI | аёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈаёӮа№ҲаёІаё§аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮаё аёұаёўаё„аёёаёҒаё„аёІаёЎ |
| **Vulnerability Scanner** | OpenVAS / Nessus | аёӘа№ҒаёҒаёҷаёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮа№Ӯаё«аё§а№Ҳ |
| **Network** | Suricata / Zeek | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡа№Җаё„аёЈаё·аёӯаёӮа№ҲаёІаёў |

### 1.4 аёӘаё–аёІаёӣаёұаё•аёўаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№Ҳаёіаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ғаёҠа№үаёҮаёІаёҷа№„аё”а№ү (MVA)

```mermaid
graph LR
    Endpoints["рҹ–ҘпёҸ Endpoints"] -->|Logs| Wazuh["Wazuh/SIEM"]
    FW["рҹ”Ҙ Firewall"] -->|Syslog| Wazuh
    AD["рҹ“Ғ AD"] -->|WinEventLog| Wazuh
    Wazuh --> Alert["вҡ пёҸ Alerts"]
    Alert --> TheHive["рҹ“Ӣ TheHive"]
    Wazuh --> Dashboard["рҹ“Ҡ Dashboard"]
```

### 1.5 аёҲа№үаёІаёҮаё—аёөаёЎа№ҒаёЈаёҒ

#### аё—аёөаёЎаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№Ҳаёі (Hybrid Model)

| аёҡаё—аёҡаёІаё— | аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷ | аё—аёұаёҒаё©аё°аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| **SOC Manager** | 1 | аё аёІаё§аё°аёңаё№а№үаёҷаёі, IR, GRC |
| **Tier 1 Analyst** | 2вҖ“3 | Alert triage, SIEM |
| **Tier 2 Analyst** | 1 | Investigation, forensics |
| **Detection Engineer** | 1 (shared) | Sigma, YARA, scripting |

#### аё«аёІаё„аёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аё«аёҷ

- аёҠаёёаёЎаёҠаёҷ cybersecurity а№ғаёҷаёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё—аёЁ (NCSA, аёӘаёЎаёІаё„аёЎ ISC2 Thailand)
- а№ӮаёӣаёЈа№ҒаёҒаёЈаёЎаёқаё¶аёҒаёҮаёІаёҷаёҲаёІаёҒаёЎаё«аёІаё§аёҙаё—аёўаёІаёҘаёұаёў
- CTF communities а№ҒаёҘаё° Bug Bounty programs
- Upskill аё—аёөаёЎ IT аё—аёөа№ҲаёЎаёөаёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳ

### 1.6 а№ҖаёҠа№Үаё„аёҘаёҙаёӘаё•а№Ңа№ҖаёҹаёӘ 1

- [ ] аёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё•а№ҒаёҘаё° mission аёӮаёӯаёҮ SOC
- [ ] а№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒ SIEM + EDR stack
- [ ] аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮа№ҒаёҘаё°аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІ SIEM
- [ ] Deploy EDR agents аёҡаёҷ critical endpoints
- [ ] аёҲа№үаёІаёҮ/аёЎаёӯаёҡаё«аёЎаёІаёў SOC Manager а№ҒаёҘаё° Analysts
- [ ] аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ shift schedule а№Җаёҡаё·а№үаёӯаёҮаё•а№үаёҷ

## а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 2: аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ (а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 4вҖ“6)

### а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ Log Source аё•аёІаёЎаёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ

| аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң | Log Source | аё—аёіа№„аёЎаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷ |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| 1 | Active Directory | а№Җаёӣа№үаёІа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёө #1 |
| 2 | Firewall | а№Җаё«а№Үаёҷ traffic |
| 3 | EDR | malware, process |
| 4 | Email gateway | phishing #1 |
| 5вҖ“6 | Cloud (AWS/Azure) | misconfig |
| 7вҖ“8 | DNS + Proxy | C2, shadow IT |

### Deploy 10 Sigma Rules а№ҒаёЈаёҒ

а№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎаёҲаёІаёҒ `win_multiple_failed_logins`, `proc_office_spawn_powershell`, `cloud_unusual_login` + аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ [Tier 1 Runbook](../05_Incident_Response/Runbooks/Tier1_Runbook.th.md)

---



### 2.3 а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ SOPs аёҠаёёаё”а№ҒаёЈаёҒ

а№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎаёҲаёІаёҒа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ 5 аёүаёҡаёұаёҡаёҷаёөа№ү:

1. **Alert Triage SOP** вҖ” аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷаё„аёұаё”аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮ alert
2. **Incident Response Playbook** вҖ” Playbook аё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ңаё«аёҘаёұаёҒ
3. **Escalation Matrix** вҖ” а№ҖаёЎаё·а№Ҳаёӯа№„аё«аёЈа№Ҳ/аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№„аёЈаё—аёөа№ҲаёҲаё° escalate
4. **Shift Handoff** вҖ” аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷаёӘа№ҲаёҮаёЎаёӯаёҡаёҒаё°
5. **Evidence Collection** вҖ” аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷа№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ

### 2.4 а№ҖаёҠа№Үаё„аёҘаёҙаёӘаё•а№Ңа№ҖаёҹаёӘ 2

- [ ] а№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№Ҳаёӯ log sources аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёҷа№үаёӯаёў 5 а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮ
- [ ] Deploy Sigma rules аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёҷа№үаёӯаёў 10 rules
- [ ] а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ SOPs аё«аёҘаёұаёҒ 5 аёүаёҡаёұаёҡ
- [ ] Tune false positives аёЈаёӯаёҡа№ҒаёЈаёҒ
- [ ] аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ dashboard аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ daily operations

## а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 3: аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ (а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 7вҖ“12)

- Deploy playbooks аё—аёұа№үаёҮ 50 аёҠаёёаё” (аё„а№Ҳаёӯаёўа№Ҷ аё—аёіа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ wave)
- аёӢа№үаёӯаёЎ [Tabletop Exercise](../05_Incident_Response/Tabletop_Exercises.th.md) аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮа№ҒаёЈаёҒ
- а№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎаё§аёұаё” metrics: MTTD < 4 аёҠаёЎ., MTTR < 8 аёҠаёЎ.

---



### 3.1 аёӮаёўаёІаёў Playbook Coverage

аё„аёЈаёӯаёҡаё„аёҘаёёаёЎ use cases аё•аёІаёЎ MITRE ATT&CK:

| Tactic | Playbook | аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---:|
| Initial Access | Phishing Response | P1 |
| Execution | Malware Execution | P1 |
| Persistence | Unauthorized Scheduled Task | P2 |
| Privilege Escalation | Admin Account Misuse | P1 |
| Lateral Movement | Pass-the-Hash | P2 |
| Exfiltration | Data Exfiltration | P1 |
| Impact | Ransomware | P1 |

### 3.2 аёҲаёұаё” Tabletop Exercise аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮа№ҒаёЈаёҒ

1. а№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒ scenario (а№ҖаёҠа№Ҳаёҷ ransomware)
2. а№ҖаёҠаёҙаёҚ stakeholders (IT, Legal, Management)
3. аё—аёі walkthrough аё•аёІаёЎ playbook
4. аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒ lessons learned
5. аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё• SOPs аёҲаёІаёҒаёңаёҘаёҘаёұаёһаёҳа№Ң

### 3.3 аёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё” Metrics

| аё•аёұаё§аёҠаёөа№үаё§аёұаё” | а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў |
|:---|:---|
| **MTTD** (Mean Time to Detect) | вүӨ 60 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| **MTTR** (Mean Time to Respond) | вүӨ 240 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| **False Positive Rate** | < 10% |
| **Alert-to-Incident Ratio** | < 20:1 |
| **Playbook Coverage** | вүҘ 80% аёӮаёӯаёҮ use cases |

### 3.4 а№ҖаёҠа№Үаё„аёҘаёҙаёӘаё•а№Ңа№ҖаёҹаёӘ 3

- [ ] Playbooks аё„аёЈаёӯаёҡаё„аёҘаёёаёЎ top 10 use cases
- [ ] аёҲаёұаё” tabletop exercise аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёҷа№үаёӯаёў 1 аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ
- [ ] аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ KPI dashboard
- [ ] аёЎаёө monthly SOC report
- [ ] а№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎ threat hunting program

## а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 4: аёһаёұаё’аёҷаёІ (аёӣаёөаё—аёөа№Ҳ 2+)

- Threat Hunting, SOAR Automation, Purple Teaming
- аёӘаёӯаёҡ SOC Maturity вҖ” а№ғаёҠа№ү [а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯаё§аёұаё”аё„аё°а№Ғаёҷаёҷ](../tools/soc_maturity_scorer.html)
- Compliance audit вҖ” а№ғаёҠа№ү [Compliance Mapping](../07_Compliance_Privacy/Compliance_Mapping.th.md)

## а№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№Ңаё•аёұаё”аёӘаёҙаёҷа№ғаёҲаёңа№ҲаёІаёҷа№Ғаё•а№ҲаёҘаё°а№ҖаёҹаёӘ (Phase Gate Decision Criteria)

| аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷа№ҖаёҹаёӘ | а№ҖаёҮаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№„аёӮаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№Ҳаёі | аёңаё№а№үаё•аёұаё”аёӘаёҙаёҷа№ғаёҲ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| аё§аёІаёҮаёЈаёІаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ вҶ’ аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ | SIEM а№ғаёҠа№үаёҮаёІаёҷаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ, onboard log source аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚаёҠаёёаё”а№ҒаёЈаёҒ, аёЎаёө owner аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ daily monitoring | CISO + SOC Manager |
| аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ вҶ’ аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ | core alerts аё–аё№аёҒ tune, Tier 1 runbook аё–аё№аёҒа№ғаёҠа№үаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ, аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡ escalation path а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ | SOC Manager |
| аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ вҶ’ аёһаёұаё’аёҷаёІ | review metrics аёЈаёІаёўа№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ, а№ғаёҠа№ү playbook аёҒаёұаёҡа№Җаё„аёӘаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ, аёҲаёҡ exercise аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёҷа№үаёӯаёў 1 аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ | CISO |

## аёңаёҘаёҘаёұаёһаёҳа№ҢаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№ҲаёіаёӮаёӯаёҮа№Ғаё•а№ҲаёҘаё°а№ҖаёҹаёӘ (Minimum Deliverables by Phase)

| а№ҖаёҹаёӘ | аёңаёҘаёҘаёұаёһаёҳа№ҢаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№Ҳаёі | аёңаё№а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҙаё”аёҠаёӯаёҡаё«аёҘаёұаёҒ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| аё§аёІаёҮаёЈаёІаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ | SOC mission, operating model, budget аё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙ, tool shortlist, staffing plan | CISO |
| аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ | log source inventory, ruleset аёҠаёёаё”а№ҒаёЈаёҒ, alert routing, Tier 1 workflow | Security Engineer |
| аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ | playbook аё—аёөа№Ҳ active, metrics dashboard, reporting cadence, evidence handling standard | SOC Manager |
| аёһаёұаё’аёҷаёІ | automation backlog, hunting plan, review cadence, control-improvement roadmap | SOC Manager + Security Engineer |

## Trigger аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮаёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ SOC (Escalation Triggers During SOC Buildout)

| а№ҖаёҮаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№„аёӮ | аёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё–аё¶аёҮ | SLA | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаё—аёі |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| аёҮаёҡаёӣаёЈаё°аёЎаёІаё“аёҘа№ҲаёІаёҠа№үаёІаёҲаёҷ block а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаё„аёҷаё—аёөа№ҲаёҲаёіа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ | Executive sponsor | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ | аёҘаё” scope аёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§аё„аёЈаёІаё§аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаё«аёІ funding аёЈаё°аёўаё°аёӘаёұа№үаёҷ |
| а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө owner аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ monitoring, incident handling аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ engineering workstream | CISO | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 24 аёҠаёЎ. | а№Ғаё•а№ҲаёҮаё•аёұа№үаёҮ accountable lead аёҒа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёӮаё¶а№үаёҷа№ҖаёҹаёӘаё–аёұаё”а№„аёӣ |
| critical log source аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ endpoint coverage аёўаёұаёҮа№„аёЎа№ҲаёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎа№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷа№Ғаёңаёҷ | SOC Manager + Business owner | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ | аёЈаёұаёҡ blind spot аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷаё—аёІаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№ҒаёҒа№ү dependency |
| аёЎаёөаёҒаёҺаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёӮа№үаёӯаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”а№ғаё«аёЎа№ҲаёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ build | CISO + Compliance Officer | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 5 аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈ | аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡаёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ roadmap а№ҒаёҘаё°аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё• |

---



### 4.1 аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаёІаёЎаёІаёЈаё–аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаёӘаё№аёҮ

| аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаёІаёЎаёІаёЈаё– | а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ/аё§аёҙаёҳаёө | а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Threat Hunting** | MITRE ATT&CK, Jupyter | а№ҖаёҠаёҙаёҮаёЈаёёаёҒ |
| **SOAR Automation** | Shuffle, XSOAR | аёҘаё”а№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮ |
| **Purple Team** | Atomic Red Team | аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡ detection |
| **CTI Program** | MISP, OpenCTI | аёӮа№ҲаёІаё§аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮа№ҖаёҠаёҙаёҮаёҒаёҘаёўаёёаё—аёҳа№Ң |
| **Forensics Lab** | SIFT, Volatility | аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ңа№ҖаёҠаёҙаёҮаёҘаё¶аёҒ |

### 4.2 аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё§аёёаё’аёҙаё аёІаё§аё° SOC

| аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ | аёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯ | аёҘаёұаёҒаё©аё“аё° |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | **Initial** | Reactive, а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө process аёҠаёұаё”а№ҖаёҲаёҷ |
| 2 | **Managed** | аёЎаёө SOPs, аёЎаёө shift rotation |
| 3 | **Defined** | Playbooks аё„аёЈаёҡ, metrics tracking |
| 4 | **Quantitative** | Data-driven, automation |
| 5 | **Optimizing** | Continuous improvement, threat hunting |

## аёҮаёҡаёӣаёЈаё°аёЎаёІаё“

| а№Ғаёҡаёҡ | аёҮаёҡаёӣаёөа№ҒаёЈаёҒ | аё«аёЎаёІаёўа№Җаё«аё•аёё |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| рҹҹў Budget (Open-Source) | аёҝ1.6вҖ“3.3M | Wazuh + 2 аё„аёҷ |
| рҹҹЎ Mid-Range | аёҝ5.8вҖ“12.5M | Elastic/Sentinel + 4 аё„аёҷ + MSSP |
| рҹ”ҙ Enterprise | аёҝ18вҖ“47M | Splunk + 10+ аё„аёҷ + 24/7 |

---



### Option A: SOC аёӣаёЈаё°аё«аёўаёұаё” (Open-Source Stack)

| аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈ | аё„а№ҲаёІа№ғаёҠа№үаёҲа№ҲаёІаёў/аёӣаёө |
|:---|:---|
| Wazuh (SIEM+EDR) | аёҹаёЈаёө |
| TheHive (Ticketing) | аёҹаёЈаёө |
| MISP (TI) | аёҹаёЈаёө |
| Suricata (IDS) | аёҹаёЈаёө |
| а№ҖаёӢаёҙаёЈа№Ңаёҹа№Җаё§аёӯаёЈа№Ң (3 nodes) | ~300,000 аёҝ |
| аёҡаёёаё„аёҘаёІаёҒаёЈ (3вҖ“4 FTE) | ~1,800,000 аёҝ |
| **аёЈаё§аёЎ** | **~2,100,000 аёҝ/аёӣаёө** |

### Option B: SOC аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ (Commercial + Open-Source)

| аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈ | аё„а№ҲаёІа№ғаёҠа№үаёҲа№ҲаёІаёў/аёӣаёө |
|:---|:---|
| Splunk/Elastic (SIEM) | ~1,500,000 аёҝ |
| CrowdStrike (EDR) | ~800,000 аёҝ |
| TheHive (Ticketing) | аёҹаёЈаёө |
| аёҡаёёаё„аёҘаёІаёҒаёЈ (6вҖ“8 FTE) | ~3,600,000 аёҝ |
| **аёЈаё§аёЎ** | **~5,900,000 аёҝ/аёӣаёө** |

### Option C: SOC аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈ

| аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈ | аё„а№ҲаёІа№ғаёҠа№үаёҲа№ҲаёІаёў/аёӣаёө |
|:---|:---|
| Sentinel/Splunk Enterprise | ~3,000,000 аёҝ |
| CrowdStrike+Cortex XDR | ~2,000,000 аёҝ |
| XSOAR (SOAR) | ~1,500,000 аёҝ |
| аёҡаёёаё„аёҘаёІаёҒаёЈ (12вҖ“15 FTE) | ~9,000,000 аёҝ |
| **аёЈаё§аёЎ** | **~15,500,000 аёҝ/аёӣаёө** |

## а№ҖаёӘа№үаёҷаё—аёІаёҮаёқаё¶аёҒ Analyst

### а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 1вҖ“3 (аёЎаё·аёӯа№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ)
| аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң | а№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷаёӯаё°а№„аёЈ | а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮ |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| 1вҖ“2 | аёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ Network | CompTIA Network+ / YouTube |
| 3вҖ“4 | Linux & Windows | TryHackMe "Pre-Security" |
| 5вҖ“6 | Security аёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ | CompTIA Security+ |
| 7вҖ“10 | SIEM + Log Analysis | TryHackMe "SOC Level 1" |
| 11вҖ“12 | IR аёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ | Repository аёҷаёөа№ү |

### Cert а№Ғаёҷаё°аёҷаёі
```
а№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎаё•а№үаёҷ вҶ’ Security+ / SC-900 (~аёҝ10K)
аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ   вҶ’ CySA+ / BTL1 (~аёҝ15вҖ“30K)
аёӘаё№аёҮ     вҶ’ SANS GCIH / GCFA (~аёҝ100вҖ“200K)
```

### аё—аёЈаёұаёһаёўаёІаёҒаёЈаёқаё¶аёҒаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎаёҹаёЈаёө

| а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮ | а№Җаёҷаё·а№үаёӯаё«аёІ |
|:---|:---|
| [LetsDefend](https://letsdefend.io) | SOC Analyst simulator |
| [CyberDefenders](https://cyberdefenders.org) | Blue team challenges |
| [MITRE ATT&CK](https://attack.mitre.org) | Threat framework |
| [Malware Traffic Analysis](https://malware-traffic-analysis.net) | PCAP analysis |

## аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈа№ғаёҷ Repository аёҷаёөа№ү

| аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ | а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ | аё§аёұаё•аё–аёёаёӣаёЈаё°аёӘаёҮаё„а№Ң |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | SOC Building Roadmap (а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаёҷаёөа№ү) | аё аёІаёһаёЈаё§аёЎа№ҒаёҘаё°а№ҒаёңаёҷаёҮаёІаёҷ |
| 2 | Technology Stack | а№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ |
| 3 | Infrastructure Setup | аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ |
| 4 | IR Framework | аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮ |
| 5 | Playbooks (35 аёүаёҡаёұаёҡ) | аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷаёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙ |
| 6 | SOC Metrics & KPIs | аё§аёұаё”аёңаёҘ |

## Quick Start 30 аё§аёұаёҷ

```
аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 1: аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷаё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯаёҷаёөа№ү + а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ SOC mission + аёӮаёӯаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙаёҮаёҡ
аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 2: аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ SIEM + deploy agent 5 а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮ + а№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎ AD
аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 3: Import 10 Sigma rules + аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡ alert а№ҒаёЈаёҒ
аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 4: аёһаёҙаёЎаёһа№Ң Severity Matrix + аёЎаёӯаёҡаё«аёЎаёІаёўаё„аёҷаё”аё№ alert + handle alert а№ҒаёЈаёҒ
```

---

## аёӮа№үаёӯаёңаёҙаё”аёһаёҘаёІаё”аё—аёөа№Ҳаёһаёҡаёҡа№Ҳаёӯаёў

| вқҢ аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаё—аёі | вң… аё—аёіа№Ғаё—аёҷ |
|:---|:---|
| аёӢаё·а№үаёӯ SIEM а№ҒаёһаёҮаёҒа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёҲа№үаёІаёҮаё„аёҷ | аёҲа№үаёІаёҮаё„аёҷаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷ а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§а№ҖаёҘаё·аёӯаёҒа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯаё”а№үаё§аёўаёҒаёұаёҷ |
| аёһаёўаёІаёўаёІаёЎ monitor аё—аёёаёҒаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаё§аёұаёҷа№ҒаёЈаёҒ | а№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎаёҲаёІаёҒ 5 log sources |
| а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө playbook | а№ғаёҠа№ү playbook аёҲаёІаёҒ repo аёҷаёөа№үаё•аёұа№үаёҮа№Ғаё•а№Ҳаё§аёұаёҷа№ҒаёЈаёҒ |
| Alert аё”аёұаёҮаё—аёұа№үаёҮаё§аёұаёҷ (alert fatigue) | а№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎ 10 rules, tune, а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§аё„а№Ҳаёӯаёўа№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ |
| 24/7 аё”а№үаё§аёў 2 аё„аёҷ (burnout) | а№ғаёҠа№ү MSSP аё”аё№аёҷаёӯаёҒа№Җаё§аёҘаёІ |

---

## Quick Wins vs Long-term Investments

### Priority Matrix

| Initiative | Effort | Impact | Timeline |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Log collection baseline | аё•а№Ҳаёі | аёӘаё№аёҮ | аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 1-2 |
| Alert triage SOP | аё•а№Ҳаёі | аёӘаё№аёҮ | аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 2-3 |
| SIEM use case tuning | аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ | аёӘаё№аёҮ | а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 1-2 |
| Threat hunting program | аёӘаё№аёҮ | аёӘаё№аёҮ | а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 3-6 |
| SOAR automation | аёӘаё№аёҮ | аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ | а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 6-12 |
| ML-based detection | аёӘаё№аёҮ | аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ | аёӣаёө 2 |

### Common Pitfalls to Avoid

> вҡ пёҸ **аёӮа№үаёӯаё„аё§аёЈаёЈаё°аё§аёұаёҮ**
> 1. аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёӢаё·а№үаёӯа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯаёҒа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё” process
> 2. аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІа№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ log аё—аёёаёҒаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№Ӯаё”аёўа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё„аёұаё”аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮ
> 3. аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҘаё°а№ҖаёҘаёўаёҒаёІаёЈаёқаё¶аёҒаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎаё—аёөаёЎ
> 4. аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаё„аёІаё”аё«аё§аёұаёҮ 24/7 аёҲаёІаёҒаё—аёөаёЎа№ҖаёҘа№ҮаёҒ
> 5. аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаё§аёұаё”а№Ғаё„а№ҲаёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷ alert аё—аёөа№Ҳаёӣаёҙаё”

### Budget Planning Template

```
аёӣаёө 1 Budget Breakdown (аё•аёұаё§аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮ):
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Personnel (60%)    в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ  60%
Technology (25%)   в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ              25%
Training (10%)     в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ                   10%
Operations (5%)    в–Ҳв–Ҳ                     5%
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Total: ~15-25M THB/year (mid-size org)
```

### Milestone Tracking Dashboard

| Phase | Milestone | Status | Target Date |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | Log collection active | вҳҗ | Week 4 |
| 2 | First playbook deployed | вҳҗ | Week 8 |
| 3 | 24/7 coverage achieved | вҳҗ | Month 6 |
| 4 | Threat hunting initiated | вҳҗ | Month 9 |
| 5 | Full automation deployed | вҳҗ | Month 18 |

### Success Criteria per Phase

| Phase | KPI | Target |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Foundation | Log sources onboarded | вүҘ 10 |
| Operations | MTTR | < 4 hrs |
| Advanced | Detection coverage | > 60% ATT&CK |
| Optimized | Automation rate | > 40% alerts |

### Vendor Selection Criteria

| Factor | Weight | Evaluation |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Feature fit | 30% | POC results |
| Cost (TCO) | 25% | 3-year model |
| Support quality | 20% | SLA review |
| Integration | 25% | API testing |

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ

- [аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҡ IR](../05_Incident_Response/Framework.th.md)
- [аё•аёІаёЈаёІаёҮаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЈаёёаёҷа№ҒаёЈаёҮ](../05_Incident_Response/Severity_Matrix.th.md)
- [аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯ Tier 1](../05_Incident_Response/Runbooks/Tier1_Runbook.th.md)
- [а№Ӯаё„аёЈаёҮаёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮаё—аёөаёЎ SOC](../06_Operations_Management/SOC_Team_Structure.th.md)
- [а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯаё§аёұаё” Maturity](../tools/soc_maturity_scorer.html)
