## аёӘаёЈаёёаёӣ Coverage

```mermaid
pie title аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё° Log Source
    "а№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ вң…" : 28
    "аёҡаёІаёҮаёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷ вҡ пёҸ" : 9
    "аёўаёұаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ вқҢ" : 5
    "аё§аёІаёҮа№Ғаёңаёҷа№„аё§а№ү рҹ”ң" : 3
```

### аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚа№ғаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёҙаё” Gap

| аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ | а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮаё—аёөа№ҲаёӮаёІаё” | аёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёўаёІаёҒ | аёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё” |
|:---:|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| рҹ”ҙ P1 | Container / K8s Audit | аёЎаёӯаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№Җаё«а№Үаёҷ container-based attacks | аёӣаёІаёҷаёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ | Q1 |
| рҹ”ҙ P1 | Certificate Authority | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ Golden Certificate (T1649) а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№ү | аё•а№Ҳаёі | Q1 |
| рҹҹЎ P2 | GCP Cloud Audit | а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө visibility а№ғаёҷ GCP workloads | аёӣаёІаёҷаёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ | Q2 |
| рҹҹЎ P2 | Google Workspace | аёӮаёІаё” email/drive monitoring аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ Google | аёӣаёІаёҷаёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ | Q2 |
| рҹҹў P3 | Wireless Controller | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ rogue AP а№„аё”а№үаёҲаёіаёҒаёұаё” | аё•а№Ҳаёі | Q3 |

---

## аёҒаёІаёЈаё§аёІаёҮа№Ғаёңаёҷ Capacity

| аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ | EPS аёЈаё§аёЎ (аёӣаёЈаё°аёЎаёІаё“) | SIEM License | Storage (90 аё§аёұаёҷ) |
|:---|:---:|:---|:---:|
| **SOC а№ҖаёҘа№ҮаёҒ** (< 500 hosts) | 5KвҖ“15K | Standard tier | 500 GB вҖ“ 1.5 TB |
| **SOC аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ** (500вҖ“5K hosts) | 15KвҖ“100K | Enterprise tier | 1.5 вҖ“ 10 TB |
| **SOC а№ғаё«аёҚа№Ҳ** (5K+ hosts) | 100KвҖ“1M+ | Premium tier | 10 вҖ“ 100+ TB |

### аёӘаё№аё•аёЈаё„аёіаёҷаё§аё“ EPS

```
Events аё•а№Ҳаёӯаё§аёұаёҷ = EPS Г— 86,400
Storage (GB/аё§аёұаёҷ) = Events аё•а№Ҳаёӯаё§аёұаёҷ Г— аёӮаёҷаёІаё”а№ҖаёүаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаё•а№Ҳаёӯ event (bytes) / 1,073,741,824
Storage 90 аё§аёұаёҷ = Storage/аё§аёұаёҷ Г— 90
```

---

## аё•аёІаёЈаёІаёҮаёҡаёіаёЈаёёаёҮаёЈаёұаёҒаё©аёІ

| аёҮаёІаёҷ | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаё–аёөа№Ҳ | аёңаё№а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҙаё”аёҠаёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡаёӘаёёаёӮаё аёІаёһ log source | аё—аёёаёҒаё§аёұаёҷ | SOC Tier 1 |
| аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ EPS а№Җаё—аёөаёўаёҡ baseline | аё—аёёаёҒаёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң | SOC Engineering |
| аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё•аё•аёІаёЈаёІаёҮ (sources а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ) | аё—аёёаёҒа№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ | SOC Lead |
| аё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷ gap analysis аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё” | аё—аёёаёҒа№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘ | SOC Manager + CISO |
| аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёЎаёӣаёҒаёұаёҡ compliance | аё—аёёаёҒаёӣаёө | GRC / Compliance |

---

### Log Source Priority Categories

| Priority | Category | Examples | SLA |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| P1 - Critical | Security devices | Firewall, IDS, EDR | 5 min |
| P2 - High | Identity systems | AD, IAM, VPN | 15 min |
| P3 - Medium | Infrastructure | DNS, DHCP, Proxy | 1 hr |
| P4 - Low | Application | Web apps, custom | 4 hrs |

### Log Format Compatibility

| Log Source | Format | Parser | Complexity |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Firewall | Syslog/CEF | Built-in | Low |
| Windows | EVTX/WEF | Built-in | Medium |
| Cloud | JSON/API | Custom | Medium |
| Application | Custom | Custom | High |

## Baseline аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№ҲаёіаёӮаёӯаёҮ Log аё•аёІаёЎ Use Case

| Use Case | Log аё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёЎаёө | Enrichment аё—аёөа№Ҳа№Ғаёҷаё°аёҷаёі | Blind Spot аё«аёІаёҒа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Identity abuse / account compromise** | AD аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ IdP sign-in logs, MFA platform, VPN gateway | DHCP, EDR, geo-IP | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаё§а№ҲаёІа№ғаё„аёЈ login аёҲаёІаёҒаё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аё«аёҷ а№ҒаёҘаё° MFA аёҘа№үаёЎа№Җаё«аёҘаё§аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ |
| **Endpoint execution / malware** | Windows Security, Sysmon, EDR, AV/EPP | DNS, proxy, threat intel | а№Җаё«а№Үаёҷ process lineage а№ҒаёҘаё°аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаё«аёҘаёұаёҮаёЈаёұаёҷа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё„аёЈаёҡ |
| **Phishing / BEC** | Email gateway, M365 UAL аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№Җаё—аёөаёўаёҡа№Җаё—а№ҲаёІ, IdP logs | sandbox verdicts, DLP, URL click logs | аёңаё№аёҒаёҒаёІаёЈаёӘа№ҲаёҮа№ҖаёЎаёҘаёҒаёұаёҡаёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаё«аёҘаёұаёҮа№Җаёӣаёҙаё”а№ғаёҠа№үаёҮаёІаёҷаёҡаёұаёҚаёҠаёөа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№ү |
| **Data exfiltration** | Proxy, DLP, storage audit, firewall | NetFlow, SaaS audit, DB audit | аёҒаёІаёЈа№ӮаёӯаёҷаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаёӮаёҷаёІаё”а№ғаё«аёҚа№ҲаёҲаё°аё”аё№а№Җаё«аёЎаё·аёӯаёҷ traffic аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ |
| **Cloud abuse** | CloudTrail/Activity/Audit logs, cloud identity logs | VPC/NSG flow logs, CSPM findings | аёЎаёӯаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№Җаё«а№Үаёҷ privileged API misuse а№ҒаёҘаё°аёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаёӣаёҙаё” public exposure |
| **Web attack / API abuse** | WAF, web server, application logs | DB audit, threat intel, CDN logs | а№Җаё«а№Үаёҷа№Ғаё„а№ҲаёӮаёӯаёҡаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡ а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№Җаё«а№ҮаёҷаёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ downstream |

## аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаё„аёёаё“аё аёІаёһаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘа№ҒаёҘаё° Trigger аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ Escalate

| аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёЈаё§аёҲ | Owner | а№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№Ңаё—аёөа№ҲаёўаёӯаёЎаёЈаёұаёҡа№„аё”а№ү | аё•а№үаёӯаёҮ escalate а№ҖаёЎаё·а№Ҳаёӯ |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаё”аёӮаёӯаёҮ ingestion** | SOC Engineering | а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө gap а№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷ SLA аёӮаёӯаёҮа№Ғаё•а№ҲаёҘаё° source | source аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ P1/P2 аёӮаёІаё”а№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷ SLA |
| **аё„аё§аёІаёЎаё„аёЈаёҡаёӮаёӯаёҮ parser** | Security Engineer | field аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚаёӮаёӯаёҮ priority sources аё–аё№аёҒ normalize | source аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚаёўаёұаёҮа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ partial а№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷ 1 аёЈаёӯаёҡаёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷ |
| **аё„аё§аёІаёЎаё•аёЈаёҮаёӮаёӯаёҮа№Җаё§аёҘаёІ** | SOC Engineering | timestamp а№ғаёҠа№ү correlate аёӮа№үаёІаёЎаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡа№„аё”а№ү | time drift аё—аёіа№ғаё«а№ү timeline а№Җаёһаёөа№үаёўаёҷаё«аёЈаё·аёӯ MTTD а№Җаёһаёөа№үаёўаёҷ |
| **а№ҖаёӘаё–аёөаёўаёЈаё аёІаёһаёӮаёӯаёҮ EPS** | SOC Lead | аёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳа№ғаёҷ baseline аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёҠа№Ҳаё§аёҮа№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷа№ҒаёӣаёҘаёҮаё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ | EPS аёҘаё”/аёһаёёа№ҲаёҮаёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙа№Ӯаё”аёўа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө planned change |
| **Retention аё•аёІаёЎаёӮа№үаёӯаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”** | SOC Manager / GRC | а№ҖаёһаёөаёўаёҮаёһаёӯаёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ incident, audit, legal | а№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ log аёӘаёұа№үаёҷаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІаёҠа№Ҳаё§аёҮаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ғаёҠа№үаёӘаё·аёҡаёӘаё§аёҷ |

## аёЎаёёаёЎаёЎаёӯаёҮаёңаё№а№үаёҡаёЈаёҙаё«аёІаёЈ: аё–а№үаёІ Log аё«аёІаёў аёҲаё°а№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮаёӯаё°а№„аёЈ

| Source аё—аёөа№ҲаёӮаёІаё”аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёўаёұаёҮ partial | аёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡаё—аёІаёҮаёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ | аё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮаё—аёІаёҮаёҳаёёаёЈаёҒаёҙаёҲ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **IdP / MFA logs** | triage account compromise а№„аё”а№үа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё”аёө | а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎаё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮа№Ӯаё”аёўа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёӯаёҷаёёаёҚаёІаё•а№Ғаёҡаёҡа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё–аё№аёҒаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёһаёҡ |
| **EDR аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ Sysmon** | аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮаё аёІаёһа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ңаёҡаёҷ endpoint а№„аё”а№үа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё„аёЈаёҡ | contain аёҠа№үаёІаёҘаёҮа№ҒаёҘаё°аёһаёҙаёӘаё№аёҲаёҷа№Ң forensic аёўаёІаёҒ |
| **Cloud audit logs** | аёЎаёӯаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№Җаё«а№Үаёҷ admin/API abuse аёҠаёұаё”а№ҖаёҲаёҷ | аё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮаёӘаё№аёҮаё”а№үаёІаёҷ cloud governance а№ҒаёҘаё°аёҒаёІаёЈаёӘаё·аёҡаёӘаё§аёҷ breach |
| **Email audit logs** | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёіаёӮаёӯаёҮаёңаё№а№үа№ғаёҠа№үаё«аёҘаёұаёҮ phishing а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№ү | аё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮ BEC а№ҒаёҘаё° mail-based attack аёҠа№үаёІаёҘаёҮ |
| **Database / SaaS audit** | а№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ data access а№„аё”а№үа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё„аёЈаёҡ | а№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮаё•а№ҲаёӯаёҒаёІаёЈаёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷаё”а№үаёІаёҷаёҒаёҺаё«аёЎаёІаёўа№ҒаёҘаё°аёӮа№үаёӯаёҡаёұаёҮаё„аёұаёҡ |

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ

-   [Threat Hunting Playbook](../05_Incident_Response/Threat_Hunting_Playbook.th.md)
-   [Detection Rule Testing SOP](Detection_Rule_Testing.th.md)
-   [TI Feeds Integration](TI_Feeds_Integration.th.md)
-   [SOC Metrics & KPIs](SOC_Metrics.th.md)
-   [Compliance Mapping](../07_Compliance_Privacy/Compliance_Mapping.th.md)
-   [Infrastructure Setup](../01_SOC_Fundamentals/Infrastructure_Setup.th.md)

## References

- [NIST SP 800-92](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-92/final)
- [MITRE ATT&CK](https://attack.mitre.org/)


---

## File: 06_Operations_Management/Log_Source_Onboarding.en.md
# Log Source Onboarding Guide

> **Document ID:** LOG-001
> **Version:** 1.0
> **Last Updated:** 2026-02-15
> **Owner:** SOC Engineering / Detection Engineering

---

## Purpose

Step-by-step guide for onboarding new log sources into the SIEM. Proper onboarding ensures detection coverage, normalization, and alert quality.

---

## Onboarding Workflow

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Request] --> B[Assessment]
    B --> C[Configure Source]
    C --> D[Parse & Normalize]
    D --> E[Validate]
    E --> F[Build Detections]
    F --> G[Document & Close]
```

---

## Phase 1: Assessment Checklist

Before onboarding any new log source:

| Item | Details |
|:---|:---|
| **Log Source Name** | [System/application name] |
| **Log Type** | Auth / Network / Endpoint / Cloud / Application |
| **Volume Estimate** | [Events per second / per day] |
| **Format** | Syslog (RFC 3164/5424) / JSON / CEF / CSV / Windows Event |
| **Transport** | Syslog TCP/UDP / Filebeat / API / S3 / Kafka |
| **Retention** | [Days to retain in hot/warm/cold storage] |
| **Compliance** | Required by ISO/PCI/PDPA? |
| **Playbooks** | Which playbooks will this data support? |
| **Owner** | [Team responsible for the source system] |

---

## Phase 2: Configure by Source Type

### Windows Event Logs
```yaml
# Winlogbeat config
winlogbeat.event_logs:
  - name: Security
    event_id: 4624, 4625, 4648, 4672, 4688, 4720, 4726, 4732, 4756, 1102
  - name: System
    event_id: 7045, 7040
  - name: Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational
  - name: Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational
    event_id: 4103, 4104

output.elasticsearch:
  hosts: ["siem.company.local:9200"]
  index: "winlogbeat-%{+yyyy.MM.dd}"
```

### Linux Syslog
```yaml
# Filebeat config
filebeat.inputs:
  - type: log
    paths:
      - /var/log/auth.log
      - /var/log/syslog
      - /var/log/secure
    fields:
      log_type: linux_syslog
    multiline:
      pattern: '^\w{3}\s+\d{1,2}'
      negate: true
      match: after

  - type: log
    paths:
      - /var/log/audit/audit.log
    fields:
      log_type: linux_audit
```

### Firewall (Palo Alto / Fortinet)
```
# Syslog config вҖ” send to SIEM
# Palo Alto: Device > Server Profiles > Syslog
Server IP:    [SIEM_IP]
Port:         514 (UDP) or 6514 (TLS)
Format:       BSD
Facility:     LOG_USER

# Fortinet FortiGate
config log syslogd setting
    set status enable
    set server [SIEM_IP]
    set port 514
    set format cef
    set facility local7
end
```

### AWS CloudTrail
```bash
# Enable CloudTrail вҶ’ S3 вҶ’ SIEM
aws cloudtrail create-trail \
  --name soc-audit-trail \
  --s3-bucket-name company-cloudtrail-logs \
  --is-multi-region-trail \
  --enable-log-file-validation

# Enable CloudWatch Logs integration
aws cloudtrail update-trail \
  --name soc-audit-trail \
  --cloud-watch-logs-log-group-arn arn:aws:logs:... \
  --cloud-watch-logs-role-arn arn:aws:iam::...
```

### Azure Activity & Sign-in Logs
```
# Azure Portal вҶ’ Azure AD вҶ’ Diagnostic Settings
# Export to:
- Log Analytics Workspace (recommended)
- Event Hub вҶ’ SIEM
- Storage Account

# Key log categories:
вң… AuditLogs
вң… SignInLogs
вң… NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
вң… ServicePrincipalSignInLogs
вң… RiskyUsers
вң… UserRiskEvents
```

### Office 365 / Microsoft 365
```powershell
# Enable Unified Audit Log
Set-AdminAuditLogConfig -UnifiedAuditLogIngestionEnabled $true

# Verify
Get-AdminAuditLogConfig | FL UnifiedAuditLogIngestionEnabled

# Key events to monitor:
# - MailItemsAccessed
# - New-InboxRule / Set-InboxRule
# - FileDownloaded / FileUploaded (SharePoint/OneDrive)
# - UserLoggedIn (from unusual locations)
```

### EDR (CrowdStrike / Defender / SentinelOne)
```
# Typically via API or Syslog streaming
# CrowdStrike: SIEM Connector вҶ’ Falcon Data Replicator
# Defender: Microsoft 365 Defender вҶ’ Streaming API
# SentinelOne: Settings вҶ’ Integrations вҶ’ Syslog/CEF

# Key events:
вң… Detections / Alerts
вң… Process execution
вң… Network connections
вң… File modifications
вң… DNS queries
```

---

## Phase 3: Parse & Normalize

### Field Mapping Standard (ECS)

Map all sources to Elastic Common Schema (ECS) or similar:

| Standard Field | Description | Example |
|:---|:---|:---|
| `@timestamp` | Event time | 2026-02-15T08:30:00Z |
| `event.category` | Category | authentication, network, process |
| `event.action` | Action | login, file_create, dns_query |
| `event.outcome` | Result | success, failure |
| `source.ip` | Source IP | 10.0.1.50 |
| `destination.ip` | Dest IP | 8.8.8.8 |
| `user.name` | Username | john.doe |
| `host.name` | Hostname | WS-JOHN-01 |
| `process.name` | Process | powershell.exe |
| `file.hash.sha256` | File hash | abc123... |

---

## Phase 4: Validation

After onboarding, verify:

```
в–Ў Logs are arriving (check last 5 min)
в–Ў Parsing is correct (spot-check 10 events)
в–Ў Timestamps are accurate (not off by timezone)
в–Ў Fields are normalized to standard schema
в–Ў No data loss (compare source count vs SIEM count, Вұ5%)
в–Ў Test search query returns expected results
в–Ў Volume matches estimate (not flooding or too low)
в–Ў Alert rules fire on test data
```

### Test Queries
```
# Check data arrival
index=* sourcetype="new_source" earliest=-5m | stats count

# Verify field extraction
index=* sourcetype="new_source" | table _time, source.ip, user.name, event.action

# Count vs expected volume
index=* sourcetype="new_source" earliest=-1d | stats count
```

---

## Phase 5: Build Detections

After validation, create detection rules:

1. Review which Sigma rules apply to this log source
2. Convert relevant Sigma rules for your SIEM
3. Test detection rules against historical data
4. Set up alert routing (email, Slack, ticket)
5. Document false positive baseline (first 2 weeks)

---

## Log Source Priority Matrix

| Priority | Log Source | Playbooks Supported |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| рҹ”ҙ P1 | EDR / Endpoint | PB-01, PB-02, PB-03, PB-11, PB-12 |
| рҹ”ҙ P1 | Active Directory / Azure AD | PB-04, PB-05, PB-06, PB-07, PB-15, PB-26 |
| рҹ”ҙ P1 | Email Gateway | PB-01, PB-17 |
| рҹҹЎ P2 | Firewall / IDS | PB-09, PB-13, PB-24 |
| рҹҹЎ P2 | Cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP) | PB-16, PB-27 |
| рҹҹЎ P2 | Proxy / Web Gateway | PB-10, PB-22, PB-29 |
| рҹҹў P3 | DNS | PB-24 |
| рҹҹў P3 | DLP | PB-08, PB-14 |
| рҹҹў P3 | MDM | PB-19, PB-28 |
| вҡӘ P4 | Badge / Physical | PB-14 |
| вҡӘ P4 | OT/ICS | PB-30 |

## Minimum Acceptance Criteria Before Close

| Requirement | Why It Matters | Owner |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Log flow is stable for agreed observation window | Confirms source is production-ready | SOC Engineer |
| Parsing and normalization meet field quality target | Ensures detections and investigations are usable | Detection Engineer |
| Source is mapped in the Log Source Matrix | Keeps ownership and coverage inventory current | SOC Analyst |
| Health-check alert exists for log stoppage or volume drift | Detects silent failures after onboarding | Security Engineer |
| At least one validated detection or monitoring use case is linked | Prevents onboarding without operational value | Detection Engineer |

## Escalation Triggers During Onboarding

| Condition | Escalate To | SLA | Required Action |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Volume exceeds estimate by > 30% or threatens budget | SOC Manager + Platform owner | Same business day | Reassess retention, filtering, and cost |
| Parsing failure rate exceeds agreed threshold | Detection Engineer + Source owner | Within 24 hours | Fix parser before go-live |
| Required security events are missing from source logs | Source owner + SOC Manager | Same business day | Enable missing audit categories |
| No owner, maintenance window, or rollback path exists | SOC Manager | Before production cutover | Pause closeout until ownership is clear |
| Source supports critical playbooks but readiness remains incomplete | SOC Manager + IR lead | Immediate for critical gap | Track as monitoring blind spot |

---

## Related Documents

- [Detection Rules Index](../08_Detection_Engineering/README.md)
- [Sigma Rule Validator](../tools/sigma_validator.py)
- [SOC Metrics & KPIs](SOC_Metrics.en.md)
- [Log Source Matrix](Log_Source_Matrix.en.md)

## References

- [NIST SP 800-61r2](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-61/rev-2/final)
- [MITRE ATT&CK](https://attack.mitre.org/)


---

## File: 06_Operations_Management/Log_Source_Onboarding.th.md
# аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯа№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ Log Source а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІ SIEM

> **аёЈаё«аёұаёӘа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ:** LOG-001
> **а№Җаё§аёӯаёЈа№ҢаёҠаёұаёҷ:** 1.0
> **аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё•аёҘа№ҲаёІаёӘаёёаё”:** 2026-02-15
> **а№ҖаёҲа№үаёІаёӮаёӯаёҮ:** SOC Engineering

---

## аё§аёұаё•аё–аёёаёӣаёЈаё°аёӘаёҮаё„а№Ң

аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯаё—аёөаёҘаё°аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷаёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ onboard log source а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІ SIEM а№Җаёһаё·а№Ҳаёӯа№ғаё«а№үа№Ғаёҷа№Ҳа№ғаёҲаё§а№ҲаёІаёЎаёө detection coverage аё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҒаёҘаё°аёЎаёөаё„аёёаё“аё аёІаёһ

---

## аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ Onboard

```
1. аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ вҶ’ 2. аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІ вҶ’ 3. Parse вҶ’ 4. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ вҶ’ 5. аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ Detection
```

---

## аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 1: аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ

| аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈ | аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё” |
|:---|:---|
| аёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯ Log Source | [аёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡ] |
| аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё аё— | Auth / Network / Endpoint / Cloud |
| аёӣаёЈаёҙаёЎаёІаё“ | [Events аё•а№Ҳаёӯаё§аёұаёҷ] |
| аёЈаё№аёӣа№Ғаёҡаёҡ | Syslog / JSON / CEF / Windows Event |
| аёҒаёІаёЈаёӘа№ҲаёҮ | Syslog / Filebeat / API / S3 |
| Retention | [аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷаё§аёұаёҷ] |
| Playbooks | аёӘаёҷаёұаёҡаёӘаёҷаёёаёҷ playbook а№„аё«аёҷаёҡа№үаёІаёҮ? |

---

## аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 2: аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІ

### Windows
```yaml
# Winlogbeat вҖ” Event IDs аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ
Security: 4624, 4625, 4648, 4672, 4688, 4720, 1102
Sysmon: аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё”
PowerShell: 4103, 4104
```

### Linux
```yaml
# Filebeat
/var/log/auth.log, /var/log/syslog, /var/log/audit/audit.log
```

### Cloud
```
AWS: CloudTrail вҶ’ S3 вҶ’ SIEM
Azure: Diagnostic Settings вҶ’ Log Analytics / Event Hub
O365: Unified Audit Log вҶ’ Streaming API
```

---

## аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 3: Normalize (ECS)

| Field аёЎаёІаё•аёЈаёҗаёІаёҷ | аё„аёіаёӯаёҳаёҙаёҡаёІаёў |
|:---|:---|
| `@timestamp` | а№Җаё§аёҘаёІа№ҖаёҒаёҙаё”а№Җаё«аё•аёё |
| `event.category` | аё«аёЎаё§аё” (authentication, network) |
| `source.ip` | IP аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёІаёҮ |
| `user.name` | аёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёңаё№а№үа№ғаёҠа№ү |
| `host.name` | аёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮ |

---

## аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 4: аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ

```
в–Ў Log аёЎаёІаё–аё¶аёҮа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ (аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ 5 аёҷаёІаё—аёөаёҘа№ҲаёІаёӘаёёаё”)
в–Ў Parse аё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮ (spot-check 10 events)
в–Ў Timestamp аё–аё№аёҒ timezone
в–Ў Fields normalize аё•аёІаёЎ schema
в–Ў аёӣаёЈаёҙаёЎаёІаё“аё•аёЈаёҮаёҒаёұаёҡаё—аёөа№ҲаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ (Вұ5%)
в–Ў Alert rules аё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷ
```

---

## аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ

| аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ | Log Source | Playbooks |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| рҹ”ҙ P1 | EDR / Endpoint | PB-01вҖ“03, PB-11вҖ“12 |
| рҹ”ҙ P1 | AD / Azure AD | PB-04вҖ“07, PB-15, PB-26 |
| рҹ”ҙ P1 | Email Gateway | PB-01, PB-17 |
| рҹҹЎ P2 | Firewall / IDS | PB-09, PB-13, PB-24 |
| рҹҹЎ P2 | Cloud (AWS/Azure) | PB-16, PB-27 |
| рҹҹў P3 | DNS / DLP / MDM | PB-08, PB-19, PB-24 |

---

## Log Source Priority Matrix

| аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ | а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮ Log | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ | MITRE Coverage |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| 1 | **Active Directory** | рҹ”ҙ Critical | T1078, T1110, T1098 |
| 2 | **Firewall / IDS** | рҹ”ҙ Critical | T1071, T1090, T1572 |
| 3 | **EDR (Endpoint)** | рҹ”ҙ Critical | T1059, T1055, T1053 |
| 4 | **Email Gateway** | рҹҹЎ High | T1566, T1534 |
| 5 | **DNS** | рҹҹЎ High | T1071.004, T1568 |
| 6 | **Web Proxy** | рҹҹЎ High | T1071.001, T1102 |
| 7 | **VPN / Remote Access** | рҹҹЎ High | T1133, T1021 |
| 8 | **Cloud Audit (AWS/Azure)** | рҹҹЎ High | T1078.004, T1537 |
| 9 | **Database Audit** | рҹҹў Medium | T1213, T1505 |
| 10 | **Application Logs** | рҹҹў Medium | T1190, T1212 |

## Onboarding Checklist (Per Source)

| # | аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈ | аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё° |
|:---:|:---|:---:|
| 1 | аёЈаё°аёҡаёё log source owner а№ҒаёҘаё° contact | вҳҗ |
| 2 | аёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”аё§аёҙаёҳаёөаёӘа№ҲаёҮ log (agent/syslog/API) | вҳҗ |
| 3 | аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІ parsing / field extraction | вҳҗ |
| 4 | аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ index pattern / data stream | вҳҗ |
| 5 | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ log volume (EPS baseline) | вҳҗ |
| 6 | аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ health check alert (log stop flowing) | вҳҗ |
| 7 | Map fields а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ normalization schema | вҳҗ |
| 8 | аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ detection rules аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ source аёҷаёөа№ү | вҳҗ |
| 9 | аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡ alert triggering | вҳҗ |
| 10 | аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒа№ғаёҷ Log Source Matrix | вҳҗ |

## Volume Planning

| а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮ Log | EPS а№Ӯаё”аёўаёӣаёЈаё°аёЎаёІаё“ | GB/аё§аёұаёҷ (аёӣаёЈаё°аёЎаёІаё“) | Retention |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---|
| Active Directory | 50вҖ“200 | 2вҖ“8 GB | 90 аё§аёұаёҷ |
| Firewall | 100вҖ“1,000 | 5вҖ“50 GB | 30 аё§аёұаёҷ |
| EDR | 20вҖ“100 | 1вҖ“5 GB | 90 аё§аёұаёҷ |
| DNS | 200вҖ“2,000 | 10вҖ“100 GB | 14 аё§аёұаёҷ |
| Web Proxy | 50вҖ“500 | 3вҖ“30 GB | 30 аё§аёұаёҷ |

## Troubleshooting Log Onboarding

| аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ | аёӘаёІа№Җаё«аё•аёё | аё§аёҙаёҳаёөа№ҒаёҒа№ү |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Log а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІ SIEM | Port аё–аё№аёҒ block | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ firewall rules |
| Parsing аёңаёҙаё”аёһаёҘаёІаё” | Format а№„аёЎа№Ҳаё•аёЈаёҮаёҒаёұаёҡ parser | аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё• regex / grok pattern |
| Timestamp а№„аёЎа№Ҳаё•аёЈаёҮ | Timezone mismatch | аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІ NTP + timezone а№ғаёҷ parser |
| Volume аёӘаё№аёҮа№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷаё„аёІаё” | Debug logging а№Җаёӣаёҙаё”аёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳ | аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡ log level а№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ INFO |

## аёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёі Normalization

### Field Mapping Standard

| Standard Field | аё•аёұаё§аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮ Source Fields | аё„аёіаёӯаёҳаёҙаёҡаёІаёў |
|:---|:---|:---|
| `src_ip` | src_addr, SrcIP, source.ip | IP аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёІаёҮ |
| `dst_ip` | dst_addr, DstIP, dest.ip | IP аёӣаёҘаёІаёўаё—аёІаёҮ |
| `user` | username, AccountName, user.name | аёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёңаё№а№үа№ғаёҠа№ү |
| `action` | EventAction, Action, event.action | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ |
| `timestamp` | @timestamp, EventTime, _time | а№Җаё§аёҘаёІа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң |
| `severity` | level, priority, rule.level | аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЈаёёаёҷа№ҒаёЈаёҮ |
| `hostname` | ComputerName, host.name | аёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮ |

### аё•аёұаё§аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮ Parser (Wazuh)

```xml
<!-- Custom decoder аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ application log -->
<decoder name="custom_app">
  <program_name>myapp</program_name>
  <regex>^(\S+) (\S+) (\S+) "(\S+)"</regex>
  <order>timestamp,src_ip,user,action</order>
</decoder>
```

## Health Monitoring аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ Log Sources

| аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ | а№ҖаёҮаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№„аёӮ Alert | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЈаёёаёҷа№ҒаёЈаёҮ |
|:---|:---|:---:|
| Log а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаёЎаёІ > 15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | `count == 0 in 15m` | рҹ”ҙ High |
| EPS аёҘаё”аёҘаёҮ > 50% | `eps < baseline * 0.5` | рҹҹЎ Medium |
| EPS а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎаёӮаё¶а№үаёҷ > 200% | `eps > baseline * 2` | рҹҹЎ Medium |
| Parsing error rate > 5% | `parse_error > 5%` | рҹҹЎ Medium |
| Timestamp drift > 5 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | `time_diff > 5m` | рҹҹЎ Medium |

## Log Source Retirement Process

| аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷ | аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ | аёңаё№а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҙаё”аёҠаёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаё§а№ҲаёІаёўаёұаёҮаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈ log source аёҷаёөа№үаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ | SOC Manager |
| 2 | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ detection rules аё—аёөа№Ҳа№ғаёҠа№ү log source аёҷаёөа№ү | Detection Engineer |
| 3 | аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё•/аёҘаёҡ rules аё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ | Detection Engineer |
| 4 | аёӣаёҙаё” log collection | IT / System Owner |
| 5 | аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё• Log Source Matrix | SOC Analyst |
| 6 | Archive аёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаё•аёІаёЎ retention policy | IT |

## Log Source Health Monitoring

### Health Check Dashboard

| Log Source | Expected EPS | Actual EPS | Status | Last Event |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Firewall | 500 | 487 | вң… аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ | 2 sec ago |
| AD/LDAP | 200 | 195 | вң… аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ | 5 sec ago |
| EDR | 300 | 45 | рҹ”ҙ аё•а№Ҳаёіаёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ | 3 min ago |
| Web Proxy | 150 | 148 | вң… аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ | 1 sec ago |
| DNS | 400 | 0 | рҹ”ҙ аё«аёўаёёаё”аёӘа№ҲаёҮ | 15 min ago |

### Troubleshooting Log Gaps

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Log gap detected] --> B{Agent running?}
    B -->|No| C[Restart agent]
    B -->|Yes| D{Network connectivity?}
    D -->|No| E[Check firewall rules]
    D -->|Yes| F{Parser working?}
    F -->|No| G[Fix parser config]
    F -->|Yes| H{Storage full?}
    H -->|Yes| I[Expand storage/archive]
    H -->|No| J[Escalate to vendor]
```

### Onboarding Checklist per Source Type

| аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷ | Firewall | Endpoint | Cloud | Application |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Network config | вң… Syslog | вң… Agent | API Key | вң… Webhook |
| Parser setup | Custom | Built-in | Built-in | Custom |
| Field mapping | 15 fields | 25 fields | 20 fields | 10 fields |
| Baseline (days) | 7 | 14 | 7 | 14 |
| Alert rules | 5 | 10 | 8 | 3 |

### Log Quality Assurance

| Check | Target | Frequency |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Field completeness | > 95% | Weekly |
| Timestamp accuracy | Вұ 1 sec NTP | Monthly |
| Parser accuracy | > 98% | Per update |
| Normalization | CIM compliant | Per onboard |

### Onboarding Timeline Template

| Day | Activity | Owner |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | Network/API config | IT + Vendor |
| 2-3 | Parser development | SOC Engineer |
| 4-5 | Baseline collection | SOC Analyst |
| 6-7 | Alert rule creation | SOC Engineer |

### Quick Reference

| Phase | Duration |
|:---|:---|
| Config | 1-2 days |
| Baseline | 7 days |

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ

- [аё”аёұаёҠаёҷаёө Detection Rules](../08_Detection_Engineering/README.th.md)
- [Sigma Validator](../tools/sigma_validator.py)
- [SOC Metrics](SOC_Metrics.th.md)
- [Log Source Matrix](Log_Source_Matrix.th.md)

## а№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№ҢаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№ҲаёіаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷаёӣаёҙаё”аёҮаёІаёҷ Onboarding (Minimum Acceptance Criteria Before Close)

| аёӮа№үаёӯаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё” | а№Җаё«аё•аёёаёңаёҘ | аёңаё№а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҙаё”аёҠаёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| log flow а№ҖаёӘаё–аёөаёўаёЈаё•аёІаёЎаёҠа№Ҳаё§аёҮа№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё—аёөа№ҲаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё” | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаё§а№ҲаёІ source аёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎа№ғаёҠа№үаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮа№ғаёҷ production | SOC Engineer |
| parsing а№ҒаёҘаё° normalization аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷа№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№Ңаё„аёёаё“аё аёІаёһ field | аё—аёіа№ғаё«а№ү detection а№ҒаёҘаё° investigation а№ғаёҠа№үаёҮаёІаёҷа№„аё”а№ү | Detection Engineer |
| source аё–аё№аёҒаёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒа№ғаёҷ Log Source Matrix а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ | аё„аёҮ inventory а№ҖаёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮ owner а№ҒаёҘаё° coverage а№ғаё«а№үа№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёӣаёұаёҲаёҲаёёаёҡаёұаёҷ | SOC Analyst |
| аёЎаёө health-check alert аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ log аё«аёўаёёаё”а№„аё«аёҘаё«аёЈаё·аёӯ volume drift | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ silent failure аё«аёҘаёұаёҮ onboard | Security Engineer |
| аёЎаёөаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёҷа№үаёӯаёў 1 detection аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ monitoring use case аё—аёөа№Ҳ validate а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ | аёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ onboard а№Ӯаё”аёўа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө operational value | Detection Engineer |

## Trigger аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ Onboarding (Escalation Triggers During Onboarding)

| а№ҖаёҮаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№„аёӮ | аёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё–аё¶аёҮ | SLA | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаё—аёі |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| volume аёӘаё№аёҮаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІаё—аёөа№ҲаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ > 30% аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮаёҠаёҷаёҮаёҡ | SOC Manager + Platform owner | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ | аё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷ retention, filtering а№ҒаёҘаё°аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёёаёҷ |
| parsing failure rate аёӘаё№аёҮаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІа№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•аёҒаёҘаёҮа№„аё§а№ү | Detection Engineer + Source owner | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 24 аёҠаёЎ. | а№ҒаёҒа№ү parser аёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷ go-live |
| security event аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚаёӮаёІаё”аё«аёІаёўаёҲаёІаёҒ source logs | Source owner + SOC Manager | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ | а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” audit category аё—аёөа№ҲаёӮаёІаё” |
| а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө owner, maintenance window аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ rollback path | SOC Manager | аёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷаё•аёұаё”а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІ production | аё«аёўаёёаё” closeout аёҲаёҷаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІаёҲаё°аёҠаёұаё”а№ҖаёҲаёҷ |
| source аёЈаёӯаёҮаёЈаёұаёҡ playbook аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚа№Ғаё•а№Ҳ readiness аёўаёұаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё„аёЈаёҡ | SOC Manager + IR lead | аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёөаёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ critical gap | аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёІаёЎа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ monitoring blind spot |

## References

- [NIST SP 800-61r2](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-61/rev-2/final)
- [MITRE ATT&CK](https://attack.mitre.org/)

## File: 06_Operations_Management/Network_Security_Monitoring.en.md
# Network Security Monitoring SOP

**Document ID**: OPS-SOP-025
**Version**: 1.0
**Classification**: Internal
**Last Updated**: 2026-02-15

> SOC procedures for **monitoring network traffic, detecting network-based attacks, and responding to network security incidents**. Covers IDS/IPS, NDR, DNS monitoring, NetFlow analysis, and network forensics.

---

## Network Monitoring Architecture

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph Perimeter["рҹҢҗ Perimeter"]
        A[Firewall Logs]
        B[IDS/IPS Alerts]
        C[WAF Logs]
        D[Proxy/Web Gateway]
    end

    subgraph Internal["рҹҸў Internal"]
        E[NetFlow/sFlow]
        F[DNS Logs]
        G[DHCP Logs]
        H[Network TAP/SPAN]
    end

    subgraph NDR["рҹ”Қ NDR Platform"]
        I[Full Packet Capture]
        J[Protocol Analysis]
        K[Behavioral Analytics]
        L[Encrypted Traffic Analysis]
    end

    Perimeter --> M["SIEM"]
    Internal --> M
    NDR --> M
    M --> N["SOC Alert Queue"]

    style M fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff
    style N fill:#dc2626,color:#fff
```

---

## Network Data Sources

| Source | Key data | Retention | Priority |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---:|
| **Firewall logs** | Accept/deny, source/dest IP, port, protocol | 90 days | рҹ”ҙ Critical |
| **IDS/IPS alerts** | Signature matches, anomalies | 90 days | рҹ”ҙ Critical |
| **DNS query logs** | Domain resolution, query types | 90 days | рҹ”ҙ Critical |
| **Proxy/web gateway** | URL, user agent, bytes, category | 90 days | рҹ”ҙ Critical |
| **NetFlow/sFlow** | Traffic flow metadata (no payload) | 30 days | рҹҹ  High |
| **Full packet capture** | Complete traffic content | 7 days | рҹҹ  High |
| **DHCP logs** | IP-to-MAC mapping | 90 days | рҹҹЎ Medium |
| **VPN logs** | Connection times, source IPs, users | 90 days | рҹҹЎ Medium |
| **WAF logs** | Web attack attempts, blocked requests | 90 days | рҹҹЎ Medium |
| **Wi-Fi controller** | SSID connections, rogue APs | 30 days | рҹҹЎ Medium |

---

## Critical Network Detections

### Perimeter Attacks

| Detection | Description | Data Source | Severity | MITRE |
|:---|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Port scan detected | Systematic port scanning from single source | Firewall / IDS | P3 | T1046 |
| Brute-force attempt | Repeated connection attempts to same port | Firewall / IDS | P2 | T1110 |
| Exploit attempt | IDS signature match for known exploit | IDS | P1 | varies |
| DDoS indicators | Abnormal traffic volume from multiple sources | Firewall / NetFlow | P1 | T1498/T1499 |
| Unauthorized VPN access | VPN connection from blocked country/IP | VPN logs | P2 | T1133 |

### Lateral Movement

| Detection | Description | Data Source | Severity | MITRE |
|:---|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Internal port scan | Host scanning multiple internal IPs | NetFlow / IDS | P2 | T1046 |
| SMB lateral movement | Unusual SMB connections between hosts | NDR / NetFlow | P1 | T1021.002 |
| RDP to unusual hosts | RDP to servers not in normal baseline | NetFlow / EDR | P2 | T1021.001 |
| Pass-the-hash | NTLM relay or overpass-the-hash detected | NDR / Sysmon | P1 | T1550.002 |
| WinRM across segments | WinRM between network zones | NetFlow / Sysmon | P2 | T1021.006 |

### Command & Control (C2)

| Detection | Description | Data Source | Severity | MITRE |
|:---|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| DNS tunneling | High-frequency DNS queries with encoded data | DNS logs | P1 | T1071.004 |
| Beaconing pattern | Regular-interval outbound connections | NDR / Proxy | P1 | T1071 |
| Domain generation algorithm | Many NXD responses from single host | DNS logs | P2 | T1568.002 |
| Known C2 infrastructure | Connection to TI-flagged IP/domain | Proxy / Firewall | P1 | T1071 |
| Encrypted C2 (JA3/JA4) | Unusual TLS fingerprint to rare domain | NDR | P2 | T1573 |
| Long DNS TXT queries | TXT records > 200 bytes | DNS logs | P2 | T1071.004 |

### Data Exfiltration

| Detection | Description | Data Source | Severity | MITRE |
|:---|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Large outbound transfer | > 500 MB to single external IP | NetFlow / Proxy | P1 | T1048 |
| Unusual upload destination | Upload to IP/domain never seen before | Proxy / NDR | P2 | T1567 |
| Exfil over DNS | DNS queries with encoded payload data | DNS logs | P1 | T1048.001 |
| After-hours data transfer | Large transfers 22:00вҖ“06:00 | NetFlow / Proxy | P2 | T1048 |
| Encrypted exfil to cloud | TLS to personal cloud storage | Proxy / CASB | P2 | T1567.002 |

---

## Network Segmentation Monitoring

### Zone Matrix

| Zone | Allowed Destinations | Blocked | Monitoring |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **DMZ** | Internet (specific ports), Internal DB (specific) | All other internal | Full PCAP + IDS |
| **Server Zone** | Other servers (specific), DMZ (response only) | Workstations (direct) | NetFlow + IDS |
| **Workstation Zone** | DMZ (via proxy), Server Zone (specific) | Direct Internet | Proxy logs + NetFlow |
| **Management Zone** | All zones (admin ports only) | Internet | Full PCAP + IDS |
| **IoT Zone** | IoT gateway only | All other zones | Full PCAP + IDS |

### Cross-Zone Violations

| Violation | Severity | Response |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Workstation вҶ’ Server (non-standard port) | P2 | Investigate, may indicate lateral movement |
| Server вҶ’ Workstation (any) | P1 | Investigate immediately, reversed connection suspicious |
| IoT вҶ’ Server/Workstation | P1 | Contain, possible IoT compromise |
| DMZ вҶ’ Internal (non-standard) | P1 | Investigate, possible DMZ breach |
| Any zone вҶ’ Management | P2 | Verify authorized admin access |

---

## DNS Security Monitoring

### DNS Detections

| Detection | Logic | Severity |
|:---|:---|:---:|
| **New domain (< 30 days old)** | Domain creation date query | P3 |
| **DGA detection** | Entropy analysis + NXD ratio | P2 |
| **DNS tunneling** | Query length > 50 chars + high frequency | P1 |
| **Typosquatting** | Edit distance < 3 from corporate domains | P2 |
| **IDN homograph** | Unicode lookalike domains | P2 |
| **DNS over HTTPS (DoH)** | TLS to known DoH providers | P3 |
| **Fast-flux DNS** | Domain resolving to many IPs rapidly | P2 |
| **Sinkhole response** | DNS resolving to known sinkhole IP | P2 |

### DNS Blocklist Integration

| Source | Update Frequency | Type |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| TI feeds (commercial) | Real-time | Malware C2 domains |
| OSINT feeds | Hourly | Known malicious domains |
| Internal blocklist | As needed | Policy-blocked categories |
| Sinkhole feeds | Daily | Compromised domains |

---

## Network Incident Response

### Response Actions

| Action | Tool | Command/Procedure | Impact |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Block IP** | Firewall | Add to deny list | Immediate, may affect legitimate traffic |
| **Block domain** | DNS Firewall/Proxy | Add to blocklist | Low impact |
| **Isolate host** | Switch/NAC/EDR | Port shutdown or VLAN change | Host offline |
| **Capture packets** | PCAP tool | Start targeted capture | Storage intensive |
| **Rate limit** | Firewall/IPS | Limit connections per source | Partial mitigation |
| **Sinkhole domain** | DNS | Redirect to internal sinkhole | Identifies infected hosts |

### Network IR Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Network Alert] --> B{Alert source?}
    B -->|IDS/IPS| C[Review signature details]
    B -->|NDR| D[Review behavioral anomaly]
    B -->|DNS| E[Review domain/query]
    B -->|NetFlow| F[Review traffic patterns]

    C --> G{True positive?}
    D --> G
    E --> G
    F --> G

    G -->|FP| H[Tune rule, close]
    G -->|TP| I[Assess scope]

    I --> J[How many hosts affected?]
    J --> K{Single host}
    J --> L{Multiple hosts}

    K --> M[Contain host + investigate]
    L --> N[рҹ”ҙ Major incident вҖ” escalate]

    M --> O[Block IOCs network-wide]
    N --> O
    O --> P[Hunt for additional compromise]
    P --> Q[Remediate + lessons learned]

    style A fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff
    style N fill:#dc2626,color:#fff
    style Q fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
```

---

## Metrics

| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Network alert MTTD | < 5 min | SIEM вҶ’ alert time |
| Network alert MTTR (P1) | < 30 min | Ticket resolution |
| IDS/IPS signature coverage | вүҘ 95% of known CVEs | Ruleset audit |
| DNS monitoring coverage | 100% of internal DNS | DNS log audit |
| NetFlow collection coverage | вүҘ 90% of segments | Coverage audit |
| Network segmentation violations detected | 100% | Zone violation alerts |
| False positive rate (network alerts) | < 15% | FP / total alerts |
| PCAP availability for P1 investigations | вүҘ 95% | Forensic readiness audit |

## Minimum Telemetry Baseline for Network Security Monitoring

| Required Source | Why It Is Required | Blind Spot If Missing |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Firewall allow/deny logs | Establishes ingress, egress, and policy violations | Missed perimeter access and blocked attack context |
| DNS query logs | Detects tunneling, DGA, typosquatting, and beacon resolution | Reduced visibility into C2 and exfiltration over DNS |
| NetFlow or equivalent flow telemetry | Shows east-west and outbound patterns at scale | Poor detection of lateral movement and large transfers |
| IDS/IPS or NDR alerts | Identifies known exploit and anomaly signals | Lower-confidence network detection and slower triage |
| VPN / remote access logs | Validates remote-entry activity and source context | Missed unauthorized access and after-hours correlation |
| DHCP / asset-to-IP mapping | Resolves IP addresses to actual hosts and users | Slower scoping and ownership validation |

## Escalation Triggers for Network Monitoring

| Condition | Escalate To | SLA | Required Action |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Confirmed beaconing, DNS tunneling, or known C2 traffic | Incident Commander + IR lead | Immediate | Open incident and contain affected hosts |
| Cross-zone traffic violates segmentation policy on critical assets | SOC Manager | Within 15 minutes | Validate legitimacy and initiate containment |
| Data transfer exceeds approved threshold or destination is unapproved | SOC Manager + Business owner | Within 30 minutes | Assess exfiltration risk and business impact |
| PCAP, NetFlow, or DNS visibility drops below agreed baseline | Security Engineer + SOC Manager | Within 1 hour | Restore telemetry and assess monitoring blind spot |
| Multiple hosts show coordinated scanning or exploit traffic | IR lead + CISO for P1/P2 | Immediate | Treat as broader compromise until disproven |

---

## Related Documents

-   [Log Source Matrix](Log_Source_Matrix.en.md) вҖ” All data sources
-   [Cloud Security Monitoring](Cloud_Security_Monitoring.en.md) вҖ” Cloud network
-   [Threat Landscape Report](Threat_Landscape_Report.en.md) вҖ” Active threats
-   [Forensic Investigation](../05_Incident_Response/Forensic_Investigation.en.md) вҖ” Network forensics
-   [Alert Tuning SOP](Alert_Tuning.en.md) вҖ” Network alert tuning
-   [DLP SOP](DLP_SOP.en.md) вҖ” Network DLP integration

## References

-   [MITRE ATT&CK](https://attack.mitre.org/)
-   [NIST SP 800-61r2](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-61/rev-2/final)


---

## File: 06_Operations_Management/Network_Security_Monitoring.th.md
# Network Security Monitoring SOP / SOP аёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаёқа№үаёІаёЈаё°аё§аёұаёҮаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӣаёҘаёӯаё”аё аёұаёўа№Җаё„аёЈаё·аёӯаёӮа№ҲаёІаёў

**аёЈаё«аёұаёӘа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ**: OPS-SOP-025
**а№Җаё§аёӯаёЈа№ҢаёҠаёұаёҷ**: 1.0
**аёҒаёІаёЈаёҲаёұаё”аёҠаёұа№үаёҷаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёҘаёұаёҡ**: а№ғаёҠа№үаё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ
**аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё•аёҘа№ҲаёІаёӘаёёаё”**: 2026-02-15

> аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷ SOC аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ **а№Җаёқа№үаёІаёЈаё°аё§аёұаёҮ traffic а№Җаё„аёЈаё·аёӯаёӮа№ҲаёІаёў, аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёө, а№ҒаёҘаё°аё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮ** аё„аёЈаёӯаёҡаё„аёҘаёёаёЎ IDS/IPS, NDR, DNS monitoring, NetFlow, а№ҒаёҘаё° network forensics

---

## а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘа№Җаё„аёЈаё·аёӯаёӮа№ҲаёІаёў

| а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮ | аёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ | а№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ | аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---:|
| **Firewall logs** | Accept/deny, source/dest, port | 90 аё§аёұаёҷ | рҹ”ҙ Critical |
| **IDS/IPS alerts** | Signature matches, anomalies | 90 аё§аёұаёҷ | рҹ”ҙ Critical |
| **DNS query logs** | Domain, query types | 90 аё§аёұаёҷ | рҹ”ҙ Critical |
| **Proxy/web gateway** | URL, user agent, bytes | 90 аё§аёұаёҷ | рҹ”ҙ Critical |
| **NetFlow/sFlow** | Flow metadata | 30 аё§аёұаёҷ | рҹҹ  High |
| **Full packet capture** | Traffic content аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё” | 7 аё§аёұаёҷ | рҹҹ  High |
| **DHCP logs** | IP-to-MAC mapping | 90 аё§аёұаёҷ | рҹҹЎ Medium |
| **VPN logs** | Connection times, IPs, users | 90 аё§аёұаёҷ | рҹҹЎ Medium |

---

## Network Detections аё—аёөа№ҲаёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ

### Perimeter Attacks

| Detection | аё„аёіаёӯаёҳаёҙаёҡаёІаёў | Severity | MITRE |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Port scan | аёӘа№ҒаёҒаёҷ port аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡ | P3 | T1046 |
| Brute-force | а№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№ҲаёӯаёӢа№үаёі port а№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§ | P2 | T1110 |
| Exploit attempt | IDS signature match | P1 | varies |
| DDoS indicators | Traffic volume аёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ | P1 | T1498 |

### Lateral Movement

| Detection | аё„аёіаёӯаёҳаёҙаёҡаёІаёў | Severity | MITRE |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Internal port scan | Host аёӘа№ҒаёҒаёҷ IP аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ | P2 | T1046 |
| SMB lateral movement | SMB connections аёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ | P1 | T1021.002 |
| RDP а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ host аёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ | RDP а№„аёӣ server аё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аёЎа№ҲаёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ | P2 | T1021.001 |
| Pass-the-hash | NTLM relay detected | P1 | T1550.002 |

### Command & Control

| Detection | аё„аёіаёӯаёҳаёҙаёҡаёІаёў | Severity | MITRE |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| DNS tunneling | DNS query аё–аёөа№Ҳ + encoded data | P1 | T1071.004 |
| Beaconing pattern | а№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№Ҳаёӯ outbound а№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёҲаёұаёҮаё«аё§аё° | P1 | T1071 |
| DGA detection | NXD responses аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷаёЎаёІаёҒ | P2 | T1568.002 |
| Known C2 | а№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№Ҳаёӯ TI-flagged IP/domain | P1 | T1071 |
| Encrypted C2 (JA3/JA4) | TLS fingerprint аёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ | P2 | T1573 |

### Data Exfiltration

| Detection | аё„аёіаёӯаёҳаёҙаёҡаёІаёў | Severity | MITRE |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Large outbound transfer | > 500 MB а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ IP аё аёІаёўаёҷаёӯаёҒ | P1 | T1048 |
| Upload аёӣаёҘаёІаёўаё—аёІаёҮа№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ | Upload а№„аёӣ IP/domain аё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№Җаё„аёўа№Җаё«а№Үаёҷ | P2 | T1567 |
| Exfil over DNS | DNS query + payload data | P1 | T1048.001 |
| After-hours data transfer | Transfer аёӮаёҷаёІаё”а№ғаё«аёҚа№Ҳ 22:00вҖ“06:00 | P2 | T1048 |

---

## аёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаёқа№үаёІаёЈаё°аё§аёұаёҮ Network Segmentation

### Zone Matrix

| Zone | аёӯаёҷаёёаёҚаёІаё• | аёҡаёҘа№ҮаёӯаёҒ | Monitoring |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **DMZ** | Internet (ports а№ҖаёүаёһаёІаё°), Internal DB (а№ҖаёүаёһаёІаё°) | Internal аёӯаё·а№Ҳаёҷа№Ҷ | Full PCAP + IDS |
| **Server** | Servers аёӯаё·а№Ҳаёҷ (а№ҖаёүаёһаёІаё°), DMZ (response) | Workstations (аё•аёЈаёҮ) | NetFlow + IDS |
| **Workstation** | DMZ (аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ proxy), Server (а№ҖаёүаёһаёІаё°) | Internet аё•аёЈаёҮ | Proxy + NetFlow |
| **Management** | аё—аёёаёҒ zone (admin ports) | Internet | Full PCAP + IDS |

### Cross-Zone Violations

| Violation | Severity | Response |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Workstation вҶ’ Server (non-standard port) | P2 | аёӘаё·аёҡаёӘаё§аёҷ lateral movement |
| Server вҶ’ Workstation | P1 | аёӘаё·аёҡаёӘаё§аёҷаё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө |
| IoT вҶ’ Server/Workstation | P1 | Contain аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө |
| DMZ вҶ’ Internal (non-standard) | P1 | аёӯаёІаёҲ DMZ breach |

---

## DNS Security Monitoring

| Detection | Logic | Severity |
|:---|:---|:---:|
| Domain а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ (< 30 аё§аёұаёҷ) | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ domain creation date | P3 |
| DGA detection | Entropy analysis + NXD ratio | P2 |
| DNS tunneling | Query > 50 chars + аё„аё§аёІаёЎаё–аёөа№ҲаёӘаё№аёҮ | P1 |
| Typosquatting | Edit distance < 3 аёҲаёІаёҒ corporate domains | P2 |
| DNS over HTTPS (DoH) | TLS а№„аёӣ DoH providers | P3 |
| Fast-flux DNS | Domain вҶ’ аё«аёҘаёІаёў IP а№ҖаёЈа№Үаё§ | P2 |

---

## Network Incident Response

### Response Actions

| Action | а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ | аёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **аёҡаёҘа№ҮаёӯаёҒ IP** | Firewall | аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө |
| **аёҡаёҘа№ҮаёӯаёҒ domain** | DNS Firewall/Proxy | аё•а№Ҳаёі |
| **а№ҒаёўаёҒ host** | Switch/NAC/EDR | Host offline |
| **Capture packets** | PCAP tool | а№ғаёҠа№ү storage аёЎаёІаёҒ |
| **Rate limit** | Firewall/IPS | аёҡаёЈаёЈа№Җаё—аёІаёҡаёІаёҮаёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷ |
| **Sinkhole domain** | DNS | аёЈаё°аёҡаёё infected hosts |

---

## аё•аёұаё§аёҠаёөа№үаё§аёұаё”

| аё•аёұаё§аёҠаёөа№үаё§аёұаё” | а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў |
|:---|:---:|
| Network alert MTTD | < 5 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| Network alert MTTR (P1) | < 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| IDS/IPS signature coverage | вүҘ 95% |
| DNS monitoring coverage | 100% |
| NetFlow coverage | вүҘ 90% |
| Zone violation detection | 100% |
| False positive rate | < 15% |

---

## NSM Architecture

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Network Tap Points"
        TAP1["рҹ”Ң Internet Edge"]
        TAP2["рҹ”Ң DMZ"]
        TAP3["рҹ”Ң Core Switch"]
        TAP4["рҹ”Ң Server VLAN"]
    end
    subgraph "NSM Stack"
        Suricata["рҹӣЎпёҸ Suricata (IDS)"]
        Zeek["рҹ”Қ Zeek (Metadata)"]
        PCAP["рҹ’ҫ Full PCAP"]
    end
    subgraph "Analysis"
        SIEM["рҹ“Ҡ SIEM"]
        Dashboard["рҹ“Ҳ Dashboard"]
    end
    TAP1 --> Suricata
    TAP2 --> Suricata
    TAP3 --> Zeek
    TAP4 --> Zeek
    TAP1 --> PCAP
    Suricata --> SIEM
    Zeek --> SIEM
    SIEM --> Dashboard
```

## Detection Categories

| аё«аёЎаё§аё” | аё•аёұаё§аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮ | а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ | аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|:---:|
| **Malware C2** | Beacon traffic, DNS tunneling | Suricata + JA3 | P1 |
| **Lateral Movement** | SMB/RDP аёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ, PsExec | Zeek + Suricata | P1 |
| **Data Exfiltration** | Large outbound transfers, encrypted channels | Zeek + DLP | P1 |
| **Reconnaissance** | Port scan, service enumeration | Suricata | P2 |
| **Protocol Anomaly** | Non-standard DNS, HTTP tunneling | Zeek | P2 |
| **Unauthorized Services** | Rogue DHCP, unauthorized VPN | Zeek | P3 |

## NSM KPIs

| аё•аёұаё§аёҠаёөа№үаё§аёұаё” | а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў | аёӣаёұаёҲаёҲаёёаёҡаёұаёҷ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Network coverage (monitored segments) | вүҘ 90% | [XX]% |
| IDS alert-to-incident ratio | < 100:1 | [XX]:1 |
| PCAP retention | вүҘ 72 аёҠаёЎ. | [XX] аёҠаёЎ. |
| Zeek log retention | вүҘ 30 аё§аёұаёҷ | [XX] аё§аёұаёҷ |
| Signature update frequency | вүӨ 24 аёҠаёЎ. | [XX] аёҠаёЎ. |

## NSM Tool Comparison

| аё„аёёаё“аёӘаёЎаёҡаёұаё•аёҙ | Suricata | Zeek | Arkime |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **Signature-based detection** | вң… | вқҢ | вқҢ |
| **Protocol parsing** | вң… | вң… (аё”аёөаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІ) | вң… |
| **Full PCAP** | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… |
| **Metadata logging** | вң… | вң… (аё”аёөаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІ) | вң… |
| **JA3/JA4 fingerprinting** | вң… | вң… | вң… |
| **аёЈаёІаё„аёІ** | аёҹаёЈаёө | аёҹаёЈаёө | аёҹаёЈаёө |
| **а№Җаё«аёЎаёІаё°аёҒаёұаёҡ** | IDS/IPS | Traffic analysis | PCAP search |

## NSM Use Cases

### Priority Detection Rules

| Use Case | Detection Method | Severity |
|:---|:---|:---|
| DNS Tunneling | Entropy analysis | High |
| Beaconing | Periodic connection pattern | High |
| Large upload | Bytes threshold | Medium |
| Unusual port | Baseline deviation | Medium |
| Cleartext credentials | Protocol inspection | Critical |

### Network Baseline Template

| Metric | Normal Range | Alert Threshold |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Outbound connections | 100-500/hr | > 1,000/hr |
| DNS queries | 50-200/min | > 500/min |
| Failed connections | < 5% | > 15% |
| Bandwidth utilization | 30-60% | > 85% |

### NSM Tool Stack

| Layer | Tool | Purpose |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Packet capture | Zeek / Suricata | Deep inspection |
| Flow analysis | NetFlow / sFlow | Traffic patterns |
| DNS monitoring | PassiveDNS | Domain tracking |
| Full PCAP | Arkime / tcpdump | Forensics |

## Telemetry аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№ҲаёіаёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ NSM (Minimum Telemetry Baseline for Network Security Monitoring)

| а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёЎаёө | а№Җаё«аё•аёёаёңаёҘ | Blind Spot аё–а№үаёІаёӮаёІаё” |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Firewall allow/deny logs | а№ғаёҠа№үа№Җаё«а№Үаёҷ ingress, egress а№ҒаёҘаё° policy violation | аёЎаёӯаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№Җаё«а№ҮаёҷаёҡаёЈаёҙаёҡаё—аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮаёҲаёІаёҒ perimeter |
| DNS query logs | а№ғаёҠа№үаё•аёЈаё§аёҲ DNS tunneling, DGA, typosquatting а№ҒаёҘаё° beacon resolution | а№Җаё«а№Үаёҷ C2 а№ҒаёҘаё° exfiltration аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ DNS аёҘаё”аёҘаёҮаёЎаёІаёҒ |
| NetFlow аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ flow telemetry а№Җаё—аёөаёўаёҡа№Җаё—а№ҲаёІ | а№ғаёҠа№үа№Җаё«а№Үаёҷ east-west а№ҒаёҘаё° outbound pattern а№ғаёҷаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёҒаё§а№үаёІаёҮ | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ lateral movement а№ҒаёҘаё° large transfer а№„аё”а№үа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё”аёө |
| IDS/IPS аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ NDR alerts | а№ғаё«а№үаёӘаёұаёҚаёҚаёІаё“ exploit а№ҒаёҘаё° anomaly аё—аёөа№ҲаёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЎаёұа№Ҳаёҷа№ғаёҲаёӮаёӯаёҮ detection аёҘаё”аёҘаёҮа№ҒаёҘаё° triage аёҠа№үаёІаёӮаё¶а№үаёҷ |
| VPN / remote access logs | а№ғаёҠа№үаёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ remote-entry а№ҒаёҘаё°аё—аёөа№ҲаёЎаёІ | аёһаёҘаёІаё” unauthorized access а№ҒаёҘаё° correlation аёҷаёӯаёҒа№Җаё§аёҘаёІ |
| DHCP / asset-to-IP mapping | а№ғаёҠа№ү map IP аёҒаёҘаёұаёҡа№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ host а№ҒаёҘаё° owner | scope аёҠа№үаёІаёҘаёҮа№ҒаёҘаё°аё«аёІа№ҖаёҲа№үаёІаёӮаёӯаёҮаёӘаёҙаёҷаё—аёЈаёұаёһаёўа№ҢаёўаёІаёҒ |

## Trigger аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ NSM (Escalation Triggers for Network Monitoring)

| а№ҖаёҮаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№„аёӮ | аёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё–аё¶аёҮ | SLA | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаё—аёі |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ beaconing, DNS tunneling аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ known C2 traffic | Incident Commander + IR lead | аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө | а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” incident а№ҒаёҘаё° contain host аё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ |
| аёһаёҡ cross-zone traffic аё—аёөа№Ҳаёңаёҙаё” segmentation policy аёҡаёҷ critical asset | SOC Manager | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаё„аё§аёІаёЎаё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҒаёҘаё°а№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎ containment аё–а№үаёІаёҲаёіа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ |
| аёҒаёІаёЈа№ӮаёӯаёҷаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘа№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷ threshold аё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёӣаёҘаёІаёўаё—аёІаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёӯаёҷаёёаёҚаёІаё• | SOC Manager + Business owner | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮ exfiltration а№ҒаёҘаё°аёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡаёҳаёёаёЈаёҒаёҙаёҲ |
| PCAP, NetFlow аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ DNS visibility аё•а№ҲаёіаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІаё„а№ҲаёІ baseline | Security Engineer + SOC Manager | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 1 аёҠаёЎ. | аёҒаё№а№ү telemetry аёҒаёҘаёұаёҡаёЎаёІа№ҒаёҘаё°аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ blind spot |
| аё«аёҘаёІаёў host аёЎаёөаёһаёӨаё•аёҙаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ scanning аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ exploit а№ҒаёҡаёҡаёӣаёЈаё°аёӘаёІаёҷаёҒаёұаёҷ | IR lead + CISO аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ P1/P2 | аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө | аёЎаёӯаёҮа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ broader compromise аёҲаёҷаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІаёҲаё°аёһаёҙаёӘаё№аёҲаёҷа№Ңа№„аё”а№үаё§а№ҲаёІа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№ғаёҠа№Ҳ |

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ

-   [Log Source Matrix](Log_Source_Matrix.th.md) вҖ” а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё”
-   [Cloud Security Monitoring](Cloud_Security_Monitoring.th.md) вҖ” Cloud network
-   [DLP SOP](DLP_SOP.th.md) вҖ” Network DLP
-   [Alert Tuning SOP](Alert_Tuning.th.md) вҖ” аёҒаёІаёЈ tune network alerts
-   [Threat Landscape Report](Threat_Landscape_Report.th.md) вҖ” аё аёІаёһаёЈаё§аёЎаё аёұаёўаё„аёёаёҒаё„аёІаёЎ
-   [Forensic Investigation](../05_Incident_Response/Forensic_Investigation.th.md) вҖ” Network forensics

## References

-   [MITRE ATT&CK](https://attack.mitre.org/)
-   [NIST SP 800-61r2](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-61/rev-2/final)


---

## File: 06_Operations_Management/SLA_Template.en.md
# SOC Service Level Agreement (SLA) Template

> **Document ID:** SLA-001
> **Version:** 1.0
> **Last Updated:** 2026-02-15
> **Between:** [SOC Team] and [Business Unit / Management]

---

## Purpose

This SLA defines the expected service levels for the Security Operations Center, including response times, escalation procedures, and reporting commitments. This template can be customized for your organization.

---

## Scope of Services

The SOC provides the following services:

| Service | Coverage | Description |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Security Monitoring | 24/7 or 8/5 | Continuous monitoring of security alerts |
| Incident Response | 24/7 or 8/5 | Investigation and response to security incidents |
| Threat Intelligence | Business hours | Threat landscape monitoring and IOC management |
| Vulnerability Alerts | Business hours | Notification of critical vulnerabilities |
| Compliance Monitoring | Business hours | Regulatory compliance monitoring |
| Reporting | Monthly | Operational reports and metrics |

> вҡ пёҸ **Out of Scope:** Penetration testing, application development security review, physical security, IT helpdesk.

---

## Incident Severity Classification

| Severity | Definition | Examples |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **P1 вҖ” Critical** | Active attack, data breach, business disruption | Ransomware, active data exfiltration, production down |
| **P2 вҖ” High** | Confirmed compromise, potential data breach | Account takeover, malware execution, lateral movement |
| **P3 вҖ” Medium** | Suspicious activity requiring investigation | Brute force attempts, policy violation, anomalous behavior |
| **P4 вҖ” Low** | Informational, minor policy violation | Failed scans, low-risk alerts, misconfigurations |

---

## Response Time SLA

### Initial Response Time

| Severity | Response Time | Escalation Time | Resolution Target |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **P1 вҖ” Critical** | вүӨ 15 minutes | вүӨ 30 minutes | вүӨ 4 hours (containment) |
| **P2 вҖ” High** | вүӨ 30 minutes | вүӨ 2 hours | вүӨ 8 hours |
| **P3 вҖ” Medium** | вүӨ 4 hours | вүӨ 8 hours | вүӨ 3 business days |
| **P4 вҖ” Low** | вүӨ 8 hours | N/A | вүӨ 5 business days |

### Definition of Terms

- **Response Time:** Time from alert detection to first analyst action
- **Escalation Time:** Time from detection to notifying the appropriate management
- **Resolution Target:** Time from detection to containment/resolution (best effort)

### SLA Clock Rules

- P1/P2: Clock runs **24/7** (no pause for weekends)
- P3/P4: Clock runs **business hours** only (MonвҖ“Fri 08:00вҖ“17:00)
- Clock pauses when: waiting for customer input, approved maintenance window

---

## Escalation Matrix

| Level | Who | When | Contact |
|:---:|:---|:---|:---|
| **L1** | SOC Analyst (T1) | All alerts | [SOC hotline / Slack] |
| **L2** | Senior Analyst (T2) | T1 can't resolve in 30 min | [Name, phone] |
| **L3** | IR Lead / SOC Manager | P1/P2 incidents | [Name, phone] |
| **L4** | CISO / CTO | Data breach, business impact | [Name, phone] |
| **L5** | CEO / Board | Regulatory notification required | [Name, phone] |

---

## Reporting Commitments

| Report | Frequency | Audience | Delivery |
|:---|:---:|:---|:---|
| Incident Notification | Real-time (P1/P2) | Management | Slack/email |
| Shift Handoff | Every shift | SOC team | Shared document |
| Weekly Summary | Weekly | SOC Manager | Email |
| Monthly SOC Report | Monthly | CISO, Management | Presentation + PDF |
| Quarterly Executive Brief | Quarterly | Board / Executive team | Presentation |

### Monthly Report Contents

```
1. Executive Summary
2. Incidents by Severity (P1/P2/P3/P4 counts)
3. MTTD / MTTR Trends
4. Top 10 Alert Categories
5. SLA Compliance %
6. Notable Incidents Summary
7. Detection Coverage Update
8. Recommendations
```

---

## Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

| KPI | Target | Measurement |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| SLA Compliance (P1 response) | вүҘ 95% | % of P1s responded within 15 min |
| SLA Compliance (P2 response) | вүҘ 90% | % of P2s responded within 30 min |
| MTTD | вүӨ 1 hour | Average time to detect incidents |
| MTTR | вүӨ 4 hours | Average time to respond/contain |
| False Positive Rate | вүӨ 40% | % of alerts that are not true threats |
| Alert Closure Rate | вүҘ 95% | % of alerts processed within SLA |
| Analyst Utilization | 60вҖ“80% | Optimal workload balance |

---

## Availability & Maintenance

| Item | Commitment |
|:---|:---|
| SIEM availability | вүҘ 99.5% uptime |
| Planned maintenance | 4-hour window, monthly, with 48h notice |
| Emergency maintenance | Justified, SOC remains operational |
| Failover | Backup analyst on-call if primary unavailable |

---

## Review & Amendments

| Item | Frequency |
|:---|:---|
| SLA review | Annually (or after major incident) |
| KPI targets review | Semi-annually |
| Contact information | Quarterly |
| Services scope | Annually |

---

## Signatures

```
SOC Manager:         _________________________ Date: ___________
CISO:                _________________________ Date: ___________
Business Unit Head:  _________________________ Date: ___________
```

---

## SLA Penalty / Credit Structure

### For Internal SOC

| SLA Breach | Impact | Escalation |
|:---|:---|:---|
| P1 MTTR > 4 hours | Incident review required | Auto-escalate to CISO |
| P2 MTTR > 8 hours | Post-mortem required | SOC Manager review |
| SLA compliance < 90% (monthly) | Improvement plan required | CISO + SOC Manager meeting |
| SLA compliance < 80% (monthly) | Staffing/tooling review | Board notification |

### For MSSP Contract

| SLA Level | Credit | Trigger |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Monthly SLA 95-99% | No penalty | Target met |
| Monthly SLA 90-94.9% | 5% monthly fee credit | Warning |
| Monthly SLA 85-89.9% | 10% monthly fee credit | Improvement plan |
| Monthly SLA < 85% | 20% credit + contract review | Remediation |
| P1 missed 3x in quarter | Contract termination right | Breach |

## Quarterly SLA Review Agenda

```markdown
## SLA Review Meeting вҖ” Q[X] 20XX

### Attendees
- SOC Manager, CISO, [MSSP rep if applicable]

### Agenda
1. **SLA Performance Summary** (10 min)
   - Overall SLA compliance rate
   - Breakdown by severity (P1/P2/P3/P4)
   - Trend vs. previous quarter

2. **SLA Breaches Analysis** (15 min)
   - List of all breaches with root cause
   - Patterns or recurring issues
   - Corrective actions taken

3. **KPI Deep Dive** (10 min)
   - MTTD / MTTR trends
   - Alert volume and FP rate trends
   - Staffing utilization

4. **Improvement Actions** (10 min)
   - Status of previous quarter's actions
   - New improvement proposals
   - Resource requests

5. **SLA Target Review** (5 min)
   - Are current targets appropriate?
   - Proposed adjustments for next quarter
```

## Related Documents

- [Severity Matrix](../05_Incident_Response/Severity_Matrix.en.md)
- [SOC Metrics](../06_Operations_Management/SOC_Metrics.en.md)
- [Communication Templates](../05_Incident_Response/Communication_Templates.en.md)
- [SOC Team Structure](../06_Operations_Management/SOC_Team_Structure.en.md)


---

## File: 06_Operations_Management/SLA_Template.th.md
# а№ҒаёЎа№Ҳа№Ғаёҡаёҡ SLA вҖ” аёӮа№үаёӯаё•аёҒаёҘаёҮаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёҡаёЈаёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ SOC

> **аёЈаё«аёұаёӘа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ:** SLA-001
> **а№Җаё§аёӯаёЈа№ҢаёҠаёұаёҷ:** 1.0
> **аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё•аёҘа№ҲаёІаёӘаёёаё”:** 2026-02-15
> **аёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ:** [аё—аёөаёЎ SOC] аёҒаёұаёҡ [аё«аёҷа№Ҳаё§аёўаёҳаёёаёЈаёҒаёҙаёҲ / аёңаё№а№үаёҡаёЈаёҙаё«аёІаёЈ]

---

## аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё•аёҡаёЈаёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ

| аёҡаёЈаёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ | а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ | аё„аёіаёӯаёҳаёҙаёҡаёІаёў |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Security Monitoring | 24/7 аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ 8/5 | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ alert аё•а№Ҳаёӯа№Җаёҷаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮ |
| Incident Response | 24/7 аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ 8/5 | аёӘаё·аёҡаёӘаё§аёҷа№ҒаёҘаё°аё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮ |
| Threat Intelligence | а№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈ | аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёІаёЎаё аёұаёўаё„аёёаёҒаё„аёІаёЎ |
| аёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷ | аёЈаёІаёўа№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ | аёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷаёңаёҘаёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ |

---

## а№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮ SLA

| аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ | аё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮ | Escalate | а№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮ |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **P1 аё§аёҙаёҒаёӨаё•** | вүӨ 15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | вүӨ 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | вүӨ 4 аёҠаёЎ. |
| **P2 аёӘаё№аёҮ** | вүӨ 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | вүӨ 2 аёҠаёЎ. | вүӨ 8 аёҠаёЎ. |
| **P3 аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮ** | вүӨ 4 аёҠаёЎ. | вүӨ 8 аёҠаёЎ. | вүӨ 3 аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈ |
| **P4 аё•а№Ҳаёі** | вүӨ 8 аёҠаёЎ. | N/A | вүӨ 5 аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈ |

**аёҒаёҺа№Җаё§аёҘаёІ:**
- P1/P2: аёҷаёұаёҡ **24/7** (а№„аёЎа№Ҳаё«аёўаёёаё”аё§аёұаёҷаё«аёўаёёаё”)
- P3/P4: аёҷаёұаёҡ **а№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈ** (аёҲ-аёЁ 08:00вҖ“17:00)

---

## Escalation

| аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ | а№ғаё„аёЈ | а№ҖаёЎаё·а№Ҳаёӯа№„аё«аёЈа№Ҳ |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| L1 | SOC Analyst | Alert аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё” |
| L2 | Senior Analyst | а№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үа№ғаёҷ 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| L3 | IR Lead / SOC Manager | P1/P2 |
| L4 | CISO / CTO | Data breach |
| L5 | CEO / Board | аё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮаё«аёҷа№Ҳаё§аёўаёҮаёІаёҷаёҒаёіаёҒаёұаёҡ |

---

## KPIs

| аё•аёұаё§аёҠаёөа№үаё§аёұаё” | а№Җаёӣа№үаёІ |
|:---|:---:|
| SLA Compliance (P1) | вүҘ 95% |
| SLA Compliance (P2) | вүҘ 90% |
| MTTD | вүӨ 1 аёҠаёЎ. |
| MTTR | вүӨ 4 аёҠаёЎ. |
| False Positive | вүӨ 40% |

---

## аёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷ

| аёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷ | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаё–аёөа№Ҳ | аёҒаёҘаёёа№ҲаёЎа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮ Incident | Real-time (P1/P2) | аёңаё№а№үаёҡаёЈаёҙаё«аёІаёЈ |
| аёӘаёЈаёёаёӣаёЈаёІаёўаёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң | аёЈаёІаёўаёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң | SOC Manager |
| аёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷа№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ | аёЈаёІаёўа№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ | CISO |
| аёӘаёЈаёёаёӣаёңаё№а№үаёҡаёЈаёҙаё«аёІаёЈ | аёЈаёІаёўа№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘ | Board |

---

## аёҘаёҮаёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯ

```
SOC Manager:    ____________________ аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ: __________
CISO:           ____________________ аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ: __________
аё«аёұаё§аё«аёҷа№үаёІаё«аёҷа№Ҳаё§аёў:    ____________________ аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ: __________
```

---

## SLA Tiers

| Tier | а№Җаё«аёЎаёІаё°аёҒаёұаёҡ | Response (P1) | Response (P2) | Monthly Report |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **Platinum** | Critical infrastructure | вүӨ 15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | вүӨ 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | вң… |
| **Gold** | Enterprise | вүӨ 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | вүӨ 1 аёҠаёЎ. | вң… |
| **Silver** | SMB | вүӨ 1 аёҠаёЎ. | вүӨ 4 аёҠаёЎ. | вң… |
| **Bronze** | Startup | вүӨ 4 аёҠаёЎ. | вүӨ 8 аёҠаёЎ. | вқҢ |

## SLA Breach Escalation

```mermaid
graph TD
    Breach["вҸ° SLA а№ғаёҒаёҘа№ү Breach"] --> Warn["рҹҹЎ Warning (75% SLA)"]
    Warn --> Lead["а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮ Shift Lead"]
    Lead --> Breach2{"Breach а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§?"}
    Breach2 -->|а№ғаёҠа№Ҳ| Manager["рҹ”ҙ а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮ SOC Manager"]
    Breach2 -->|а№„аёЎа№Ҳ| Resolve["вң… а№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮаё—аёұаёҷ"]
    Manager --> Report["рҹ“Ҡ аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒа№ғаёҷ SLA Report"]
```

## Penalty & Credit Structure

| SLA Target | Uptime/Compliance | Credit |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| вүҘ 99.9% | All SLAs met | 0% credit |
| 99.0вҖ“99.8% | Minor breaches | 5% credit |
| 95.0вҖ“98.9% | Moderate breaches | 10% credit |
| < 95.0% | Major breaches | 15% credit + review |

## SLA Reporting Template

| аё•аёұаё§аёҠаёөа№үаё§аёұаё” | а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў | аёңаёҘаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ | аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё° |
|:---|:---|:---|:---:|
| MTTA (P1) | вүӨ 15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | [XX] аёҷаёІаё—аёө | вң…/вқҢ |
| MTTA (P2) | вүӨ 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | [XX] аёҷаёІаё—аёө | вң…/вқҢ |
| MTTR (P1) | вүӨ 4 аёҠаёЎ. | [XX] аёҠаёЎ. | вң…/вқҢ |
| MTTR (P2) | вүӨ 8 аёҠаёЎ. | [XX] аёҠаёЎ. | вң…/вқҢ |
| FP Rate | < 10% | [XX]% | вң…/вқҢ |
| Report Delivery | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 5 аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈ | [аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ] | вң…/вқҢ |

## SLA Tier Definitions

### Response Time SLA

| Priority | Response | Update | Resolution |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| P1 Critical | 15 min | аё—аёёаёҒ 30 min | 4 hrs |
| P2 High | 30 min | аё—аёёаёҒ 1 hr | 8 hrs |
| P3 Medium | 2 hrs | аё—аёёаёҒ 4 hrs | 24 hrs |
| P4 Low | 8 hrs | аё—аёёаёҒ 24 hrs | 72 hrs |

### SLA Breach Escalation

| Breach Time | Escalation To | Action |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 50% of SLA | SOC Lead | Reprioritize resources |
| 75% of SLA | SOC Manager | Additional support |
| 100% of SLA | CISO | Executive escalation |

### SLA Reporting Template

| Period | P1 Met | P2 Met | P3 Met | Overall |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Week 1 | 100% | 95% | 98% | 97% |
| Week 2 | 90% | 100% | 95% | 95% |
| Month | 95% | 97% | 96% | 96% |

### SLA Review Cycle

| Activity | Frequency |
|:---|:---|
| SLA review | Monthly |
| SLA renegotiation | Annual |

## а№Ӯаё„аёЈаёҮаёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ SLA Penalty / Credit

### аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ SOC аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ

| SLA Breach | аёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ | Escalation |
|:---|:---|:---|
| P1 MTTR > 4 аёҠаёЎ. | аё•а№үаёӯаёҮ incident review | Auto-escalate а№„аёӣ CISO |
| P2 MTTR > 8 аёҠаёЎ. | аё•а№үаёӯаёҮ post-mortem | SOC Manager review |
| SLA compliance < 90% (а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ) | аё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҒаёңаёҷаёӣаёЈаёұаёҡаёӣаёЈаёёаёҮ | аёӣаёЈаё°аёҠаёёаёЎ CISO + SOC Manager |
| SLA compliance < 80% (а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ) | аё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷ staffing/а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ | а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮ Board |

### аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёӘаёұаёҚаёҚаёІ MSSP

| SLA Level | Credit | Trigger |
|:---|:---|:---|
| SLA а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 95-99% | а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө penalty | аё–аё¶аёҮа№Җаёӣа№үаёІ |
| SLA а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 90-94.9% | Credit 5% аё„а№ҲаёІаёҡаёЈаёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ | а№Җаё•аё·аёӯаёҷ |
| SLA а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 85-89.9% | Credit 10% аё„а№ҲаёІаёҡаёЈаёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ | а№ҒаёңаёҷаёӣаёЈаёұаёҡаёӣаёЈаёёаёҮ |
| SLA а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ < 85% | Credit 20% + аё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷаёӘаёұаёҚаёҚаёІ | а№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮ |
| P1 аёһаёҘаёІаё” 3 аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ/а№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘ | аёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙа№ҢаёўаёҒа№ҖаёҘаёҙаёҒаёӘаёұаёҚаёҚаёІ | Breach |

## аё§аёІаёЈаё°аёӣаёЈаё°аёҠаёёаёЎ SLA Review аёЈаёІаёўа№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘ

```markdown
## аёӣаёЈаё°аёҠаёёаёЎаё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷ SLA вҖ” а№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘ [X] 25XX

### аёңаё№а№үа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаёЈа№Ҳаё§аёЎ
- SOC Manager, CISO, [аё•аёұаё§а№Ғаё—аёҷ MSSP аё–а№үаёІаёЎаёө]

### аё§аёІаёЈаё°
1. **аёӘаёЈаёёаёӣ SLA Performance** (10 аёҷаёІаё—аёө)
   - аёӯаёұаё•аёЈаёІ compliance аёЈаё§аёЎ
   - а№ҒаёўаёҒаё•аёІаёЎ severity (P1/P2/P3/P4)
   - а№Ғаёҷаё§а№Ӯаёҷа№үаёЎа№Җаё—аёөаёўаёҡа№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷ

2. **аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң SLA Breaches** (15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө)
   - аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈ breach аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё”аёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎаёӘаёІа№Җаё«аё•аёё
   - аёЈаё№аёӣа№Ғаёҡаёҡаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёҙаё”аёӢа№үаёі
   - аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮаё—аёөа№Ҳаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ

3. **KPI Deep Dive** (10 аёҷаёІаё—аёө)
   - а№Ғаёҷаё§а№Ӯаёҷа№үаёЎ MTTD / MTTR
   - а№Ғаёҷаё§а№Ӯаёҷа№үаёЎ alert volume а№ҒаёҘаё° FP rate
   - Staffing utilization

4. **аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёЈаёұаёҡаёӣаёЈаёёаёҮ** (10 аёҷаёІаё—аёө)
   - аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё° actions а№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷ
   - аёӮа№үаёӯа№ҖаёӘаёҷаёӯаёӣаёЈаёұаёҡаёӣаёЈаёёаёҮа№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ
   - аёӮаёӯаё—аёЈаёұаёһаёўаёІаёҒаёЈ

5. **аё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў SLA** (5 аёҷаёІаё—аёө)
   - а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаёӣаёұаёҲаёҲаёёаёҡаёұаёҷа№Җаё«аёЎаёІаё°аёӘаёЎа№„аё«аёЎ?
   - аёӮа№үаёӯа№ҖаёӘаёҷаёӯаёӣаёЈаёұаёҡа№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘаё«аёҷа№үаёІ
```

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ

- [аё•аёІаёЈаёІаёҮаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЈаёёаёҷа№ҒаёЈаёҮ](../05_Incident_Response/Severity_Matrix.th.md)
- [аё•аёұаё§аёҠаёөа№үаё§аёұаё” SOC](SOC_Metrics.th.md)
