---

## 1. аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө (15 аёҷаёІаё—аёөа№ҒаёЈаёҒ)

| # | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ | аёңаё№а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҙаё”аёҠаёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | аёЈаё°аёҡаёё VPN session аё—аёөа№Ҳаёҷа№ҲаёІаёӘаёҮаёӘаёұаёў (user, IP, duration) | SOC T1 |
| 2 | аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң GeoIP + impossible travel | SOC T1 |
| 3 | аё•аёҙаё”аё•а№Ҳаёӯ user аё—аёІаёҮа№Ӯаё—аёЈаёЁаёұаёһаё—а№Ңа№Җаёһаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ | SOC T1 |
| 4 | аё–а№үаёІа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёӯаёҷаёёаёҚаёІаё• вҖ” аё•аёұаё” VPN session аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө | VPN Admin |
| 5 | Reset password + revoke MFA token | IAM Team |
| 6 | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ unauthorized session | SOC T2 |

## 2. аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ

### аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң VPN Session
- [ ] Source IP address а№ҒаёҘаё° GeoIP location
- [ ] а№Җаё§аёҘаёІа№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎ/аёӘаёҙа№үаёҷаёӘаёёаё” session а№ҒаёҘаё° duration
- [ ] Bandwidth аё—аёөа№Ҳа№ғаёҠа№ү (data transfer аёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ?)
- [ ] VPN client version а№ҒаёҘаё° device fingerprint
- [ ] MFA method аё—аёөа№Ҳа№ғаёҠа№ү (bypass а№„аё”а№үаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ?)
- [ ] Sessions аёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎаёҒаёұаёҷ (user login аёҲаёІаёҒ 2 аё•аёіа№Ғаё«аёҷа№ҲаёҮ?)

### аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎа№Җаё„аёЈаё·аёӯаёӮа№ҲаёІаёўаёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ Session
- [ ] Hosts аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮаёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ VPN session
- [ ] File shares аё—аёөа№Ҳ mount аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮ
- [ ] RDP/SSH sessions аё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎ
- [ ] DNS queries (internal reconnaissance?)
- [ ] аёӣаёЈаёҙаёЎаёІаё“аёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаё—аёөа№Ҳа№Ӯаёӯаёҷ (аё•аёұаё§аёҡа№ҲаёҮаёҠаёөа№ү exfiltration)

## 3. аёҒаёІаёЈаё„аё§аёҡаё„аёёаёЎ (Containment)

### аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ Credentials
- [ ] аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ dark web/paste sites аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ credentials аё—аёөа№Ҳаё«аёҘаёёаё”
- [ ] аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӣаёЈаё°аё§аёұаё•аёҙаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷ password
- [ ] аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаё§а№ҲаёІ credentials а№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷа№ғаёҠа№үаё—аёөа№Ҳаёӯаё·а№Ҳаёҷаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ (password reuse)
- [ ] аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ MFA enrollment/аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷа№ҒаёӣаёҘаёҮ

## 2.1 Evidence Checklist

| аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё аё—аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ | аёӘаёҙа№ҲаёҮаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ | а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ | а№Җаё«аё•аёёаёңаёҘаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ session | source IP, GeoIP, а№Җаё§аёҘаёІа№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎ/аёҲаёҡ, duration, bandwidth, device fingerprint | VPN logs / SIEM | а№ғаёҠа№үаёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаё§а№ҲаёІ session аёҷаёөа№үаёӘаёӯаё”аё„аёҘа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҡаёһаёӨаё•аёҙаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаёӣаёҒаё•аёҙаёӮаёӯаёҮаёңаё№а№үа№ғаёҠа№үаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ |
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷаё”а№үаёІаёҷаё•аёұаё§аё•аёҷ | username, group membership, MFA method, token change, аёӣаёЈаё°аё§аёұаё•аёҙ reset/lockout | IAM / IdP | а№ғаёҠа№үаё”аё№аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙа№Ңа№ҒаёҘаё°аё”аё№аё§а№ҲаёІаёҒаёІаёЈаё„аё§аёҡаё„аёёаёЎ identity аёҘа№үаёЎаё•аёЈаёҮа№„аё«аёҷ |
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷаёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎа№Җаё„аёЈаё·аёӯаёӮа№ҲаёІаёў | host аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮ, RDP/SSH, DNS, file-share activity, transfer volume | Firewall / VPN / NDR / DNS / NetFlow | а№ғаёҠа№үаё”аё№аё§а№ҲаёІаёңаё№а№үа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёөаё—аёіаёӯаё°а№„аёЈаё«аёҘаёұаёҮа№„аё”а№үаёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙа№Ңа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ |
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷаёҲаёІаёҒ VPN appliance | admin action, config change, patch level, exploit indicator | VPN admin console / syslog | а№ғаёҠа№үа№ҒаёўаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ credential abuse аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ appliance compromise |
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаёҒаёұаёҡаёңаё№а№үа№ғаёҠа№ү | call note, travel context, business justification, device ownership | Ticket / call log | а№ғаёҠа№үаёӣаёҙаё” false positive а№ҒаёҘаё°аёЈаёӯаёҮаёЈаёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёҳаёҙаёҡаёІаёўа№„аё”а№ү |

## 2.2 Minimum Telemetry Required

| а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮ Telemetry | а№ғаёҠа№үа№Җаёһаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёӯаё°а№„аёЈ | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ | Blind Spot аё–а№үаёІа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| VPN authentication а№ҒаёҘаё° session logs | аё”аё№ user, source IP, duration, concurrent session, protocol detail | Required | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ session аё—аёөа№Ҳаёҷа№ҲаёІаёӘаёҮаёӘаёұаёўаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёңаё№аёҒаёҒаёұаёҡаёңаё№а№үа№ғаёҠа№үа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№ү |
| IAM а№ҒаёҘаё° MFA telemetry | аё”аё№ token reset, MFA change, account state, risk signal | Required | аёҡаёӯаёҒа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаё§а№ҲаёІ identity protection аё–аё№аёҒ bypass аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ |
| Network а№ҒаёҘаё° DNS telemetry | аё”аё№ internal access, lateral movement, exfiltration indicator | Required | аёЎаёӯаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№Җаё«а№ҮаёҷаёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаё«аёҘаёұаёҮ login а№ҖаёҒаё·аёӯаёҡаё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё” |
| VPN appliance logs а№ҒаёҘаё°аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё°аёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮа№Ӯаё«аё§а№Ҳ | аё”аё№ admin change, exploit trace, version/CVE exposure | Required | а№ҒаёўаёҒ credential abuse аёӯаёӯаёҒаёҲаёІаёҒ appliance compromise а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№ү |
| Asset, user schedule, а№ҒаёҘаё° ticket context | аё”аё№ on-call, аёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёҙаёҷаё—аёІаёҮ, remote work exception | Recommended | benign event аё•аёӯаёҷаёҒаёҘаёІаёҮаё„аё·аёҷаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮа№Җаё”аёҙаёҷаё—аёІаёҮаёӯаёІаёҲаё–аё№аёҒаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡа№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ |

## 2.3 False Positive а№ҒаёҘаё° Tuning Guide

| Scenario | аё—аёіа№„аёЎаёҲаё¶аёҮаё”аё№аёҷа№ҲаёІаёӘаёҮаёӘаёұаёў | аё§аёҙаёҳаёөаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ | Tuning Action | аё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡа№ҖаёЎаё·а№Ҳаёӯ |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| аёңаё№а№үа№ғаёҠа№үа№Җаё”аёҙаёҷаё—аёІаёҮаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷаёЈаё°аёўаё°а№„аёҒаёҘ | аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё—аёЁа№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёҷаёӯаёҒа№Җаё§аёҘаёІаёҮаёІаёҷаё”аё№а№Җаё«аёЎаё·аёӯаёҷ session theft | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ itinerary, аёҒаёІаёЈаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙаёҲаёІаёҒаё«аёұаё§аё«аёҷа№үаёІ, device compliance, а№ҒаёҘаё° MFA success | tune geo/time-of-day logic аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ pattern аёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёҙаёҷаё—аёІаёҮ/remote work аё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙ | аёЎаёө concurrent session, geo а№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮаёӘаё№аёҮ, аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙаё•аёІаёЎаёЎаёІ |
| corporate VPN exit аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ carrier а№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҷаёӮаё“аё° roaming | source IP а№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷа№ҖаёЈа№Үаё§аё—аёіа№ғаё«а№ү location logic а№Җаёһаёөа№үаёўаёҷ | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ carrier/VPN ASN а№ҒаёҘаё° device fingerprint а№Җаё”аёҙаёЎ | tune impossible-travel а№ғаё«а№үаёЈаё№а№үаёҲаёұаёҒ ASN а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№ғаёҠа№Ҳаё”аё№аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё—аёЁаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§ | аёЎаёө MFA change аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ device fingerprint а№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷ |
| аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№ғаёҠа№үаёҮаёІаёҷаёӮаёӯаёҮ on-call аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёүаёёаёҒа№Җаёүаёҙаёҷ | login аё•аёӯаёҷаёҒаёҘаёІаёҮаё„аё·аёҷ/аё§аёұаёҷаё«аёўаёёаё”аё”аё№аёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ on-call roster, incident ticket, а№ҒаёҘаё°аёЈаё°аёҡаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮ | аёҘаё” severity аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ identity а№ҒаёҘаё°аёҠа№Ҳаё§аёҮа№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙ | аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё•аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮа№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷаёҮаёІаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаё—аёіаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎаёЎаёө privileged movement |
| аёҒаёІаёЈаё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡаё”а№үаёІаёҷ security аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ network | аёҒаёІаёЈа№ғаёҠа№ү VPN а№Ӯаё”аёў scanner/red-team аё”аё№а№Җаё«аёЎаё·аёӯаёҷ abuse | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ source, аёҠа№Ҳаё§аёҮа№Җаё§аёҘаёІ, а№ҒаёҘаё° owner | allowlist а№ҖаёүаёһаёІаё° test account а№ҒаёҘаё° source range аё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙ | activity а№„аёӣа№Ғаё•аё°аёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ production аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№ғаёҠа№ү credential аёӮаёӯаёҮаёңаё№а№үа№ғаёҠа№үаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ |

| аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё• | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ | аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё” |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **VPN Session** | аё•аёұаё”аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө | Kill active session |
| **Credentials** | Reset password + MFA | Enroll token а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ |
| **Source IP** | Block аё—аёөа№Ҳ perimeter | Firewall rule |
| **Internal access** | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡа№ҒаёҘаё°а№ҖаёһаёҙаёҒаё–аёӯаёҷ | File shares, RDP |

## 4. аёҒаёІаёЈаёҒаёіаёҲаёұаё”а№ҒаёҘаё°аёҒаё№а№үаё„аё·аёҷ

1. аёҡаёұаёҮаё„аёұаёҡ reset password аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёҡаёұаёҚаёҠаёөаё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ
2. Enroll MFA а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ (аёӯаёёаёӣаёҒаёЈаё“а№Ң/token а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ)
3. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаё—аёёаёҒаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮаёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ unauthorized session
4. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ persistence (аёҡаёұаёҚаёҠаёөа№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ, scheduled tasks, backdoors)
5. Patch VPN appliance аё–а№үаёІа№ғаёҠа№үаёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮа№Ӯаё«аё§а№Ҳ

## 5. аё«аёҘаёұаёҮа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң (Post-Incident)

### аёҡаё—а№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷ
| аё„аёіаё–аёІаёЎ | аё„аёіаё•аёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|
| VPN credentials аё–аё№аёҒа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёөаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№„аёЈ? | [Phishing/dark web/reuse] |
| MFA а№Җаёӣаёҙаё”а№ҒаёҘаё°аёҡаёұаёҮаё„аёұаёҡа№ғаёҠа№үаёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ? | [а№ғаёҠа№Ҳ/а№„аёЎа№Ҳ] |
| Anomaly detection trigger аё—аёұаёҷа№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ? | [а№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ] |
| аёҷа№ӮаёўаёҡаёІаёў split tunneling а№Җаё«аёЎаёІаё°аёӘаёЎаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ? | [аё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷ] |

## 6. Detection Rules (Sigma)

```yaml
title: VPN Login from Unusual Country
logsource:
    product: vpn
detection:
    selection:
        action: 'login_success'
    filter:
        src_country|contains:
            - 'TH'
            - 'US'
            - 'SG'
    condition: selection and not filter
    level: high
```

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ
- [Account Compromise Playbook](Account_Compromise.th.md)
- [Impossible Travel Playbook](Impossible_Travel.th.md)
- [Brute Force Playbook](Brute_Force.th.md)
- [аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯ Tier 1](../Runbooks/Tier1_Runbook.th.md)

## References
- [MITRE T1133 вҖ” External Remote Services](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1133/)
- [CISA вҖ” VPN Security](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories)


---

## File: 05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Watering_Hole.en.md
# Playbook: Watering Hole Attack Response

**ID**: PB-43
**Severity**: High | **Category**: Initial Access
**MITRE ATT&CK**: [T1189](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1189/) (Drive-by Compromise), [T1587.001](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1587/001/) (Exploit Development)
**Trigger**: Threat intel (sector-specific compromise), EDR (exploit after browsing), IDS (redirect to exploit kit), user report (unusual behavior after visiting known site)

> вҡ пёҸ **WARNING**: Watering hole attacks target entire industries by compromising trusted websites. The attacker waits for victims to visit вҖ” no phishing email required.

### Watering Hole Attack Chain

```mermaid
graph LR
    A["1пёҸвғЈ Reconnaissance\nIdentify target org"] --> B["2пёҸвғЈ Compromise Website\nInject exploit code"]
    B --> C["3пёҸвғЈ Visitor Profiling\nFilter by IP/org"]
    C --> D["4пёҸвғЈ Exploit Delivery\nBrowser/plugin exploit"]
    D --> E["5пёҸвғЈ Payload Install\nBackdoor on victim"]
    E --> F["6пёҸвғЈ C2 & Exfil\nData theft"]
    style A fill:#ffcc00,color:#000
    style B fill:#ff9900,color:#fff
    style D fill:#ff4444,color:#fff
    style F fill:#660000,color:#fff
```

### Watering Hole vs Phishing

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Watering Hole"
        WH1["Compromise trusted site"]
        WH2["Wait for victims"]
        WH3["Selective targeting by IP"]
        WH4["No email needed"]
        WH5["Harder to detect"]
    end
    subgraph "Phishing"
        PH1["Send email with link"]
        PH2["Active targeting"]
        PH3["Mass or spear"]
        PH4["Email is the vector"]
        PH5["Email filters can detect"]
    end
    style WH1 fill:#ff6600,color:#fff
    style WH5 fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
    style PH1 fill:#ff9900,color:#fff
```

---

## Decision Flow

```mermaid
graph TD
    Alert["рҹҡЁ Potential Watering Hole"] --> Source{"Alert source?"}
    Source -->|"Threat Intel"| TI["Industry alert:\ntrusted site compromised"]
    Source -->|"EDR"| EDR["Exploit chain detected\nafter web browsing"]
    Source -->|"IDS/Proxy"| Net["Redirect to exploit kit\nor suspicious JS injection"]
    TI --> Block["Block site at proxy/DNS"]
    EDR --> Isolate["Isolate endpoint\nCapture artifacts"]
    Net --> Analyze["Analyze traffic\nExtract IOCs"]
    Block --> Check["Check logs: who visited site?"]
    Isolate --> Triage["Triage endpoint for compromise"]
    Analyze --> IOC["Extract IOCs\nHash, domain, IP"]
    Check --> Scope{"Any endpoints compromised?"}
    Scope -->|Yes| Contain["рҹ”ҙ CONTAIN all visited endpoints"]
    Scope -->|No| Monitor["Enhanced monitoring"]
    style Alert fill:#ff6600,color:#fff
    style Contain fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
```

### Investigation Process

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant TI as Threat Intel
    participant SOC as SOC Analyst
    participant Proxy as Proxy/DNS
    participant EDR
    participant IR as IR Team

    TI->>SOC: рҹҡЁ Industry site compromised
    SOC->>Proxy: Block compromised site immediately
    SOC->>Proxy: Pull access logs вҖ” who visited?
    Proxy->>SOC: List of users who accessed site
    SOC->>EDR: Check endpoints for exploitation
    EDR->>SOC: 3 endpoints show post-exploit activity
    SOC->>IR: Escalate вҖ” confirmed watering hole
    IR->>EDR: Isolate affected endpoints
    IR->>SOC: Sweep all endpoints for IOCs
```

### Targeted Industry Assessment

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "High-Value Targets for Watering Hole"
        F1["Financial sector\nBank/trading sites"]
        F2["Government\nPolicy/procurement sites"]
        F3["Defense\nContractor portals"]
        F4["Energy\nIndustry forums"]
        F5["Healthcare\nMedical portals"]
        F6["Technology\nDeveloper resources"]
    end
    style F1 fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
    style F2 fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
    style F3 fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
```

### Exploit Kit Detection Flow

```mermaid
graph TD
    Visit["User visits trusted site"] --> Redirect{"Hidden redirect?"}
    Redirect -->|No| Safe["Normal browsing"]
    Redirect -->|"Yes вҖ” iframe/JS"| EK["Exploit Kit landing"]
    EK --> Profile["Browser/OS profiling"]
    Profile --> Vuln{"Vulnerable?"}
    Vuln -->|No| Benign["No exploit delivered"]
    Vuln -->|Yes| Exploit["Exploit delivered"]
    Exploit --> Payload["Payload downloaded"]
    Payload --> C2["C2 beacon"]
    style EK fill:#ff6600,color:#fff
    style Exploit fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
    style C2 fill:#660000,color:#fff
```

### Response Timeline

```mermaid
gantt
    title Watering Hole Response Timeline
    dateFormat HH:mm
    axisFormat %H:%M
    section Detection
        Threat intel received   :a1, 00:00, 5min
        Block site at proxy     :a2, after a1, 10min
    section Scoping
        Pull proxy access logs  :a3, after a2, 15min
        Identify visitors       :a4, after a3, 30min
    section Investigation
        Check EDR for exploits  :a5, after a4, 60min
        IOC sweep all endpoints :a6, after a5, 120min
    section Recovery
        Remediate compromised   :a7, after a6, 120min
```

---

## 1. Immediate Actions (First 15 Minutes)

| # | Action | Owner |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | Block compromised website at proxy/DNS | SOC T1 |
| 2 | Pull proxy logs: list all users who visited the site | SOC T1 |
| 3 | Check EDR for exploitation indicators on visitors | SOC T2 |
| 4 | Alert IT to distribute IOCs to all security tools | SOC T2 |
| 5 | Contact threat intel sources for additional IOCs | TI Team |
| 6 | Notify affected site owner (if possible) | SOC Manager |

## 2. Investigation Checklist

### Web Traffic Analysis
- [ ] Proxy logs: all visits to compromised site (30+ days)
- [ ] Look for redirects to exploit kit domains
- [ ] Check for injected JavaScript or iframe on the site
- [ ] Analyze any files downloaded during visits
- [ ] Check for unusual POST requests (data exfiltration)

### Endpoint Analysis (for visitors)
- [ ] EDR: process creation after browser exploitation
- [ ] Check for new services, scheduled tasks, or registry changes
- [ ] Look for suspicious child processes of browser
- [ ] Memory analysis for exploit shellcode
- [ ] Network connections to C2 infrastructure

### Threat Intelligence
- [ ] Cross-reference with known APT campaigns
- [ ] Check if exploit targets specific browser/plugin version
- [ ] Submit samples to sandbox (Any.Run, Hybrid Analysis)
- [ ] Share IOCs with industry ISAC

## 3. Containment

| Scope | Action |
|:---|:---|
| **Website** | Block at proxy, DNS sinkhole, firewall |
| **Endpoints** | Isolate confirmed compromised hosts |
| **Network** | Block C2 domains/IPs |
| **Browser** | Force-update browsers + disable vulnerable plugins |

## 4. Post-Incident

| Question | Answer |
|:---|:---|
| Which site was compromised and how? | [Site + method] |
| How many employees visited during the compromise window? | [Count] |
| Were any endpoints fully compromised? | [Count + details] |
| Were browser and plugins up to date? | [Status] |

## 6. Detection Rules (Sigma)

```yaml
title: Browser Child Process Spawning Suspicious Executable
logsource:
    product: windows
    category: process_creation
detection:
    selection:
        ParentImage|endswith:
            - '\chrome.exe'
            - '\msedge.exe'
            - '\firefox.exe'
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '\rundll32.exe'
    condition: selection
    level: high
```

## Related Documents
- [IR Framework](../Framework.en.md)
- [Sigma Rules Index](../../08_Detection_Engineering/README.md)
- [Drive-By Download Playbook](Drive_By_Download.en.md)
- [Exploit Playbook](Exploit.en.md)
- [Malware Infection Playbook](Malware_Infection.en.md)
- [Web Attack Playbook](Web_Attack.en.md)

## References
- [MITRE T1189 вҖ” Drive-by Compromise](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1189/)
- [CISA вҖ” Watering Hole Advisories](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories)


---

## File: 05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Watering_Hole.th.md
# Playbook: аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮ Watering Hole Attack

**ID**: PB-43
**аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЈаёёаёҷа№ҒаёЈаёҮ**: аёӘаё№аёҮ | **аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё аё—**: Initial Access
**MITRE ATT&CK**: [T1189](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1189/) (Drive-by Compromise), [T1587.001](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1587/001/) (Exploit Development)
**Trigger**: Threat intel (аёҒаёІаёЈа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёөа№ҖаёүаёһаёІаё°аёӯаёёаё•аёӘаёІаё«аёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ), EDR (exploit аё«аёҘаёұаёҮа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡ), IDS (redirect а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ exploit kit), user аёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷ (аёһаёӨаё•аёҙаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ)

> вҡ пёҸ **аё„аёіа№Җаё•аё·аёӯаёҷ**: Watering hole а№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёөаё—аёұа№үаёҮаёӯаёёаё•аёӘаёІаё«аёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎа№Ӯаё”аёўаёҒаёІаёЈ compromise а№Җаё§а№Үаёҡа№„аёӢаё•а№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯаё–аё·аёӯ аёңаё№а№үа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёөаёЈаёӯа№Җаё«аёўаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаёІа№Җаёўаёөа№ҲаёўаёЎаёҠаёЎ вҖ” а№„аёЎа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёӘа№ҲаёҮ phishing email

### Watering Hole Attack Chain

```mermaid
graph LR
    A["1пёҸвғЈ аёӘаёіаёЈаё§аёҲ\nаёЈаё°аёҡаёёаёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў"] --> B["2пёҸвғЈ Compromise а№Җаё§а№Үаёҡ\nаёқаёұаёҮ exploit code"]
    B --> C["3пёҸвғЈ аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮаёңаё№а№үа№Җаёўаёөа№ҲаёўаёЎаёҠаёЎ\nFilter аё•аёІаёЎ IP/org"]
    C --> D["4пёҸвғЈ аёӘа№ҲаёҮ Exploit\nBrowser/plugin exploit"]
    D --> E["5пёҸвғЈ аё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ Payload\nBackdoor аёҡаёҷа№Җаё«аёўаё·а№Ҳаёӯ"]
    E --> F["6пёҸвғЈ C2 & Exfil\nаёӮа№ӮаёЎаёўаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ"]
    style A fill:#ffcc00,color:#000
    style B fill:#ff9900,color:#fff
    style D fill:#ff4444,color:#fff
    style F fill:#660000,color:#fff
```

### Watering Hole vs Phishing

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Watering Hole"
        WH1["Compromise а№Җаё§а№Үаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯаё–аё·аёӯ"]
        WH2["аёЈаёӯа№Җаё«аёўаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаёІа№Җаёўаёөа№ҲаёўаёЎаёҠаёЎ"]
        WH3["аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёўаё•аёІаёЎ IP"]
        WH4["а№„аёЎа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёӘа№ҲаёҮаёӯаёөа№ҖаёЎаёҘ"]
        WH5["аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡаёўаёІаёҒаёҒаё§а№ҲаёІ"]
    end
    subgraph "Phishing"
        PH1["аёӘа№ҲаёҮаёӯаёөа№ҖаёЎаёҘаёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎ link"]
        PH2["а№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёөа№ҖаёҠаёҙаёҮаёЈаёёаёҒ"]
        PH3["Mass аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ spear"]
        PH4["аёӯаёөа№ҖаёЎаёҘа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷа№Җаё§аёҒа№Җаё•аёӯаёЈа№Ң"]
        PH5["Email filters аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡа№„аё”а№ү"]
    end
    style WH1 fill:#ff6600,color:#fff
    style WH5 fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
    style PH1 fill:#ff9900,color:#fff
```

---

## Decision Flow

```mermaid
graph TD
    Alert["рҹҡЁ Watering Hole аё—аёөа№Ҳа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷа№„аёӣа№„аё”а№ү"] --> Source{"а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮ alert?"}
    Source -->|"Threat Intel"| TI["а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮа№Җаё•аё·аёӯаёҷаёӯаёёаё•аёӘаёІаё«аёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ:\nа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯаё–аё·аёӯаё–аё№аёҒ compromise"]
    Source -->|"EDR"| EDR["Exploit chain аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёһаёҡ\nаё«аёҘаёұаёҮа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡ"]
    Source -->|"IDS/Proxy"| Net["Redirect а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ exploit kit\nаё«аёЈаё·аёӯ JS injection аёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ"]
    TI --> Block["Block а№Җаё§а№Үаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳ proxy/DNS"]
    EDR --> Isolate["а№ҒаёўаёҒ endpoint\nа№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ artifacts"]
    Net --> Analyze["аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң traffic\nаё”аё¶аёҮ IOCs"]
    Block --> Check["аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ logs: а№ғаё„аёЈа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡаёҷаёөа№ү?"]
    Isolate --> Triage["аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ endpoint аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ compromise"]
    Analyze --> IOC["аё”аё¶аёҮ IOCs\nHash, domain, IP"]
    Check --> Scope{"Endpoints аё–аё№аёҒ compromise?"}
    Scope -->|а№ғаёҠа№Ҳ| Contain["рҹ”ҙ CONTAIN endpoints аё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡ"]
    Scope -->|а№„аёЎа№Ҳ| Monitor["аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаё•аёҙаё”аё•аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӮа№үаёЎаёӮа№үаёҷ"]
    style Alert fill:#ff6600,color:#fff
    style Contain fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
```

### аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаёӘаё·аёҡаёӘаё§аёҷ

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant TI as Threat Intel
    participant SOC as SOC Analyst
    participant Proxy as Proxy/DNS
    participant EDR
    participant IR as IR Team

    TI->>SOC: рҹҡЁ а№Җаё§а№Үаёҡаёӯаёёаё•аёӘаёІаё«аёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаё–аё№аёҒ compromise
    SOC->>Proxy: Block а№Җаё§а№Үаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳаё–аё№аёҒ compromise аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө
    SOC->>Proxy: аё”аё¶аёҮ access logs вҖ” а№ғаё„аёЈа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡ?
    Proxy->>SOC: аёЈаёІаёўаёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯ users аё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡ
    SOC->>EDR: аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ endpoints аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ exploitation
    EDR->>SOC: 3 endpoints аёЎаёөаёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ post-exploit
    SOC->>IR: Escalate вҖ” аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ watering hole
    IR->>EDR: а№ҒаёўаёҒ endpoints аё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ
    IR->>SOC: Sweep аё—аёёаёҒ endpoints аё«аёІ IOCs
```

### аёӯаёёаё•аёӘаёІаё«аёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёўаё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӮаёӯаёҮ Watering Hole"
        F1["аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёҮаёҙаёҷ\nа№Җаё§а№ҮаёҡаёҳаёҷаёІаё„аёІаёЈ/аёҒаёІаёЈаёӢаё·а№үаёӯаёӮаёІаёў"]
        F2["аёЈаёұаёҗаёҡаёІаёҘ\nа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡаёҷа№ӮаёўаёҡаёІаёў/аёҲаёұаё”аёӢаё·а№үаёӯ"]
        F3["аёҒаёҘаёІа№Ӯаё«аёЎ\nPortals аёңаё№а№үаёЈаёұаёҡа№Җаё«аёЎаёІ"]
        F4["аёһаёҘаёұаёҮаёҮаёІаёҷ\nForum аёӯаёёаё•аёӘаёІаё«аёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ"]
        F5["аёӘаёІаёҳаёІаёЈаё“аёӘаёёаёӮ\nPortals аёҒаёІаёЈа№Ғаёһаё—аёўа№Ң"]
        F6["а№Җаё—аё„а№Ӯаёҷа№ӮаёҘаёўаёө\nаё—аёЈаёұаёһаёўаёІаёҒаёЈаёҷаёұаёҒаёһаёұаё’аёҷаёІ"]
    end
    style F1 fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
    style F2 fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
    style F3 fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
```

### Exploit Kit Detection Flow

```mermaid
graph TD
    Visit["User а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯаё–аё·аёӯ"] --> Redirect{"аёЎаёө redirect аёӢа№Ҳаёӯаёҷ?"}
    Redirect -->|а№„аёЎа№Ҳ| Safe["аё—а№ҲаёӯаёҮа№Җаё§а№ҮаёҡаёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ"]
    Redirect -->|"а№ғаёҠа№Ҳ вҖ” iframe/JS"| EK["Exploit Kit landing"]
    EK --> Profile["аёӘаёіаёЈаё§аёҲ Browser/OS"]
    Profile --> Vuln{"аёЎаёөаёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮа№Ӯаё«аё§а№Ҳ?"}
    Vuln -->|а№„аёЎа№Ҳ| Benign["а№„аёЎа№ҲаёӘа№ҲаёҮ exploit"]
    Vuln -->|а№ғаёҠа№Ҳ| Exploit["аёӘа№ҲаёҮ exploit"]
    Exploit --> Payload["Download payload"]
    Payload --> C2["C2 beacon"]
    style EK fill:#ff6600,color:#fff
    style Exploit fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
    style C2 fill:#660000,color:#fff
```

### Timeline аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮ

```mermaid
gantt
    title Watering Hole Response Timeline
    dateFormat HH:mm
    axisFormat %H:%M
    section Detection
        а№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡ threat intel     :a1, 00:00, 5min
        Block а№Җаё§а№Үаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳ proxy     :a2, after a1, 10min
    section Scoping
        аё”аё¶аёҮ proxy access logs  :a3, after a2, 15min
        аёЈаё°аёҡаёёаёңаё№а№үа№Җаёўаёөа№ҲаёўаёЎаёҠаёЎ        :a4, after a3, 30min
    section Investigation
        аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ EDR for exploits  :a5, after a4, 60min
        IOC sweep аё—аёёаёҒ endpoints:a6, after a5, 120min
    section Recovery
        а№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаё—аёөа№Ҳ compromise :a7, after a6, 120min
```

---

## 1. аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө (15 аёҷаёІаё—аёөа№ҒаёЈаёҒ)

| # | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ | аёңаё№а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҙаё”аёҠаёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | Block а№Җаё§а№Үаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳаё–аё№аёҒ compromise аё—аёөа№Ҳ proxy/DNS | SOC T1 |
| 2 | аё”аё¶аёҮ proxy logs: аёЈаёІаёўаёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯ users аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё”аё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡ | SOC T1 |
| 3 | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ EDR аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ exploitation indicators аёҡаёҷ visitors | SOC T2 |
| 4 | а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮ IT аёӘа№ҲаёҮ IOCs а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ security tools аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё” | SOC T2 |
| 5 | аё•аёҙаё”аё•а№Ҳаёӯа№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮ threat intel аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ IOCs а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎа№Җаё•аёҙаёЎ | TI Team |
| 6 | а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮа№ҖаёҲа№үаёІаёӮаёӯаёҮа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡа№„аёӢаё•а№Ң (аё–а№үаёІа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷа№„аёӣа№„аё”а№ү) | SOC Manager |

## 2. аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ

### аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң Web Traffic
- [ ] Proxy logs: аё—аёёаёҒаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳ compromise (30+ аё§аёұаёҷ)
- [ ] аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ redirects а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ exploit kit domains
- [ ] аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ JavaScript аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ iframe аё—аёөа№ҲаёқаёұаёҮа№ғаёҷа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡ
- [ ] аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ңа№„аёҹаёҘа№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳ download аёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡ
- [ ] аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ POST requests аёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ (data exfiltration)

### аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң Endpoint (аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ visitors)
- [ ] EDR: process creation аё«аёҘаёұаёҮ browser exploitation
- [ ] аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ services, scheduled tasks, аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ registry а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ
- [ ] аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ child processes аёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙаёӮаёӯаёҮ browser
- [ ] Memory analysis аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ exploit shellcode
- [ ] Network connections а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ C2

## 3. аёҒаёІаёЈаё„аё§аёҡаё„аёёаёЎ (Containment)

| аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё• | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ |
|:---|:---|
| **а№Җаё§а№Үаёҡа№„аёӢаё•а№Ң** | Block аё—аёөа№Ҳ proxy, DNS sinkhole, firewall |
| **Endpoints** | а№ҒаёўаёҒа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаё—аёөа№Ҳаёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ compromise |
| **а№Җаё„аёЈаё·аёӯаёӮа№ҲаёІаёў** | Block C2 domains/IPs |
| **Browser** | аёҡаёұаёҮаё„аёұаёҡаёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё• browsers + аёӣаёҙаё” plugins аё—аёөа№ҲаёЎаёөаёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮа№Ӯаё«аё§а№Ҳ |

### аёЎаёІаё•аёЈаёҒаёІаёЈаёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷ
- [ ] а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” browser isolation аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡа№„аёӢаё•а№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮ
- [ ] Deploy DNS filtering а№ғаёҷаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёҒаёЈ
- [ ] а№ғаёҠа№ү virtual browser аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡа№„аёӢаё•а№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё§а№үаё§аёІаёҮа№ғаёҲ
- [ ] аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ browser extensions аё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёӯаёҷаёёаёҚаёІаё•
- [ ] аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё• browser а№ҒаёҘаё° plugins аё—аёёаёҒ endpoints аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө
- [ ] а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” Enhanced Safe Browsing (Chrome/Edge)

## 4. аё«аёҘаёұаёҮа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң (Post-Incident)

| аё„аёіаё–аёІаёЎ | аё„аёіаё•аёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|
| а№Җаё§а№Үаёҡа№„аё«аёҷаё–аё№аёҒ compromise а№ҒаёҘаё°аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№„аёЈ? | [а№Җаё§а№Үаёҡ + аё§аёҙаёҳаёө] |
| аёһаёҷаёұаёҒаёҮаёІаёҷаёҒаёөа№Ҳаё„аёҷа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№Җаё§а№ҮаёҡаёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮаёҠа№Ҳаё§аёҮ compromise? | [аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷ] |
| аёЎаёө endpoints аё–аё№аёҒ compromise аёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ? | [аёҲаёіаёҷаё§аёҷ + аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё”] |
| Browser а№ҒаёҘаё° plugins аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё•аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ? | [аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё°] |

## 6. Detection Rules (Sigma)

```yaml
title: Browser Child Process Spawning Suspicious Executable
logsource:
    product: windows
    category: process_creation
detection:
    selection:
        ParentImage|endswith:
            - '\chrome.exe'
            - '\msedge.exe'
            - '\firefox.exe'
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '\rundll32.exe'
    condition: selection
    level: high
```

## File: 05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Web_Attack.en.md
# Playbook: Web Application Attack

**ID**: PB-10
**Severity**: High/Critical | **Category**: Application Security
**MITRE ATT&CK**: [T1190](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1190/) (Exploit Public-Facing Application), [T1059.007](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1059/007/) (JavaScript)
**Trigger**: WAF alert, IDS/IPS, SIEM correlation, Bug bounty report

### Web Attack Chain

```mermaid
graph LR
    Recon["рҹ”Қ Recon"] --> Scan["рҹ“Ў Vuln Scan"]
    Scan --> Exploit["рҹ’Ҙ Exploit"]
    Exploit --> Shell["рҹҗҡ Web Shell"]
    Shell --> Pivot["рҹ”Җ Pivot"]
    Pivot --> Exfil["рҹ“Ө Exfiltrate"]
    style Recon fill:#3498db,color:#fff
    style Exploit fill:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style Shell fill:#c0392b,color:#fff
    style Exfil fill:#8e44ad,color:#fff
```

### Defense in Depth

```mermaid
graph TD
    Traffic["рҹҢҗ Web Traffic"] --> WAF["рҹӣЎпёҸ WAF"]
    WAF -->|Block| Blocked["вқҢ Blocked"]
    WAF -->|Pass| App["рҹ“ұ Application"]
    App --> RASP["рҹ”Қ RASP"]
    RASP -->|Alert| SOC["рҹҡЁ SOC"]
    RASP -->|Clean| DB["рҹ—„пёҸ Database"]
    style WAF fill:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style RASP fill:#f39c12,color:#fff
    style SOC fill:#e74c3c,color:#fff
```

---

## Decision Flow

```mermaid
graph TD
    Alert["рҹҡЁ WAF / IDS Alert"] --> Decode["рҹ”Қ Decode Payload"]
    Decode --> Malicious{"Malicious Syntax?"}
    Malicious -->|No| FP["вң… False Positive"]
    Malicious -->|Yes| Response{"рҹ“Ҡ Server Response?"}
    Response -->|200 OK + Data| Breach["рҹ”ҙ Successful Exploitation"]
    Response -->|403 Blocked| Blocked["рҹҹЎ Attempt Blocked"]
    Response -->|500 Error| Partial["рҹҹ  Possible Partial Success"]
    Breach --> RCE{"RCE / Shell?"}
    RCE -->|Yes| Offline["рҹҡЁ Take App OFFLINE"]
    RCE -->|No, Data Leak| Contain["рҹӣЎпёҸ Block + Investigate"]
    Blocked --> Ban["рҹҡ« Ban IP + Monitor"]
    Partial --> Investigate["рҹ”Қ Investigate Impact"]
    Offline --> FullIR["Full Incident Response"]
```

---

## 1. Analysis

### 1.1 Attack Classification

| Attack Type | Detection Pattern | OWASP | Severity |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **SQL Injection** | `UNION SELECT`, `' OR 1=1`, `--` | A03:2021 | Critical |
| **XSS (Cross-Site Scripting)** | `<script>`, `onerror=`, `javascript:` | A03:2021 | High |
| **Remote Code Execution** | System commands, reverse shell syntax | A03:2021 | Critical |
| **Local File Inclusion (LFI)** | `../../../etc/passwd`, `php://filter` | A01:2021 | High |
| **Server-Side Request Forgery** | Internal IP requests, metadata URLs | A10:2021 | High |
| **Command Injection** | `; ls`, `| cat`, backtick execution | A03:2021 | Critical |
| **Path Traversal** | `..%2F`, `..%5C` | A01:2021 | High |
| **Authentication Bypass** | Token manipulation, JWT tampering | A07:2021 | Critical |
| **XML External Entity (XXE)** | `<!DOCTYPE`, `ENTITY`, `SYSTEM` | A05:2021 | High |

### 1.2 Assessment Checklist

| Check | How | Done |
|:---|:---|:---:|
| Decode the payload (Base64/URL encoding) | WAF logs, CyberChef | вҳҗ |
| What was the HTTP response code? | Access logs, WAF | вҳҗ |
| Was sensitive data returned to the attacker? | Response body analysis | вҳҗ |
| Automated scanner or manual attack? | Request frequency, User-Agent | вҳҗ |
| What is the target application and its criticality? | CMDB, business context | вҳҗ |
| Is the vulnerability known (CVE) or 0-day? | Vulnerability scanner, advisories | вҳҗ |
| Any evidence of web shell upload? | File system scan, new files | вҳҗ |

### 1.3 Scope Assessment

- [ ] Was the attack from a single IP or multiple (botnet)?
- [ ] Were multiple endpoints / APIs targeted?
- [ ] Any evidence of data exfiltration from the database?
- [ ] Was a web shell or backdoor uploaded?
- [ ] Were any credentials harvested?

---

## 2. Containment

### 2.1 Immediate Actions

| # | Action | Tool | Done |
|:---:|:---|:---|:---:|
| 1 | **Block attacker IP** at WAF and firewall | WAF, Firewall | вҳҗ |
| 2 | **Apply virtual patch** (WAF rule for specific exploit pattern) | WAF | вҳҗ |
| 3 | **If RCE confirmed**: Take application OFFLINE immediately | DevOps | вҳҗ |
| 4 | **Block User-Agent** if automated scanner | WAF | вҳҗ |
| 5 | **Enable enhanced WAF logging** (full request/response body) | WAF | вҳҗ |

### 2.2 If Exploitation Confirmed

| # | Action | Done |
|:---:|:---|:---:|
| 1 | Scan web directories for uploaded web shells | вҳҗ |
| 2 | Check for new files: `*.php`, `*.jsp`, `*.aspx` in web root | вҳҗ |
| 3 | Check outbound connections from web server (reverse shell) | вҳҗ |
| 4 | Disable database access from web server (if SQLi confirmed) | вҳҗ |
| 5 | Capture memory dump and disk image of web server | вҳҗ |

---

## 3. Eradication

| # | Action | Done |
|:---:|:---|:---:|
| 1 | Remove web shell or backdoor files | вҳҗ |
| 2 | **Patch the vulnerability** in source code | вҳҗ |
| 3 | Apply vendor security patches / updates | вҳҗ |
| 4 | Reset database credentials (if SQLi) | вҳҗ |
| 5 | Rotate API keys and session secrets | вҳҗ |
| 6 | Clear any crontab/scheduled task persistence by attacker | вҳҗ |

---

## 4. Recovery

| # | Action | Done |
|:---:|:---|:---:|
| 1 | Deploy patched code to production | вҳҗ |
| 2 | Run vulnerability scan to verify fix | вҳҗ |
| 3 | Perform penetration test on the fixed endpoint | вҳҗ |
| 4 | Restore database from backup if data was modified | вҳҗ |
| 5 | Bring application back online | вҳҗ |
| 6 | Monitor for attack resumption for 72 hours | вҳҗ |

---

## 5. IoC Collection

| Type | Value | Source |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Attacker IP(s) | | WAF / Access logs |
| Attack payload | | WAF (decoded) |
| Target URL / Endpoint | | Access logs |
| User-Agent string | | Access logs |
| Web shell path | | File system scan |
| Web shell hash | | Forensics |
| Exfiltrated data type | | DB audit |

---

## 6. Evidence Checklist

| Evidence Type | What to Collect | Source | Why It Matters |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Request evidence | Full request path, parameters, headers, cookies, timestamps, source IPs | WAF / access logs | Reconstructs the exploit attempt and target surface |
| Application evidence | Error traces, stack logs, session context, vulnerable endpoint behavior | App logs / APM | Confirms exploit success and affected code path |
| Persistence evidence | Web shell path, created files, cron/tasks, new users, modified configs | File integrity / forensics | Shows post-exploitation foothold |
| Data impact evidence | Queried tables, exported data, modified records, customer impact | DB audit / app logs | Supports breach and legal decisions |
| Infrastructure evidence | Load balancer, WAF, CDN, and server actions taken during attack | Infra logs / ticketing | Helps validate containment and root cause timeline |

---

## 7. Minimum Telemetry Required

| Telemetry Source | Required For | Priority | Blind Spot If Missing |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| WAF and web access logs | Attack payloads, source behavior, rate, endpoint targeting | Required | Cannot reconstruct exploit sequence or targeting scope |
| Application and APM logs | Error conditions, code path, auth/session behavior | Required | Cannot tell whether requests reached vulnerable logic successfully |
| Host and file-integrity telemetry | Web shell, persistence, process activity, server compromise | Required | RCE or post-exploitation activity may go unseen |
| Database audit logs | Query abuse, extraction, destructive changes | Required | Data impact remains unproven |
| Release and change-management records | New code, config changes, rollout timing | Recommended | Analysts may misclassify release regressions as attacks |

---

## 8. False Positive and Tuning Guide

| Scenario | Why It Looks Suspicious | How to Validate | Tuning Action | Escalate If |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Vulnerability scan or DAST exercise | Payloads and request bursts resemble real exploitation | Confirm scanner source, target scope, and schedule | Allowlist approved scanner ranges and headers in defined windows | Scanner appears in production without approval or triggers successful exploitation signs |
| Search engine crawler or partner integration | High request volume or unusual URL traversal can look abusive | Validate user agent, IP range, and expected path coverage | Tune known crawler or partner profiles narrowly | Requests hit admin paths, auth endpoints, or malicious payload patterns |
| QA or staging-to-prod release validation | Test payloads and elevated errors may spike after deployment | Confirm release window, test owner, and affected endpoint list | Lower severity only within approved release window and endpoint scope | Errors coincide with suspicious payloads, data access, or shell artifacts |
| WAF false positive on encoded business input | Complex but valid payloads may look like injection or traversal | Reproduce request with business owner and inspect response safely | Tune exact rule exceptions for validated parameters only | Payload evolves, expands to new endpoints, or reaches sensitive functions |

---

## 9. Escalation Criteria

| Condition | Escalate To |
|:---|:---|
| RCE confirmed (web shell, reverse shell) | SOC Lead + DevOps + CISO |
| Database breach (SQLi with data extraction) | CISO + Legal + DPO |
| Customer PII exposed | Legal + PDPA notification |
| Zero-day vulnerability | CISO + Vendor + CERT |
| Revenue-generating app taken offline | Business unit + Executive |
| Attacker targeting multiple applications | Major Incident |

---

## 10. Post-Incident

- [ ] Patch the exploited vulnerability and deploy to production
- [ ] Conduct code review for similar vulnerability patterns
- [ ] Update WAF rules with observed attack signatures
- [ ] Run DAST/SAST scans on the affected application
- [ ] Review and tighten input validation and output encoding
- [ ] Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers
- [ ] Schedule penetration test for affected application
- [ ] Document findings in [Incident Report](../../11_Reporting_Templates/incident_report.en.md)

---

### Web Security Architecture

```mermaid
graph LR
    User["рҹ‘Ө User"] --> CDN["вҳҒпёҸ CDN"]
    CDN --> WAF["рҹӣЎпёҸ WAF"]
    WAF --> LB["вҡ–пёҸ Load Balancer"]
    LB --> App["рҹ“ұ App Server"]
    App --> DB["рҹ—„пёҸ DB (parameterized)"]
    style WAF fill:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style DB fill:#3498db,color:#fff
```

### Secure SDLC

```mermaid
graph TD
    Dev["рҹ’» Develop"] --> SAST["рҹ”Қ SAST scan"]
    SAST --> PR["рҹ“Ӣ Code review"]
    PR --> DAST["рҹҢҗ DAST scan"]
    DAST --> Deploy{"вң… Pass?"}
    Deploy -->|Yes| Prod["рҹҡҖ Production"]
    Deploy -->|No| Fix["рҹ”§ Fix + rescan"]
    Fix --> SAST
    style SAST fill:#f39c12,color:#fff
    style DAST fill:#3498db,color:#fff
    style Prod fill:#27ae60,color:#fff
```

| Rule | File |
|:---|:---|
| SQL Injection Pattern | [web_sqli_pattern.yml](../../08_Detection_Engineering/sigma_rules/web_sqli_pattern.yml) |
| WAF Detected Exploit Attempt | [web_waf_exploit.yml](../../08_Detection_Engineering/sigma_rules/web_waf_exploit.yml) |
| High Web Request Rate | [web_high_rate_limit.yml](../../08_Detection_Engineering/sigma_rules/web_high_rate_limit.yml) |

## File: 05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Web_Attack.th.md
# Playbook: Web Attack / аёҒаёІаёЈа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёөа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡа№ҒаёӯаёӣаёһаёҘаёҙа№Җаё„аёҠаёұаёҷ

**ID**: PB-10
**аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЈаёёаёҷа№ҒаёЈаёҮ**: аёӘаё№аёҮ/аё§аёҙаёҒаёӨаё• | **аё«аёЎаё§аё”аё«аёЎаё№а№Ҳ**: аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӣаёҘаёӯаё”аё аёұаёўа№ҒаёӯаёӣаёһаёҘаёҙа№Җаё„аёҠаёұаёҷ
**MITRE ATT&CK**: [T1190](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1190/) (Exploit Public-Facing Application)
**аё—аёЈаёҙаёҒа№ҖаёҒаёӯаёЈа№Ң**: WAF alert, IDS/IPS, SIEM correlation, Bug bounty report


## аё«аёҘаёұаёҮа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң (Post-Incident)

- [ ] Patch vulnerability аё—аёөа№Ҳаё–аё№аёҒ exploit
- [ ] аё—аёі code review аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ vulnerability patterns аё—аёөа№Ҳаё„аёҘа№үаёІаёўаёҒаёұаёҷ
- [ ] аёӯаёұаёһа№Җаё”аё— WAF rules аё”а№үаё§аёў attack signatures аё—аёөа№Ҳаёһаёҡ
- [ ] аёЈаёұаёҷ DAST/SAST scans
- [ ] а№ғаёҠа№ү Content Security Policy (CSP) headers
- [ ] аёҲаёұаё”аё—аёі [Incident Report](../../11_Reporting_Templates/incident_report.th.md)

### аёңаёұаёҮа№ҖаёӘа№үаёҷаё—аёІаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёөа№Җаё§а№Үаёҡ

```mermaid
graph LR
    Recon["рҹ”Қ Recon"] --> Scan["рҹ“Ў Vuln Scan"]
    Scan --> Exploit["рҹ’Ҙ Exploit"]
    Exploit --> Shell["рҹҗҡ Web Shell"]
    Shell --> Pivot["рҹ”Җ Pivot"]
    Pivot --> Exfil["рҹ“Ө Exfiltrate"]
    style Recon fill:#3498db,color:#fff
    style Exploit fill:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style Shell fill:#c0392b,color:#fff
    style Exfil fill:#8e44ad,color:#fff
```

### аёңаёұаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈаёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷаё«аёҘаёІаёўаёҠаёұа№үаёҷ

```mermaid
graph TD
    Traffic["рҹҢҗ Web Traffic"] --> WAF["рҹӣЎпёҸ WAF"]
    WAF -->|Block| Blocked["вқҢ Blocked"]
    WAF -->|Pass| App["рҹ“ұ Application"]
    App --> RASP["рҹ”Қ RASP"]
    RASP -->|Alert| SOC["рҹҡЁ SOC"]
    RASP -->|Clean| DB["рҹ—„пёҸ Database"]
    style WAF fill:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style RASP fill:#f39c12,color:#fff
    style SOC fill:#e74c3c,color:#fff
```

---

## аёңаёұаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёұаё”аёӘаёҙаёҷа№ғаёҲ

```mermaid
graph TD
    Alert["рҹҡЁ Web Attack Alert"] --> Type{"вҡҷпёҸ аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё аё— OWASP?"}
    Type -->|SQLi| SQL["рҹ’ү SQL Injection"]
    Type -->|XSS| XSS["рҹ“ң Cross-Site Scripting"]
    Type -->|RCE/RFI| RCE["рҹ’Ҙ Remote Code Execution"]
    Type -->|Path Traversal| Path["рҹ“Ӯ File Access"]
    Type -->|Auth Bypass| Auth["рҹ”“ Authentication Bypass"]
    SQL --> Data{"рҹ“Ҡ аёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаёЈаёұа№Ҳаё§а№„аё«аёҘ?"}
    XSS --> Session{"рҹҚӘ Session Hijack?"}
    RCE --> Shell{"рҹҗҡ Web Shell?"}
    Path --> Sensitive{"рҹ“Ғ а№„аёҹаёҘа№ҢаёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ?"}
    Auth --> Access{"рҹ‘Ө а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮ Admin?"}
    Shell -->|а№ғаёҠа№Ҳ| Critical["рҹ”ҙ Isolate аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө"]
```

---

## 1. аёҒаёІаёЈаё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң

### 1.1 аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё аё— Web Attack (OWASP Top 10 Mapping)

| аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё аё— | OWASP | аёҘаёұаёҒаё©аё“аё° | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЈаёёаёҷа№ҒаёЈаёҮ |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **SQL Injection** | A03 | `' OR 1=1--`, UNION SELECT | рҹ”ҙ аё§аёҙаёҒаёӨаё• |
| **Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)** | A03 | `<script>`, stored/reflected | рҹҹ  аёӘаё№аёҮ |
| **Remote Code Execution (RCE)** | A03 | Command injection, file upload | рҹ”ҙ аё§аёҙаёҒаёӨаё• |
| **LFI/RFI** | A01 | `../../etc/passwd`, remote include | рҹ”ҙ аёӘаё№аёҮ |
| **SSRF** | A10 | `http://169.254.169.254/` | рҹ”ҙ аёӘаё№аёҮ |
| **Authentication Bypass** | A07 | Broken auth, JWT manipulation | рҹ”ҙ аё§аёҙаёҒаёӨаё• |
| **Broken Access Control** | A01 | IDOR, privilege escalation | рҹ”ҙ аёӘаё№аёҮ |
| **Deserialization** | A08 | Object injection вҶ’ RCE | рҹ”ҙ аё§аёҙаёҒаёӨаё• |

### 1.2 аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ

| аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈ | аё§аёҙаёҳаёөаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ | а№ҖаёӘаёЈа№ҮаёҲ |
|:---|:---|:---:|
| URL / endpoint аё—аёөа№Ҳаё–аё№аёҒа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёө | WAF / access logs | вҳҗ |
| аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё аё—аёҒаёІаёЈа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёө (аё”аё№аё•аёІаёЈаёІаёҮ 1.1) | WAF rule / payload analysis | вҳҗ |
| а№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёөаёӘаёіа№ҖаёЈа№ҮаёҲ аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаё–аё№аёҒ WAF block? | WAF logs (block vs detect) | вҳҗ |
| Source IP | WAF / access logs | вҳҗ |
| аёЎаёө web shell аё–аё№аёҒаё§аёІаёҮ? | File integrity / EDR | вҳҗ |
| аёЎаёөаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘаёЈаёұа№Ҳаё§а№„аё«аёҘ? (SQLi вҶ’ DB dump) | DB query logs | вҳҗ |
| аёЎаёөаё«аёҘаёІаёў endpoint аё–аё№аёҒа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёө? (automated scan) | WAF | вҳҗ |
| аёЎаёө lateral movement аё•аёІаёЎаёЎаёІ? | SIEM | вҳҗ |

### 1.3 Web Shell Indicators

| аё•аёұаё§аёҡа№ҲаёҮаёҠаёөа№ү | а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ |
|:---|:---|
| а№„аёҹаёҘа№Ңа№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳа№ғаёҷ web root (*.php, *.aspx, *.jsp) | File integrity monitoring |
| POST requests а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮа№„аёҹаёҘа№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЈаё№а№үаёҲаёұаёҒ | Access logs |
| Outbound connections аёҲаёІаёҒ web server | Netflow / EDR |
| Process spawn аёҲаёІаёҒ web server (cmd, bash) | EDR / Sysmon |

---

## 2. аёҒаёІаёЈаё„аё§аёҡаё„аёёаёЎ

### 2.1 Attack Blocked (WAF caught)

| # | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ | а№ҖаёӘаёЈа№ҮаёҲ |
|:---:|:---|:---:|
| 1 | **Block** source IP аё—аёөа№Ҳ WAF/firewall | вҳҗ |
| 2 | **аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ** аё§а№ҲаёІаёЎаёө bypass attempts аёӯаё·а№Ҳаёҷ | вҳҗ |
| 3 | **а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ** WAF virtual patch аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ vulnerability | вҳҗ |

### 2.2 Attack Succeeded

| # | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ | а№ҖаёӘаёЈа№ҮаёҲ |
|:---:|:---|:---:|
| 1 | **аёўа№үаёІаёў** web application а№„аёӣаё«аёҘаёұаёҮ WAF (аё–а№үаёІаёўаёұаёҮа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө) | вҳҗ |
| 2 | **Virtual patch** аё—аёөа№Ҳ WAF аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ attack pattern | вҳҗ |
| 3 | **аё„а№үаёҷаё«аёІа№ҒаёҘаё°аёҘаёҡ web shell** | вҳҗ |
| 4 | **Block** source IP + C2 IPs | вҳҗ |
| 5 | **Isolate** web server (аё–а№үаёІ RCE confirmed) | вҳҗ |
| 6 | **аё«аёЎаёёаёҷа№Җаё§аёөаёўаёҷ** DB credentials (SQLi) / API keys | вҳҗ |

---

## 3. аёҒаёІаёЈаёҒаёіаёҲаёұаё”

| # | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ | а№ҖаёӘаёЈа№ҮаёҲ |
|:---:|:---|:---:|
| 1 | **а№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮ source code** вҖ” fix vulnerability | вҳҗ |
| 2 | аёҘаёҡ web shell + backdoor | вҳҗ |
| 3 | аёҘаёҡ persistence (cron, scheduled tasks) | вҳҗ |
| 4 | аё«аёЎаёёаёҷа№Җаё§аёөаёўаёҷ credentials аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё” (DB, API, session secrets) | вҳҗ |
| 5 | Rebuild web server аёҲаёІаёҒ clean image (аё–а№үаёІ RCE) | вҳҗ |

---

## 4. аёҒаёІаёЈаёҹаё·а№үаёҷаёҹаё№

| # | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ | а№ҖаёӘаёЈа№ҮаёҲ |
|:---:|:---|:---:|
| 1 | Deploy **WAF** аёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎ OWASP Core Rule Set | вҳҗ |
| 2 | аёӘаёұа№ҲаёҮ **SAST/DAST** security scan | вҳҗ |
| 3 | а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” **parameterized queries** (prevent SQLi) | вҳҗ |
| 4 | а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” **Content-Security-Policy** headers (prevent XSS) | вҳҗ |
| 5 | а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” **file integrity monitoring** аёҡаёҷ web root | вҳҗ |
| 6 | аё•аёұа№үаёҮ **web application pen test** аё—аёёаёҒ 6 а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ | вҳҗ |

---

## 5. а№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№ҢаёҒаёІаёЈаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ

| а№ҖаёҮаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№„аёӮ | аёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡа№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ |
|:---|:---|
| Web shell confirmed (RCE) | SOC Lead + CISO |
| SQLi + data exfiltrated | Legal + DPO (PDPA 72 аёҠаёЎ.) |
| аё«аёҘаёІаёў applications аё–аё№аёҒа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёө | Major Incident |
| Zero-day а№ғаёҷ web framework | [PB-24 Zero-Day](Zero_Day_Exploit.th.md) |
| SSRF вҶ’ cloud metadata access | [PB-16 Cloud IAM](Cloud_IAM.th.md) |

---

## 6. Evidence Checklist

| аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё аё—аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ | аёӘаёҙа№ҲаёҮаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ | а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ | а№Җаё«аё•аёёаёңаёҘаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ request | full request path, parameter, header, cookie, timestamp, source IP | WAF / access logs | а№ғаёҠа№ү reconstruct аёҒаёІаёЈа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёөа№ҒаёҘаё° target surface |
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷа№ҒаёӯаёӣаёһаёҘаёҙа№Җаё„аёҠаёұаёҷ | error trace, stack log, session context, аёһаёӨаё•аёҙаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ endpoint | App logs / APM | а№ғаёҠа№үаёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаё§а№ҲаёІ exploit аёӘаёіа№ҖаёЈа№ҮаёҲаё–аё¶аёҮ code path а№ғаё” |
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷ persistence | web shell path, file аё—аёөа№Ҳаё–аё№аёҒаёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ, cron/task, user а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ, config change | File integrity / forensics | а№ғаёҠа№үаё”аё№ post-exploitation foothold |
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷаёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ | table аё—аёөа№Ҳаё–аё№аёҒ query, data export, record аё—аёөа№Ҳаё–аё№аёҒа№ҒаёҒа№ү, customer impact | DB audit / app logs | а№ғаёҠа№үаёЈаёӯаёҮаёЈаёұаёҡ breach а№ҒаёҘаё° legal decision |
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷа№Ӯаё„аёЈаёҮаёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮаёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ | action аёӮаёӯаёҮ load balancer, WAF, CDN, server аёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёө | Infra logs / ticketing | а№ғаёҠа№ү validate containment а№ҒаёҘаё° root cause timeline |

---

## 7. Minimum Telemetry Required

| а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮ Telemetry | а№ғаёҠа№үа№Җаёһаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёӯаё°а№„аёЈ | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ | Blind Spot аё–а№үаёІа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| WAF а№ҒаёҘаё° web access logs | аё”аё№ payload, source behavior, rate, endpoint targeting | Required | reconstruct exploit sequence а№ҒаёҘаё°аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё•аёҒаёІаёЈаёўаёҙаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№ү |
| Application а№ҒаёҘаё° APM logs | аё”аё№ error condition, code path, auth/session behavior | Required | аёҡаёӯаёҒа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаё§а№ҲаёІ request а№„аёӣаё–аё¶аёҮ logic аё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёӣаёЈаёІаё°аёҡаёІаёҮаёӘаёіа№ҖаёЈа№ҮаёҲаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ |
| Host а№ҒаёҘаё° file-integrity telemetry | аё”аё№ web shell, persistence, process activity, server compromise | Required | аёЎаёӯаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№Җаё«а№Үаёҷ RCE аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ post-exploitation activity |
| Database audit logs | аё”аё№ query abuse, extraction, destructive change | Required | аёһаёҙаёӘаё№аёҲаёҷа№ҢаёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡаё•а№ҲаёӯаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№ү |
| Release а№ҒаёҘаё° change-management records | аё”аё№ code/config change а№ҒаёҘаё° rollout timing | Recommended | аёӯаёІаёҲаё•аёө release regression а№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёө |

---

## 8. False Positive а№ҒаёҘаё° Tuning Guide

| Scenario | аё—аёіа№„аёЎаёҲаё¶аёҮаё”аё№аёҷа№ҲаёІаёӘаёҮаёӘаёұаёў | аё§аёҙаёҳаёөаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ | Tuning Action | аё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡа№ҖаёЎаё·а№Ҳаёӯ |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| vulnerability scan аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ DAST exercise | payload а№ҒаёҘаё° request burst аё”аё№а№Җаё«аёЎаё·аёӯаёҷ exploit аёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ scanner source, target scope, а№ҒаёҘаё° schedule | allowlist source range а№ҒаёҘаё° header аёӮаёӯаёҮ scanner а№ғаёҷаёҠа№Ҳаё§аёҮа№Җаё§аёҘаёІаё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙ | scanner а№ӮаёңаёҘа№Ҳа№ғаёҷ production а№Ӯаё”аёўа№„аёЎа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёЎаёөаёӘаёұаёҚаёҚаёІаё“ exploit аёӘаёіа№ҖаёЈа№ҮаёҲ |
| search engine crawler аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ partner integration | request volume аёӘаё№аёҮаё«аёЈаё·аёӯ URL traversal а№ҒаёӣаёҘаёҒ | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ user agent, IP range, а№ҒаёҘаё° path coverage аё—аёөа№Ҳаё„аёІаё”а№„аё§а№ү | tune profile аёӮаёӯаёҮ crawler/partner а№Ғаёҡаёҡа№Ғаё„аёҡ | request а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІ admin path, auth endpoint, аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёЎаёө malicious payload |
| QA аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ release validation аё«аёҘаёұаёҮ deploy | test payload а№ҒаёҘаё° error spike аёӯаёІаёҲаёӘаё№аёҮаёңаёҙаё”аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ release window, owner, а№ҒаёҘаё° endpoint аё—аёөа№Ҳаё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡ | аёҘаё” severity а№ҖаёүаёһаёІаё°а№ғаёҷ release window а№ҒаёҘаё° endpoint scope | error а№ҖаёҒаёҙаё”аёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎ payload а№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮ, data access, аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ shell artifact |
| WAF false positive аёҲаёІаёҒ business input аё—аёөа№Ҳ encode аёӢаёұаёҡаёӢа№үаёӯаёҷ | payload аё—аёөа№Ҳаё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёӯаёІаёҲаё”аё№а№Җаё«аёЎаё·аёӯаёҷ injection/traversal | reproduce request аёҒаёұаёҡ business owner а№ҒаёҘаё°аё”аё№ response аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёӣаёҘаёӯаё”аё аёұаёў | tune exception а№ҖаёүаёһаёІаё° parameter а№ҒаёҘаё° rule аё—аёөа№Ҳаёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ | payload а№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҷаёЈаё№аёӣа№Ғаёҡаёҡ, аёӮаёўаёІаёў endpoint, аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№Ғаё•аё° function аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚ |

---

### аёңаёұаёҮ Web Security Architecture

```mermaid
graph LR
    User["рҹ‘Ө User"] --> CDN["вҳҒпёҸ CDN"]
    CDN --> WAF["рҹӣЎпёҸ WAF"]
    WAF --> LB["вҡ–пёҸ Load Balancer"]
    LB --> App["рҹ“ұ App Server"]
    App --> DB["рҹ—„пёҸ DB (parameterized)"]
    style WAF fill:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style DB fill:#3498db,color:#fff
```

### аёңаёұаёҮ Secure SDLC

```mermaid
graph TD
    Dev["рҹ’» Develop"] --> SAST["рҹ”Қ SAST scan"]
    SAST --> PR["рҹ“Ӣ Code review"]
    PR --> DAST["рҹҢҗ DAST scan"]
    DAST --> Deploy{"вң… Pass?"}
    Deploy -->|Yes| Prod["рҹҡҖ Production"]
    Deploy -->|No| Fix["рҹ”§ Fix + rescan"]
    Fix --> SAST
    style SAST fill:#f39c12,color:#fff
    style DAST fill:#3498db,color:#fff
    style Prod fill:#27ae60,color:#fff
```

## аёҒаёҺаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ (Sigma)

| аёҒаёҺ | а№„аёҹаёҘа№Ң |
|:---|:---|
| SQL Injection Pattern | [web_sqli_pattern.yml](../../08_Detection_Engineering/sigma_rules/web_sqli_pattern.yml) |
| WAF Detected Exploit Attempt | [web_waf_exploit.yml](../../08_Detection_Engineering/sigma_rules/web_waf_exploit.yml) |
| High Web Request Rate | [web_high_rate_limit.yml](../../08_Detection_Engineering/sigma_rules/web_high_rate_limit.yml) |

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ

- [аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮаё•а№Ҳаёӯа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң](../Framework.th.md)
- [PB-30 API Abuse](API_Abuse.th.md)
- [PB-18 Exploit](Exploit.th.md)

## OWASP Top 10 Detection Rules

| Attack | WAF Rule | SIEM Correlation |
|:---|:---|:---|
| SQL Injection | Pattern match | DB error spike |
| XSS | Script tag filter | Reflected content |
| SSRF | Internal IP block | Unusual outbound |
| Path Traversal | ../ pattern | File access logs |
| Broken Auth | Rate limiting | Failed login pattern |

### Web Attack Forensics

| Evidence | Location | Collection |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Access logs | Web server | Export + preserve |
| WAF logs | WAF console | API export |
| Application logs | App server | Centralized logging |
| Client-side | Browser console | Screenshot/HAR |

## References

- [OWASP Top 10](https://owasp.org/Top10/)
- [MITRE ATT&CK T1190](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1190/)

## File: 05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Wiper_Attack.en.md
# Playbook: Wiper / Destructive Attack Response

**ID**: PB-38
**Severity**: Critical | **Category**: Impact
**MITRE ATT&CK**: [T1485](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1485/) (Data Destruction), [T1561](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1561/) (Disk Wipe), [T1490](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1490/) (Inhibit System Recovery)
**Trigger**: EDR alert (mass file deletion), SIEM (MBR overwrite pattern), multiple systems offline simultaneously

> вҡ пёҸ **CRITICAL**: Wiper attacks are IRREVERSIBLE. Speed is everything вҖ” isolate before the wiper propagates. Do NOT attempt to remediate on infected systems.

### Known Wiper Malware Families

```mermaid
graph TD
    Wiper["рҹ’Җ Wiper Malware"] --> NotPetya["NotPetya (2017)\nSMB + EternalBlue"]
    Wiper --> Shamoon["Shamoon (2012)\nMBR overwrite"]
    Wiper --> WhisperGate["WhisperGate (2022)\nUkraine targeted"]
    Wiper --> HermeticWiper["HermeticWiper (2022)\nPartition corruption"]
    Wiper --> CaddyWiper["CaddyWiper (2022)\nFile + partition wipe"]
    Wiper --> Industroyer2["Industroyer2 (2022)\nOT/ICS targeted"]
    Wiper --> AcidRain["AcidRain (2022)\nModem/router wipe"]
    style Wiper fill:#660000,color:#fff
    style NotPetya fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
    style HermeticWiper fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
```

### Wiper Kill Chain

```mermaid
graph LR
    A["1пёҸвғЈ Access\nPhishing/Exploit"] --> B["2пёҸвғЈ Staging\nDrop wiper"]
    B --> C["3пёҸвғЈ Disable Recovery\nDelete VSS/backups"]
    C --> D["4пёҸвғЈ Propagation\nSMB/PsExec/GPO"]
    D --> E["5пёҸвғЈ Execution\nOverwrite MBR/files"]
    E --> F["6пёҸвғЈ Systems Offline\nрҹ’Җ"]
    style A fill:#ff9900,color:#fff
    style C fill:#ff4444,color:#fff
    style E fill:#cc0000,color:#fff
    style F fill:#660000,color:#fff
```

---

## Decision Flow

```mermaid
graph TD
    Alert["рҹҡЁ Destructive Activity Detected"] --> Type{"Type of destruction?"}
    Type -->|"MBR overwrite"| MBR["Systems unbootable\nрҹ’Җ CRITICAL"]
    Type -->|"Mass file deletion"| Files["Mass delete/encrypt\nCheck if ransomware"]
    Type -->|"Volume shadow delete"| VSS["Recovery disabled\nPrecursor to wiper"]
    MBR --> Isolate["рҹ”ҙ ISOLATE ALL AFFECTED SEGMENTS"]
    Files --> Ransom{"Ransom note present?"}
    Ransom -->|Yes| RansomPB["вҶ’ Ransomware Playbook PB-02"]
    Ransom -->|"No вҖ” Pure destruction"| Isolate
    VSS --> Monitor["Monitor for follow-up wiper"]
    Isolate --> Scope{"Spreading?"}
    Scope -->|Yes| Emergency["рҹҡЁ EMERGENCY\nSegment entire network"]
    Scope -->|"Contained to single host"| Investigate["Investigate wiper binary"]
    Emergency --> BCP["Activate BCP/DR"]
    style Alert fill:#ff4444,color:#fff
    style MBR fill:#660000,color:#fff
    style Emergency fill:#660000,color:#fff
```

### Incident Communication

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant SOC as SOC Analyst
    participant Manager as SOC Manager
    participant CISO
    participant CEO
    participant Legal
    participant BCP as BCP Team

    SOC->>Manager: рҹҡЁ Wiper detected вҖ” multiple systems down
    Manager->>CISO: CRITICAL вҖ” destructive attack in progress
    CISO->>CEO: BCP activation required
    CISO->>Legal: Assess regulatory notification
    CISO->>BCP: Activate disaster recovery
    BCP->>BCP: Begin rebuild from clean backups
    SOC->>Manager: Status update every 30 minutes
    Manager->>CISO: Scope assessment: X systems affected
```

### Wiper Propagation Methods

```mermaid
graph TD
    Prop["Propagation Methods"] --> SMB["SMB/EternalBlue\nNetwork shares"]
    Prop --> PsExec["PsExec/WMI\nAdmin credentials"]
    Prop --> GPO["Group Policy\nDomain-wide deployment"]
    Prop --> Supply["Supply Chain\nSoftware update mechanism"]
    Prop --> USB["USB/Removable\nAir-gapped networks"]
    SMB --> Wide["рҹ”ҙ Network-wide impact"]
    PsExec --> Wide
    GPO --> Wide
    style Prop fill:#333,color:#fff
    style Wide fill:#660000,color:#fff
```

### Response Timeline

```mermaid
gantt
    title Wiper Attack Response Timeline
    dateFormat HH:mm
    axisFormat %H:%M
    section Detection
        Alert triggered        :a1, 00:00, 5min
        Confirm destructive    :a2, after a1, 10min
    section Containment
        Network segmentation   :a3, after a2, 15min
        Isolate all affected   :a4, after a3, 30min
    section Recovery
        Assess backup status   :a5, after a4, 60min
        Begin rebuild          :a6, after a5, 180min
        System restoration     :a7, after a6, 480min
    section Hardening
        Root cause analysis    :a8, after a7, 120min
```

### Impact Severity Matrix

```mermaid
graph TD
    Impact["Impact Assessment"] --> Single{"Single host?"}
    Single -->|Yes| Low["рҹҹЎ Medium\nRebuild single system"]
    Single -->|Multiple| Domain{"Domain controller affected?"}
    Domain -->|No| Med["рҹҹ  High\nMultiple system rebuild"]
    Domain -->|Yes| DC{"AD database intact?"}
    DC -->|Yes| High["рҹ”ҙ Critical\nDC rebuild + credential reset"]
    DC -->|No| Cat["рҹ’Җ Catastrophic\nFull AD rebuild from backup"]
    style Impact fill:#333,color:#fff
    style Cat fill:#660000,color:#fff
```

---

## 1. Immediate Actions (First 10 Minutes)

| # | Action | Owner |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | **ISOLATE** affected network segments immediately | Network Team |
| 2 | Power OFF systems showing wiper activity (preserve evidence) | SOC T1 |
| 3 | Block lateral movement: disable SMB, PsExec, WMI | Network Team |
| 4 | Verify backup integrity BEFORE connecting backup systems | SOC T2 |
| 5 | Alert CISO вҖ” activate BCP/DR plan | SOC Manager |
| 6 | Preserve at least ONE infected system for forensics | IR Team |

## 2. Investigation Checklist

### Malware Analysis
- [ ] Capture wiper binary (if system still running)
- [ ] Identify wiper family (hash lookup in VT, MalwareBazaar)
- [ ] Determine propagation mechanism (SMB, PsExec, GPO, scheduled task)
- [ ] Check for self-propagation capabilities
- [ ] Identify kill switch or C2 communication

### Scope Assessment
- [ ] How many systems are affected?
- [ ] Is the wiper still spreading?
- [ ] Are domain controllers compromised?
- [ ] Are backups accessible and clean?
- [ ] Are OT/ICS systems at risk?

### Initial Access
- [ ] How did the wiper enter the network?
- [ ] Check email logs for initial phishing
- [ ] Check VPN/RDP logs for unauthorized access
- [ ] Review supply chain components

## 3. Containment

| Priority | Action | Details |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **P0** | Network segmentation | Block all SMB (445), RDP (3389) between VLANs |
| **P0** | Disable admin shares | `net share C$ /delete` across network |
| **P1** | Disconnect backups | Ensure backup networks are air-gapped |
| **P1** | Disable scheduled tasks | Remove GPO-deployed tasks |
| **P2** | Block C2 domains/IPs | Firewall + DNS sinkhole |

## 4. Eradication & Recovery

### Recovery Priority
1. **Domain Controllers** вҖ” Rebuild AD from clean backup
2. **DNS/DHCP** вҖ” Restore network services
3. **Backup infrastructure** вҖ” Verify and protect
4. **Critical business systems** вҖ” ERP, email, file servers
5. **Workstations** вҖ” Reimage from gold image

### Recovery Checklist
- [ ] Verify backup integrity before restoration
- [ ] Rebuild systems from clean images (not from infected backups)
- [ ] Reset ALL domain credentials (including KRBTGT twice)
- [ ] Re-deploy EDR agents on rebuilt systems
- [ ] Implement network segmentation before reconnecting

## 5. Post-Incident

### Lessons Learned
| Question | Answer |
|:---|:---|
| Was the wiper detected before execution? | [Timeline] |
| Were backups properly air-gapped? | [Yes/No] |
| How fast was network segmentation? | [Time] |
| Was BCP/DR plan effective? | [Assessment] |

### Recovery Metrics
| Metric | Target | Actual |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Time to detect | < 15 min | [Actual] |
| Time to contain | < 30 min | [Actual] |
| Systems affected | 0 | [Count] |
| Data permanently lost | 0 | [Assessment] |
| Time to full recovery | < 72h | [Actual] |

## 6. Detection Rules (Sigma)

```yaml
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion (Wiper Precursor)
logsource:
    product: windows
    category: process_creation
detection:
    selection:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'vssadmin delete shadows'
            - 'wmic shadowcopy delete'
            - 'bcdedit /set.*recoveryenabled.*no'
            - 'wbadmin delete catalog'
    condition: selection
    level: critical
```

```yaml
title: Mass File Deletion Pattern
logsource:
    product: windows
    category: file_delete
detection:
    selection:
        TargetFilename|endswith:
            - '.doc'
            - '.xls'
            - '.pdf'
            - '.mdb'
    timeframe: 1m
    condition: selection | count() > 100
    level: critical
```

## Related Documents
- [IR Framework](../Framework.en.md)
- [Sigma Rules Index](../../08_Detection_Engineering/README.md)
- [Ransomware Playbook](Ransomware.en.md)
- [Malware Infection Playbook](Malware_Infection.en.md)
- [Disaster Recovery & BCP](../Disaster_Recovery_BCP.en.md)
- [Tier 3 Runbook](../Runbooks/Tier3_Runbook.en.md)

## References
- [MITRE T1485 вҖ” Data Destruction](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1485/)
- [CISA вҖ” Destructive Malware](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories)
- [Microsoft вҖ” Wiper Malware Analysis](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/)
