## File: 04_Troubleshooting/Common_Issues.th.md
# аё§аёҙаёҳаёөаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІаёЎаёІаё•аёЈаёҗаёІаёҷ (Standard Troubleshooting Methodology)

а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаёҷаёөа№үаёЈаё°аёҡаёёа№Ғаёҷаё§аё—аёІаёҮаё—аёөа№Ҳа№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡа№ғаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡа№Ӯаё„аёЈаёҮаёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮаёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ SOC

## 1. аёҷаёҙаёўаёІаёЎаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ (Defining the Problem)

```mermaid
graph TD
    Issue[аёЈаёұаёҡа№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ] --> Define[аёЈаё°аёҡаёёаёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё•/аёӯаёІаёҒаёІаёЈ]
    Define --> Layer1{аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ Network?}
    Layer1 -->|аёҘа№ҲаёЎ| FixNet[а№ҒаёҒа№үаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№Ҳаёӯ]
    Layer1 -->|аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ| Layer2{аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ App?}
    Layer2 -->|аёҘа№ҲаёЎ| FixApp[Restart/Debug Service]
    Layer2 -->|аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ| Layer3{аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡ Data?}
    Layer3 -->|аёҘа№ҲаёЎ| FixData[а№ҖаёҠа№Үаё„ Config/Logs]
    Layer3 -->|аёӣаёҒаё•аёҙ| RCA[аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ңа№ҖаёҠаёҙаёҮаёҘаё¶аёҒ]
```

-   **аёӯаёІаёҒаёІаёЈ**: аёӯаё°а№„аёЈаёҘа№үаёЎа№Җаё«аёҘаё§? (а№ҖаёҠа№Ҳаёҷ "Alert а№„аёЎа№ҲаёӮаё¶а№үаёҷ", "Login а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№ү")
-   **аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё•**: аёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡаёңаё№а№үа№ғаёҠа№үаё„аёҷа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§, Sensor аё•аёұаё§а№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§, аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаё—аёұа№үаёҮаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡ?
-   **а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ**: а№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷа№ҖаёЎаё·а№Ҳаёӯа№„аё«аёЈа№Ҳ? аёЎаёөаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷа№ҒаёӣаёҘаёҮаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡа№ҖаёЈа№Үаё§а№Ҷ аёҷаёөа№үаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ (Deployment/RFC)?

## 2. аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ (The Troubleshooting Workflow)

### 2.1 аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡа№Җаё„аёЈаё·аёӯаёӮа№ҲаёІаёў (Physical/Network Layer)
-   **аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№Ҳаёӯ**: Ping/Telnet/Netcat аё«аёІаёӣаёҘаёІаёўаё—аёІаёҮа№ҖаёҲаёӯаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ?
-   **Firewall**: аёһаёӯаёЈа№Ңаё•аё–аё№аёҒаёҡаёҘа№ҮаёӯаёҒаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ? (а№ҖаёҠа№Үаё„ Log Firewall)
-   **DNS**: аёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯ Hostname а№ҒаёӣаёҘаёҮа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ IP аё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ? (`nslookup`, `dig`)

### 2.2 аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡа№ҒаёӯаёӣаёһаёҘаёҙа№Җаё„аёҠаёұаёҷ (Application/Service Layer)
-   **аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё°аёҡаёЈаёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ**: Process аё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷаёӯаёўаё№а№Ҳаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ? (`systemctl status`, `docker ps`)
-   **аё—аёЈаёұаёһаёўаёІаёҒаёЈ**: а№ҖаёҠа№Үаё„ CPU/RAM/Disk (`top`, `df -h`) а№Ӯаё«аёҘаё”аёӘаё№аёҮаёӯаёІаёҲаё—аёіа№ғаё«а№ү Timeout
-   **Logs**: **аё•а№үаёӯаёҮ** аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ Log а№ҖаёӘаёЎаёӯ
    -   `/var/log/syslog`
    -   Application specific logs

### 2.3 аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈа№„аё«аёҘаёӮаёӯаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ (Data Flow Verification)
-   **аё•а№үаёҷаё—аёІаёҮ**: Agent аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷа№„аёҹаёҘа№Ңа№ҖаёҲаёӯаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ?
-   **аёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮаё—аёІаёҮ**: аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё°аёҡаёҷ Log Forwarder/Broker (Kafka/RabbitMQ) а№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№„аёЈ?
-   **аёӣаёҘаёІаёўаё—аёІаёҮ**: аёЎаёө Error а№ғаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ Index а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІ SIEM аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ?

## 3. аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳаёһаёҡаёҡа№Ҳаёӯаёў (Common Failure Scenarios)

### 3.1 Log Source аё«аёўаёёаё”аёӘа№ҲаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ
1.  а№ҖаёҠа№Үаё„ Network/VPN аёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮаё•а№үаёҷаё—аёІаёҮа№ҒаёҘаё° SOC
2.  а№ҖаёҠа№Үаё„аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё° Agent service аёҡаёҷа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаё•а№үаёҷаё—аёІаёҮ
3.  а№ҖаёҠа№Үаё„аёһаё·а№үаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳаё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ Disk аёҡаёҷа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаё•а№үаёҷаё—аёІаёҮ (Agent аёЎаёұаёҒаё«аёўаёёаё”аё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷаё–а№үаёІ Disk а№Җаё•а№ҮаёЎ)

### 3.2 а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮа№Җаё•аё·аёӯаёҷаёңаёҙаё”аёһаёҘаёІаё”аёһаёёа№ҲаёҮаёӘаё№аёҮ (False Positives Spikes)
1.  аёЈаё°аёҡаёёаёҒаёҺаё—аёөа№Ҳа№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ
2.  аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң Pattern аё—аёөа№Ҳаё—аёіа№ғаё«а№үа№ҖаёҒаёҙаё” Alert
3.  аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡ Logic аёӮаёӯаёҮаёҒаёҺ аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ Whitelist

## 4. аёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёіа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ (Documentation)
-   аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒаёҒаёІаёЈаё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№ҢаёӘаёІа№Җаё«аё•аёёаё—аёөа№Ҳа№Ғаё—а№үаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ (RCA)
-   аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё• Knowledge Base (KB) а№ҒаёҘаё° SOP а№Җаёһаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёҒаёҙаё”аёӢа№үаёі

### а№Җаё—аёЎа№ҖаёһаёҘаё• RCA

| аёҹаёҙаёҘаё”а№Ң | аё„аёіаёӯаёҳаёҙаёҡаёІаёў |
|:---|:---|
| **аёЈаё«аёұаёӘаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ** | аё«аёЎаёІаёўа№ҖаёҘаёӮа№ҖаёүаёһаёІаё° |
| **аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёһаёҡ** | а№ҖаёЎаё·а№ҲаёӯаёһаёҡаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІаё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮа№ҒаёЈаёҒ |
| **аёЈаё°аёҡаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ** | SIEM, EDR, log sources аёҜаёҘаёҜ |
| **аёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ** | а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮа№Җаё•аё·аёӯаёҷаёһаёҘаёІаё”, FP, аёӣаёЈаё°аёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙаё аёІаёһаёҘаё”аёҘаёҮ |
| **аёӘаёІа№Җаё«аё•аёёаё«аёҘаёұаёҒ** | аё„аёіаёӯаёҳаёҙаёҡаёІаёўаё—аёІаёҮа№Җаё—аё„аёҷаёҙаё„ |
| **аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮ** | аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ |
| **аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷ** | аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷа№ҒаёӣаёҘаёҮа№Җаёһаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёҒаёҙаё”аёӢа№үаёі |

## аё„аёіаёӘаёұа№ҲаёҮаё§аёҙаёҷаёҙаёҲаёүаёұаёўаёӯа№үаёІаёҮаёӯаёҙаёҮ

| аё§аёұаё•аё–аёёаёӣаёЈаё°аёӘаёҮаё„а№Ң | аё„аёіаёӘаёұа№ҲаёҮ | а№ҒаёһаёҘаё•аёҹаёӯаёЈа№ҢаёЎ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡаёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё°аёҡаёЈаёҙаёҒаёІаёЈ | `systemctl status <service>` | Linux |
| аё”аё№аёҘа№ҮаёӯаёҒаёҘа№ҲаёІаёӘаёёаё” | `journalctl -u <service> --since "1 hour ago"` | Linux |
| аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡаёһаё·а№үаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳаё”аёҙаёӘаёҒа№Ң | `df -h` | Linux/macOS |
| аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№Ҳаёӯ TCP | `nc -zv <host> <port>` | Linux/macOS |
| аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ DNS | `dig <hostname>` / `nslookup <hostname>` | аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё” |
| аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ Container | `docker ps` / `docker logs <container>` | Docker |

## аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ңаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёҘа№үаёЎа№Җаё«аёҘаё§а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎа№Җаё•аёҙаёЎ

### SIEM Alert аёҘа№ҲаёІаёҠа№үаёІ
1. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡаёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё° Queue аёӮаёӯаёҮ SIEM indexing
2. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ Data pipeline (Kafka/Logstash)
3. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡаёһаё·а№үаёҷаё—аёөа№ҲаёҲаёұаё”а№ҖаёҒа№Үаёҡ Hot storage

### EDR Agent а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷ
1. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ Agent service аёҡаёҷ Endpoint
2. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№Ҳаёӯа№Җаё„аёЈаё·аёӯаёӮа№ҲаёІаёўа№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ EDR server
3. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡа№Җаё§аёӯаёЈа№ҢаёҠаёұаёҷ Agent аё§а№ҲаёІаёЈаёӯаёҮаёЈаёұаёҡ

### SOAR Playbook аёҘа№үаёЎа№Җаё«аёҘаё§
1. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ API connectivity
2. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ API key/token аё§а№ҲаёІаё«аёЎаё”аёӯаёІаёўаёёаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ
3. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ Rate limiting аёӮаёӯаёҮ API endpoint

## 7. аёӘаё„аёЈаёҙаёӣаё•а№Ңа№ҒаёҒа№үаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ

### аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ SIEM Data Pipeline
```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёёаёӮаё аёІаёһ Data Pipeline ==="

# 1. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ Elasticsearch cluster health
curl -s http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | python3 -m json.tool

# 2. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ Logstash pipeline
curl -s http://localhost:9600/_node/stats/pipelines | python3 -m json.tool | grep -E "events|queue"

# 3. аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ Filebeat status
systemctl status filebeat | head -5

echo "=== аё•аёЈаё§аёҲа№ҖаёӘаёЈа№ҮаёҲ ==="
```

### аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ EDR Agent
```powershell
# аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ endpoint аё—аёөа№Ҳ EDR check-in аёҠа№үаёІ (>24 аёҠаёЎ.)
$threshold = (Get-Date).AddHours(-24)

# Sysmon (аё•аёЈаё§аёҲа№ғаёҷа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮ)
Get-WinEvent -LogName "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" -MaxEvents 1 |
    Select-Object TimeCreated, Message |
    Format-Table -AutoSize
```

### аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ Log Source аё„аёЈаёҡаё–а№үаё§аёҷ
```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ Log Source ==="

EXPECTED_SOURCES=(
    "firewall" "active_directory" "dns" "proxy"
    "endpoint_edr" "email_gateway" "vpn" "waf"
    "database" "cloud_trail"
)

for source in "${EXPECTED_SOURCES[@]}"; do
    count=$(curl -s "http://localhost:9200/logs-*/_count?q=source_type:${source}%20AND%20@timestamp:>now-1h" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['count'])" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ "${count:-0}" -gt 0 ]; then
        echo "  вң… ${source}: ${count} events/hour"
    else
        echo "  вқҢ ${source}: а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёөаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ вҖ” аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ!"
    fi
done
```

## 8. Escalation Matrix аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ Infrastructure

| аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ | аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёӯаёҡа№ҒаёЈаёҒ | Escalate аё«аёҘаёұаёҮ | Escalate а№„аёӣаёўаёұаёҮ |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| SIEM search аёҠа№үаёІ | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ cluster health | 15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | SOC Engineer |
| Log source offline | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ agent/network | 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | IT + SOC Engineer |
| EDR console а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№ү | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ cloud status page | 5 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | Vendor support |
| SOAR playbook fail | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ API connectivity | 15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | SOC Engineer |
| Alert queue > 200 | а№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎ analyst | 1 аёҠаёЎ. | SOC Manager |
| Disk space > 90% | аё«аёІ index а№ғаё«аёҚа№ҲаёӘаёёаё” | 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | SOC Engineer |
| SSL certificate аё«аёЎаё”аёӯаёІаёўаёё | Renew аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө | аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө | SOC Engineer |
| MFA аёҘа№ҲаёЎ | а№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷа№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷ backup auth | 5 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | IT + IAM team |

## 9. аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёҗаёІаёҷаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№ҲаёіаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷ Escalate

- [ ] **аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё•а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§**: аёЈаё°аёҡаёёаё§а№ҲаёІаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡаёңаё№а№үа№ғаёҠа№үаё„аёҷа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§ а№Ғаё«аёҘа№ҲаёҮаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§ аёҡаёЈаёҙаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§ аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаё—аёұа№үаёҮа№ҒаёһаёҘаё•аёҹаёӯаёЈа№ҢаёЎ
- [ ] **аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒаёҠа№Ҳаё§аёҮа№Җаё§аёҘаёІа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§**: аёЈаё°аёҡаёёаё§а№ҲаёІа№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎа№ҖаёҒаёҙаё”аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІа№ҖаёЎаё·а№Ҳаёӯа№ғаё” аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёһаёҡаё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮа№ҒаёЈаёҒа№ҖаёЎаё·а№Ҳаёӯа№ғаё” а№ҒаёҘаё°аёЎаёө change а№ғаё”а№ҖаёҒаёҙаё”аёӮаё¶а№үаёҷаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷаё«аёҷа№үаёІаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№Ҳ
- [ ] **аё—аёі basic checks а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§**: аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ connectivity, service status, resource health а№ҒаёҘаё° recent logs а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§
- [ ] **аёЈаё°аёҡаёёаёңаёҘаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡаё—аёІаёҮаёҳаёёаёЈаёҒаёҙаёҲа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§**: аёӯаёҳаёҙаёҡаёІаёў missed alerts, delayed response, user lockout аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ service downtime а№Җаёӣа№Үаёҷаё аёІаё©аёІаё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аёЎа№ҲаёҒаёіаёҒаё§аёЎ
- [ ] **аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒ workaround аёӣаёұаёҲаёҲаёёаёҡаёұаёҷа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§**: аёЈаё°аёҡаёёаёЎаёІаё•аёЈаёҒаёІаёЈаёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§аё„аёЈаёІаё§аё—аёөа№Ҳа№ғаёҠа№үа№„аёӣа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§

## 10. Trigger аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮаёңаё№а№үаёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҒаёҘаё°аёңаё№а№үаёҡаёЈаёҙаё«аёІаёЈ

| Trigger | аё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮа№ғаё„аёЈ | а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаёІаёЎаёІаёЈаё–а№ғаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ monitoring аёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚа№ҖаёӘаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаёӘаё аёІаёһ** | SOC Manager | аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө |
| **аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІаёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡ incident response аёӮаёӯаёҮа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ңаё—аёөа№ҲаёҒаёіаёҘаёұаёҮ active** | SOC Manager + incident lead | аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө |
| **outage аёҒаёЈаё°аё—аёҡаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡаёӘаёіаё„аёұаёҚаё—аёІаёҮаёҳаёёаёЈаёҒаёҙаёҲаё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёңаё№а№үаёҡаёЈаёҙаё«аёІаёЈа№ғаёҠа№үаёҮаёІаёҷ** | CISO, business owner, platform owner | аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө |
| **аё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ҒаёҒа№үаёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІаёЈаё№аёӣа№Ғаёҡаёҡа№Җаё”аёҙаёЎаёӢа№үаёіаё«аёҘаёІаёўаё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ** | SOC Manager | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ |

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ (Related Documents)
-   [аёҒаёҘаёўаёёаё—аёҳа№ҢаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёҠаё·а№ҲаёӯаёЎаё•а№Ҳаёӯа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ](../03_User_Guides/Integration_Hub.th.md)
-   [аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёҙаё”аё•аёұа№үаёҮ SOC](../10_Training_Onboarding/System_Activation.th.md)
-   [аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈ Deploy](../02_Platform_Operations/Deployment_Procedures.th.md)

## аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ Network Connectivity
```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ Network Connectivity ==="

TARGETS=(
    "siem.internal:9200"
    "edr.internal:443"
    "soar.internal:443"
    "misp.internal:443"
    "ticketing.internal:443"
)

for target in "${TARGETS[@]}"; do
    host=$(echo $target | cut -d: -f1)
    port=$(echo $target | cut -d: -f2)
    if nc -zw3 "$host" "$port" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "  вң… $target вҖ” reachable"
    else
        echo "  вқҢ $target вҖ” UNREACHABLE"
    fi
done
```

## References
-   [USE Method (Brendan Gregg)](https://www.brendangregg.com/usemethod.html)
-   [Google SRE Handbook](https://sre.google/sre-book/table-of-contents/)

## аёӯа№үаёІаёҮаёӯаёҙаёҮаё”а№Ҳаё§аёҷ (Quick Fix Reference)

| аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ | Action а№ҒаёЈаёҒ | Escalate аё–а№үаёІ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| SIEM аёҠа№үаёІ | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ indexer load | CPU > 90% а№ҖаёҒаёҙаёҷ 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө alert | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ log ingestion | Source offline > 15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| Login аёҘа№үаёЎа№Җаё«аёҘаё§ | Reset password/MFA | Account locked 3 аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ |
| Agent offline | Restart service | > 3 endpoints |
| Dashboard error | Clear cache, reload | аёўаёұаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё«аёІаёўаё«аёҘаёұаёҮ restart |
| API timeout | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ rate limit | аёҘа№үаёЎа№Җаё«аёҘаё§аё•а№Ҳаёӯа№Җаёҷаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮ > 5 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |

## File: .agent/workflows/update-docs.md
---
description: How to update documentation when adding, modifying, or removing SOC SOPs
---

# Update SOC Documentation Workflow

When adding, modifying, or removing SOC SOP documents, follow these steps to keep versioning and tracking up to date.

// turbo-all

## 1. Create or modify the document

- Create both EN (`.en.md`) and TH (`.th.md`) versions
- Include standard metadata at the top of each document:
  ```markdown
  **Document ID**: [SECTION]-SOP-[NUMBER]
  **Version**: 1.0
  **Classification**: Internal
  **Last Updated**: YYYY-MM-DD
  ```
- Place the file in the correct directory (e.g., `06_Operations_Management/`, `05_Incident_Response/`)

## 2. Update README.md

- Add the new document to the appropriate section table in `README.md`
- Use the format: `| **рҹ“Ң Document Name** | [Read](path/to/file.en.md) | [аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ](path/to/file.th.md) |`
- Place it in the correct sub-group within the section

## 3. Update mkdocs.yml

- Add nav entries for both EN and TH versions under the appropriate section
- Place them near related documents

## 4. Update CHANGELOG.md

- Add a new version entry at the top of `CHANGELOG.md` (below the header)
- Follow [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com) format:
  ```markdown
  ## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD

  ### Added
  - **Document Name** (EN+TH) вҖ” Brief description

  ### Changed
  - Description of any modifications to existing documents
  ```
- Version bump rules:
  - **Patch** (X.Y.Z+1): Typo fixes, formatting
  - **Minor** (X.Y+1.0): New documents, content updates
  - **Major** (X+1.0.0): Major restructuring, breaking changes

## 5. Update VERSION_TRACKER.md

- Add a new row in the appropriate section table:
  ```markdown
  | [Document Name](path/to/file.en.md) | 1.0 | YYYY-MM-DD | вң… Current | YYYY+1-MM-DD |
  ```
- If modifying an existing document, update its Version, Last Updated, and Next Review columns
- Update the **Summary** table at the bottom if document counts changed
- Update the `Repository version` and `Last tracker update` at the bottom

## 6. Validate and push

```bash
cd /Users/pop7/Code/SOCSOP
python3 tools/check_links.py
python3 tools/export_docs.py
git add -A
git commit -m "feat: add [Document Name] (EN+TH)"
git push
```

## 7. Tag version (for minor/major releases)

```bash
git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m "Description of changes"
git push origin vX.Y.Z
```

## Quick Reference

| File | When to update |
|:---|:---|
| `README.md` | Every new/removed document |
| `mkdocs.yml` | Every new/removed document |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Every commit batch |
| `VERSION_TRACKER.md` | Every new/modified document |
| Git tag | Minor and major releases |


---

## File: 10_Training_Onboarding/Analyst_Onboarding_Path.en.md
# SOC Analyst Onboarding Curriculum (4-Week Path)

Welcome to the Security Operations Center (SOC). This 4-week program is designed to take you from "Day 1" to "Production Ready".

```mermaid
gantt
    title SOC Analyst Onboarding Timeline
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD
    axisFormat  Week %W

    section Foundation
    Access & Tools       :a1, 2023-10-01, 3d
    Data Governance      :a2, after a1, 2d

    section Operations
    Incident Response    :b1, 2023-10-08, 5d
    Playbook Study       :b2, after b1, 5d

    section Detection
    Rule Engineering     :c1, 2023-10-22, 5d

    section Validation
    Purple Team Drill    :d1, 2023-10-29, 5d
```

## Week 1: Foundations (Infrastructure & Governance)
**Goal**: Understand *where* we work and *what* we protect.

-   **Day 1: Orientation & Access**
    -   Read: [System Activation](../10_Training_Onboarding/System_Activation.en.md) - Understand our generic stack (SIEM, EDR, SOAR).
    -   Task: Verify access to all dashboards.
-   **Day 2: Data & Privacy**
    -   Read: [Data Governance](../02_Platform_Operations/Database_Management.en.md) - Learn data classification (Restricted vs Public).
    -   Task: Locate where "Critical" data is stored in our SIEM.
-   **Day 3: Change Management**
    -   Read: [Deployment Procedures](../02_Platform_Operations/Deployment_Procedures.en.md) - Learn how we deploy changes (RFC/CAB).
    -   Read: [Template - Change Request](../11_Reporting_Templates/change_request_rfc.en.md).

## Week 2: Incident Response (The "How-To")
**Goal**: Learn how to handle alerts using Standard Playbooks.

-   **Day 1: The Framework**
    -   Read: [Incident Response Framework](../05_Incident_Response/Framework.en.md) - The NIST lifecycle.
    -   Read: [Template - Incident Report](../11_Reporting_Templates/incident_report.en.md).
-   **Day 2: Core Playbooks (Identity)**
    -   Read: [PB-01 Phishing](../05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Phishing.en.md).
    -   Read: [PB-04 Brute Force](../05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Brute_Force.en.md).
-   **Day 3: Core Playbooks (Endpoint)**
    -   Read: [PB-02 Ransomware](../05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Ransomware.en.md).
    -   Read: [PB-03 Malware](../05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Malware_Infection.en.md).

## Week 3: Detection Engineering (The "Why")
**Goal**: Understand why an alert triggered and how to tune it.

-   **Day 1: The Lifecycle**
    -   Read: [Content Management](../03_User_Guides/Content_Management.en.md) - How we create rules.
-   **Day 2: Sigma Rules**
    -   Explore: `08_Detection_Engineering/sigma_rules/` - Review the YAML files.
    -   Task: Compare `proc_office_spawn_powershell.yml` with **PB-01**.

## Week 4: Simulation & Validation (Purple Team)
**Goal**: Prove your skills by defending against a simulated attack.

-   **Day 1: Testing Tools**
    -   Read: [Simulation Guide](../09_Simulation_Testing/Simulation_Guide.en.md).
    -   Read: [Atomic Test Map](../09_Simulation_Testing/Atomic_Test_Map.en.md).
-   **Day 2: Final Exam (Drill)**
    -   Task: Run `Invoke-AtomicTest T1059.001` (Phishing Simulation) in the Test Lab.
    -   Task: Triage the alert in SIEM.
    -   Task: Submit a mock [Incident Report](../11_Reporting_Templates/incident_report.en.md).

## Competency Assessment

| Skill | Assessment Method | Pass Criteria |
|:---|:---|:---|
| SIEM Query Writing | Practical exam | Write 3 correct queries in 30 min |
| Alert Triage | Mock alert exercise | Correctly classify 8/10 alerts |
| Incident Documentation | Submit mock IR report | Manager review score вүҘ 80% |
| Playbook Execution | Tabletop exercise | Follow playbook with no critical gaps |
| Tool Proficiency | Hands-on demo | Navigate SIEM, EDR, SOAR independently |
| Communication | Escalation role-play | Correct escalation path and content |

## Mentorship Program

| Week | Mentor Activity | New Analyst Activity |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Week 1 | Shadow mentor during shift | Observe triage, take notes, ask questions |
| Week 2 | Mentor reviews analyst's first triages | Triage alerts with mentor review before closing |
| Week 3 | Mentor available for questions only | Triage independently, escalate if unsure |
| Week 4 | Mentor evaluates final drill | Complete drill independently |

## Graduation Criteria

To be cleared for independent production shifts, new analysts must:

- [ ] Complete all 4 weeks of curriculum
- [ ] Pass competency assessment (all skills вүҘ 80%)
- [ ] Complete at least 1 purple team drill
- [ ] Submit 1 mock incident report (approved by manager)
- [ ] Demonstrate proficiency in SIEM, EDR, and ticketing tools
- [ ] Receive mentor sign-off
- [ ] Complete PDPA awareness training

## Continuing Education

| Certification | Target Timeline | Relevance |
|:---|:---|:---|
| CompTIA Security+ | Month 3 | Foundational security knowledge |
| SANS SEC401 / GISF | Month 6 | SOC operations fundamentals |
| CySA+ / GCIH | Year 1 | Incident handling expertise |
| SANS SEC504 / GCIH | Year 1-2 | Advanced incident response |
| BTL1 (Blue Team Level 1) | Year 1 | Practical blue team skills |

## 90-Day Onboarding Plan

### Week 1-2: Foundation
| Day | Activity | Deliverable |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | Welcome, badge, account setup | Access to all SOC tools |
| 2 | Read SOC 101 + Glossary | Quiz: 80% pass |
| 3 | SIEM orientation (read-only) | Can navigate dashboards |
| 4 | Ticketing system training | Can create/update tickets |
| 5 | Shadow T1 analyst (full shift) | Observation notes |
| 6-10 | Complete Security+ self-study modules | Module completion |

### Week 3-4: Skill Building
| Day | Activity | Deliverable |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 11-12 | Read top 5 playbooks | Playbook summary |
| 13-14 | SIEM query lab exercises | 10 queries completed |
| 15 | EDR tool orientation | Can navigate EDR console |
| 16-17 | Log analysis workshop | Analyze 20 sample alerts |
| 18-20 | Shadow T1 + handle low-priority alerts | First 5 tickets closed |

### Month 2: Supervised Practice
| Week | Activity | Deliverable |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 5 | Handle alerts independently (T2 reviews) | 80%+ accuracy |
| 6 | Complete all 10 common alert type training | Scorecard: 80%+ |
| 7 | First solo shift (senior on standby) | Shift report |
| 8 | Escalation practice scenarios | 3 scenario passes |

### Month 3: Independence
| Week | Activity | Deliverable |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 9 | Full independent shift work | MTTD within SLA |
| 10 | Purple team exercise participation | Exercise report |
| 11 | Write 1 knowledge base article | Published KB article |
| 12 | 90-day review with SOC Manager | Performance evaluation |

### Completion Criteria
```
в–Ў Can independently triage all 10 common alert types
в–Ў MTTD consistently within SLA (вүӨ 60 min)
в–Ў FP identification rate > 80%
в–Ў Correct escalation decisions > 90%
в–Ў Complete and accurate ticket documentation
в–Ў Security+ exam passed (or scheduled)
в–Ў Positive feedback from shift mentors
```

## Minimum Deliverables by Training Stage

| Stage | Minimum Deliverables | Owner |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Foundation | Access complete, baseline reading done, tool navigation verified | Mentor |
| Operations | Mock tickets, escalation practice, IR framework comprehension | SOC Analyst |
| Detection | Query exercises, Sigma comparison, IOC enrichment practice | Mentor + Analyst |
| Validation | Drill result, mock incident report, final assessment record | SOC Manager |

## Escalation Triggers for Training Path

| Condition | Escalate To | SLA | Required Action |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Analyst cannot complete required prerequisites on schedule | SOC Manager | Within 24 hours | Re-sequence plan or add support |
| Simulation or drill cannot be run because tooling is unavailable | Security Engineer + SOC Manager | Same business day | Fix lab dependency or use approved fallback |
| Repeated weak performance in triage or documentation | Mentor + SOC Lead | Within 24 hours | Open targeted improvement plan |

## Related Documents
-   [Training Checklist](Training_Checklist.en.md)

## File: 10_Training_Onboarding/Analyst_Onboarding_Path.th.md
# аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаёҒаёІаёЈаёқаё¶аёҒаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎа№ҖаёҲа№үаёІаё«аёҷа№үаёІаё—аёөа№Ҳ SOC (аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈ 4 аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң)

аёўаёҙаёҷаё”аёөаё•а№үаёӯаёҷаёЈаёұаёҡаёӘаё№а№ҲаёЁаё№аёҷаёўа№ҢаёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҒаёІаёЈаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӣаёҘаёӯаё”аё аёұаёў (SOC) а№ӮаёӣаёЈа№ҒаёҒаёЈаёЎаёҷаёөа№үаёӯаёӯаёҒа№ҒаёҡаёҡаёЎаёІа№Җаёһаё·а№Ҳаёӯа№ғаё«а№үаё„аёёаё“аёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎаёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҮаёІаёҷаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮаё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 4 аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң

```mermaid
gantt
    title аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҡа№Җаё§аёҘаёІаёҒаёІаёЈаёқаё¶аёҒаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎ (Timeline)
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD
    axisFormat  аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳ %W

    section аёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ
    аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ    :a1, 2023-10-01, 3d
    аёҳаёЈаёЈаёЎаёІаё аёҙаёҡаёІаёҘаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ       :a2, after a1, 2d

    section аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҮаёІаёҷ
    аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң   :b1, 2023-10-08, 5d
    аёЁаё¶аёҒаё©аёІ Playbook      :b2, after b1, 5d

    section аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ
    аё§аёҙаёЁаё§аёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаёҒаёҺаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ     :c1, 2023-10-22, 5d

    section аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ
    аёӢа№үаёӯаёЎаёЈаёҡ (Drill)       :d1, 2023-10-29, 5d
```

## аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳ 1: аёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ (Infrastructure & Governance)
**а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў**: а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№ғаёҲаё§а№ҲаёІа№ҖаёЈаёІаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷ *аё—аёөа№Ҳа№„аё«аёҷ* а№ҒаёҘаё°аёӣаёҒаёӣа№үаёӯаёҮ *аёӯаё°а№„аёЈ*

-   **аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 1: аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёҗаёЎаёҷаёҙа№Җаё—аёЁа№ҒаёҘаё°аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡ**
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [System Activation](../10_Training_Onboarding/System_Activation.th.md) - аё—аёіаё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№ғаёҲ Stack аёӮаёӯаёҮа№ҖаёЈаёІ (SIEM, EDR, SOAR)
    -   аёҮаёІаёҷ: аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡаёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙа№ҢаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮ Dashboard аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё”
-   **аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 2: аёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘа№ҒаёҘаё°аё„аё§аёІаёЎа№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёӘа№Ҳаё§аёҷаё•аёұаё§**
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [Data Governance](../02_Platform_Operations/Database_Management.th.md) - а№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷаёЈаё№а№үаёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё аё—аёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ (Restricted vs Public)
    -   аёҮаёІаёҷ: аёЈаё°аёҡаёёаё•аёіа№Ғаё«аёҷа№ҲаёҮаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒа№ҮаёҡаёӮа№үаёӯаёЎаё№аёҘ "Critical" а№ғаёҷ SIEM
-   **аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 3: аёҒаёІаёЈаёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷа№ҒаёӣаёҘаёҮ**
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [Deployment Procedures](../02_Platform_Operations/Deployment_Procedures.th.md) - а№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷаёЈаё№а№үаё§аёҙаёҳаёөаёҒаёІаёЈ Deploy (RFC/CAB)
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [а№ҒаёҡаёҡаёҹаёӯаёЈа№ҢаёЎ - Change Request](../11_Reporting_Templates/change_request_rfc.th.md)

## аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳ 2: аёҒаёІаёЈаёЈаёұаёҡаёЎаё·аёӯаё аёұаёўаё„аёёаёҒаё„аёІаёЎ (Incident Response)
**а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў**: а№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷаёЈаё№а№үаё§аёҙаёҳаёөаёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈаёҒаёұаёҡ Alert а№Ӯаё”аёўа№ғаёҠа№ү Playbook аёЎаёІаё•аёЈаёҗаёІаёҷ

-   **аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 1: аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷ**
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [Incident Response Framework](../05_Incident_Response/Framework.th.md) - аё§аёҮаёҲаёЈаёҠаёөаё§аёҙаё•аё•аёІаёЎ NIST
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [а№ҒаёҡаёҡаёҹаёӯаёЈа№ҢаёЎ - Incident Report](../11_Reporting_Templates/incident_report.th.md)
-   **аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 2: Playbook аё«аёҘаёұаёҒ (Identity)**
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [PB-01 Phishing](../05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Phishing.th.md)
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [PB-04 Brute Force](../05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Brute_Force.th.md)
-   **аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 3: Playbook аё«аёҘаёұаёҒ (Endpoint)**
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [PB-02 Ransomware](../05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Ransomware.th.md)
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [PB-03 Malware](../05_Incident_Response/Playbooks/Malware_Infection.th.md)

## аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳ 3: аё§аёҙаёЁаё§аёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ (Detection Engineering)
**а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў**: а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№ғаёҲаё§а№ҲаёІ Alert аё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№„аёЈа№ҒаёҘаё°аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡаёҲаё№аёҷаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№„аёЈ

-   **аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 1: аё§аёҮаёҲаёЈаёҠаёөаё§аёҙаё•**
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [Content Management](../03_User_Guides/Content_Management.th.md) - аё§аёҙаёҳаёөаёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮаёҒаёҺ
-   **аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 2: Sigma Rules**
    -   аёӘаёіаёЈаё§аёҲ: `08_Detection_Engineering/sigma_rules/` - аё”аё№а№„аёҹаёҘа№Ң YAML
    -   аёҮаёІаёҷ: а№ҖаёӣаёЈаёөаёўаёҡа№Җаё—аёөаёўаёҡ `proc_office_spawn_powershell.yml` аёҒаёұаёҡ **PB-01**

## аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ңаё—аёөа№Ҳ 4: аёҒаёІаёЈаёҲаёіаёҘаёӯаёҮа№ҒаёҘаё°аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ (Purple Team)
**а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў**: аёһаёҙаёӘаё№аёҲаёҷа№Ңаё—аёұаёҒаё©аё°аёӮаёӯаёҮаё„аёёаё“а№Ӯаё”аёўаёҒаёІаёЈаёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёөаёҲаёіаёҘаёӯаёҮ

-   **аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 1: а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯаё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡ**
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [Simulation Guide](../09_Simulation_Testing/Simulation_Guide.th.md)
    -   аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ: [Atomic Test Map](../09_Simulation_Testing/Atomic_Test_Map.th.md)
-   **аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 2: аёҒаёІаёЈаёӘаёӯаёҡаёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙ (Drill)**
    -   аёҮаёІаёҷ: аёЈаёұаёҷ `Invoke-AtomicTest T1059.001` (Phishing Simulation) а№ғаёҷаё«а№үаёӯаёҮ Lab
    -   аёҮаёІаёҷ: Triage Alert а№ғаёҷ SIEM
    -   аёҮаёІаёҷ: аёӘа№ҲаёҮ [Incident Report](../11_Reporting_Templates/incident_report.th.md) аёҲаёіаёҘаёӯаёҮ

## аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаёӘаёЎаёЈаёЈаё–аёҷаё°

| аё—аёұаёҒаё©аё° | аё§аёҙаёҳаёөаёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ | а№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№Ңаёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| аёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ SIEM Query | аёӘаёӯаёҡаёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙ | а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ 3 Query аё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№ғаёҷ 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| аёҒаёІаёЈаё„аёұаё”аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮа№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮа№Җаё•аё·аёӯаёҷ | а№Ғаёҡаёҡаёқаё¶аёҒаё«аёұаё”аёҲаёіаёҘаёӯаёҮ | аёҲаёіа№ҒаёҷаёҒ 8/10 а№ҒаёҲа№үаёҮа№Җаё•аё·аёӯаёҷаё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮ |
| аёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёіа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң | аёӘа№ҲаёҮаёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷ IR аёҲаёіаёҘаёӯаёҮ | аё„аё°а№ҒаёҷаёҷаёЈаёөаё§аёҙаё§ вүҘ 80% |
| аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаё•аёІаёЎ Playbook | а№Ғаёҡаёҡаёқаё¶аёҒаё«аёұаё” Tabletop | а№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёөаёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮаё§а№ҲаёІаёҮаё§аёҙаёҒаёӨаё• |
| аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёҠаёіаёҷаёІаёҚа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ | аёӘаёІаёҳаёҙаё•аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙ | а№ғаёҠа№ү SIEM, EDR, SOAR а№„аё”а№үа№ҖаёӯаёҮ |

## а№ӮаёӣаёЈа№ҒаёҒаёЈаёЎаёһаёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҘаёөа№үаёўаёҮ

| аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң | аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаёһаёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҘаёөа№үаёўаёҮ | аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаёҷаёұаёҒаё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ңа№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 1 | Shadow аёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮаёҒаё° | аёӘаёұаёҮа№ҖаёҒаё•аёҒаёІаёЈаё„аёұаё”аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮ, аёҲаё”аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒ |
| аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 2 | аёЈаёөаё§аёҙаё§аёҒаёІаёЈаё„аёұаё”аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮа№ҒаёЈаёҒ | аё„аёұаё”аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮаёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎаёЈаёөаё§аёҙаё§аёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷаёӣаёҙаё” |
| аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 3 | аёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎаё•аёӯаёҡаё„аёіаё–аёІаёЎ | аё„аёұаё”аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҮа№ҖаёӯаёҮа№„аё”а№ү |
| аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 4 | аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ Drill аёӘаёёаё”аё—а№үаёІаёў | аё—аёі Drill аё”а№үаё§аёўаё•аёұаё§а№ҖаёӯаёҮ |

## а№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№ҢаёӘаёіа№ҖаёЈа№ҮаёҲ

- [ ] а№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷаё„аёЈаёҡ 4 аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң
- [ ] аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаёӘаёЎаёЈаёЈаё–аёҷаё° (вүҘ 80%)
- [ ] аё—аёі Purple team drill аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёҷа№үаёӯаёў 1 аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ
- [ ] аёӘа№ҲаёҮаёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷ IR аёҲаёіаёҘаёӯаёҮ 1 аёүаёҡаёұаёҡ (аёңаё№а№үаёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙ)
- [ ] а№ҒаёӘаё”аёҮаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёҠаёіаёҷаёІаёҚ SIEM, EDR, Ticketing
- [ ] аёһаёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҘаёөа№үаёўаёҮаёҘаёҮаёҷаёІаёЎаёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ
- [ ] аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎ PDPA

## а№ҖаёӘа№үаёҷаё—аёІаёҮа№ғаёҡаёЈаёұаёҡаёЈаёӯаёҮ

| а№ғаёҡаёЈаёұаёҡаёЈаёӯаёҮ | а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў | аё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| CompTIA Security+ | а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 3 | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёЈаё№а№үаё”а№үаёІаёҷаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӣаёҘаёӯаё”аё аёұаёўаёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ |
| CySA+ / GCIH | аёӣаёө 1 | аё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёҠаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёҠаёІаёҚаёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң |
| SANS SEC504 | аёӣаёө 1-2 | аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёҷаёӯаёҮа№Җаё«аё•аёёаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№ҢаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаёӘаё№аёҮ |
| BTL1 | аёӣаёө 1 | аё—аёұаёҒаё©аё° Blue team аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙ |

## а№Ғаёңаёҷ Onboarding 90 аё§аёұаёҷ

### аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 1-2: аёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ
| аё§аёұаёҷ | аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ | аёңаёҘаёҘаёұаёһаёҳа№Ң |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | аё•а№үаёӯаёҷаёЈаёұаёҡ, аёҡаёұаё•аёЈ, аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІ account | а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ SOC аё—аёұа№үаёҮаё«аёЎаё” |
| 2 | аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ SOC 101 + Glossary | аё—аёі Quiz: аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ 80% |
| 3 | аёӣаёҗаёЎаёҷаёҙа№Җаё—аёЁ SIEM (read-only) | аёҷаёіаё—аёІаёҮ dashboard а№„аё”а№ү |
| 4 | аёқаё¶аёҒ ticketing system | аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ/аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё• tickets а№„аё”а№ү |
| 5 | а№ҖаёҮаёІ T1 analyst (аёҒаё°а№Җаё•а№ҮаёЎ) | аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒаёӘаёұаёҮа№ҖаёҒаё•аёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң |
| 6-10 | а№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷ Security+ modules | Module completion |

### аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 3-4: аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮаё—аёұаёҒаё©аё°
| аё§аёұаёҷ | аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ | аёңаёҘаёҘаёұаёһаёҳа№Ң |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 11-12 | аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ top 5 playbooks | аёӘаёЈаёёаёӣ playbook |
| 13-14 | Lab аёқаё¶аёҒ SIEM query | аё—аёі 10 queries аёӘаёіа№ҖаёЈа№ҮаёҲ |
| 15 | аёӣаёҗаёЎаёҷаёҙа№Җаё—аёЁ EDR tool | аёҷаёіаё—аёІаёҮ EDR console а№„аё”а№ү |
| 16-17 | Workshop аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң log | аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң 20 sample alerts |
| 18-20 | а№ҖаёҮаёІ T1 + аёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈ alert аёҮа№ҲаёІаёў | аёӣаёҙаё”аё•аёұа№Ӣаё§ 5 а№ғаёҡа№ҒаёЈаёҒ |

### а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 2: аёқаё¶аёҒаё аёІаёўа№ғаё•а№үаёҒаёІаёЈаё”аё№а№ҒаёҘ
| аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң | аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ | аёңаёҘаёҘаёұаёһаёҳа№Ң |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 5 | аёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈ alerts аёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё° (T2 аё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷ) | аё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҒаёЎа№Ҳаёҷаёўаёі 80%+ |
| 6 | аёқаё¶аёҒаё„аёЈаёҡ 10 alert types | Scorecard: 80%+ |
| 7 | аёҒаё°а№ҒаёЈаёҒаё„аёҷа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§ (senior standby) | Shift report |
| 8 | аёқаё¶аёҒ escalation scenarios | аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ 3 scenarios |

### а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ 3: а№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё°
| аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң | аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ | аёңаёҘаёҘаёұаёһаёҳа№Ң |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 9 | аё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷаёҒаё°аёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё°а№Җаё•а№ҮаёЎаё—аёөа№Ҳ | MTTD аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ SLA |
| 10 | а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаёЈа№Ҳаё§аёЎ Purple team exercise | Exercise report |
| 11 | а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ Knowledge Base article 1 аёҠаёҙа№үаёҷ | KB article published |
| 12 | аё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷ 90 аё§аёұаёҷ аёҒаёұаёҡ SOC Manager | аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаёңаёҘаёҮаёІаёҷ |

### а№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№ҢаёӘаёіа№ҖаёЈа№ҮаёҲ
```
в–Ў Triage 10 alert types аёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё°а№„аё”а№ү
в–Ў MTTD аёӘаёЎа№Ҳаёіа№ҖаёӘаёЎаёӯаё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ SLA (вүӨ 60 аёҷаёІаё—аёө)
в–Ў FP identification rate > 80%
в–Ў Escalation аё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮ > 90%
в–Ў аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒ ticket аё„аёЈаёҡаё–а№үаё§аёҷа№ҒаёҘаё°аё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮ
в–Ў аёӘаёӯаёҡ Security+ аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ (аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёҷаёұаё”аёӘаёӯаёҡа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§)
в–Ў Feedback а№ҖаёҠаёҙаёҮаёҡаё§аёҒаёҲаёІаёҒ shift mentors
```

## аёңаёҘаёҘаёұаёһаёҳа№ҢаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№Ҳаёіа№ғаёҷа№Ғаё•а№ҲаёҘаё°аёҠа№Ҳаё§аёҮаёқаё¶аёҒ (Minimum Deliverables by Training Stage)

| аёҠа№Ҳаё§аёҮ | аёңаёҘаёҘаёұаёһаёҳа№ҢаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№Ҳаёі | аёңаё№а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҙаё”аёҠаёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| аёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ | access аё„аёЈаёҡ, аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ baseline а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§, аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаёҷаёіаё—аёІаёҮа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯа№„аё”а№ү | Mentor |
| аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙаёҮаёІаёҷ | mock tickets, аёқаё¶аёҒ escalation, а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№ғаёҲ IR framework | SOC Analyst |
| аёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёҲаёұаёҡ | а№Ғаёҡаёҡаёқаё¶аёҒ query, аёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё—аёөаёўаёҡ Sigma, аёҒаёІаёЈ enrich IOC | Mentor + Analyst |
| аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ | аёңаёҘ drill, mock incident report, аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒ final assessment | SOC Manager |

## Trigger аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡа№ғаёҷа№ҖаёӘа№үаёҷаё—аёІаёҮаёқаё¶аёҒ (Escalation Triggers for Training Path)

| а№ҖаёҮаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№„аёӮ | аёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё–аё¶аёҮ | SLA | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаё—аёі |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Analyst аё—аёі prerequisite а№„аёЎа№Ҳаё—аёұаёҷаё•аёІаёЎа№Ғаёңаёҷ | SOC Manager | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 24 аёҠаёЎ. | аёӣаёЈаёұаёҡаёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡа№Ғаёңаёҷаё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№Җаёһаёҙа№ҲаёЎаёҒаёІаёЈаёҠа№Ҳаё§аёўа№Җаё«аёҘаё·аёӯ |
| simulation аё«аёЈаё·аёӯ drill аёЈаёұаёҷа№„аёЎа№Ҳа№„аё”а№үа№ҖаёһаёЈаёІаё°а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯа№„аёЎа№ҲаёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎ | Security Engineer + SOC Manager | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ | а№ҒаёҒа№ү lab dependency аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№ғаёҠа№ү fallback аё—аёөа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙ |
| аёңаёҘаёҮаёІаёҷаё”а№үаёІаёҷ triage аё«аёЈаё·аёӯа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаёӯа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёӢа№үаёіаё«аёҘаёІаёўаё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ | Mentor + SOC Lead | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 24 аёҠаёЎ. | а№Җаёӣаёҙаё” targeted improvement plan |

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ (Related Documents)
-   [Training Checklist](Training_Checklist.th.md)
-   [аёҒаёЈаёӯаёҡ Incident Response](../05_Incident_Response/Framework.th.md)
-   [аё„аё№а№ҲаёЎаё·аёӯаёҲаёіаёҘаёӯаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈа№ӮаёҲаёЎаё•аёө](../09_Simulation_Testing/Simulation_Guide.th.md)

### Onboarding Milestones

| Week | Milestone |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Tools setup complete |
| 4 | First solo triage |

## References
-   [SANS SOC Analyst Training](https://www.sans.org/cyber-security-courses/)
-   [NICE Cybersecurity Workforce Framework](https://niccs.cisa.gov/workforce-development/nice-framework)

## File: 10_Training_Onboarding/SOC_Onboarding.en.md
# SOC Analyst Onboarding Checklist

**Document ID**: OPS-SOP-017
**Version**: 1.0
**Classification**: Internal
**Last Updated**: 2026-02-15

> A **structured 90-day onboarding program** for new SOC analysts. Covers access provisioning, tool training, shadow shifts, and competency validation. Goal: new hires become independently productive within 90 days.

---

## Onboarding Timeline

```mermaid
gantt
    title SOC Analyst Onboarding (90 Days)
    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
    section Week 1-2: Foundation
        HR & admin onboarding        :a1, 2026-03-01, 3d
        System access provisioning    :a2, 2026-03-01, 5d
        SOC overview & culture        :a3, after a1, 2d
        Tool walkthrough (SIEM/EDR)   :a4, after a3, 5d
    section Week 3-4: Shadow
        Shadow Tier 1 shifts          :b1, after a4, 10d
        SOP & playbook study          :b2, after a4, 10d
    section Week 5-8: Guided
        Handle alerts with mentor     :c1, after b1, 20d
        First incident report         :c2, after b1, 20d
        Detection rule authoring      :c3, 2026-04-15, 5d
    section Week 9-12: Independent
        Independent shift work        :d1, after c1, 15d
        Competency assessment         :d2, 2026-05-20, 5d
        30-60-90 review               :d3, 2026-05-25, 3d
```

---

## Phase 1: Foundation (Week 1вҖ“2)

### Day 1: Welcome & Admin

- [ ] Complete HR paperwork & security clearance
- [ ] Receive laptop, badges, and physical access cards
- [ ] Review and sign SOC NDA / acceptable use policy
- [ ] Meet SOC Manager and team leads
- [ ] Receive onboarding packet (this document + reading list)
- [ ] Set up communication channels (Slack, Teams, email groups)
- [ ] Add to on-call rotation calendar (observer only)

### Day 2вҖ“3: Access Provisioning

| System | Access Level | Provisioned | Verified |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---:|
| **SIEM** (search & view) | Read-only | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **EDR Console** | Read-only | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **Ticketing System** | Create/edit tickets | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **SOAR** | View playbooks | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **TI Platform** | Search IOCs | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **Email** | SOC distribution list | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **Wiki / Knowledge Base** | Read + contribute | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **VPN** | SOC VPN profile | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **Active Directory** | SOC security group | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **SOC physical room** | 24/7 badge access | в¬ң | в¬ң |

> вҡ пёҸ Write access to SIEM rules and EDR policies granted only after competency assessment (Week 9+).

### Day 3вҖ“5: SOC Overview

| Topic | Duration | Presenter | Materials |
|:---|:---:|:---|:---|
| SOC mission, charter, and structure | 1 hr | SOC Manager | SOC Charter |
| SOC tiers (1/2/3) and responsibilities | 1 hr | Team Lead | Roles & Responsibilities |
| Shift schedule and handoff process | 30 min | Shift Lead | [SOC Checklists](../06_Operations_Management/SOC_Checklists.en.md) |
| Communication protocols | 30 min | SOC Lead | [Communication SOP](../06_Operations_Management/SOC_Communication.en.md) |
| Escalation procedures | 1 hr | SOC Lead | [Escalation Matrix](../05_Incident_Response/Escalation_Matrix.en.md) |
| Incident classification | 1 hr | Tier 2 Analyst | [Incident Classification](../05_Incident_Response/Incident_Classification.en.md) |

### Day 6вҖ“10: Tool Training

| Tool | Training Type | Duration | Trainer | Competency Check |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|:---|
| **SIEM** | Hands-on lab | 4 hrs | Detection Engineer | Write 3 queries |
| **EDR** | Walkthrough + lab | 2 hrs | Tier 2 Analyst | Investigate 1 endpoint |
| **Ticketing** | Hands-on | 1 hr | Tier 1 Analyst | Create & close test ticket |
| **SOAR** | Demo | 1 hr | SOAR Engineer | Run 1 playbook in sandbox |
| **TI Platform** | Hands-on | 1 hr | TI Analyst | Look up 5 IOCs |
| **Internal Wiki** | Self-study | 2 hrs | Self | Navigate to 5 key SOPs |

---

## Phase 2: Shadow (Week 3вҖ“4)

### Shadow Shift Requirements

| Requirement | Details |
|:---|:---|
| **Total shadow hours** | Minimum 40 hours (5 full shifts) |
| **Shift variety** | At least 1 day shift + 1 night shift |
| **Mentor** | Assigned Tier 1/2 analyst for each shadow shift |
| **Activities** | Observe triage, watch investigations, ask questions |
| **Restrictions** | May NOT close tickets or take actions independently |
| **Log** | Document 10 interesting alerts and how they were triaged |

### Required Reading (Complete During Weeks 3вҖ“4)

| Document | Priority | Completed |
|:---|:---:|:---:|
| [IR Framework](../05_Incident_Response/Framework.en.md) | рҹ”ҙ Must | в¬ң |
| [Tier 1 Runbook](../05_Incident_Response/Runbooks/Tier1_Runbook.en.md) | рҹ”ҙ Must | в¬ң |
| Top 5 Playbooks (Phishing, Malware, Unauth Access, Ransomware, BEC) | рҹ”ҙ Must | в¬ң |
| [Alert Tuning SOP](../06_Operations_Management/Alert_Tuning.en.md) | рҹҹЎ Should | в¬ң |
| [Evidence Collection](../05_Incident_Response/Evidence_Collection.en.md) | рҹҹЎ Should | в¬ң |
| [Log Source Matrix](../06_Operations_Management/Log_Source_Matrix.en.md) | рҹҹЎ Should | в¬ң |
| [SOC Automation Catalog](../06_Operations_Management/SOC_Automation_Catalog.en.md) | рҹҹў Nice | в¬ң |

### Shadow Shift Log

| Date | Shift | Mentor | Alerts Observed | Key Learning | Mentor Sign-off |
|:---|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|:---:|
| ____-__-__ | Day/Night | ____________ | _____ | ________________________ | в¬ң |
| ____-__-__ | Day/Night | ____________ | _____ | ________________________ | в¬ң |

---

## Phase 3: Guided Work (Week 5вҖ“8)

### Guided Shift Requirements

| Requirement | Details |
|:---|:---|
| **Mentor presence** | Mentor available (same shift, not necessarily same desk) |
| **Alert handling** | Handle real alerts with mentor review before closing |
| **Escalation** | Must escalate anything P2+ to mentor |
| **Ticket quality** | Mentor reviews first 20 tickets for completeness |
| **First incident** | Write first incident report (mentor reviews) |

### Milestone Checklist

- [ ] Triage 100+ alerts independently (mentor-reviewed)
- [ ] Create 20+ properly documented tickets
- [ ] Correctly escalate at least 3 incidents
- [ ] Write 1 complete incident report
- [ ] Perform 1 guided evidence collection
- [ ] Participate in 1 shift handoff (giving handoff)
- [ ] Propose 1 alert tuning request (real FP observed)
- [ ] Pass mid-point knowledge quiz (score вүҘ 70%)

### Mid-Point Knowledge Quiz Topics

| Topic | Questions | Weight |
|:---|:---:|:---:|
| Alert triage process | 5 | 20% |
| Incident classification (P1вҖ“P4) | 5 | 20% |
| SIEM query basics | 5 | 20% |
| Escalation procedures | 3 | 15% |
| Evidence handling | 3 | 15% |
| Communication protocols | 2 | 10% |

---

## Phase 4: Independent (Week 9вҖ“12)

### Independent Shift Criteria

- [ ] Mid-point quiz passed (вүҘ 70%)
- [ ] Mentor recommends independence
- [ ] SOC Lead approves independent shift status
- [ ] After-hours emergency contact list provided

### Week 9вҖ“12 Activities

| Activity | Completion |
|:---|:---:|
| Handle full shift independently (mentor available by phone) | в¬ң |
| Handle at least 1 P2+ incident end-to-end | в¬ң |
| Write detection rule (Sigma format) with peer review | в¬ң |
| Participate in threat hunting exercise | в¬ң |
| Complete final competency assessment | в¬ң |

---

## Competency Assessment (Day 85вҖ“90)

### Assessment Components

| Component | Weight | Assessor |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| **Written exam** (MC + short answer) | 30% | SOC Lead |
| **Practical lab** (triage 5 scenarios) | 40% | Tier 2 Mentor |
| **Ticket quality audit** (random 10 tickets) | 15% | SOC Lead |
| **Mentor evaluation** | 15% | Assigned Mentor |

### Practical Lab Scenarios

| # | Scenario | Expected Actions | Time Limit |
|:---:|:---|:---|:---:|
| 1 | Phishing email with malicious attachment | Email analysis, IOC extract, block | 20 min |
| 2 | Brute-force login followed by success | Log review, account assessment, escalate | 15 min |
| 3 | EDR alert: suspicious process | Process investigation, containment decision | 15 min |
| 4 | Data exfiltration indicators | Network analysis, scope, escalate | 20 min |
| 5 | False positive triage and documentation | Identify FP, document, propose tuning | 10 min |

### Pass Criteria

| Criteria | Required |
|:---|:---:|
| Written exam score | вүҘ 70% |
| Practical lab score | вүҘ 75% |
| Ticket quality score | вүҘ 80% |
| Mentor recommendation | Positive |
| **Overall** | **All criteria met** |

---

## 30-60-90 Day Reviews

| Review | Date | Participants | Focus |
|:---|:---:|:---|:---|
| **30-day** | Week 4 | Analyst + Mentor + SOC Lead | Comfort level, access issues, learning gaps |
| **60-day** | Week 8 | Analyst + Mentor + SOC Lead | Skill progress, ticket quality, readiness for independence |
| **90-day** | Week 12 | Analyst + SOC Manager | Assessment results, formal role confirmation, development plan |

### Review Template

| Topic | 30-Day | 60-Day | 90-Day |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Tool proficiency | в¬ң | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| Alert triage quality | вҖ” | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| Communication & teamwork | в¬ң | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| SOP adherence | вҖ” | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| Escalation judgment | вҖ” | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| Documentation quality | вҖ” | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| Areas for improvement | в¬ң | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| Recommended training | в¬ң | в¬ң | в¬ң |

---

## Ongoing Development (Post-90 Days)

| Activity | Frequency | Goal |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| 1-on-1 with SOC Lead | Monthly | Career development, feedback |
| Advanced training courses | Quarterly | Skill building (threat hunting, forensics, cloud) |
| Certification pursuit | Annual | BTL1, CySA+, GCIH, GCIA |
| Cross-training (Tier 2 skills) | After 6 months | Prepare for promotion |
| Purple team participation | Quarterly | Hands-on adversary techniques |
| Conference / CTF | Annual | Industry engagement |

## Minimum Readiness Criteria Before Independent Shift

| Requirement | Why It Matters | Owner |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Required tool access verified and basic tasks demonstrated | Prevents access gaps during live handling | SOC Analyst + Mentor |
| Ticket quality and escalation judgment meet agreed score | Confirms the analyst can work safely | SOC Lead |
| Core playbooks and IR framework completed | Ensures decisions follow the repo, not guesswork | SOC Analyst |
| Mentor sign-off and assessment pass recorded | Creates clear accountability for release to production | SOC Manager |
| After-hours contact path and support model confirmed | Reduces risk on first live independent shift | Shift Lead |

## Onboarding Escalation Triggers

| Condition | Escalate To | SLA | Required Action |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| Required access is still missing after Week 1 target | SOC Manager + IAM owner | Same business day | Fix access block before next phase |
| Analyst fails checkpoint twice in the same phase | SOC Manager | Within 24 hours | Add remediation plan or extend supervised period |
| Mentor availability is insufficient for guided work | SOC Lead | Same business day | Reassign mentor or pause milestone |
| Analyst is placed on independent shift without readiness evidence | SOC Manager | Immediate | Pull back to supervised mode |

---

## Related Documents

-   SOC Charter вҖ” Mission and structure
-   Roles & Responsibilities вҖ” Tier definitions
-   Training Program вҖ” Long-term training
-   [Tier 1 Runbook](../05_Incident_Response/Runbooks/Tier1_Runbook.en.md) вҖ” Day-to-day procedures
-   [IR Framework](../05_Incident_Response/Framework.en.md) вҖ” Incident response lifecycle
-   [SOC Checklists](../06_Operations_Management/SOC_Checklists.en.md) вҖ” Operational checklists
-   [Escalation Matrix](../05_Incident_Response/Escalation_Matrix.en.md) вҖ” When to escalate


---

## File: 10_Training_Onboarding/SOC_Onboarding.th.md
# SOC Analyst Onboarding Checklist / Checklist аёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё•аёЈаёөаёўаёЎаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎ SOC Analyst

**аёЈаё«аёұаёӘа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ**: OPS-SOP-017
**а№Җаё§аёӯаёЈа№ҢаёҠаёұаёҷ**: 1.0
**аёҒаёІаёЈаёҲаёұаё”аёҠаёұа№үаёҷаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёҘаёұаёҡ**: а№ғаёҠа№үаё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ
**аёӯаёұаёӣа№Җаё”аё•аёҘа№ҲаёІаёӘаёёаё”**: 2026-02-15

> **а№ӮаёӣаёЈа№ҒаёҒаёЈаёЎ onboarding 90 аё§аёұаёҷ** аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ SOC analyst а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳ аё„аёЈаёӯаёҡаё„аёҘаёёаёЎаёҒаёІаёЈа№ғаё«а№үаёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙа№Ң, аёҒаёІаёЈаёқаё¶аёҒаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ, аёҒаёІаёЈаёҘаёҮаёҒаё° shadow, а№ҒаёҘаё°аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаёІаёЎаёІаёЈаё– а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў: analyst а№ғаё«аёЎа№Ҳаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷа№„аё”а№үаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё°аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 90 аё§аёұаёҷ

---

## а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 1: аёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷ (аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 1вҖ“2)

### аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 1: аё•а№үаёӯаёҷаёЈаёұаёҡ

- [ ] аё—аёіа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ HR & security clearance
- [ ] аёЈаёұаёҡ laptop, аёҡаёұаё•аёЈаёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ, physical access
- [ ] аё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷа№ҒаёҘаё°аёҘаёҮаёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯ NDA / аёҷа№ӮаёўаёҡаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈа№ғаёҠа№үаёҮаёІаёҷ
- [ ] аёһаёҡ SOC Manager а№ҒаёҘаё°аё«аёұаё§аё«аёҷа№үаёІаё—аёөаёЎ
- [ ] аёЈаёұаёҡа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ onboarding (а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаёҷаёөа№ү + аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈаёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ)
- [ ] аё•аёұа№үаёҮаё„а№ҲаёІаёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮаё—аёІаёҮаёӘаё·а№ҲаёӯаёӘаёІаёЈ (Slack, Teams, email groups)

### аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 2вҖ“3: аёҒаёІаёЈа№ғаё«а№үаёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙа№Ңа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮ

| аёЈаё°аёҡаёҡ | аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙа№Ң | а№ғаё«а№үа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---:|
| **SIEM** (аё„а№үаёҷаё«аёІ & аё”аё№) | аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§ | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **EDR Console** | аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§ | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **Ticketing** | аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ/а№ҒаёҒа№үа№„аёӮ ticket | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **SOAR** | аё”аё№ playbook | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **TI Platform** | аё„а№үаёҷаё«аёІ IOC | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **Email** | SOC distribution list | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **Wiki / KB** | аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ + а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **VPN** | SOC VPN profile | в¬ң | в¬ң |
| **Active Directory** | SOC security group | в¬ң | в¬ң |

> вҡ пёҸ аёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙа№Ңа№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ SIEM rules / EDR policies аёҲаё°а№„аё”а№үаё«аёҘаёұаёҮаёңа№ҲаёІаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ (аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 9+)

### аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 3вҖ“5: аё аёІаёһаёЈаё§аёЎ SOC

| аё«аёұаё§аёӮа№үаёӯ | аёЈаё°аёўаё°а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ | аёңаё№а№үаёҷаёіа№ҖаёӘаёҷаёӯ | а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ |
|:---|:---:|:---|:---|
| аё аёІаёЈаёҒаёҙаёҲ, аёҒаёҺаёҡаёұаё•аёЈ, а№Ӯаё„аёЈаёҮаёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ SOC | 1 аёҠаёЎ. | SOC Manager | SOC Charter |
| SOC tiers (1/2/3) а№ҒаёҘаё°аё«аёҷа№үаёІаё—аёөа№Ҳ | 1 аёҠаёЎ. | Team Lead | Roles & Responsibilities |
| аё•аёІаёЈаёІаёҮаёҒаё° & handoff | 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | Shift Lead | SOC Checklists |
| аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷаёӘаё·а№ҲаёӯаёӘаёІаёЈ | 30 аёҷаёІаё—аёө | SOC Lead | Communication SOP |
| аёҒаёІаёЈ escalate | 1 аёҠаёЎ. | SOC Lead | Escalation Matrix |
| аёҒаёІаёЈаёҲаёіа№ҒаёҷаёҒ incident | 1 аёҠаёЎ. | Tier 2 Analyst | Incident Classification |

### аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 6вҖ“10: аёқаё¶аёҒаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ

| а№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯ | аёӣаёЈаё°а№Җаё аё—аёқаё¶аёҒ | аёЈаё°аёўаё°а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ | аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| **SIEM** | Hands-on lab | 4 аёҠаёЎ. | а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ query 3 аёӮа№үаёӯ |
| **EDR** | Walkthrough + lab | 2 аёҠаёЎ. | аёӘаё·аёҡаёӘаё§аёҷ 1 endpoint |
| **Ticketing** | Hands-on | 1 аёҠаёЎ. | аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ & аёӣаёҙаё” test ticket |
| **SOAR** | Demo | 1 аёҠаёЎ. | аёЈаёұаёҷ 1 playbook а№ғаёҷ sandbox |
| **TI Platform** | Hands-on | 1 аёҠаёЎ. | аё„а№үаёҷаё«аёІ 5 IOCs |

---

## а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 2: Shadow (аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 3вҖ“4)

### аёӮа№үаёӯаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё” Shadow Shift

| аёӮа№үаёӯаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё” | аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё” |
|:---|:---|
| **аёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§а№ӮаёЎаёҮ shadow аёЈаё§аёЎ** | аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёҷа№үаёӯаёў 40 аёҠаёЎ. (5 аёҒаё°а№Җаё•а№ҮаёЎ) |
| **аё„аё§аёІаёЎаё«аёҘаёІаёҒаё«аёҘаёІаёў** | аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёҷа№үаёӯаёў 1 аёҒаё°аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮаё§аёұаёҷ + 1 аёҒаё°аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮаё„аё·аёҷ |
| **Mentor** | аёЎаёӯаёҡаё«аёЎаёІаёў Tier 1/2 analyst а№Ғаё•а№ҲаёҘаё°аёҒаё° |
| **аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ** | аёӘаёұаёҮа№ҖаёҒаё• triage, аё”аё№аёҒаёІаёЈаёӘаё·аёҡаёӘаё§аёҷ, аё–аёІаёЎаё„аёіаё–аёІаёЎ |
| **аёӮа№үаёӯаёҲаёіаёҒаёұаё”** | **аё«а№үаёІаёЎ** аёӣаёҙаё” ticket аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё° |
| **аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒ** | аёҲаё” 10 alerts аё—аёөа№Ҳаёҷа№ҲаёІаёӘаёҷа№ғаёҲа№ҒаёҘаё°аё§аёҙаёҳаёө triage |

### а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ

| а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ | аёҘаёіаё”аёұаёҡ | а№ҖаёӘаёЈа№ҮаёҲ |
|:---|:---:|:---:|
| IR Framework | рҹ”ҙ аё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ | в¬ң |
| Tier 1 Runbook | рҹ”ҙ аё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ | в¬ң |
| Playbook 5 аёӯаёұаёҷаё”аёұаёҡа№ҒаёЈаёҒ (Phishing, Malware, Unauth Access, Ransomware, BEC) | рҹ”ҙ аё•а№үаёӯаёҮаёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ | в¬ң |
| Alert Tuning SOP | рҹҹЎ аё„аё§аёЈаёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ | в¬ң |
| Evidence Collection | рҹҹЎ аё„аё§аёЈаёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ | в¬ң |
| Log Source Matrix | рҹҹЎ аё„аё§аёЈаёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷ | в¬ң |

---

## а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 3: аё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷа№ҒаёҡаёҡаёЎаёөаёһаёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҘаёөа№үаёўаёҮ (аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 5вҖ“8)

### аёӮа№үаёӯаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё”

| аёӮа№үаёӯаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё” | аёЈаёІаёўаёҘаё°а№Җаёӯаёөаёўаё” |
|:---|:---|
| **Mentor** | аёӯаёўаё№а№ҲаёҒаё°а№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ, аё–аёІаёЎа№„аё”а№ү |
| **аёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈ alert** | аёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ а№Ғаё•а№Ҳ mentor review аёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷаёӣаёҙаё” |
| **Escalation** | P2+ аё•а№үаёӯаёҮ escalate а№ғаё«а№ү mentor |
| **аё„аёёаё“аё аёІаёһ ticket** | Mentor review 20 ticket а№ҒаёЈаёҒ |

### Milestone Checklist

- [ ] Triage 100+ alerts (mentor review)
- [ ] аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ 20+ tickets аё—аёөа№ҲаёЎаёөа№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё„аёЈаёҡ
- [ ] Escalate аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёҷа№үаёӯаёў 3 incidents аё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮ
- [ ] а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ incident report 1 аёүаёҡаёұаёҡ
- [ ] аё—аёі evidence collection 1 аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ (аёЎаёөаёһаёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҘаёөа№үаёўаёҮ)
- [ ] а№ҖаёӘаёҷаёӯ alert tuning request 1 аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈ
- [ ] аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ quiz аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮа№Җаё—аёӯаёЎ (вүҘ 70%)

---

## а№ҖаёҹаёӘ 4: аёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё° (аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 9вҖ“12)

### а№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№Ңа№ҖаёЈаёҙа№ҲаёЎаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷаёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё°

- [ ] аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ quiz аёҒаёҘаёІаёҮа№Җаё—аёӯаёЎ (вүҘ 70%)
- [ ] Mentor а№Ғаёҷаё°аёҷаёіа№ғаё«а№үа№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё°
- [ ] SOC Lead аёӯаёҷаёёаёЎаёұаё•аёҙ

### аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎаёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 9вҖ“12

- [ ] аёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈаёҒаё°а№Җаё•а№ҮаёЎаёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё° (mentor а№Ӯаё—аёЈаё–аёІаёЎа№„аё”а№ү)
- [ ] аёҲаёұаё”аёҒаёІаёЈ P2+ incident аё„аёЈаёҡаё§аёҮаёҲаёЈ аёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёҷа№үаёӯаёў 1 аё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮ
- [ ] а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ detection rule (Sigma) + peer review
- [ ] а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаёЈа№Ҳаё§аёЎ threat hunting exercise
- [ ] аё—аёі competency assessment аёӘаёёаё”аё—а№үаёІаёў

---

## аёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаёІаёЎаёІаёЈаё– (аё§аёұаёҷаё—аёөа№Ҳ 85вҖ“90)

### аёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёӣаёЈаё°аёҒаёӯаёҡ

| аёӯаёҮаё„а№ҢаёӣаёЈаё°аёҒаёӯаёҡ | аёҷа№үаёіаё«аёҷаёұаёҒ | аёңаё№а№үаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| **аёӮа№үаёӯаёӘаёӯаёҡ** (MC + аё„аёіаё•аёӯаёҡаёӘаёұа№үаёҷ) | 30% | SOC Lead |
| **Lab аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙ** (triage 5 аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң) | 40% | Tier 2 Mentor |
| **Audit аё„аёёаё“аё аёІаёһ ticket** (аёӘаёёа№ҲаёЎ 10 tickets) | 15% | SOC Lead |
| **аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаёҲаёІаёҒ mentor** | 15% | Mentor аё—аёөа№ҲаёЎаёӯаёҡаё«аёЎаёІаёў |

### аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң Lab аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙ

| # | аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё“а№Ң | аёӘаёҙа№ҲаёҮаё—аёөа№Ҳаё„аёІаё”аё«аё§аёұаёҮ | а№Җаё§аёҘаёІ |
|:---:|:---|:---|:---:|
| 1 | Phishing email + а№„аёҹаёҘа№Ңа№Ғаёҷаёҡаёӯаёұаёҷаё•аёЈаёІаёў | аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң email, аё”аё¶аёҮ IOC, аёҡаёҘа№ҮаёӯаёҒ | 20 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| 2 | Brute-force login аё•аёІаёЎаё”а№үаё§аёў success | аё•аёЈаё§аёҲ log, аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ account, escalate | 15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| 3 | EDR alert: process аёҷа№ҲаёІаёӘаёҮаёӘаёұаёў | аёӘаё·аёҡаёӘаё§аёҷ process, аё•аёұаё”аёӘаёҙаёҷа№ғаёҲ contain | 15 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| 4 | аёӘаёұаёҚаёҚаёІаё“ data exfiltration | аё§аёҙа№Җаё„аёЈаёІаё°аё«а№Ң network, аёӮаёӯаёҡа№ҖаёӮаё•, escalate | 20 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |
| 5 | False positive вҖ” triage & document | аёЈаё°аёҡаёё FP, а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ, а№ҖаёӘаёҷаёӯ tuning | 10 аёҷаёІаё—аёө |

### а№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№Ңаёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ

| а№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№Ң | аё•а№үаёӯаёҮа№„аё”а№ү |
|:---|:---:|
| аё„аё°а№ҒаёҷаёҷаёӮа№үаёӯаёӘаёӯаёҡ | вүҘ 70% |
| аё„аё°а№Ғаёҷаёҷ lab аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙ | вүҘ 75% |
| аё„аёёаё“аё аёІаёһ ticket | вүҘ 80% |
| а№Ғаёҷаё°аёҷаёіаёҲаёІаёҒ mentor | аёҡаё§аёҒ |

---

## аё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷ 30-60-90 аё§аёұаёҷ

| аё—аёҡаё—аё§аёҷ | аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң | аёңаё№а№үа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаёЈа№Ҳаё§аёЎ | а№ӮаёҹаёҒаёұаёӘ |
|:---|:---:|:---|:---|
| **30 аё§аёұаёҷ** | 4 | Analyst + Mentor + SOC Lead | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёӘаё°аё”аё§аёҒаёӘаёҡаёІаёў, аёӣаёұаёҚаё«аёІ access, аёҠа№ҲаёӯаёҮаё§а№ҲаёІаёҮаёҒаёІаёЈа№ҖаёЈаёөаёўаёҷаёЈаё№а№ү |
| **60 аё§аёұаёҷ** | 8 | Analyst + Mentor + SOC Lead | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёҒа№үаёІаё§аё«аёҷа№үаёІ, аё„аёёаё“аё аёІаёһ ticket, аё„аё§аёІаёЎаёһаёЈа№үаёӯаёЎаёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё° |
| **90 аё§аёұаёҷ** | 12 | Analyst + SOC Manager | аёңаёҘаёҒаёІаёЈаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ, аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаёҡаё—аёҡаёІаё—, а№Ғаёңаёҷаёһаёұаё’аёҷаёІ |

---

## аёҒаёІаёЈаёһаёұаё’аёҷаёІаё•а№Ҳаёӯа№Җаёҷаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮ (аё«аёҘаёұаёҮ 90 аё§аёұаёҷ)

| аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ | аё„аё§аёІаёЎаё–аёөа№Ҳ | а№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёў |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| 1-on-1 аёҒаёұаёҡ SOC Lead | аёЈаёІаёўа№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ | аёһаёұаё’аёҷаёІаёӯаёІаёҠаёөаёһ, feedback |
| аё«аёҘаёұаёҒаёӘаё№аё•аёЈаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаёӘаё№аёҮ | аёЈаёІаёўа№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘ | Threat hunting, forensics, cloud |
| Certification | аёЈаёІаёўаёӣаёө | BTL1, CySA+, GCIH, GCIA |
| Cross-training (аё—аёұаёҒаё©аё° Tier 2) | аё«аёҘаёұаёҮ 6 а№Җаё”аё·аёӯаёҷ | а№Җаё•аёЈаёөаёўаёЎа№ҖаёҘаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷаё•аёіа№Ғаё«аёҷа№ҲаёҮ |
| Purple team | аёЈаёІаёўа№„аё•аёЈаёЎаёІаёӘ | а№Җаё—аё„аёҷаёҙаё„ adversary аёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ |

---

## Onboarding Timeline

| аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң | аё«аёұаё§аёӮа№үаёӯ | аёҒаёҙаёҲаёҒаёЈаёЈаёЎ | аёңаё№а№үаё”аё№а№ҒаёҘ |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 1** | Orientation | аё—аёұаё§аёЈа№Ң SOC, а№Ғаёҷаё°аёҷаёіаё—аёөаёЎ, а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаё–аё¶аёҮаёЈаё°аёҡаёҡ | SOC Manager |
| **аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 2** | Tools | SIEM training, EDR console, ticketing | Tier 2 Mentor |
| **аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 3** | Process | Playbooks, escalation, shift handoff | Shift Lead |
| **аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 4** | Shadow | а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаёҒаё°аёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ (shadow Tier 1 analyst) | Buddy |
| **аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 5-6** | Supervised | Triage alerts аё”а№үаё§аёўаё•аёұаё§а№ҖаёӯаёҮ + review | Shift Lead |
| **аёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ң 7-8** | Independent | аё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷаёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё° + аёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷаёңаёҘ | SOC Manager |

## Day 1 Checklist

| # | аёЈаёІаёўаёҒаёІаёЈ | аёӘаё–аёІаёҷаё° |
|:---:|:---|:---:|
| 1 | Badge / Physical access аёӘаёіа№ҖаёЈа№ҮаёҲ | вҳҗ |
| 2 | AD account + email а№Җаёӣаёҙаё”а№ғаёҠа№үаёҮаёІаёҷ | вҳҗ |
| 3 | VPN access аё—аё”аёӘаёӯаёҡа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ | вҳҗ |
| 4 | SIEM console access | вҳҗ |
| 5 | EDR console access | вҳҗ |
| 6 | Ticketing system access | вҳҗ |
| 7 | Chat/Teams channel а№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаёЈа№Ҳаё§аёЎа№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ | вҳҗ |
| 8 | Wiki/Knowledge base access | вҳҗ |
| 9 | аёҘаёҮаёҠаё·а№Ҳаёӯ NDA + Acceptable Use Policy | вҳҗ |
| 10 | аёһаёҡ mentor/buddy | вҳҗ |

## Competency Assessment

| аё—аёұаёҒаё©аё° | аёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё—аёөа№Ҳаё„аёІаё”аё«аё§аёұаёҮ (30 аё§аёұаёҷ) | аё§аёҙаёҳаёөаёӣаёЈаё°а№ҖаёЎаёҙаёҷ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Alert triage | Triage а№„аё”а№ү 10+ alerts/аёҠаёЎ. | аёӘаёұаёҮа№ҖаёҒаё• + review |
| SIEM query | а№ҖаёӮаёөаёўаёҷ basic query а№„аё”а№ү | Lab exercise |
| Playbook execution | аё—аёіаё•аёІаёЎ playbook а№„аё”а№үаё–аё№аёҒаё•а№үаёӯаёҮ | Tabletop |
| Escalation judgment | аёЈаё№а№үа№ҖаёЎаё·а№Ҳаёӯа№„аё«аёЈа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮ escalate | Scenario test |
| Documentation | аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒ investigation а№„аё”а№үаё„аёЈаёҡ | Ticket review |

## 90-Day Competency Assessment

### Skills Matrix

| Skill Area | Day 30 | Day 60 | Day 90 |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Alert Triage | аё—аёіа№„аё”а№үаё”а№үаё§аёўаё•аёұаё§а№ҖаёӯаёҮ | аёЎаёөаёӣаёЈаё°аёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙаё аёІаёһ | Expert |
| SIEM Queries | Basic searches | Complex queries | Custom dashboards |
| IR Procedures | аёЈаё№а№үаёҲаёұаёҒ playbooks | аёӣаёҸаёҙаёҡаёұаё•аёҙа№„аё”а№ү | аёҷаёіаё—аёөаёЎа№„аё”а№ү |
| Communication | аёЈаё№а№ү escalation path | аёЈаёІаёўаёҮаёІаёҷа№„аё”а№үаё”аёө | аёӘаёӯаёҷаё„аёҷаёӯаё·а№Ҳаёҷа№„аё”а№ү |
| Tools (EDR/FW) | Basic navigation | Investigation | Advanced hunting |

### Onboarding Feedback Survey

| Week | Survey Focus | Questions |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Week 2 | Initial experience | 5 аёӮа№үаёӯ (scale 1-5) |
| Week 4 | Tool proficiency | 8 аёӮа№үаёӯ + open-ended |
| Week 8 | Process confidence | 6 аёӮа№үаёӯ + self-assess |
| Week 12 | Overall readiness | 10 аёӮа№үаёӯ + manager eval |

### Buddy System Assignment

| Week | Focus | Buddy Role |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1-2 | Tool navigation | Senior Analyst |
| 3-4 | Alert triage | Tier 2 |
| 5-8 | Investigation | Tier 2/3 |
| 9-12 | Independent work | Manager |

## а№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№ҢаёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•а№ҲаёіаёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаёҒаё°аёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё° (Minimum Readiness Criteria Before Independent Shift)

| аёӮа№үаёӯаёҒаёіаё«аёҷаё” | а№Җаё«аё•аёёаёңаёҘ | аёңаё№а№үаёЈаёұаёҡаёңаёҙаё”аёҠаёӯаёҡ |
|:---|:---|:---|
| аё•аёЈаё§аёҲаёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙа№Ңа№ҒаёҘаё°аёӘаёІаёҳаёҙаё•аёҮаёІаёҷаёһаё·а№үаёҷаёҗаёІаёҷа№ғаёҷа№Җаё„аёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮаёЎаё·аёӯаё«аёҘаёұаёҒа№„аё”а№ү | аёӣа№үаёӯаёҮаёҒаёұаёҷ access gap аё•аёӯаёҷаё—аёіа№Җаё„аёӘаёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ | SOC Analyst + Mentor |
| аё„аёёаё“аё аёІаёһ ticket а№ҒаёҘаё° judgment а№ҖаёЈаё·а№ҲаёӯаёҮ escalation аёңа№ҲаёІаёҷа№ҖаёҒаё“аё‘а№Ң | аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷаё§а№ҲаёІаё—аёіаёҮаёІаёҷа№„аё”а№үаёӯаёўа№ҲаёІаёҮаёӣаёҘаёӯаё”аё аёұаёў | SOC Lead |
| аёӯа№ҲаёІаёҷа№ҒаёҘаё°аё—аёіаё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№ғаёҲ core playbooks аёҒаёұаёҡ IR framework а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ | аё—аёіа№ғаё«а№үаёҒаёІаёЈаё•аёұаё”аёӘаёҙаёҷа№ғаёҲаёўаё¶аё”а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈ а№„аёЎа№Ҳа№ғаёҠа№Ҳа№Җаё”аёІ | SOC Analyst |
| аёЎаёө mentor sign-off а№ҒаёҘаё°аёңаёҘ assessment аёҡаёұаёҷаё—аё¶аёҒа№„аё§а№ү | аёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ accountability аёҒа№ҲаёӯаёҷаёӣаёҘа№Ҳаёӯаёўа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаёҒаё°аёҲаёЈаёҙаёҮ | SOC Manager |
| аёўаё·аёҷаёўаёұаёҷ after-hours contact path а№ҒаёҘаё° support model а№ҒаёҘа№үаё§ | аёҘаё”аё„аё§аёІаёЎа№ҖаёӘаёөа№ҲаёўаёҮа№ғаёҷаёҒаё°а№ҒаёЈаёҒаё—аёөа№Ҳаё—аёіа№ҖаёӯаёҮ | Shift Lead |

## Trigger аёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡаёҒаёІаёЈаёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаёЈаё°аё«аё§а№ҲаёІаёҮ Onboarding (Onboarding Escalation Triggers)

| а№ҖаёҮаё·а№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№„аёӮ | аёўаёҒаёЈаё°аё”аёұаёҡаё–аё¶аёҮ | SLA | аёҒаёІаёЈаё”аёіа№ҖаёҷаёҙаёҷаёҒаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳаё•а№үаёӯаёҮаё—аёі |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| аёӘаёҙаё—аёҳаёҙа№Ңаё—аёөа№ҲаёҲаёіа№Җаёӣа№ҮаёҷаёўаёұаёҮа№„аёЎа№Ҳаё„аёЈаёҡаё«аёҘаёұаёҮаёҲаёҡа№Җаёӣа№үаёІаё«аёЎаёІаёўаёӘаёұаёӣаё”аёІаё«а№Ңа№ҒаёЈаёҒ | SOC Manager + IAM owner | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ | а№ҒаёҒа№ү access block аёҒа№Ҳаёӯаёҷа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІа№ҖаёҹаёӘаё–аёұаё”а№„аёӣ |
| Analyst а№„аёЎа№Ҳаёңа№ҲаёІаёҷ checkpoint аёӘаёӯаёҮаё„аёЈаёұа№үаёҮа№ғаёҷа№ҖаёҹаёӘа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ | SOC Manager | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷ 24 аёҠаёЎ. | аё—аёі remediation plan аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаёӮаёўаёІаёўаёҠа№Ҳаё§аёҮ supervised |
| mentor а№„аёЎа№ҲаёһаёӯаёӘаёіаё«аёЈаёұаёҡ guided work | SOC Lead | аё аёІаёўа№ғаёҷаё§аёұаёҷаё—аёіаёҒаёІаёЈа№Җаё”аёөаёўаё§аёҒаёұаёҷ | а№ҖаёӣаёҘаёөа№Ҳаёўаёҷ mentor аё«аёЈаё·аёӯаё«аёўаёёаё” milestone аёҠаёұа№Ҳаё§аё„аёЈаёІаё§ |
| Analyst аё–аё№аёҒаёӣаёҘа№Ҳаёӯаёўа№ҖаёӮа№үаёІаёҒаё°аёӯаёҙаёӘаёЈаё°а№Ӯаё”аёўа№„аёЎа№ҲаёЎаёө readiness evidence | SOC Manager | аё—аёұаёҷаё—аёө | аё”аё¶аёҮаёҒаёҘаёұаёҡаёЎаёІ supervised mode |

## а№ҖаёӯаёҒаёӘаёІаёЈаё—аёөа№Ҳа№ҖаёҒаёөа№Ҳаёўаё§аёӮа№үаёӯаёҮ

-   SOC Charter вҖ” аё аёІаёЈаёҒаёҙаёҲа№ҒаёҘаё°а№Ӯаё„аёЈаёҮаёӘаёЈа№үаёІаёҮ
-   Roles & Responsibilities вҖ” аёҷаёҙаёўаёІаёЎ tier
-   Training Program вҖ” а№Ғаёңаёҷаёқаё¶аёҒаёӯаёҡаёЈаёЎаёЈаё°аёўаё°аёўаёІаё§
-   [Tier 1 Runbook](../05_Incident_Response/Runbooks/Tier1_Runbook.th.md) вҖ” аёӮаёұа№үаёҷаё•аёӯаёҷаёЈаёІаёўаё§аёұаёҷ
-   [IR Framework](../05_Incident_Response/Framework.th.md) вҖ” аё§аёҮаёҲаёЈ IR
-   [Escalation Matrix](../05_Incident_Response/Escalation_Matrix.th.md) вҖ” а№ҖаёЎаё·а№Ҳаёӯа№„аёЈаё•а№үаёӯаёҮ escalate


---

## File: 10_Training_Onboarding/System_Activation.en.md
# Standard SOC Infrastructure Setup

This document outlines the standard infrastructure requirements and setup procedures for a modern Security Operations Center (SOC).

## 1. Core Components

```mermaid
graph LR
    Logs[Log Sources] -->|Ingest| SIEM[SIEM]
    Endpoints[Endpoints] -->|Agent| EDR[EDR]
    SIEM -->|Alert| SOAR[SOAR]
    EDR -->|Alert| SOAR
    SOAR -->|Create| Ticket[Ticketing]
    Ticket -->|Track| Analyst[SOC Analyst]
```

A fully functional SOC requires the following core components:

### 1.1 SIEM (Security Information and Event Management)
-   **Purpose**: Centralized log aggregation, correlation, and alerting.
-   **Requirements**:
    -   High availability and scalability.
    -   Retention policy compliance (e.g., 90 days hot, 1 year cold).
    -   Support for common log formats (Syslog, CEF, JSON).

### 1.2 EDR (Endpoint Detection and Response)
-   **Purpose**: Real-time monitoring and prevention on endpoints.
-   **Deployment**: Agents must be deployed to all workstations, servers, and critical assets.
-   **Policy**: Enforce prevention policies (Block/Quarantine) for known malware.

### 1.3 SOAR (Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response)
-   **Purpose**: Automate repetitive tasks and orchestrate incident response workflows.
-   **Integration**: Must connect with SIEM, EDR, and Ticketing systems.

### 1.4 Ticketing / Case Management
-   **Purpose**: Track incidents, investigations, and analyst activities.
-   **Workflow**: Seamless integration from SIEM alerts to case creation.

## 2. Infrastructure Hardening

### 2.1 Access Control
-   **MFA**: Enforce Multi-Factor Authentication for all SOC tools.
-   **RBAC**: Implement Role-Based Access Control (Analyst, Engineer, Manager).
-   **Segmentation**: SOC infrastructure should reside in a secure, segmented network zone.

### 2.2 Monitoring
-   **Health Checks**: Continuous monitoring of tool availability and performance.
-   **Audit Logs**: Enable audit logging for all SOC analyst actions.

## 3. Network Architecture

-   **Log Shippers**: Use dedicated forwarders to send logs to the SIEM securely (TLS encryption).
-   **Jump Host**: Use a secure Jump Host or VPN for administrative access to SOC infrastructure.

## 4. Tool Access Matrix

| Tool | Tier 1 Analyst | Tier 2 Analyst | Tier 3/IR | SOC Engineer | SOC Manager |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| SIEM (Read) | вң… | вң… | вң… | вң… | вң… |
| SIEM (Write/Config) | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | вң… |
| EDR (View/Triage) | вң… | вң… | вң… | вң… | вң… |
| EDR (Isolate/Contain) | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | вң… | вң… |
| SOAR (Execute Playbooks) | вң… | вң… | вң… | вң… | вң… |
| SOAR (Configure Workflows) | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | вң… |
| Ticketing (Create/Update) | вң… | вң… | вң… | вң… | вң… |
| Ticketing (Admin) | вқҢ | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… | вң… |
| Firewall/Proxy (View Logs) | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | вң… | вң… |
| Firewall/Proxy (Block) | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… | вң… | вң… |

## 5. Health Check Schedule

| Component | Check | Frequency | Owner |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| SIEM Indexing | Verify all parsers active, no queue backlog | Hourly (automated) | SOC Engineering |
| EDR Coverage | Verify agent health on вүҘ95% endpoints | Daily | SOC Engineering |
| SOAR Connectivity | Test all integrations (API health check) | Daily | SOC Engineering |
| Log Forwarding | Confirm all critical sources reporting | Every shift | Shift Lead |
| Backup Status | Verify last backup success | Daily | SOC Engineering |
| Certificate Expiry | Check TLS certs for all SOC tools | Weekly | SOC Engineering |

## 6. Initial Setup Checklist

| # | Task | Owner | Done |
|:---:|:---|:---|:---:|
| 1 | Provision network segment for SOC infrastructure | Network Team | вҳҗ |
| 2 | Deploy SIEM cluster with HA configuration | SOC Engineering | вҳҗ |
| 3 | Install EDR agents on all endpoints | Endpoint Team | вҳҗ |
| 4 | Configure SOAR integrations (SIEM, EDR, Ticketing) | SOC Engineering | вҳҗ |
| 5 | Set up ticketing system and workflow rules | SOC Engineering | вҳҗ |
| 6 | Enable audit logging on all SOC tools | SOC Engineering | вҳҗ |
| 7 | Configure MFA for all SOC analyst accounts | IAM Team | вҳҗ |
| 8 | Implement RBAC roles (Analyst, Engineer, Manager) | IAM Team | вҳҗ |
| 9 | Set up jump host / VPN access | Network Team | вҳҗ |
| 10 | Validate log ingestion from all critical sources | SOC Engineering | вҳҗ |
| 11 | Import baseline detection rules | Detection Engineering | вҳҗ |
| 12 | Conduct initial health check validation | SOC Manager | вҳҗ |

## Pre-Flight Validation Checklist

Before declaring the SOC operational, validate every component:

### Infrastructure
```
в–Ў SIEM server(s) provisioned and running
в–Ў SIEM storage sized for 90-day hot retention
в–Ў EDR management console accessible
в–Ў EDR agents deployed to all endpoints (target: 95%+)
в–Ў Network sensors deployed at critical egress points
в–Ў Log forwarders (Filebeat/Winlogbeat/Syslog) running
в–Ў Ticketing system configured with severity levels
в–Ў SOAR platform connected to SIEM + EDR
```

### Data Flow Validation
```
в–Ў Firewall logs вҶ’ SIEM (test: block event visible in SIEM)
в–Ў Active Directory logs вҶ’ SIEM (test: logon event visible)
в–Ў DNS logs вҶ’ SIEM (test: DNS query visible)
в–Ў Email gateway logs вҶ’ SIEM (test: email event visible)
в–Ў Endpoint logs вҶ’ SIEM (test: process event visible)
в–Ў VPN logs вҶ’ SIEM (test: VPN connect visible)
в–Ў Cloud audit trail вҶ’ SIEM (test: API call visible)
```

### Alerting
```
в–Ў At least 5 detection rules active
в–Ў Test alert fires correctly (trigger and verify)
в–Ў Alert routing to ticketing system works
в–Ў Email/Slack/Teams notification works
в–Ў Escalation phone tree tested
```

## Recommended Log Sources Priority

| Priority | Log Source | Why | EPS Estimate |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| рҹ”ҙ P1 | Firewall/IDS/IPS | Network perimeter visibility | 500-5,000 |
| рҹ”ҙ P1 | Active Directory (DC) | Authentication monitoring | 200-2,000 |
| рҹ”ҙ P1 | Email Gateway | Phishing detection | 100-1,000 |
| рҹҹ  P2 | DNS | C2 and tunneling detection | 1,000-10,000 |
| рҹҹ  P2 | EDR/Sysmon | Endpoint behavior analysis | 500-5,000 |
| рҹҹ  P2 | Web Proxy | Web-based threats | 500-5,000 |
| рҹҹЎ P3 | VPN | Remote access monitoring | 50-500 |
| рҹҹЎ P3 | Cloud Trail (AWS/Azure/GCP) | Cloud activity | 100-1,000 |
| рҹҹў P4 | Database audit | Data access monitoring | 50-500 |
| рҹҹў P4 | WAF | Web application attacks | 100-1,000 |

## Go-Live Notification Template

Send to all stakeholders when SOC is declared operational:

```markdown
Subject: рҹҹў SOC Go-Live вҖ” [Organization Name] Security Operations Center Active

Dear Team,

I am pleased to announce that the Security Operations Center (SOC) is
now officially operational as of [DATE/TIME].

### What This Means
- 24/7 security monitoring is now active
- All security alerts will be triaged within [SLA] minutes
- Incident response procedures are in effect

### Key Contacts
- SOC Phone: [NUMBER]
- SOC Email: soc@company.com
- Emergency Escalation: [MANAGER NAME] вҖ” [PHONE]

### What You Should Do
1. Report suspicious emails to soc@company.com
2. Do NOT attempt to investigate security issues yourself
3. Call the SOC hotline for urgent security concerns

### Coverage Hours
- Monitoring: 24/7/365
- Tier 1 Response: 24/7
- Tier 2/3 Response: Business hours + on-call

Thank you for your cooperation in keeping our organization secure.

[CISO Name]
Chief Information Security Officer
```

-   [Deployment Procedures](../02_Platform_Operations/Deployment_Procedures.en.md)

-   [CISA SOC Best Practices](https://www.cisa.gov/topics/cyber-threats-and-advisories)
